column3: translation
Having placed the thing on the palm, why lick the back of the hand?
He speaks artfully.
Heaven is the help of the helpless.
God Himself is the help of the helpless.

The word of the destitute does not reach the assembly.An assembly of learned men or men in power. The words of the poor, whether they relate to oppression, or to other injuries, or to opinion, are not likely to find admission where alone they can avail.
Light breaks on the head of the destitute.Blame, or suspicion, will fall on the head of the unprotected and friendless. The poor are at work by break of day.

column2: அகதி பெறுவது பெண்பிள்ளை, அதுவும் வெள்ளி பூராடம்.
column3: The destitute brings forth a female child, and that on Friday, under the star Púrádam.Used of one suffering from an accumulation of evils. The condition of the parent, the sex of the child, the day of its birth and its ruling star are alike inauspicious.
What! is tribute demanded of the destitute?
The beauty of the mind appears in the face.

column2: அகத்தி ஆயிரம் காய் காய்த்தாலும் புறத்தி புறத்தியே
column3: Though the Agatti—Coronilla grandiflora,—brings forth by thousands, its fruit remains ungathered.Spoken of the beneficent acts of one not held in estimation, and whose kindness is not therefore appreciated. The proverb is also used of a miser whose treasures are useless.
Does the thief steal in expectation of being caught?

column2: அகப்பட்டவனுக்கு அஷ்டமத்துச்சனி ஓடிப்போனவனுக்கு ஒன்பதாம் இடத்து இராஜா
column3: He who was caught was under the influence of Saturn in the eighth sign, and he who escaped, did so, under the influence of Saturn in the ninth.Saturn situated in the eighth sign from that of one's birth is supposed to exercise a most malignant influence. This opinion of the Hindus appears in many of their proverbs.Hindu Astronomy is made the foundation of a vast system of Astrology. The real movements and the relative positions of the planets are wrought into a systematic connection with a great variety of arbitrary divisions of the signs of the Zodiac, and of the twenty-seven Lunar Mansions. To these bodies are added various Mythological appendages, as beasts, birds, trees, &c., all of which, of course, being a part of the fine-spun theory, help to form in the view of the people generally, a more recondite and imposing system than that of Astronomy itself.The Astrological dogmas of India have an important bearing on all the domestic arrangements and practices of the people. They extend to a great variety of popular superstitions which run out into every department of life. This is seen in the lucky and unlucky months, days end other divisions of time, in the horoscope, which has a powerful controlling influence in marriage, and in other matters relating to the settlement of families and in the more general prognostics for the year as given in the Hindu Calendar. Astrology in its popular developments exerts a powerful influence on all classes of Hindu Society.

column2: அகப்பை பிடித்தவன் தன்னவனானால், அடிப்பந்தியில் இருந்தாலென்ன, கடைப்பந்தியில் இருந்தாலென்ன?
column3: If he who has the ladle be one’s own servant, what matters it whether one be seated first or last at the feast?The Agappai—ladle—here referred to is made of a piece of cocoanut shell with a suitable wooden handle. The shell being very hard and not porous is well suited for the purposes of a spoon or ladle. It is easily cleaned, and well adapted for earthen cooking vessels. It may be seen in every cooking room or shed throughout the Tamil country.
As grain becomes cheaper, enjoyment increases.

column2: அகம் மலிந்தால் அஞ்சும் மலியும், அகம் குறைந்தால் அஞ்சும் குறையும்.
column3: If grain abound, the five also will abound; if grain be scarce, the five will be so.

column2: அகம் மலிந்தால் அஞ்சும் மலியும், அகம் குறைந்தால் அஞ்சும் குறையும்.
column3: If grain abounds, all things abound, if grain he scarce, all things are scarce.

column2: அகல இருந்தால் நீள உறவு, கிட்ட இருந்தால் முட்டப் பகை.
column3: If apart, long friendship, if together, the least touch will provoke hatred.
When apart, even enmity becomes friendship.
Separation secures manifest friendship.
A large halo—a rainy day.
He who knows not the price of grain, knows not sorrow.
The most into which the alligator has plunged is to itself Vaikundam. (Paradise.)
He speaks glibly.
Is it to see the face of one who acts improperly?
A terrible ascetic, an atrocious cheat.
In austerity, severe; in perversity, an adept.Prone to extremes.
Green pasture on this shore for the cattle on that.

column2: அக்காடு வெட்டிப் பருத்தி விதைக்கிறேன் என்றால், அப்பா எனக்கொரு துப்பட்டி என்கிறான் பிள்ளை.
column3: If I say that I am about to clear away the jungle to sow cotton seed, my child exclaims, O father, give me a cloth.Sanguine and very anticipatory.

column2: அக்காடு வெட்டிப் பருத்தி விதைத்தால், அப்பா முழச்சிற்றாடை என்கிறதாம் பெண்.
column3: If I clear away the jungle and sow cotton seed, it is said that the girl exclaimed, O father, a cubit of cloth.
The friendship of a brother-in-law lasts while one's sister lives.
If one has a sister, he may have a brother-in-law.

column2: அக்காள் உடைமை அரிசி, தங்கைச்சி உடைமை தவிடா?
column3: Is the property of the elder sister rice, and that of the younger bran?Said of one who is extremely chary about his own property, and indifferent about that of another.
Whilst cherishing friendship for the sister, are you at enmity with the brother-in-law?
Having taken in marriage the elder sister, why inquire after the rank of the younger?
Having reproached her elder sister, the younger played the harlot.
My sister was indeed born of the same mother: was my brother-in-law also?
Will a dog understand the Vedas, although born in a Brahman village?

column2: அக்கிராரத்துக்கு ஒரு ஆடு வந்தால், ஆளுக்கு ஒரு மயிர்.
column3: Should a sheep come into a Brahman village, each person will get but a hair.After a sheep has been sacrificed in a Brahmanical village and the carcass consumed, the Brahmans may use the hair when performing púja; this interpretation is disputed.

As a dog longed for the consecrated things of a Brahman village.Said when great expectations are entertained of something that is likely to prove illusory: an amusing story is told about this proverb.
As one discharged a camphor arrow at a burning mountain.Spoken of an act done to injure another, fruitless or inadequate, and possibly destructive of the means employed.

He is as if a libation had been poured out to the god of fire.Used of one naturally very black, who on hearing the proverb if he understands its purport, is of course very angry. When water is poured on burning charcoal the cinders appear exceedingly black. The intended reflection is not the less insulting because of the complimentary way in which it is conveyed.
A cauterized wound will not fester much.
Whence sorrow to him who has no connections?Said of one who is so obscure and isolated as to have no one whose griefs he can share or assuage.
Is it improper to bathe in a river?

column2: அங்கத்தைக் கொண்டுபோய் ஆற்றில் அலைசினாலும், தோஷம் இல்லை.
column3: Although he take his body and wash it in the river, no fault will be found.
A body that has been deprived of life (murdered) may not be put into a river.

column2: அங்காங்கு வைபோகமாயிருக்கிறான், இங்கே பார்த்தால் அரைக்காசு முதலும் இல்லை.
column3: He is seen everywhere enjoying himself: when looked at in his homestead, he is not worth half a cash.Said of one who, when out, affects to be well off, whereas he has nothing at all to depend upon.The word (காசு) Cash, is used for several kinds of coin, also for money.
Do not beat down the market price.Do not contravene the established opinions and practices of the people with whom you are associated.

column2: அங்காடிக்காரியைச் சங்கீதம் பாடச்சொன்னால், வெங்காயம் கருவேப்பிலை என்பாள்.
column3: If a song be demanded of a woman going along with her market basket, she will exclaim venkaiyam, karuvéppilai, (Onions, curry leaves.)
One who frequently changes his party will receive two slaps here and three cuffs there.

column2: அங்கும் தப்பி இங்கும் தப்பி அகப்பட்டுக்கொண்டான் தும்மட்டிப்பான்.
column3: Tummattippattan, who had escaped here and there, was caught.Referring to the adventures of a sorcerer.

column2: அங்கும் இருப்பான் இங்கும் இருப்பான், ஆக்கின சோற்றுக்குப் பங்கும் இருப்பான்.
column3: He is here and there and has a share also in the boiled rice.

column2: அங்கேண்டி மகளே கஞ்சிக்கு அழுகிறாய்? இங்கே வந்தால், காற்றாய்ப் பறக்கலாம்.
column3: Why, my daughter, are you crying there for kanji? come hither and you may fly as the wind.Spoken of a proffered change which may be for the worse.

column2: அசல் வாழ்ந்தால், ஐந்து நாள் பட்டினி கிடப்பாள்.
column3: If her neighbour prospers, she will starve herself for five days.
Is one to go to a foreign country, because his neighbour prospers?

column2: அசலும் பிசலும் அறியாமல் அடுத்தவனைக் கெடுக்கப் பார்க்கிறான்.
column3: Regardless of circumstances, he aims to destroy his neighbour.
Stunted grain—friendship at sight.Both valueless.

column2: அசைந்து தின்கிறது மாடு, அசையாமல் தின்கிறது வீடு.
column3: A cow eats moving; a house eats standing.
The fearless goes into the assembly.Used of those who have more courage than discretion.
A car without a linch-pin will not move even three spans.Intimating that appliances however trivial cannot be disposed with, and that the best instruments and weapons require due management.

Though taken to Achchi, a wooden ladle will fetch only half a cash.Achchi being a prosperous place, cooking utensils are there in great demand, yet even there this inferior article will bring no more than its value.Spoken of the intrinsic value of a thing.
There are beggars even in Achchi.See the preceding proverb.
The cheat has got on the shoulders of the conjurer.Said when one deceiver has got the better of another.
Deferential respect is agreeable to every one.
Who will tolerate a presumptuous or impudent man?
A fifth born female cannot be obtained though earnestly sought.A fifth born female is regarded as the special favourite of fortune; an eighth,—as the very opposite.
Will that which was not bent at five, bend at fifty?
Even a fledgeling may scare the timid.
Will one ignorant at five understand at fifty?Early instruction essential to future knowledge. The proverb may also suggest that growth in years will not make one, that is naturally dull, bright.
Demons strike the timid.
Act manfully, but modestly.
Those who were once feared may be made to beg; and they who were once oppressed may be made prosperous.
To the timid the sky is full of demons.

column2: அஞ்சு காசுக்குக் குதிரையும் வேண்டும், அதுவும் ஆற்றைக் கடக்கப் பாயவும் வேண்டும்.
column3: A horse is required for five cash, and he must be able to leap over a river.Used in derision when great results are expected from inadequate means.

column2: அஞ்சு குஞ்சும் கறியாமே? அறியாப் பெண்ணும் பெண்டாமோ?
column3: Are five young birds a curry? Is a young girl a wife?
Of the five senses two are uninjured.Used with reference to some, of several desiderated objects that have been accomplished, or of some things that remain uninjured.

column2: அஞ்சு பணம் கொடுத்தாலும், இத்தனை ஆத்திரம் ஆகாது.
column3: Even if you were to give away five fanams, such haste or precipitation will not do.Precipitance in judgment is worse than actual loss.

column2: அஞ்சும் மூன்றும் உண்டானால், அறியாப் பெண்ணும் கறி சமைக்கும்.
column3: If possessed of five and three, even a young girl may make a curry.The five are acid, pepper, salt, mustard, and cumin; the three, are water, fuel and fire.This proverb is used by a man when his wife has prepared a curry that does not please him.

column2: அஞ்சுருத்தாலி நெஞ்சுருவக் கட்டிக்கொண்டு வந்தாற்போல் வலக்காரமாய்ப் பேசுகிறாய்.
column3: Thou speakest ostentatiously as a matron who has come having her bosom adorned with a táli ornamented with five figures.The táli is a marriage symbol used as a wedding ring is in Europe. Various astrological observances attend the melting of the gold &c. The five forms referred to are the five weapons of Vishnu. Viz. the discus, the club, the conch, the bow and the sword.The Romanists ornament the táli with the figure of a dove.

column2: அஞ்சு வயது ஆண்பிள்ளைக்கு ஐம்பது வயதுப் பெண் கால் முடக்கவேண்டும்.
column3: A woman of fifty must bend the knee before a boy of five.Referring to the deference paid to the male sex by the Hindus.
He aims to humble the timid by force.

column2: அஞ்சூர்ச்சண்டை சுண்டாங்கி, ஐங்கல அரிசி ஒரு கவளம்.
column3: Conflict in five villages is but curry, five maracals of rice is only a mouthful.Spoken of one who delights in the misfortunes of others.
The five letters and the sentiment are like him.Spoken of a child who in feature and disposition resembles its father.
Of what use is beauty to a damsel living in retirement?
The well-ordered are wise; the disorderly are fools.
Modesty is the ornament of a woman.The proverb may be used of moderation generally and self-restraint.
A dwarf without restraint—an unused tomtom-—he wanders about.Used of one irrecoverably worthless.
A bamboo staff is the king of a vicious snake.
A disobedient wife is an evil both to her mother and to one’s self.Spoken of a person inimical alike to two parties.
A disobedient wife and a self-willed husband.Said of uncongenial society.

column2: அடங்கின பிடி பிடிக்கவேண்டும், அடங்காத பிடி பிடிக்கலாகாது.
column3: Seize that which can be grasped, not that which cannot.Aim at the practicable.
Even tender creepers when united are strong.
He will speak abusively, but will not come out.
They who do what they ought not, will suffer what they might have avoided.It serves him right.
A slap is a cake, a cuff is sweetmeat.Spoken of one who is beyond ordinary discipline,
Will an elder or younger brother aid one as effectually as discipline or punishment?
The more a ball is struck, the more it rebounds.
Beware of a beating wind and of falling rain.Beware of things beyond human control.
Does the beating wind fear the sunshine?Spoken of irrelevant means to subdue an evil.
Winnow while the wind blows.

column2: அடிக்கும் ஒரு கை, அணைக்கும் ஒரு கை.
column3: One hand smites, the other embraces.Discipline regulated by love; used sometimes of Divine chastisements.
Can one make a somersault in the bottom of a chatti?
What he received for his labour (ploughing) only paid for the kanji he drank.
Is that which ripens by force fruit?(A favour done under constraint is no favour.)
Is a child to be beaten in order to pour milk into its mouth?

column2: அடித்து வளர்க்காத பிள்ளையும் முறுக்கி வளர்க்காத மீசையும் செவ்வையாகா.
column3: Neither the child that is unchastised, nor the mustache that is untwirled will be right.Implying that due care must be exercised to secure a desiderated result.
Though you may trust one who has administered correction, you may not trust one who has betrayed you.
What! is it nectar at the tip of the tongue while poison is at the root?Said of one, or to one whose professions are at variance with his intentions.
Is the stem nochchi, and the top a castor plant?Intimating that the same characteristics will prevail in a family or tribe.
Why beat him, why seize him? we will subdue him by proper means.
Even a grindstone will move under repeated strokes.
It is the duty of one who has a slave to employ him.
He fell as a palmira tree severed from its root.So perfect was his prostration. This may be used of obeisance or ruin.
As a tree without root.
If the root fail, will not the top fall?
A province in which authority is not enforced will not submit.
An unchastised bullock will not obey.
That which exists in the bottom is likewise in the middle and top.
And besides being beaten, must (one) also eat sour mangoes?Spoken of something imposed on one who is already suffering from questionable inflictions.

column2: அடியென்று அழைக்கப் பெண்டீர் இல்லை, ஆண் எத்தனை பெண் எத்தனை என்கிறதுபோல்.
column3: Like saying to a man how many boys have you, and how many girls, when he has not a wife to whom he can say, Adi.
If the horizon becomes black, the household of the farmer will begin to parch.
Like the springing up of nectar in a kitchen well.

column2: அடுக்களைக்கு ஒரு பெண்ணும் அம்பலத்துக்கு ஒரு ஆணும் இருக்கிறதென்கிறான்.
column3: He says, to cook, there is a woman; for outdoor work, a man.Spoken of needful help already at hand.

Does the dog that breaks (the cooking pots) know how difficult it is to arrange them?Used when a heedless clumsy person has disarranged or spoiled some cherished work on which great pains had been bestowed.

column2: அடுத்த கூரை வேவுங்கால் தன் கூரைக்கு மோசம்.
column3: When a neighbour's roof is in flames one’s own is in danger.
Is it right to destroy one’s neighbour?
Because his neighbour prospers he removes in the day-time i.e., at once.

column2: அடுத்தாரைக் கெடுத்து அன்னம் இட்டாரைக் கன்னம் இடுகிறான்.
column3: He destroys those about him and robs those who fed him.

column2: அடுத்து அடுத்துச் சொன்னால் தொடுத்த காரியம் முடியும்.
column3: By continually urging, the work undertaken may be completed.

column2: அடுத்துக் கெடுப்பான் கபடன், தொடுத்துக் கெடுப்பாள் வேசி.
column3: A deceiver destroys when near, a harlot in contact.
He is a teacher or spiritual guide who gives wholesome counsel to those who resort to him.
A story about butter being placed near the hearth.
Is ornamentation requisite in a hearth or fireplace?The needful is sufficient in common things.
No fire in the hearth, no bran in the mouth.
Should the fire burn, you may parch grain.
Take care of those who are under your protection.
A snake will bite him who removes a fence.
As a lamb died in heavy rain.Said of one coming to grief from want of due care.

Though the heavy rain is over, the dropping from the trees continues.Though the greater evils have passed away, the lesser remain. Though the spoiler is not upon us, the ordinary imposts are demanded by men in power.
As Saturn in the eighth sign approached.Some person or thing ominous of evil.
Saturn in the eighth sign will bring loss.See the preceeding.

column2: அட்டமத்துச் சனி பிடித்தது, அரைத் துணியும் உரிந்துகொண்டது.
column3: Saturn in the eighth sign seized, and stripped off even the waistcloth.
Like borrowing Saturn in the eighth sign on interest.
The leech is not satisfied, nor is fire.Inordinate desire is never satisfied.
As a reptile caught and placed on a cushion.Said of a person in an unnatural situation.

column2: அட்டையை எடுத்து மெத்தையில் வைத்தாலும் செத்தையையே நாடும்.
column3: Although you take a reptile and place it on a cushion, it will seeka heap of dried leaves.
As a dog looks up at a squirrel that has ascended and escaped his grasp.A despairing look after a thing irrecoverably lost.

column2: அணிற்பிள்ளைக்கு நுங்கு அரிதோ ? ஆண்டிச்சி பிள்ளைக்குச் சோறு அரிதோ?
column3: Does the squirrel lack the pulp of the young palmyra fruit, or the child of a Saiva mendicant, rice?To give to religious mendicants is held, by the Hindus generally, to be meritorious.

Is an atom the great Meru?The sacred mountain Meru, forming the centre of the seven continents according to the Mythological geography of the Hindus; it appears to mean the highland of Tartary north of the Himálaya,
An atom is become a mountain, a mountain an atom.

Though one cry after it, will the flood that has burst its bund return?As a premonition, this proverb is used to inculcate caution, and as a remark on misfortune, it suggests the uselessness of regret.
Who can stop a flood that has burst its bund?Spoken of insuperable evils.
Whatever exists in the universe exists in the human body.

Does that exist in the body which does not exist in the universe?The greater includes the lesser. This and the preceeding proverb really relate to the dialectics of the learned and philosophic schools.
Will a fruit be burdensome to Him who bears the universe?Used when some favour is sought of one who is known to possess ample resources.
Of the universe and the body, the former is hidden, the latter open.
Whom will the unapproachable demoness obey?The term Pidári is generally applied to a village goddess who is regarded as an evil being.
A quarrel in a neighbouring house is refreshing.Spoken of envious persons who may be supposed to delight in a neighbour's shortcomings or misfortunes.

column2: அண்டை வீட்டுப் பார்ப்பான் சண்டை மூட்டித் தீர்ப்பான்.
column3: The neighbouring Brahman will breed a quarrel and settle it.Said of one who promotes evil in order that he may gain by it.

column2: அண்டையிற் சமர்த்தன் இல்லாத ராஜாவுக்கு அபகீர்த்தி வரும்.
column3: Disgrace will attach to a king who has not a competent person near him.
She is going to eat rice in her elder brother’s house.

column2: அண்ணனிடத்தில் ஆறு மாதம் வாழ்ந்தாலும், அண்ணியிடத்தில் அரை நாழிகை வாழலாமா?
column3: Although one may live six months with an elder brother, one cannot abide with his wife even half an hour.The first condition is proverbially difficult, how much more so the second?

column2: அண்ணனுக்குப் பெண் பிறந்தால், அத்தை அசல் நாட்டாள்.
column3: If my elder brother has a daughter, my paternal aunt becomes an alien.
An elder and younger brother are natural (birth) enemies.They are supposed to be subject to envy, jealousy end hatredon account of the rights they inherit.

column2: அண்ணனைக் கொன்ற பழி, சந்தையிலே தீர்த்துக்கொள்கிறது போல.
column3: Like taking revenge in the market-place, or in the place where the Vedas are read, on account of an elder brother who has been murdered.
My elder brother is superior, father, blow the fire.Said when an inferior is treated with greater deference than one really entitled to distinction.

The horn of my elder brother is as soft (harmless) as a shivered stick.The foxes of a certain region familiar with the hare had, for a long time, an idea that its fine long ears were formidable horns, and therefore kept aloof from it. At length an old fox proposed that they should try to ascertain the nature of the extraordinary excrescences on its head; and suggested that some of their neighbours should be invited to a feast. This was done; and when all things were ready the foxes led their guests into the festive hall and they were seated a fox and a hare alternately, at table. During the feast one of the foxes professing great admiration for the fine horns of their guests, laid his hand upon one and looking round uttered the Proverb. On hearing this, each fox seized and devoured the hare next to him.
Whatever is left uneaten by my elder brother is an advantage to my sister-in-law.
Elder brother and younger brother are still wanted, O God.
Should a teacher make a slip, it is attributed to his art.

column2: அண்ணாவி பிள்ளைக்குப் பணம் பஞ்சமா, அம்பட்டன் பிள்ளைக்கு மயிர் பஞ்சமா?
column3: Is the child of an artist (a dancing-master) without money; is the son of a barber in want of hair?
It obstructed the throat and the uvula so as to cause them to quake.
If the favour of Annámalai (Siva) be obtained, will the god Mannár pull out a hair?Safe as regards an inferior, if protected by a superior.

column2: அண்ணாமலையாருக்கு அறுபத்துநாலு பூசை, ஆண்டிகளுக்கு எழுபத்துநாலு பூசை.
column3: The forms of worship prescribed for Siva are sixty-four; whereas the seasons for feeding religious mendicants are seventy-four.
Excessive desire entails excessive loss.

Excessive desire or greed ends in eight forms of poverty.The eight forms of poverty are the absence or negation of the eight sources of fruition or enjoyment. These are பெண் women; ஆடை apparel; அணிகலன் jewels; போசனம் food; தாம்பூலம் betel; பரிமளம் perfumes; பாட்டு songs; பூவமளி reclining on flowers.
If the chief and headman are united, stealing may be carried on till day-break.

column2: அதிகாரி வீட்டில் திருடித் தலையாரி வீட்டில் வைத்ததுபோல.
column3: As one placed in the house of the headman what he had stolen in the house of the chief.Said of a thing done unwittingly.

column2: அதிகாரி வீட்டுக் கோழிமுட்டை குடியானவன் வீட்டு அம்மியை உடைக்கும்.
column3: An egg obtained from the house of the chief will break the grind-stone in the house of the peasant.Implying that authority imparts to the insignificant overwhelming power.
A short thick person favoured by fortune.
Fortune flows as a river.
When fortune smiles one may reign as a king.

column2: அதிஷ்டம் இல்லாதவனுக்குக் கலப்பால் வந்தாலும், அதையும் பூனை குடிக்கும்.
column3: Although the unfortunate gets a large measure of milk, the cat will lap it all up.Destiny prevails to thwart benefits when one is unfortunate.
When touched by the fortunate even earth becomes gold.
If thoroughly ploughed, the yield will be abundant.

column2: அதிலும் இது புதுமை, இதிலும் அது புதுமை.
column3: This is stranger than that, and that is stranger than this.
O, priest there is no lack, proceed with the ceremony.Said to a Pujáchári by way of encouragement.

column2: அது அதற்கு ஒரு கவலை, ஐயாவுக்கு எட்டுக் கவலை.
column3: Each object is attended by a single anxiety, whereas the master has eight.
He will eat it all and say come.

column2: அதுக்கு இட்ட காசு மினக்கெட்டு அரிவாள்மணைக்குச் சுறுக்கிட்டதா?
column3: Did the money, diverted from the object for which it was given, avail for the purchase of a sickle?
May you hereafter weep because that also is too little?
Like calling a coward a fearless hero.Said of false adulation.
If that is renounced there is no help, if you go further there is no way.Spoken of evils from which there is no escape.

column2: அத்தம் மிகுதியினால் அல்லவோ அம்பட்டன் பெண் கேட்கிறான்?
column3: It is by reason of his great wealth, is it not, that the barber asks a maiden in marriage?
Although she put in so many ingredients, she omitted salt.Said of something complete in all but one essential.

column2: அத்தான் செத்தான் மயிராச்சு, கம்பளி மெத்தை நமக்காச்சு.
column3: My brother-in-law’s death has not affected me any more than the loss of a hair; but his blanket and mattress have become mine.
If you break open a fig, you will see cavities here and there.
His coming is like the flowering of the fig-tree.Something that never happens.
The more a fig is opened, the greater will be the number of worms found.
Who ever saw the flower of a fig-tree?

column2: அத்திப்பூவைக் கண்டவர்கள் உண்டா? ஆந்தைக் குஞ்சைப் பார்த்தவர்கள் உண்டா?
column3: Are there any who have seen the blossom of the fig-tree? are there any who have seen the young of an owl?Said of things that rarely or never happen.
Like fruit sticking to a fig-tree.
He transgressed and became mad.
Should the mustache of one’s aunt grow we may call her uncle.Referring to improbable contingencies.
Will beauty emanate from one void of symmetry?
The Veda is the staff of the Brahman.
The house of the Brahman is perfumed.It being a requisite for ceremonial usages.
Like a blind man who has thrown his staff into the air.
If white-ants take wing in the evening, it prognosticates excessive rain.
The evening rain will not cease even if one should weep.
I am become as useless as a grain of paddy eaten by insects.

column2: அப்பச்சி குதம்பையைச் சூப்பப் பிள்ளை முற்றின தேங்காய்க்கு அழுகிறதுபோல.
column3: While the father is sucking the husk of the cocoanut his son is crying for the kernel or pulp.Indicating desires beyond one’s means.

column2: அப்பச்சி கோவணத்தைப் பருந்து கொண்டோடுகிறது, பிள்ளை வீரவாளிப் பட்டுக்கு அழுகிறது.
column3: While the hawk snatches away the father’s waist-cloth his daughter is crying for a silk dress.Said in reproof when wishes are entertained beyond one’s means.

column2: அப்பத்தை எப்படிச் சுட்டீர்கள்? தித்திப்பை எப்படி நுழைந்தீர்கள்?
column3: How did you bake the cake? how did you sweeten it?

column2: அப்பம் சுட்டது சட்டியில், அவல் இடித்தது திட்டையில்.
column3: Cakes are baked in a chatty, steeped rice is flattened in a mortar.Means must be suited to the end.
If bread, is it necessary to break and prove it?

column2: அப்பன் அருமை அப்பன் மாண்டால் தெரியும், உப்பின் அருமை உப்புச் சமைந்தால் தெரியும்.
column3: The value of a father is known after his decease, that of salt when exhausted.

column2: அப்பன் சோற்றுக்கு அழுகிறான், பிள்ளை கும்பகோணத்தில் கோதானம் செய்கிறான்.
column3: The father is crying for rice and his son is performing the ceremony of giving a cow at Kumbhakónam.

column2: அப்பன் பெரியவன், சிற்றப்பா சுருட்டுக்கு நெருப்புக் கொண்டு வா என்கிறதுபோல.
column3: Like saying, my father is a great person, uncle, bring fire to light my cigar.Pride of wealth leads one to neglect the observance of established social rules.
If one exclaim, alas! alas! will the crown of the head become cool?Expressions of sympathy are unavailing if not associated with real help.
Experience is knowledge that maketh not ashamed.
Thoroughly acquired knowledge does not fail.Said in commendation of obvious efficiency.
Why ask of the military officer if there is any compulsory service?Why gratuitously seek avoidable evil?
The latter and former halves of the moon—its dark and bright sides.These symbolize the light and darkness, the gladness and sadness of human life.
Quietness is worth thousands of gold.

column2: அமர்த்தனுக்கும் காணி வேண்டாம், சமர்த்தனுக்கும் காணி வேண்டாம்.
column3: An oppressor and a clever man need no landed property.
In the darkness of the new moon the bandycoote finds a way wherever he goes.
Is the food peculiar to the new moon to be had every day?
Will poison be received by the mouth accustomed to nectar?
The arrogance of a barber and the affectation of a washerman.Said when inferiors give themselves airs of importance.

column2: அம்பட்டன் குப்பையைக் கிளறினால் மயிர் மயிராய்ப் புறப்படும்.
column3: If the rubbish heap of the barber be stirred, nothing but hair turns up.The more you examine an inferior thing, the viler will it appear.
Is hair a rare sight to the child of a barber?

column2: அம்பலக் கழுதை அம்பலத்திற் கிடந்தால் என்ன, அடுத்த திருமாளிகையிற் கிடந்தால் என்ன?
column3: What matters it whether the helpless ass lies in an open place or in the adjoining palace.

column2: அம்பலத்தில் ஏறும் பேச்சை அமசடக்கம் பண்ணப் பார்க்கிறான்.
column3: He is aiming to conceal the rumour which is to be brought before the public assembly (for discussion.)
As boiled rice tied up for a journey is untied in an open place.
It is not good to unpack in an open place.Unnecessary exposure of one’s personal affairs is to be avoided.
He speaks like piercing arrows.

column2: அம்பிகொண்டு ஆறு கடப்போர், நம்பிக்கொண்டு நாரிவால் கொள்ளுவார்களா?
column3: Will those who cross the river on a raft entrust themselves to the tail of a jackal?
Are nakedness and misery to continue to the end of life?
Is a veil necessay for a woman in a bad condition?

column2: அம்மானும் மருமகனும் ஒரு வீட்டுக்கு ஆள் அடிமை.
column3: The father-in-law and son-in-law are slaves in the same house.
Why enquire after the relationship of the daughter of one’s maternal uncle?

column2: அம்மான் வீட்டு வெள்ளாட்டியை அடிக்க அதிகாரியைக் கேட்க வேண்டுமா?
column3: Having to chastise a maid-servant in a father-in-law’s house, is it necessary to ask the chief permission to do so?
Whether is stronger, the grindstone or she who grinds?

Like one standing on a grindstone and looking at Arundathi.Arundathi is a star in the Great Bear regarded as the wife of Vasishta, a pattern of chastity. This star is pointed out to the bride at the marriage ceremony.
She will bring forth a king on the grindstone.During the time of child-birth it is not uncommon for the mother to be seated on a grindstone.

column2: அம்மியும் குழவியும் ஆகாயத்தில் பறக்கச்சே, எச்சிற் கல்லை எனக்கு என்ன புத்தி என்றாற்போல்.
column3: As a leaf-plate sought advice when the grindstone and its roller were flying in the air.
If a thievish woman eat the cream will there be any butter?
The strife of Ammai, (a village goddess) has begun, boil and present (rice.)

column2: அம்மையார் எப்பொழுது சாவான்? கம்பளி எப்பொழுது நமக்கு மிச்சமாகும்?
column3: When will my mistress die? when shall I get her blanket?

column2: அம்மையார் நூற்கிற நூலுக்கும், பேரன் கட்டுகிற அரைஞாட்கயிற்றுக்கும் சரி.
column3: The yarn spun by the old dame will only just suffice to form waist-string for her grandson.

column2: அம்மையார் பெறுகிறது அரைக்காசு, தலை சிரைக்கிறது முக்காற்காசு.
column3: Three fourths of a cash is demanded for shaving the head of a old woman worth only half a cash.
What sorrow has the old dame? that of raggedness.
Come, old lady, and receive the old man.
He flies at his neighbours’ property as a demon.
Be blind as regards your neighbours’ property.
No one escapes the decree of Brahma.
The writing of Brahma will not fail in the least.

column2: அயிரையும் சற்றே அருக்குமாம் பிட்டுக்குள் போட்டுப் பிசறாமல்.
column3: Even the Airai (small as it is) will give itself airs if it be not mixed with the food.
Beauty in the unworthy is poison in a casket of fine gold.
As God measured.
Is Appaiyangar the dispenser of Aiyavaiyar’s gruel?

column2: ஹர ஹர என்பது பெரிதோ, ஆண்டிக்கு இடுவது பெரிதோ?
column3: Whether is greater as regards merit, to say Hara, Hara, or to give to the mendicant?

column2: அரக்கன் ஆண்டால் என்ன, மனிதன் ஆண்டால் என்ன?
column3: What matters it whether a demon or a man rules?
To play draughts without a board and to speak without a knowledge of the shastras are alike.
It reached even the king.

column2: அரசன் இல்லாத நாடு, புருஷன் இல்லாத வீடு.
column3: A country without a king, a family without a head.
The sky is the witness regarding the king’s property.

column2: அரசன் அன்று கொல்லும், தெய்வம் நின்று கொல்லும்.
column3: The king kills at once, God delays and kills.

column2: அரசன் கல்லின்மேல் கத்தரி காய்க்கிறது என்றால், கொத்து ஆயிரம் குலை ஆயிரம் என்பார்கள்.
column3: If it be reported that brinjals are growing on a stone in the king’s courtyard they will say that the outcome is thousands of bunches and thousands of clusters.
As is the king such are his subjects.
The people follow the lead of their ruler.
It is the earth i. e., its inhabitants that follows the king.
No one is exempt from duty to the king.
As regards the king it is a word, to the subject it is a burden.
The fear of the king among those in power operates auspeciously as regards the poor.
The help of a king is his mighty sword.
A king and a snake are alike.
A king and fire are alike.
A king, fire. and snake are alike.
Like losing a husband through trusting in a king.
A king observes not the distinction between little and great.
The heavens guard the king.
To the eye of the timid every thing obscure is a demon.

column2: அரத்தை அரம் அறுக்கும், வயிரத்தை வயிரம் அறுக்கும்.
column3: A file will cut a fie, diamonds cut diamonds.
An Aiyampettah pony is preferable to an Arab steed.
If a mungoose see a snake will it let it escape?
The distinction of big and little does not apply to snakes.
Not an atom will move without the permission of God.
Should the favour of God fail, every one will come to nought.

column2: அரி அரி என்றால், ராமா ராமா என்கிறான்.
column3: If I say Ari, Ari, he cries Rama, Rama.

column2: அரி என்கிற அக்ஷரம் தெரிந்தால் அதிகாரம் பண்ணலாம்.
column3: If one understand the import of the word Ari, he may exercise regal power.

column2: அரி என்கிற அக்ஷரம் தெரிந்தால் அதிக்கிரமம் பண்ணலாமா?
column3: May one transgress the bounds of propriety if he comprehends the purport of the syllable Ari?

column2: அரி என்றால் ஆண்டிக்குக் கோபம், அர என்றால் தாதனுக்குக் கோபம்.
column3: If one says Ari the Saiva mendicant is angry, if one says Ara the Vaishnava mendicant is angry.
Like a crow that has taken up rice.
Though there be only one álak of rice, three supporting points in the hearth are required.Alak—a small measure of capacity.

column2: அரிசி உண்டானால் வரிசையும் உண்டு, அக்காள் உண்டானால் மச்சானும் உண்டு.
column3: If there be rice there will be order—honour also, if a sister exist a brother-in-law may.
Though there be only one ulak of rice, three supporting points in the hearth are required.An ulak is two álaks or the fourth of a measure.

column2: அரிசி என்று அள்ளிப் பார்ப்பாரும் இல்லை, உமி என்று ஊதிப் பார்ப்பாரும் இல்லை.
column3: No one takes up rice to examine it, nor does any one blow upon chaff.

column2: அரிசி கொண்டு உண்ண அக்காள் வீட்டுக்குப் போவானேன்?
column3: Seeing you have to pay for the rice you eat why go to the house of your elder sister?

column2: அரிசிக்கு தக்க உலையும் ஆமுடையானுக்குத் தக்க வீறாப்பும்.
column3: Water in a pot proportioned to the rice, and a display of authority in a husband suited to his condition.
Does hatred against rice or a husband exist?
Tiruvaroor with a bag of rice.Spoken of a double benefit—the grain is valuable, and a visit to a sacred place is auspicious.
Of what use is salt in a pot of boiling water without rice?
His house is next to Harischandra’s.Used ironically of a notorious liar. Harischandra, a king famed for veracity.

It is difficult indeed to comprehend the purport of the five letters.The famous five lettered incantation called the மூலமந்திரம் as being the foundation of all spiritual wisdom and all religion, and the means prescribed and proper for obtaining emancipation from births and all the evils thereunto belonging, and union with the supreme.

column2: அரித்து எரிக்கிற சுப்பிக்கு ஆயம் தீர்வை உண்டா?
column3: Is tax levied on dry twigs scraped together for fuel?
The precious body is a stone thrown into the air.
Having performed difficult things he wanders about alarmed at that which is easy.

column2: அரியும் சிவனும் ஒன்று, அல்ல என்கிறவன் வாயில் மண்.
column3: Vishnu and Siva are one; let sand be put into the mouth of him who denies it.
The sickle must be moved, and the landlord’s family must be ruined.
Is it attempted to remove the heat of a burning fever as if it were that of a sickle?
Was it the dexterity of the reaper, or the sharpness of the sickle.Success in an enterprise is mainly owing to tho means employed.
He who listens to the words of a woman will be accounted worthless.
Although the fruit of the wood-apple tree (Feronia elephantum) close by ripens, bats will not approach it.
The haughty dame is said to have brought forth a monkey child.
She who was a precious pearl has become black as coal.
Is it a rare gem or a Murukkam flower?
Through severe drought every blade has perished.

column2: அருட்செல்வம் ஆருக்கும் உண்டு, பொருட்செல்வம் ஆருக்கும் இல்லை.
column3: Divine favour is common to all, material wealth is not.
A cloth tinged red indicates the divine favour.Refers to the dress of religious mendicants.

column2: அருமை அறியாதவன் ஆண்டு என்ன, மாண்டு என்ன?
column3: What matters it whether he who has no discrimination rules or dies?

column2: அருமை மருமகன் தலைபோனாலும் போகட்டும், ஆதிகாலத்து உரல் போகல் ஆகாது.
column3: If it must be so, let the head of my son-in-law go; but the old mortar must not go.Said of things which long possession has made peculiarly precious,
Even a buffalo will not abide in a house where it is not regarded.

column2: அரும் சுனை நீர் உண்டால், அப்பொழுதே ரோகம்.
column3: The drinking of water from a hill reservoir will at once produce disease.

column2: அருள் வேணும், பொருள் வேணும், அடக்கம் வேணும்.
column3: Kindness, wealth and self-restraint are essentials. _

column2: அரைக்காசுக்கு அழிந்த கற்பு ஆயிரம்பொன் கொடுத்தாலும் கிடையாது.
column3: Chastity forfeited for half a cash, cannot be recovered though one should give thousands of gold.

column2: அரைக்காசுக்குப் போன மானம் ஆயிரம் கொடுத்தாலும் வாராது.
column3: The honour that has been forfeited for half a cash, will not return though one give thousands.
Though sandal wood be rubbed, its fragrance will not be destroyed.
The water in a pot half full wabbles, if full it wabbles not.
As one leaped over an Erukku leaf, Calotropis gigantea, under which he had concealed half a challi.A challi is a small copper coin, a fractional part of a larger coin.
Is the hand to be complimented for tying the waist-cloth?
Can one go before an assembly with half a word?

column2: அரைச்சொற் கொண்டு அம்பலம் ஏறினால், அரைச்சொல் முழுச்சொல்லாகும்.
column3: Half a word admitted into the assembly becomes a whole word.
As if macerated gram should sprout.

column2: அரைத்து மீந்தது அம்மி சிரைத்து மீந்தது குடுமி.
column3: After grinding the stone remains; after shaving the head, the kudumi (tuft of hair) remains.A tuft of hair left on the head of a boy or man with a knot at the end. It is considered becoming; and is usually about a foot in length.

column2: அரைப்பணம் கொடுத்து அழச்சொல்லி ஒருபணம் கொடுத்து ஓயச்சொல்லுவானேன்?
column3: Having given half a fanam to weep, why give a fanam to ease?Fanam—a small coin of gold or silver,

column2: அரைப்பணச் சேவகம் ஆனாலும் அரண்மனைச் சேவகம்போல் ஆகுமா?
column3: Though the service bring half a fanam, will it be equal to service in the king’s house?

column2: அரைப்பணம் கொடுக்கப் பால்மாறி, ஐம்பது பொன் கொடுத்துச் சேர்வை செய்த கதை.
column3: A story of one who gave fifty gold pieces for a compound medicine after having hesitated to give half a fanam.
Sores about the waist, and debt to one’s neighbour are both bad.

column2: அரைவித்தை கொண்டு அம்பலம் ஏறினால் அரைவித்தை முழுவித்தை ஆகுமா?
column3: If one goes before an assembly with half learning will it become perfect?

column2: அலுத்துப் பிலுத்து அக்காள் வீட்டுக்குப் போனால் அக்காள் இழுத்து மச்சானிடத்திற் போட்டாளாம்.
column3: When he went to his elder sister’s wearied and exhausted, it is said that she sent him to his brother-in-law.

column2: அலை எப்பொழுது ஒழியும், தலை எப்பொழுது முழுகுகிறது?
column3: When will the waves be still, when will he wash his head?
Bathing in the midst of beating waves.Said of attempting to do a thing under adverse circumstances,
Like a straw on a wave.
Showing his teeth like a jackal of the ginger fields.Said of one who shows signs of disappointment.
As vice wanes, virtue waxes.

column2: அல்லற்பட்டு அழுத கண்ணீர் செல்வத்தைத் தேய்க்கும் படை.
column3: The bitter tears of the oppressed are a file that wears away the wealth (of the oppressor.)
A bed free from anxiety is the most agreeable of all things.
The kind-hearted are free from sorrow.
A time for adversity, and a time for prosperity.
Pour toddy into the mouth of the wicked.Add fuel to the fire.

column2: அல்லாத வழியால் பொருள் ஈட்டல் காமம் துய்த்தல் இவை ஆகா.
column3: Ill-gotten wealth and illicit pleasure are both bad.
Study day and night to be free from impurity.
A wily man does not know the value (of friendship.)
The vicious will evaporate into thin air.
The hasty are deficient in sense.
For hasty ornamentation I take up and sprinkle.
Is ceremonious behaviour demanded when one is in a hurry?
A thing done in an emergency is-not criminal.
When in haste the hand will not enter even into a large chatty.

column2: அவசாரிபோகவும் ஆசை இருக்கிறது அடிப்பான் என்று பயமும் இருக்கிறது.
column3: She is inclined to play the harlot, and is afraid that her husband will beat her.

column2: அவசாரிக்கு வாய் பெரிது, அஞ்சு ஆறு அரிசிக்குக் கொதி பெரிது.
column3: The mouth of the harlot is large, and the bubbling of five or six grains of rice when boiling is excessive.

column2: அவசாரிக்கு ஆணை இல்லை, திருடிக்குத் தெய்வம் இல்லை.
column3: The harlot has no oath, the thief has no god.Spoken of one wholly derelict.

column2: அவசாரி ஆடினாலும் அதிஷ்டம் வேண்டும் திருடப் போனாலும் திறமை வேண்டும்.
column3: If one plays the harlot fortune is wanted, and when one goes to steal ability is needed.

column2: அவசாரியென்று ஆனைமேல் ஏறலாம் திருடியென்று தெருவில் வரலாமா?
column3: As an abandoned woman one may ride on an elephant, but can one go along a street as a thief?
Chicanery ends in one’s ruin.
God himself is the help of a family in extremities.
The worthless and the cheat have no need of land.
Time is better spent in austerities than in vanity.
Having acted reproachfully he speaks flatteringly.
He is surely as sharp as the edge of a sickle.Spoken ironically of a man of vain pretensions.
His wing is broken.

column2: அவர் அவர் மனசே அவர் அவர்க்குச் சாட்சி.
column3: Each one's mind is the witness that acquits or condemns.
The supreme Ruler knows the purposes of every one.

column2: அவலட்சணம் உள்ள குதிரைக்குச் சுழி சுத்தம் பார்க்கிறதில்லை.
column3: The circular curls of an ugly horse are not examined.
He who leads a useless life will die a miserable death.
As if one beat an empty mortar thinking it contained unhusked steeped rice.Spoken of absence of mind.
Her singing is like the voice of the kuyil (an Indian cuckoo.)
Her best cooking-pot was broken in the middle of the road.
This person will rise and eat before him.
He has neither a cloth nor a knife.Said of one utterly destitute.
That which is in his keeping is my livelihood.
He is now under the auspicious influence of the planet Venus.Spoken of one who is the favourite of fortune.
The intimacy or friendship of these two persons is that of the buffalo.
His speech consists of quarters and halves of words.Undecisive.
His prosperity is scattered like crabs from a broken vessel.

column2: அவனே இவனே என்பதைவிடச் சிவனே சிவனே என்கிறது நல்லது.
column3: It is better to cry Siva, Siva, than opprobriously to reproach others.
He has authority both to behead and to release.

column2: அவனை அவன் பேச்சிட்டுப் பேச்சு வாங்கி, ஆமை மல்லாத்துகிறாப்போல் மல்லாத்திப்போட்டான்.
column3: Having fished out his secrets by cunning he turned him upside down as a turtle is turned.
He cannot equal him though he raise himself to the top of a ladder.Said of one who is far inferior to another either socially or intellectually.
When his tongue moves the country moves.

column2: அவன் அருள் அற்றால் அனைவரும் அற்றார், அவன் அருள் உற்றால் அனைவரும் உற்றார்.
column3: Without his favour all are destitute, if he favour, all are prosperous.Said of God’s grace and displeasure.
He is a cold firebrand.Said of a person really dangerous though apparently not so.
Even the grass under his footsteps will not die.Used when speaking of one remarkable for gentle habits.
The place whereon he treads bursts into flames.Spoken of the reckless and violent.
His words exceed all bounds.Said of one who delights in abusing others.

column2: அவன் காலால் இட்ட வேலையைக் கையால் செய்வான்.
column3: The work that was indicated by the foot he will perform by the hand.

column2: அவன் சாதி எந்தப் புத்தி குலம் எந்த ஆசாரமோ அதுதான் வரும்.
column3: The devices of his caste and habits of his tribe will predominate.
That which he tied with his foot cannot be loosened by the hand.Spoken of one of superior skill.
Unless he move not an atom will move.

column2: அவன் அவன் எண்ணத்தை ஆண்டவன் ஆக்கினாலும் ஆக்குவான், அழித்தாலும் அழிப்பான்.
column3: Every man’s purpose will be either effectuated or frustrated by the Supreme Ruler.
He is sharpening a knife for another's throat.
His mind is full of sores, his body is covered with blisters.
To me he is Saturn in the eighth sign.The most malignant of enemies.
He seeks friendship and prays for a skin.

column2: அவன் தன்னாலே தான் கெட்டால் அண்ணாவி என்ன செய்வான்.
column3: What can his teacher do if he ruin himself?

column2: அவன் காலால் கீறினதை நான் நாவால் அழிக்கிறேன்.
column3: What he has scored with his feet I efface with my tongue.
Do you consider him as a green-herb nipped off?
He took another’s hand and struck his eyes.
He is a bird without wings.
He is preparing to cook my head.

column2: அவன் அவன் செய்த வினை அவன் அவனுக்கே.
column3: Every one is responsible for his own actions.
He aims to scatter me to the winds.
He breaks out saying burn him, fry him.Said of one who suddenly bursts into a rage.
Having run and sung, he at length sank like the pulse.
He is the man who encountered and fought the champion.
Is he a man of limited wealth?Spoken of a slovenly miser.
Can fire be kindled in mid-air?No, except by the Almighty "who holds the lightning in his hand and tells it whom to strike."
Like the story of the germination of boiled peas.
Better is the hatred of the wise than the friendship of fools.

column2: அவிழ் என்ன செய்யும் அஞ்சு குணம் செய்யும் பொருள் என்ன செய்யும் பூவை வசம் செய்யும்.
column3: What can rice do? it can work the five senses, what can wealth do? it can subdue the world.
One who belongs to this convention and to that convention.
If I had so much should I not arrange my chatties earthen cooking vessels and prosper.Spoken regretfully of succour which ought to be and is not afforded.

column2: அழகிலே பிறந்த பவளக்கொடி அகத்திலே பிறந்த சாணிக்கூடை.
column3: In beauty equal to natural coral, at home a cow-dung basket.
Are there any who cut the nose for the sake of beauty.

column2: அழகு இருந்து அழும், அதிஷ்டம் இருந்து உண்ணும்.
column3: Beauty will sit and weep, fortune will sit and eat.
O beautiful maid, frail kite, the comedian calls you.Spoken of a female whose demeanour is inconsistent with modesty.
A jewel worn as an ornament may aid in adversity.Gold ornaments are often given as security for money borrowed to trade with, as well as to serve in pressing emergencies.
They who advise weeping are one’s friends, those who advise laughing are strangers.Spoken with reference to the character of advisers.
He who counsels us to weep does so to favour life; he who counsels mirth seeks our ruin.
God dements him who is to be destroyed.
What matters it with whom an abandoned woman goes?

column2: அழிந்த நந்தவனத்தில் அசுவம் மேய்ந்து என்ன, கழுதை மேய்ந்து என்ன?
column3: What matters it whether a horse or an ass grazes in a garden that lies waste?
One who seeks to maintain an unjust claim and a king who extenuates crime.Both are injurious to society.
Weeping hypocrites, canting sycophants and religious formalists.
A weeping man and a smiling woman are not to be trusted.

column2: அழுகிற வீட்டில் இருந்தாலும் இருக்கலாம், ஒழுகுகிற வீட்டில் இருக்கலாகாது.
column3: Though one may abide in a house of mourning, it is not possible to remain in one that is leaky.
Like showing a ripe plantain to a crying child.A premium on naughtiness.
He tickles when one is weeping.Spoken of something that is obtruded at an inconvenient time.

column2: அழுகிறதற்கு அரைப்பணம் கொடுத்து, ஓய்கிறதற்கு ஒரு பணம் கொடு.
column3: Having given half a fanam for weeping, give a fanam to cease.
Are grief and self-will things to be made sport of?
A ruby in a dirty rag.

column2: அழுக்கை அழுக்குக் கொல்லும் இழுக்கை இழுக்குக் கொல்லும்.
column3: Dirt will remove dirt, reproach will overcome reproach.

column2: அழுக்கைத் துடைத்து மடிமேல் வைத்தாலும் புழுக்கைக் குணம் போகாது.
column3: Though cleaned and placed on one's knee, the mean disposition of a slave or low person will not leave him.
A weeping eye and a running nose.
He who is weeping has no pride.
A crying child obtains milk.
The weeping child will gain strength.
Although she may weep, she herself must bare the child.

column2: அழுத்த நெஞ்சன் ஆருக்கும் உதவான், இளகின நெஞ்சன் எவர்க்கும் உதவுவான்.
column3: The hard-hearted will be of use to none, the tender hearted will be of use to all.

column2: அழுவார் அற்ற பிணமும் ஆற்றுவார் அற்ற சுடலையும்.
column3: A corpse unwept and a funeral pyre unquenched.An affair or person forsaken by all.

column2: அழுவார் அழுவார் தம் தம் துக்கமே திருவன்பெண்டீருக்கு அழுவாரில்லை.
column3: All weep on account of their own griefs, none oh account of Tiruvan’s wife.Spoken of feigned sympathy and also of that outward expression of sorrow which is occasioned by surrounding circumstances.
The mother of a bride or bridegroom will not visit their new relations unless invited.
There are firewood carriers even in Alagapuri, (the city of Kuvéra.)Kuvéra a wealthy king; now regent of the North and the guardian of riches.

column2: அளகாபுரி கொள்ளையானாலும், அதிஷ்ட ஈனனுக்கு ஒன்றும் இல்லை.
column3: Although Alagapuri be given up to plunder, the unfortunate gains nothing thereby.

column2: அளகேசன் ஆனாலும் அளவு அறிந்து செலவு செய்யவேண்டும்.
column3: Though as wealthy as Kuvéra you must keep your expenses within due bounds.
Does the measure know the price of grain?
He will measure in the accustomed measure.

column2: அளந்தால் ஒரு சாணில்லை அரிந்தால் ஒரு சட்டிகாணாது.
column3: When measured it is not a span long, when chopped up it does not fill a chatti.

column2: அளந்து அளந்த நாழி ஒழிந்து ஒழிந்து வரும்.
column3: By repeated measurement articles will be diminished.Constant use wears out a thing.

column2: அளவளாவில்லாதவன் வாழ்க்கை குளவளாக் கோடின்றி நீர் நிறைந்தற்று.
column3: The prosperity of him who does not cultivate sociality is like the filling of a tank without a bank.
If in excess even nectar is poison.
That of which no part is taken out will not lessen, that which is not uttered will not get out.

column2: அள்ளிக் கொடுத்தால் சும்மா, அளந்துக் கொடுத்தால் கடன்.
column3: If taken up and given in handsful it is free, if measured out it is charged for.

column2: அள்ளிப் பால் வார்க்கையிலே சொல்லிப் பால் வார்த்திருக்குது.
column3: Whilst teeming milk already laded, the quantity is given.

column2: அள்ளுகிறவன் இடத்தில் இருக்கல் ஆகாது கிள்ளுகிறவன் இடத்தில் இருந்தாலும்.
column3: One cannot afford to live with a plunderer though he might live with a pilferer.
It is said that a dog imagines every thing taken up by the hand is intended for himself.
An over-suspicious man lost his wife and carried her sixty miles weeping.Some mischievous persons having concealed her in a box employed him to carry it to the sea-shore.
She will over-boil the rice; or she will boil it imperfectly.
Excessive sharpness is perfect bluntness.
Hard dealing ends in loss.
The streaked lizard opens not its mouth.
If a streaked lizard bite, death ensues.
That is happiness which springs from virtue.
No cold to them that are completely wet.
On a full understanding of the party admit to friendship.
When the hypercritical go to market they neither buy nor sell.
One highly learned fell into a pot of gruel.

column2: அறப்பத்தினி ஆமுடையானை அப்பா என்று அழைத்த கதை.
column3: The story of a scrupulousy chaste wife who addressed her husband as Appa, father.
As the thoroughly practised musk-rat was drowned in the refuse-pan.
When twisted to excess, fibres snap.
If over-twisted it will snap.
When excessively twisted, strands break.
Virtue, wealth and pleasure are not common to all.
As when being thoroughly strained the uppermost portion of the boiled rice fell into the refuse-pan.
(Better) to live in subjection to a mountaineer than to be the subject of a cruel tyrant.
It is the merit of the wise to gain knowledge by meditation.

column2: அறிந்த ஆண்டையென்று கும்பிடப் போனால், உங்கள் அப்பன் பத்துப் பணம் கொடுக்கவேண்டும் கொடு என்றான்.
column3: When I went to a master whom I knew, to pay my respects, he said, your father owes ten fanams, give it me.

column2: அறிந்த பார்ப்பான் சிநேகிதன் ஆறு காசுக்கு மூன்று தோசையா?
column3: A known brahman is a friend; are there only three pancakes for six cash?Said of taking advantage of previous acquaintance in a bargain.

column2: அறிந்தும் கெட்டேன் அறியாமலும் கெட்டேன் சொறிந்து புண் ஆயிற்று.
column3: Knowingly and unknowingly I have been ruined; scratching has caused sores.
The story of one who claimed as his slave the man who had paid his respects to him because he was an acquaintance.
Do any become vicious by increasing knowledge?
All the days before the age of discretion are as the days before birth.

column2: அறியாமல் தாடியை வளர்த்து அம்பட்டன் கையில் கொடுக்கவா?
column3: Have I grown my beard not knowing that it would fall into the hands of the barber?Said with reference to the loss of a cherished object.
Like the idea of an innocent child.
Do not ask advice of the ignorant.
Though fools are told a thousand times the thing is useless.
Who possesses information? Those who have examined a thing thoroughly.
An ignorant man is despised even by women.
So to sleep as to be incapable of feeling is an impossibility.
He that increaseth knowledge increaseth sorrow.
The ignorant are not manly.
As a chrysalis destitute of intelligence.
Wisdom is regulated by knowledge, good conduct by love.
Conscious guilt will fret the heart.

column2: அறிவு தரும் வாயும் அன்பு உரைக்கும் நாவும்.
column3: A mouth that instructs and a tongue uttering words of love.Spoken of one characterized by wisdom and love.
Even a king will approve of wise men.
To obtain the favour of the wise is enough.
I know, I know (i.e., I know it well) the leaf of the banyan tree is like that of the tamarind tree.
Fifty-eight sickles on the hip of one that cannot reap.Used in contempt of mere show and parade.
The son of a widow has evil gestures throughout his body.Said of persons irreclaimably and naturally vicious.
Fluttering as a fowl with its throat cut.
The story of a widow who gave birth to a son.
Seventy rags for sixty days.

column2: அறுபத்துநாலு அடிக் கம்பத்தின்மேல் ஆடினாலும், கீழே வந்து தியாகம் வாங்க வேண்டும்.
column3: Although one may balance himself on a pole sixty-four feet high, he has to descend to receive gifts.
Sixty-eight tricks.Said of the various impediments which one meets with in carrying out a scheme.
In harvest time even a rat has five wives.

column2: அறுவாய்க்கு வாய் பெரிது, அரிசிக்கு கொதி பெரிது.
column3: The mouth of the rice-pot being enlarged by fracture, the rice must be boiled the longer.
One must appear in the assembly, must he not, after having practised privately?
One who leads private persons into a place of justice and perverts judgment.
The least unlawful desire destroys a myriad acts of austerity.
Is beauty (self-respect) to be forfeited for a mere trifle?
Although the broom is inferior it will lay the dust in the house.
The friendship of the mean will issue in fatal results.
The friendship of the base is dangerous.
If a low-bred man obtain wealth, he will carry an umbrella at midnight.

column2: அற்பன் பணம் படைத்தால் வைக்க வகை அறியான்.
column3: When a mean person acquires wealth he knows not how to take care of it.
Fitted to the opportunity.

column2: அனல் குளிர் வெதுவெதுப்பு இம்மூன்று காலமும் ஆறு காலத்திற்குள் அடக்கம்.
column3: Three kinds of weather, hot, cold and temperate are included in the six seasons.A year is divided into six seasons of two months each, beginning with August. The names of the seasons are 1. கார் cloudy. 2 கூதிர் cold. 3. முன்பனி evening dew. 4. பின்பனி morning dew. 5. இளவேனில் mild heat. 6. முதுவேனில் very hot.
Even a common medicine may prove effectual after a disease has passed the crisis.

column2: அன்பற்ற மாமியாருக்கு கால் பட்டாலும் குற்றம், கை பட்டாலும் குற்றம்.
column3: The touch whether of the foot or the hand is an offence to one’s unkind mother-in-law.
Even obeisance is an offence to an unloving mother-in-law.
Don’t enter the portal of the uncharitable.
Know a loving friend in adversity.
They who are destitute of love are void of influence.
A loving disposition is a river without a ripple.
Where love reigns the impossible may be attained.

column2: அன்புடையானைக் கொடுத்து அலையச்சே அசல் வீட்டுக்காரன் வந்து அழைத்த கதை.
column3: The story of a neighbour who invited a woman in distress at the loss of her husband.
Love is all-important, and it is it’s own reward.

column2: அன்றற ஆயிரம் பொன்னிலும், நின்றற ஒரு காசு பெரிது.
column3: A single cash saved is greater than thousands of gold spent as soon as acquired.
Pay at once, delay is bad.
The body we then saw has suffered no decay.The word மேனி is also used for beauty, freshness.

column2: அன்றும் இல்லைத் தையல், இன்றும் இல்லை பொத்தல்.
column3: There was no stitching then, nor is there any hole now.
Buy when you require, be slow to make purchases, do not make daily purchases.

column2: அன்று தின்ற சோறு ஆறு மாதத்திற்கு ஆமா?
column3: Will the boiled rice eaten then suffice for six months?

column2: அன்று தின்ற ஊண் ஆறு மாசத்துப் பசியை அறுக்கும்.
column3: The food which was then eaten will keep one free from hunger for six months.
Will he who wrote then, erase and write again?Referring to the changeless preordination of God.

column2: அன்றைக்கு கிடைக்கிற ஆயிரம் பொன்னிலும் இன்றைக்குக் கிடைத்த அரைக்காசு பெரிது.
column3: The half cash obtained to-day is greater than thousands of gold to be got hereafter.

column2: அன்றைக்குத் தின்கிற பலாக்காயைவிட இன்றைக்கு தின்கிற களாக்காய் பெரிது.
column3: The Kalà fruit, carissa diffusa, of to-day is better than the jack fruit of the future.
A handful of boiled rice has become as precious as a handful of sugar.
The property of food is the support of life.The supporting, nourishing quality of grain is its special property, without which, as life is now conditioned, it cannot exist.

column2: அன்ன மயம் இன்றிப் பின்னை மயம் இல்லை.
column3: There is no physical virtue in aught but food.The same as the preceding.

column2: அன்னம் மிகக் கொள்வானும் ஆடை அழுக்கு ஆவானும் பதர்.
column3: The glutton and the sloven are alike worthless.
May we break through the wall of the house of those who fed us?
When food fails, the five senses fail.Referring to the exercise of the senses as dependent on the health of the body and its dependence on food.

column2: அன்ன நடை நடக்கபோய்த் தன் நடையும் கெட்டாற்போல.
column3: As in attempting to walk like a swan, the crow lost even its natural gait.
When food or grain is scarce, all is scarce.

column2: அன்னப்பாலுக்குச் சிங்கியடித்தவள் ஆவின் பாலுக்குச் சர்க்கரை தேடுகிறாள்.
column3: She who was wandering about for rice water is seeking sugar to mix in cow’s milk.
What gift is there that equals the gift of food?
Strange women will not avail in avail in adversity.
Whom will he help that does not help his mother?
A crimson sunset betokens abiding rain.
The difference is as great as that between an elephant and a mosquito.
A scoffer will be destroyed.

column2: ஆகாத நாளையில் பிள்ளை பிறந்தால், அண்டை வீட்டுக்காரனை என்ன செய்யும்?
column3: If a child be born at an inauspicious time what evil will it occasion to a neighbour?

In a false almanac, the sixty hours are to be rejected.In astrological calculations a time of three and three quarters to four Indian hours (நாழிகை 24 minutes) for the asterism that rules the day is considered unlucky: it is called தியாச்சியம் rejected.An imperfect or uncertain formula should be wholly rejected.
The family of the wicked must be destroyed by associating with it.
If about to undertake a difficult work do so after due inquiry.
Is a brahmanicide a suitable witness for an abandoned wretch?
Like pointing out the middle of the sky.An impossibility.
Can a hawk carry away the sky?

column2: ஆகாயத்தில் பறக்க உபதேசிப்பேன், என்னைத் தூக்கி ஆற்றுக்கு அப்பால் விடு என்கிறான் குரு.
column3: The spiritual guide observed, I will teach you how to fly through the air, take me up and convey me to the other side of the river.
A powerful thunder-clap will occasion tremor.
Will white ants destroy an iron pillar that reaches to the clouds?The proverb was used by Sita when speaking to Ravana.
Is there not sufficient space for one to go and look at the sky?
Can the air be bit so as to leave a mark?
The sky brought forth, the earth supported.
If favoured by fortune medicine will take effect in due time.
In times of prosperity, even a slave woman may bring forth a female child.
A sound fruit may be known when it begins to set.
In auspicious times it succeeds, in inauspicious times it fails.
Will he who waited till it was cooked, not wait till it cools?
Should I cook I shall spoil the rice, either by under or over doing it.
Rule will last but half an hour where discipline and authority do not exist.

column2: ஆக்குகிறவள் சலித்தால் அடுப்புப் பாழ், குத்துகிறவள் சலித்தாள் குந்தாணி பாழ்.
column3: If the cook be weary, the hearth will be useless; if she who pounds the rice be weary the mortar will be useless.
The chief of the proud.

column2: ஆங்காலம் எல்லாம் அவசாரி ஆடிச் சாங்காலம் சங்கரா சங்கரா என்கிறாள்.
column3: She exclaims Sangará, Sangará at death having through life been given up to lewd behaviour.
All the deities that were venerated have entirely perished.

column2: ஆசாரம் இல்லா அசடருடன் கூடிப் பாசாங்கு பேசிப் பதி இழந்துபோனேன்.
column3: By being associated with the base and by speaking hypocritically I have forfeited my dwelling-place.
The virtuous are not affected by blessing and cursing.

column2: ஆசை அவள் மேலே ஆதரவு பாய் மேலே.
column3: His mind is fixed upon her, his body is on the mat.

column2: ஆசை அறுபது நாள், மோகம் முப்பதுநாள், தொண்ணூறு நாளும் போனால் துடைப்பக்கட்டை.
column3: Sixty days of excessive desire, thirty of enjoyment; when the ninety are over, the remaining time is as useless as a worn-out broom.
If evil desire spring not sorrow will not approach.
If there be heartfelt love, there will be deferential regard.Spoken of a devoted wife who has a cruel husband.
As long as desire continues, there will be anxiety also.
Persons of inordinate desire have no shame.
Desire has no limits.
Is it to deceive after using enticing words?
What remedy is there for love-sickness?
As desire increases, anxiety increases.
Which is greater, (human) desire or a mountain?
What was desired is now disgusting.
She beat him with the winnowing fan to banish all his desires.
Desire knows not shame.
Cherished desire ends in ruin.
O mother, mother, do not speak so much.

column2: ஆச்சி is a provincialism for ஆயி.
column3: ஆடப் பாடத் தெரியாது இரண்டு பங்கு உண்டு.Neither able to sing nor dance, but he has two shares.
The temple girl who could not dance said that the hall was not large enough.
Is it to look at the drama after having encouraged a profligate course?Spoken ironically.
Are those unfit for the drama unfit for every thing?
All is done and the avarai fruit is cut?Indicating decayed circumstances.
July harvest having failed, the price of dry grain is that of paddy.
The comedy began and ended in an hour.
A top that spins no longer.
If the embryo clouds of July fail rain will be scant.

column2: ஆடிக்கறக்கிற மாட்டை ஆடிக் கறக்கவேண்டும், பாடிக் கறக்கிற மாட்டைப் பாடிக் கறக்கவேண்டும்.
column3: A restless cow must be milked by force, and a gentle cow must be milked with kindness.Treatment to be regulated by circumstances.
As dry leaves falling in the winds of July.
Is there any way of escape for a leaf-plate before the wind of July?
Like silk-cotton scattered by the wind of July.

column2: ஆடிக்கு அழைக்காத மாமியாரைத் தேடி மயிரைப் பிடித்துச் செருப்பால் அடி.
column3: Seek, seize her by the hair, and slipper a mother-in-law who does not invite you in July.The parents of a newly married woman separate her from her husband, that she may be under their care during the first year of marriage, in the month of July. This is necessary to prevent conception in that month and delivery in the following April. The birth of a first child in April if a son is believed to bring ruin upon a family.

column2: ஆடிமாசத்தில் குத்தின குத்து ஆவணிமாசத்தில் உளைவு எடுத்ததாம்.
column3: They say that the cuff was given in July, and the pain felt in August.
A dancing foot and a singing throat.
Provide seed-corn and sow in July.
While there are sheep will it (the tiger or wolf) swallow the shepherd?
He stares like a thief who has stolen a sheep.
The shepherd is trembling aloft fearing the sheep may bite him.
The price of the sheep is a quarter of a fanam that of its tail three fourths of a fanam.

column2: ஆடு கிடந்த இடத்திலே மயிர்தானும் கிடையாமற் போயிற்று.
column3: Not even hair is found where sheep were penned.

column2: ஆடு கிடந்த இடமும் அகம்படியான் இருந்த இடமும் உருப்படாது.
column3: A place where sheep were penned and a place once occupied by an Agambadyan are profitless.
Not a leaf will be found where the sheep lay.
The fattening of the sheep is an advantage to the shepherd.

column2: ஆடு கொண்டவன் ஆடித்திரிவான், கோழி கொண்டவன் கூவித்திரிவான்.
column3: He that has bought a sheep will wander about; he that has bought fowls will go about crying them for sale.
Will the shepherd who refused to give a sheep give a cow?
Will a sheep lead itself when the shepherd is absent?

column2: ஆடுங்காலம் தலைகீழாய் விழுந்தாலும் கூடும் புசிப்புத்தான் கூடும்.
column3: Though he may fall headlong when dancing, he will not thereby meet with greater success.

column2: ஆடுங்காலத்துத் தலைகீழாக விழுந்தால் ஓடும் கப்பரையும் உடையவன் ஆவான்.
column3: If one should fall headlong when he ought to be active, he will be so impoverished as to possess only broken pots and an alms-dish.Indolence in youth leads to poverty in old age.

column2: ஆடுதவுமா குருக்களே என்றால், கொம்பும் குளம்பும் தவிரச் சமூலமும் ஆகும் என்கிறார்.
column3: If one say, O my teacher, can the sheep be of any use to you, he replies yes, all except the horns and the hoofs.
Like a goat cropping leaves.
It is said that the wolf wept because the sheep were wet.
Will the pen be put up where the sheep may wish?
If the sheep should recover, he will not give even a hair.Said where benefits are not requited.
The dam at home, the lamb in the jungle.
He who is naked is but half a man.Self-respect essential.
If the cream be consumed, can butter be obtained?
The lapwing will by it’s cries betray whether he who approaches be the owner or a thief.

column2: ஆட்காட்டி தெரியாமல் திருடப்போகிறவன் கெட்டிக்காரனோ, அவன் அறியாமல் அவன் காலடிபிடித்துப் போகிறவன் கெட்டிக்காரனோ?
column3: Which is the cleverer, the thief that goes out to steal taking care that the lapwing does not see him, or he that follows the track of that thief?
A stupid servant to a shepherd.

column2: ஆட்டில் ஆயிரம், மாட்டில் ஆயிரம், வீட்டிலே கரண்டி பால் இல்லை.
column3: Possessed of sheep by the thousand, of cattle by the thousand, he has not a spoonful of milk at home.
The tail of the sheep is proportioned to its size.

column2: ஆட்டுக்கும் மாட்டுக்கும் இரண்டு கொம்பு, ஐயம்பிடாரிக்கு மூன்று கொம்பு.
column3: Sheep and oxen have two horns, an Aiyam pidári has three.
Can an old tiger be overcome by a sheep?
Are relationships observed among sheep and oxen? have woods and uncultivated tracts any line of demarkation?
As the wolf entered the sheepfold.
The fate of the lamb is that of its dam.
As the washerman flies at the sheep's head.
The butter the sheep yields is not enough (to stew) the marrow with.
He causes them to dance and beats the drum.
What, thousands of gold for a young sheep?
Sacrifice an elephant for the sake of a kid.

column2: ஆட்டுக்குட்டியைத் தோளிலே வைத்துக் காடெங்கும் தேடின கதை.
column3: The story of one who wandered through the jungle in search of a lamb that he had on his shoulder.
He aims to catch the leopard by exposing the sheep.

column2: ஆட்டைத் தேடி அயலாள் கையிற் கொடுப்பதைப்பார்க்க, வீட்டைக் கட்டி நெருப்பு வைப்பது நல்லது.
column3: It is better to build a house and set fire to it, than to buy sheep and place them under the care of others.
Have you no desire to see us at least once a year?
Broth and curry ought to be prepared with all their ingredients.
Do not destroy yourself by pride.

column2: ஆணிக்கு இணங்கின பொன்னும் மாமிக்கு இணங்கின பெண்ணும் அருமை.
column3: Standard gold, and a woman at one with her mother-in-law are rare.

column2: ஆணுக்கு அவகேடு செய்தாலும் பெண்ணுக்குப் பிழைகேடு செய்யலாகாது.
column3: Although one may injure a man, a woman may not be injuriously treated.

column2: ஆணை அடித்த வளர், பெண்ணைப் போற்றி வளர்.
column3: Train a boy strictly, but a girl kindly.

column2: ஆணையும் வேண்டாம் சத்தியமும் வேண்டாம் துணியைப்போட்டுத் தாண்டு.
column3: Neither swearing nor oaths are required, put down the cloth and stride over it.

column2: ஆண்ட பொருளை அறியாதார் செய்த தவம் மாண்ட மரத்துக்கு அணைத்த மண்.
column3: The austerity of those who are ignorant of the Supreme is as profitless as soil at the foot of a dead tree.
Do you give to mendicants, or to the hard shell of the bottle-gourd?
While the head of a family lives, prosperity may be enjoyed.
Like mendicants combining to build a choultry.
The mendicant lies in the choultry and his bag in the street.
When told to give rice to the Saiva mendicant, he says give it to the Vaishnava mendicant.
To the religious mendicant a word, to the brahman not even that.
The five children borne by the mendicant’s wife are all weaklings.

column2: ஆண்டி மகன் ஆண்டியானால் நேரம் அறிந்து சங்கு ஊதுவான்.
column3: When the son of a mendicant assumes a mendicant’s profession, he will blow his conch at the proper time.
It is said that he beat the mendicant and broke his alms-dish.
The Saivite and the Vaishnavite mendicants are as a water-pot and its cord.United or separate.

column2: ஆண்டியைக் கண்டால் லிங்கன் என்கிறான், தாதனைக் கண்டால் ரங்கன் என்கிறான்.
column3: When he meets a Saiva mendicant he is a Lingan, and when he meets a Vaishnava mendicant he is a Rangan.

column2: ஆண்டியும் ஆண்டியும் கட்டிக்கொண்டால் சாம்பலும் சாம்பலும் ஒட்டிக்கொள்ளும்.
column3: When two mendicants embrace each other, the ashes of the one cleave to the other.
Though he assumed the guise of a mendicant, his troubles did not cease.
If a mendicant be struck on his posteriors, ashes fly.

column2: ஆண்டு மாறின காரும் அன்று அறுத்த சம்பாவும் ஆளன் கண்ணுக்கு அரிது.
column3: Kár rice a year old, and newly reaped Samba (superior rice) are rare to the eye of a cultivator.
If the year withhold her increase, even the menial servants will neglect to do obeisance.
He wreaked his anger against his master on the male buffalo.

column2: ஆண்பிள்ளைகள் ஆயிரம் ஒத்திருப்பார்கள், அக்காள் தங்கைச்சி ஒத்திரார்கள்.
column3: A thousand men may live together in harmony, whereas two women are unable to do so although they be sisters.
The coward blames his weapon.

column2: ஆதரவற்ற வார்த்தையும் ஆணிகிடாவாத கைமரமும் பலன் செய்யாது.
column3: A comfortless word, and a palmira rafter without a bolt are of no use.
Will a chetty (a merchant,) take his departure by a river without gain?
Will a chetty dam up a river and drain out its waters for nothing?
Is a man possessed of landed property without a legal instrument?
The over-hasty is wanting in sense.
Will the over-hasty be nice about family pedigrees?
The hasty is gratified at the instant.

column2: ஆத்தை படுகிற பாட்டுக்குள்ளே மகன் மோருக்கு அழுகிறான்.
column3: While his mother is in extremity, her grown up son is crying for butter-milk.
He who possesses no property is but half a man.
A man of wealth is the slave of his possessions.
The wealthy are not exposed to immediate ruin.
The owl is small, its screech is loud.
He who helps another in his misfortune becomes his master.
Prosperity and adversity are common to all.
A thing done through necessity is no crime.
He is a friend who aids in adversity.
The condition of one’s elder sister may be ascertained by attending her wedding.
As the monkey perished by drawing out a wedge.
If castor seed (palma christi) be sown will àchcha (ebony) spring up?
Are the nuts of the castor plant pearls?
Will he sow castor and cotton seeds in close proximity?
The tortoise or a turtle is in the well, the squirrel is on a branch.Said of two things or persons not found together.

column2: ஆமை பிடிப்பார் மல்லாத்துவார் நாம் அது சொன்னாற் பாவம்.
column3: They will catch the turtle, they will turn it on its back, for me to say so would be a crime.Indicating that it is more dangerous to speak of the crime of another than to commit it.
A most abandoned woman who murdered her own husband.
The woman became wise after the death of her husband.
A blear-eyed man became the husband of a woman who had been crying for one.

column2: ஆமுடையான் அடித்ததற்கு அழவில்லை, சக்களத்தி சிரிப்பாளென்று அழுகிறேன்.
column3: I do not weep because my husband has beaten me, but because my rival will laugh at me.

column2: ஆமுடையான் செத்து அவதிப்படுகிறபோது, அண்டைவீட்டுக்காரன் வந்து அக்குளில் குத்தினானாம்.
column3: When, in great extremity she was mourning the death of her husband, a neighbour came and attempted to tickle her under the arm.

column2: ஆமுடையான் கோப்பில்லாக் கூத்தும், குருவில்லா ஞானமும் போல் இருக்கிறான்.
column3: The husband resembles a merry, making without food and knowledge without a competent teacher.
No matter what the circumambulation of a husband may be, he enters his house by the door.

column2: ஆமுடையானுக்குப் பொய் சொன்னாலும் அடுப்புக்குக் பொய் சொல்லலாமா?
column3: True, you may utter a falsehood to your husband, but can you deal falsely with the hearth?
It is while she has a husband, is it not, that a woman should play the harlot?
Of what use is the favor of a midwife to her who has lost her husband?

column2: ஆமுடையான் பலம் இருந்தால் குப்பை ஏறிச் சண்டை செய்யலாம்.
column3: If your husband be a powerful man, you may ascend the dunghill to fight.
As if a tortoise or turtle crept up a hillock.
It is said that when the hare went with the tortoise to lay eggs it strained its eyes out and died.
It is not difficult for one to win who understands the tricks of dice.
The brahmanicide is the witness of the custom officer.

column2: ஆயக்காரன் அஞ்சு பணம் கேட்பான் அதாவெட்டுக்காரன் ஐம்பது பணம் கேட்பான்.
column3: The under-hand extortioner demands fifty fanams whilst the custom officer demands five.
Even in gain justice is required.
Hire for the horse, nothing for its colt.
Like swimming the river to avoid the tax.
Do not commit injustice at the custom-house.
One grain destroying insect will consume a thousand grains of rice.
As a swan in the midst of a thousand crows.
Like a single stone thrown at a thousand crows.
Can a thousand props support the universe?
A thousand bonds may equal the strength of an elephant.
Though a thousand times admonished, a faithless woman will not become a faithful wife.
Like one born with a thousand stretched out his legs with five-hundred.
Beauty is found only in one of a thousand.

column2: ஆயிரம் நட்சத்திரம் கூடினாலும் ஒரு சந்திரன் ஆகாது.
column3: The combined light of a thousand stars is not equal to a single moon.
A thousand sixteenths make sixty-two and a half.

column2: ஆயிரம் உடையார் அமர்ந்திருப்பார் துணிபொறுக்கி தொந்தோம் தொந்தோம் என்று கூத்தாடுவான்.
column3: He whose fortune amounts to thousands is quiet, while the rag-gatherer leaps for joy.Dignity is characteristic of the worthy, and levity of the worthless.
Though it may bring in thousands, the occupation of a tax-gatherer is bad.

column2: ஆயிரம் வருஷம் சென்று செத்தாலும் அநீதச்சா ஆகாது.
column3: Although one may live a thousand years an unnatural death is bad.

column2: ஆயிரம் வித்தைகள் கற்றாலும் உலகத்தில் ஆடம்பரங்கள் வேண்டும்.
column3: Although one has acquired a thousand arts, he will still feel the necessity of earthly show.
He who has killed a thousand persons is half a doctor.
Though a thousand may thereby be obtained, impetuosity is not good.

column2: ஆயிரம் பெயர் கூடினாலும் ஒரு அந்துப்பூச்சியைக் கொல்லக்கூடாது.
column3: Though a thousand persons unite, they cannot kill a single grain-insect.

column2: ஆயிரம் பொன் போட்டு யானை வாங்கி அரைப்பணத்து அங்குசத்துக்குப் பால்மாறுகிறதா?
column3: After laying out thousands for an elephant why hesitate to give half a fanam for a goad?
A thousand eighths of a fanam.
Half a letter is equal to a thousand words.

column2: ஆயிரம் பசுக்களில் ஒரு பசு உதைத்துக்கொண்டால் நஷ்டமா?
column3: Will it prove a loss if one of a thousand cows kicks?

column2: ஆயிரம் பனையுள்ள அப்பனுக்குப் பிறந்தும் பல்லுக் குத்த ஒரு ஈர்க்கும் இல்லை.
column3: Although born of a father possessed of a thousand palmira trees, he has not a fibre with which to pick his teeth.
Like a toad among a thousand serpents.

column2: ஆயிரம் பொன் பெற்ற குதிரைக்கு அரைப்பணத்துச் சவுக்கு.
column3: Half a fanam to buy a whip for a horse worth thousands of gold.
A single avaricious desire will destroy a thousand good qualities.
Even a horse worth thousands of gold may require a whip.
Tell lies by the thousand and build a temple.
One of a hundred makes a skilful swords-man.
What! strike the unarmed?
Like seeking a weapon in the face of battle.
It rains in successive torrents.

column2: ஆய்ந்து பாராதான் காரியம் தான் சாந்துயரம் தரும்.
column3: An ill-considered undertaking may occasion the agony of death.
A female with an inauspicious mark on the neck will prove an evil in a king’s palace.
Sirrah! who invested you with a free tenure, I did it myself.A long continued favour is regarded as a right.
Has any one measured out my daily allowance?

column2: ஆராருக்கு ஆளாவேன் ஆகாத உடம்பையும் புண்ணையும் கொண்டு.
column3: How many shall I serve with this debilitated and ulcerated body?
By whom was I ruined, by twisted hemp.
Although engaged in a mere puppet-show, one ought to keep the eye on the main chance.
He is fearless and shrinks not in the presence of any foe.
Who is really true to another?
To whom were you born? you cry for butter-milk.

column2: ஆருக்காகிலும் துரோகம் செய்தால் ஐந்தாறு நாட்பொறுத்துக் கேட்கும், ஆத்துமத்துரோகம் செய்தால் அப்போதே கேட்கும்.
column3: Treachery against any man will be slowly requited, but treachery against one’s self will meet with immediate retribution.
