A wife who has no connections is to be preferred and so the friendship of one.

column2: ஆரும் ஆரும் உறவு? தாயும் பிள்ளையும் உறவு.
column3: Who are related to each other? the mother and her child.
He is said to have performed Ashwamedha, (the sacrifice of a horse) in an uninhabited country.

column2: ஆரை நம்பித் தோழா காருக்கு ஏற்றம் போட்டாய்?
column3: My friend, on whom did you depend for assistance when you put up a picotta to irrigate the kár rice?An efficient picotta is worked by two, frequently by three men.
Some one died: some one cried.

column2: ஆர் ஆத்தாள் செத்ததும் பொழுது விடிந்தால் தெரியும்.
column3: In the morning it will be known whose mother is dead.
Whomsoever you delay to pay, the debt of Mári must be at once discharged.Mári is the Goddess who presides over pestilence, &c.

column2: ஆர் குடியைக் கெடுக்க ஆண்டி வேஷம் போட்டாய்?
column3: Whose family did you intend to ruin when you assumed the guise of a religious mendicant?
No matter who pounds it if we obtain the rice.
He who is as dangerous to society as the poison of a serpent will be ruined.
Indolence changes nectar into poison.
Who informed the birds that the banyan tree was in fruit?
A parasite on a banyan tree.

column2: ஆலும் வேலும் பல்லுக்கு உறுதி நாலும் இரண்டும் சொல்லுக்கு உறுதி.
column3: As the banyan and the acacia strengthen the teeth, so Náladiyar and Kural give force to speech.Náladiyar and Kural two celebrated poetic works on moral subjects.
I am become a bruised reed tossed on the waves.
The temple understands not the hidden meaning of the Vedas.
The flowers of the olive, Bassia, are regarded as sugar in a place without a sugar-cane mill.
As the pendulous roots of its branches support the banyan tree.

column2: ஆல் பழுத்தால் அங்கே அரசு பழுத்தால் இங்கே.
column3: If the banyan be in fruit, thither, if the arasu, hither, the birds flock.

column2: ஆல்போல் விழுதுவிட்டு, அறுகுபோல் வேரோடி மூங்கில்போற் சுற்றம் முசியாமல் வாழ்ந்திருக்க.
column3: May you prosper as the banyan with its daughter-stems, take root like the wide-spreading arugu grass, and as the bamboo amid unfailing friends.A congratulatory expression to a newly married couple.
The month of August is attended with incessant drizzling.
He is not subject to calamity who pursues not a sinful course.

column2: ஆவல்மாத்திரம் இருந்தால் என்ன, அன்னம் இறங்கினால் அல்லவோ பிழைப்பான்?.
column3: What avails mere desire, he will live, will he not, if he swallows rice?
Even the leaves of the àvárai may be useful in misfortune.
After his last gasp who will distribute our food?
Will the colour of the skin of a cow affect that of its milk?
The ónkil fish is acquainted with depth, and the snake knows the sound of a drum.This reference to the instincts of animals serves to indicate the aptitude &c., of individuals.
Do not step down unless you know the depth.

column2: ஆழாக்கு அரிசி மூழாக்குப்பானை முதலியார் வருகிற வீறாப்பைப் பாரும்.
column3: With an àlak of rice in hand and a pot that will suffice to boil only three fourths of it look at the ostentation the Mudaliar displays.

column2: ஆழியெல்லாம் வயலானால் என்ன அவனி யெல்லாம் அன்னமயமானால் என்ன ?
column3: What if the great deep were an extended field and all the world standing corn?Even then each could enjoy only what he might consume.
Of what use is a wife to him who cannot govern her?
The grief occasioned by the death of a husband will not be removed by a flood of tears.
She who has no husband is like sand in the bed of a river.Uncertain as to position.

column2: ஆளான ஆட்களுக்கு அவிழ் அகப்படாக் காலத்திலே காகா பிசாசு கஞ்சிக்கு அழுகிறது.
column3: The raven demon crying for kanji when the well-to-do cannot obtain a grain of boiled rice.

column2: ஆளுக்கு ஒரு குட்டுக் குட்டினாலும் அவனுக்குப் புத்தி வராது.
column3: Although each of you may cuff the fellow, he will not thereby become wiser.

column2: ஆளுக்கு ஒரு குட்டுக் குட்ட அடியேன் தலை மிடாப்போல.
column3: Being buffeted by every one, my head has become as large as a water-pot.
He may escape recognition in a crowd.
After ascertaining the character of the person he undermines his interests.

column2: ஆளை ஆள் அறியவேண்டும் மீனைப் புளியங்காய் அறியவேண்டும்.
column3: Man must be tested by man and fish by tamarind acid.

column2: ஆளை ஆள் குத்தும் ஆள்மிடுக்குப் பத்துப் பேரைக் குத்தும்.
column3: Personal valor enables one to encounter an enemy, but outward show will scare away ten.
What, do you take your measure of a person without eyeing him all around?
Look at the person, look at his face.Spoken in derision of a boaster.
He eyed the man and deceived him by his talk.

column2: ஆள் ஆளைக் குத்தும், பகடம் பத்துப் பேரைக் குத்தும்.
column3: One man may stab one, threatening may stab ten.
Will a sword cut unwielded?
Is he who is yet alive, murdered?
Mere weapons are ineffective.

column2: ஆள் இளந்தலை கண்டால் தோணி மிதக்கப் பாயும்.
column3: When a dhony finds her steersman unskilful she pitches.Spoken of something going wrong from want of skill.
As men embark, the surrounding water rises.
Though feeble in person, his weapon is powerful.

column2: ஆள் மெத்தக் கூடினால் மீன் மெத்தப் பிடிக்கலாம்.
column3: When numerous fishermen combine together, multitudes of fish may be caught.
Neither six nor a hundred are required, let that be which will be.

column2: ஆறாம் பேறு பெண்ணாய்ப் பிறந்தால் ஆறான குடித்தனம் நீறா விடும்.
column3: If the sixth-born be a female, a family of overflowing wealth will be reduced to powder.
Death may occur at six, or at a hundred years of age.
Cold kanji is old kanji.
There will be a scab even after the wound is healed.Interrupted amity though restored will occasion suspicion.

column2: ஆறினால் அச்சிலே வார் ஆறாவிட்டால் மிடாவிலே வார்.
column3: If cool pour it into the mould, if not pour it into the large earthen pot.

column2: ஆறு கடக்குமளவும் அண்ணன் தம்பியெனல், ஆறு கடந்தால் நீயார் நானார்.
column3: To avow fraternal relationship until the river is crossed; and to ignore it om arriving at the opposite bank.

column2: ஆறு கலியாணம் மூன்று பெண்கள் மாரோடு மார் தள்ளுகிறது.
column3: Where six marriages are being celebrated three women are pressing and pushing.
Why loose your waist-cloth on mentioning that the river is yet ten miles off?

column2: ஆறு கெட நாணல் இடு ஊர்கெட நூலை இடு.
column3: Plant reeds to destroy a river, to destroy a country introduce yarn.Weavers who neglect tillage, and foul the water by dying processes &c are here referred to.

column2: ஆறு கொண்டது பாதி தூறு கொண்டது பாதி.
column3: The river has destroyed one half and the jungle the other.

column2: ஆறு நிறையச் சலம் போனாலும் பாய்கிறது கொஞ்சம் சாய்கிறது கொஞ்சம்.
column3: Though the river is in full flood part of it flows on in its course, and part diverges.

column2: ஆறு நிறையப் போனாலும் நாய்க்கு நக்குத் தண்ணீர்.
column3: Though the river is full to overflowing, a dog laps.Amid the greatest abundance one can only enjoy what is required.

column2: ஆறு நீந்தின எனக்குக் குளம் நீந்துவது அரிதோ ?
column3: Having swum a river, will it be difficult for me to swim over a tank?
What is a mere channel to him who has swum a river?
A river never flows straight.
A town in the course of a river will not endure.

column2: ஆறு பார்க்க போக ஆய்ச்சிக்குப் பிடித்தது சலுப்பு.
column3: The old dame caught a cold by going to look at the river.

column2: ஆறு போவதே போக்கு அரசன் சொல்வதே தீர்ப்பு.
column3: The flow of a river is its course, the utterance of a ruler is his decision.

column2: ஆறு மாசப் பயணம் அஞ்சி நடந்தால் முடியுமா ?
column3: Can a six months journey be accomplished if the party walks hesitatingly?An arduous enterprise requites unremitted exertion.

column2: ஆறு மாதம் காட்டிலே, ஆறு மாதம் வீட்டிலே.
column3: Six months in the jungle and six at home.
As Saturn seized one for six months together.

column2: ஆறும் கடன் நூறும் கடன் பெருக்கச் சுடெடா பணிகாரத்தை
column3: Debt is debt whether it amounts to six or a hundred; you fellow bake the cakes large.
Their severity is most felt, when a flood subsides, and a famine terminates.
All the way is bedewed with tears, and the foot prints are red with blood.
He aims to expose me, after comforting and abusing me.

column2: ஆற்றிலே போகிற தண்ணீரை அப்பா குடி, ஐயா குடி.
column3: Drink sir, and drink the water that is flowing in the river.Spoken of apparent generosity which in reality costs nothing.

column2: ஆற்றிலே ஒரு காலும் சேற்றிலே ஒரு காலுமாய் இருக்கிறான்.
column3: He has one foot in the river and one in mud.Said of one involved in inextricable difficulty.
Although you are throwing it into the river, measure it first.

column2: ஆற்றிலே கணுக்கால் தண்ணீரிலும் அஞ்சி நடப்பது உண்டு.
column3: Though water in a river is up to one’s ankle only, some will wade with hesitation.
Creeping along a river and sinking in the sand.
I am tossed about like a tuft of sacred grass that has been thrown into a river.
It is the pulp of the tamarind fruit dissolved in a river, and chaff sent to the bazaar.Said of things useless and unavailing.
The river is in flood, but what of that to a dog? it is only a fordable stream.

column2: ஆற்றுக்குப் பார்ப்பான் துணையோ, சோற்றுக்குப் பயற்றங்காய் கறியா ?
column3: Will a Brahman be of any use when one crosses a river, or pulse in pod serve for a curry?
Both the river and weather are dreaded.

column2: ஆற்றுக்குப் போனதும் இல்லை செருப்புக் கழற்றினதும் இல்லை.
column3: I neither went to the river, nor put off my shoes.
The flood of a river and the reign of a king last but half an hour.
The sands of a river cannot be counted.

column2: ஆற்று நீரை நாய் நக்கிக் குடிக்குமோ எடுத்துக் குடிக்குமோ ?
column3: How does a dog drink the water of a river, by lapping or by lading?
Before ascending its opposite bank it is necessary first, is it not, to cross the river?
After crossing the river the boatman gets a cuff.

column2: ஆற்றைக் கடத்திவிடு ஆகாசத்திற் பறக்கக் குளிகை தருகிறேன் என்கிறான்.
column3: He says, carry me over the river and I will give you a pill that will enable you to fly through the air.
Can one comprehend the future as well as the past?

column2: ஆன தெய்வத்தை ஆறு கொண்டுபோகிறது அனுமந்தராயனுக்குத் தெப்பத்திருவிழாவா ?
column3: After the river has carried away every imaginable deity, do you stay to celebrate an aquatic festival in honour of Hanuman?Said of one seeking the lesser aid when the greater has failed.
Having spent all he stares like an owl.
Having lost his capital, it will not be difficult for him to lose his reputation.
The good and the bad exist among all.

column2: ஆனால் அச்சிலே வார்க்கிறேன் ஆகாவிட்டால் மிடாவிலே வார்க்கிறேன்.
column3: If I succeed I will pour it into a mould, if not, into a large pot.Accommodating one’s conduct to circumstances.
Do not begin to build in June; nor set out to occupy a house in March.

column2: ஆனி அரை ஆறு, அவணி முழு ஆறு.
column3: In June half a river, in August a full river.
In June sugar-cane is like an elephant’s tail.

column2: ஆனை அசைந்து தின்னும் வீடு அசையாமல் தின்னும்.
column3: An elephant moves when eating, a house eats without moving.

column2: ஆனை ஆசாரவாசலைக் காக்கும், பூனை புழுத்த மீனைக் காக்கும்.
column3: An elephant waits before the outer hall of a king’s court; a cat watches the putrid fish.

column2: ஆனை ஆயிரம் பெற்றால் அடியும் ஆயிரம் பெறுமா ?
column3: If an elephant fetch a thousand, will the mark of his footstep be worth so much?

column2: ஆனை இருந்து அரசாண்ட இடத்திலே பூனை இருந்து புலம்பி அழுகிறது.
column3: A cat sits and mews in the place once occupied by the royal elephant.
An ordinary curry for an elephant and a superior curry for a cat.
Like a blighted wood-apple swallowed by an elephant.
Why enter by a corner when mounted on an elephant?
Will one mounted on an elephant enter through a wicket?

column2: ஆனை ஒரு குட்டிபோட்டும் பலன், பன்றி பல குட்டிபோட்டும் பயன் இல்லை.
column3: It is of value though an elephant brings forth a single young one; but the many young ones of a pig are of no value.One good thing is better than ten bad ones.
The stalks of the grain are so strong that an elephant may be tied to them, and the stacks are as high as the heavens.Said in exaggeration of an abundant harvest.
As the blind quarrelled about an elephant they had examined.
Though black, an elephant will fetch a thousand gold pieces.
If an elephant be lost, is it to be sought in an earthen pot?
Is the elephant in the rice-pot or in the water-pot?

column2: ஆனை கேட்ட வாயால், ஆட்டுக் குட்டி கேட்கிறதா ?
column3: What, does the mouth that asked for an elephant ask for a lamb?
Having given the elephant is there a dispute about the goad?
How long shall I conceal it as an elephant incloses a thing in his jaws?
The deceit of an elephant and the thievishness of a cat.

column2: ஆனைக்கு அறுபது முழம் அறக்குள்ளனுக்கு எழுபது முழம்.
column3: Keep away from an elephant sixty cubits, and from a dwarf seventy cubits.
The same reason is applicable alike to elephants and earthen pots.
The elephant and tiger are afraid of fire.
Even an elephant may slip.
Will a lamb be famished in a place where elephants are fed?
As if a lion had entered a herd of elephants.
Are toads fit for elephant’s food?
An elephant requires a goad, and boiled rice a chilli (condiment.)
An elephant is not affected by sunshine or rain.

column2: ஆனைக்கு ஒரு காலம் பூனைக்கு ஒரு காலம்.
column3: The elephant has its time, the cat also has its time.
Although dead, an elephant will fetch a thousand gold pieces.
What! does the hand that has caressed an elephant caress a sheep?

column2: ஆனை தன் தலையில் தானே மண்ணைப் போட்டுக்கொள்ளும்.
column3: The elephant will put sand on its own head.To see this done by wild elephants when travelling through a jungle is very interesting.

column2: ஆனை தன்னைக் கட்டச் சங்கிலியைத் தானே கொடுத்ததுபோல.
column3: As an elephant gives the chain to tether himself.Said of one who, whether consciously or not, supplies means to his own disadvantage.
Though chased by an elephant, it is not right to enter a temple.
As a frog trembled at the shadow of an elephant.
When an elephant lies down its height will equal the stature of a man.
When an elephant lies down, will he be lower than a lamb?
Are your eyes too dim to see an elephant?You must be blind indeed.
The street by which an elephant passed.
Has any survived after being trodden under foot by an elephant?
Stubble as high as an elephant, stacks towering to the skies.

column2: ஆனை மேயும் காட்டில் ஆட்டுக்குட்டி மேய இடம் இல்லையா ?
column3: Is there not room for a lamb to crop its food in a jungle where elephants feed?
Like asking chunam (lime) of one who is going along on an elephant.
As if an elephant’s load was transferred to a cat.
Even an elephant may be impeded by arugu, Agrostis linearis, grass.This grass is sacred to Ganèsa.

column2: ஆனையும் ஆனையும் முட்டும்போது இடையில் அகப்பட்ட கொசுகுபோல
column3: As a gnat between two elephants that are brought into collision.
Give an elephant to a pundit, and a cat to a mountaineer.
Is he who slew an elephant unable to overcome a cat?

column2: ஆனையை ஆயிரம் பொன்னுக்கு வாங்கி இருப்பு அங்குசத்திற்கு ஏமாந்து நிற்பானேன் ?
column3: After having paid a thousand gold pieces for an elephant, why hesitate to buy an iron goad?
She will stab the elephant and cover it with a sieve.
Having tied the elephant she will cover it with a winnowing fan?
Like putting one’s hand into a water-pot in search of a missing elephant.
Having sold the elephant he begs for the goad.

column2: ஆனையைத் தண்ணீரில் இழுக்கிற முதலை, பூனையைத் தரையில் இழுக்குமா ?
column3: Can an alligator which can draw an elephant in water, drag a cat on dry ground?

column2: ஆனை வரும் பின்னே மணி ஓசை வரும் முன்னே.
column3: The sound of the bell is heard before the elephant makes its appearance.Spoken of events which cast their shadows before.

column2: ஆனை வால் பிடித்துக் கரையேறலாம் ஆட்டுக்குட்டியின் வால் பிடித்துக் கரையேறலாமா ?
column3: One can cross a river by holding the tail of an elephant, can one do so by holding the tail of a sheep?

column2: ஆனை விழுந்தால் கொம்பு, புலி விழுந்தால் தோல்.
column3: On the death of an elephant the tusk remains, on the death of a tiger the skin.
A cat will be a small thing to an old dame who swallowed an elephant.
When down, an elephant is as high as a horse.
The force of an elephant is subdued by the goad.
The contemptible will not be praised.

column2: இக்கரையில் பாகலைப் போட்டு அக்கறையில் கொழுகொம்பு நடுகிறான்.
column3: He puts down the seed pagal (a creeper) on this side of the river and erects a supporting pole on that.
Like a vessel that contained assafœtida.
A thing that is greatly desired is not secret.
Like a monkey that has eaten green ginger.
Where is the way of the perverse? on the head of the man that is lying down.
If permitted he will pull down the choultry.Intimating that persons of violent tendencies must be at once repressed.

column2: இடதுகைக்கு வலதுகை துணை வலதுகைக்கு இடதுகை துணை.
column3: The right hand helps the left and the left the right.
One may not make friendship with him who does not know the right side from the left.Intimating that one must have nothing to do with fools.
Like a snake that has heard thunder.
Will an umbrella be of any use in a thunder-storm?
A liberal deity will give every-where.
In every place on which he reclined he constructed a hearth.Spoken of one who takes care of himself.

column2: இடுப்பு ஒடிந்த கோழிக்கு உரற் குழியே கதி.
column3: The mortar hole is the asylum of the fowl with a broken back.

column2: இடும்பு செய்வார்க்கு இராப் பகல் நித்திரை இல்லை.
column3: Oppressors sleep not day or night.
Alms-giving secures heavenly bliss.
The evil-disposed will destroy the good deeds of the beneficent.

column2: இடுவாள் இடுவாளென்று ஏக்கற்று இருந்தாளாம், நாழி நெல்லுக் கொடுத்து நாலாசையும் தீர்ந்தாளாம்.
column3: It is said that she waited patiently for her mistress assured she would be rewarded; and at length she got a measure of rice, and her four desires were met.
The curnam of the shepherd caste is dead, henceforth the peasant may prosper.
The water-pot remains aslant as when put down.

column2: இடைத்தெருவில் கோலம் வரும்போது குசத்தெரு எங்கே என்கிறாள்?
column3: When the marriage procession comes along the shepherd’s street she asks where the potter's street is.
Middle and lower ranks in life are not good.
The sense of the shepherd is in his neck.

column2: இடையன் கெடுத்தது பாதி மடையன் கெடுத்தது பாதி.
column3: The shepherd destroyed half, and the simpleton destroyed half.
A shepherd’s marriage takes place at day-break.
Among shepherds there are no Saiva mendicants, among potters there are no Vaishnava mendicants.
Even a demon will not haunt the dwelling of him that defeats the hopes of another.

column2: இட்ட எழுத்திற்கு ஏற ஆசைப் பட்டால் கிடைக்குமா?
column3: If one’s desires are in excess of destiny, will they be obtained?
Is it fit to wish for more than what is already given?
If the giver cease to give, mortal hatred will ensue.
Give to those who have given, weep for the dead.

column2: இட்டு வைத்தால் தின்னவும் எடுத்து வைத்தால் சுமக்கவும் தெரியும்.
column3: He knows how to eat if food be set before him, and he knows how to carry if the burden be laid upon him.
To associate with the obstinate will bring disgrace.
What! is this a town in which water-melons were sold?

column2: இத்தனை பெரியவன் கையைப் பிடித்தால் எப்படி மாட்டோம் என்கிறது?
column3: When so great a personage takes one by the hand, how can one say nay?

column2: இத்தனை அத்தனை ஆனால் அத்தனை எத்தனை ஆகும்?
column3: If this amouts to that how much will that come to?
Such a blow would kill any dog.
Why gnaw this bone, and why lose one’s own teeth?
Is it for this gruel that so many sacred namams are used?
What, is it for this gruel that I have made twenty-eight námams sectarial marks?

column2: இந்தக்கரிப்பிற் செத்தால், இன்னம் ஒரு கரிப்பு மயிரைப் பிடுங்குமா?
column3: If one die during this famine will another famine pull off his hair?

column2: இந்தப் பூனையும் பால் குடிக்குமா என்றாற்போல் இருக்கிறான்?
column3: He is as if one said of a cat, will this also drink milk?

column2: இந்தப் பெருமையும் பந்தல் அழகும் பார்த்தையா பண்ணைக்காரா?
column3: O, headman, hast thou noticed this greatness, and the beauty of the Pandal?
Why do you treat this dog in this way?

column2: இந்திரனைச் சந்திரனை இலையாலே மறைப்பாள், எமதருமராஜனைக் கையாலே மறைப்பாள்.
column3: She will conceal Jupiter and the moon with a leaf, and Yama, the regent of the dead, with her hand.
The defects in the eye-lashes are not apparent to the eye.

column2: இமயன் ஒருவனைக் கொல்வான், ஏற்றம் மூவரைக் கொல்லும்.
column3: Yama the regent of the dead, kills one at a time, a picotta may kill three at once.
Is the habit natural or acquired?
The heart of one void of compassion is harder than iron.
Though you go a-begging, go decently attired.

column2: இரங்காதவர் உண்டா? பெண் என்றால் பேயும் இரங்கும்.
column3: Are there those who have no pity? If the word woman be uttered even a demon will be moved with compassion.
Like a lamb allowed to suck two sheep.Spoken of a youth full of strength and activity.

column2: இரண்டு பட்ட ஊரில் குரங்கும் குடி இராது.
column3: Even a monkey will not stay in a village divided against itself.
Is it to plant your feet on two dhonies?
Why fire in the house of a man that has two wives?

column2: இரண்டு வீட்டிலும் கலியாணம் இடையிலே செத்ததாம் நாய்க்குட்டி.
column3: It is said that the pup died between two houses where marriage festivities were going on.
As a bone thrown to two dogs.
Though you have to beg them, make your offerings to God.
It is the duty of the rich to give alms to beggars.
Is it difficult for a beggar to get fine rice?
A beggar nowhere suffers from famine.

column2: இரவல் உடைமை இசைவாய் இருக்கிறது, என் பிள்ளை ஆணை நான் கொடுக்கமாட்டேன்.
column3: The thing borrowed suits; I swear by my child that I will not return it.
Is a thing borrowed a durable possession, is a thief a friend?
Will borrowed rice prove a security against famine?

column2: இரவற் சீலையை நம்பி இடுப்புக் கந்தையை எறிந்தாளாம்.
column3: It is said, that relying on a borrowed cloth, she threw away her tattered cloth.

column2: இராகு திசையில் வாழ்ந்தவனும் இல்லை, ராஜ திசையிற் கெட்டவனும் இல்லை.
column3: None ever prospered under Ràhu, nor was any one ever ruined under Jupiter.Rahu is the name of a monstrous serpent or dragon, the ascending node, and regarded as one of the nine planets, which, with Kèthu the descending node, is said to grasp the sun and moon periodically, and thus to cause eclipses. When the supernals assembled at a feast to drink the water of immortality obtained from the churning of the milky ocean Rahu and Kethu, two Asuras, clandestinely got admission. The sun and moon saw them and at once informed Vishnu of their presence. Vishnu dispatched his marvellous disc and decapitated them just as the coveted nectar was about to enter their throats. They to avenge themselves resolved periodically to swallow the sun and moon.

column2: இராக் கண்ட சனி மிடாப்போல வீங்கின கதை.
column3: The story of one who in a dream of the night saw Saturn swelling as a large pot.
Like a horse ridden by a king.
As a lemon offered to a king.A gift to procure his favour: a hindu when offering a lemon or fruit of any kind invariably does so with the right hand.
Like carrying a royal mongoose.
Though a king, he is the son of his mother.
A king’s sceptre extends to the limit of his territory.
Though a princess, she is the wife of the man who has taken her in marriage.
Like a heron which saw a falcon.
As a slave lived on gruel because the king was in debt.

column2: இராப்பட்டினி கிடந்தவன் உரித்த வாழைப்பழம் விற்கிறதா என்று விசாரித்தானாம்.
column3: It is said that he who starved the whole night inquired if peeled plantains could be obtained.
Does he who lies down at night fasting, enquire after the price of grain?
The sense of hunger ends on the mat.
In the eye night and day.
Ayodhya was the city of Ràma.
Acquiescence with the wishes of his wife was fatal to Ràma.

column2: இராமனைப்போல ராஜா இருந்தால் அனுமானைப்போலச் சேவகனும் இருப்பான்.
column3: If the king be equal to Ráma, his servant may be equal to Hanumàn.Hanumán was the monkey chief, ally of Rama in his expedition against Lanka.

column2: இரா முழுதும் ராமாயணம் கேட்டுச் சீதைக்கு ராமன் என்ன என்ற கதை.
column3: The story of one who having listened to the reading of the Rámayana a whole night, asked what relationship existed between Síta and Ráma.
Though he went on a pilgrimage to Ràmèswaram, Saturn has not left him.

column2: இராவணன் குடிக்கு மகோதரன் போலும், சுயோதனன் குடிக்குச் சகுனிபோலும்.
column3: He is what Mahódharan was to the family of Rávana and what Sakuni was to Suyodhana.Said of one who under professions of friendship secretly plots one’s ruin.
He is as Ràvana in the guise of a hermit.

column2: இராவுத்தனைத் தள்ளினதும் அல்லாமல் குழியும் பறித்ததாம் குதிரை.
column3: It is said that the horse not only threw his rider, but dug his grave also.
Like a buffalo he consumes the day's supply.

column2: இருக்கிறவன் செவ்வையாய் இருந்தால் சிரைக்கிறவன் செவ்வையாய்ச் சிரைப்பான்.
column3: If he who is being shaved sits properly, the barber will shave well.
If you wish to live long, eat iron.
In time all will be in harmony.
Will the place on which we sit, incite?
Oblations made to a demoness must not be kept till the morning.

column2: இரு சுழி, இருந்து உண்டாலும் உண்ணும், இரந்து உண்டாலும் உண்ணும்.
column3: He who has a double twirl of hair, may live either on his own resources or by begging.
Was the work done in the dark or by the blind?
Will his thievish hand be restrained when he enters a dark room?
It is a sign of thievishness for a family to live in obscurity.
Like a snake that has a head at both ends.
I have become like an ant on a fire-brand lighted at both ends.Spoken when in danger from opposite quarters.
Must one not sit before lying down?

column2: இருந்த நாள் எல்லாம் இருந்துவிட்டு ஊர்ப்பறையனுக்குத் தாரைவார்த்த கதை.
column3: The story of one who after leading an unexceptionable course of life gave his daughter in marriage to the village Pariah.
As the thunder-bolt fell on the head of the one who remained.

column2: இருந்த கால் மூதேவி நடந்த கால் சீதேவி.
column3: The sluggish foot is Múdévi the goddess of adversity, the active foot is Shrídèvi the goddess of prosperity.These two personages were produced when the milky ocean was churned to obtain ambrosia. They play an important part in the economy of the Hindus.
He that went away was better than he who stayed.

column2: இருந்தவன் எழுந்திருக்கிறதற்குள்ளே நின்றவன் ஒரு காதம் போவான்.
column3: Whilst he who was seated was rising, the-man who was standing had gone ten miles.
While squatting, a cat, when springing, a tiger.
If sitting you sit, if you rise you stand.

column2: இருந்து பணம் கொடுத்து நடந்து வாங்கவேண்டியதாய் இருக்கிறது.
column3: One must walk a long way to get back the money lent when seated.
The twenty-one kinds of rain were enumerated and poured out.

column2: இருப்புக் கதவு இடித்துத் தவிட்டுக் கொழுக்கட்டை எடுக்கிறதா?
column3: What! knocking down an iron door to obtain a bran cake?

column2: இருப்புச் சலாகையை விழுங்கிப்போட்டு இஞ்சிச்சாறு குடித்தால் தீருமா?
column3: If one has swallowed a bar of iron, will its effects be removed by drinking a decoction of ginger?
Like an iron fort and a stone door.
To associate with a double minded woman is fatal.
If iron becomes copper, a straw may become a pillar.One impossibility is as like as another.
Can white-ants eat an iron pillar?
If iron can be converted into copper, copper may be reconverted into gold.

column2: இரும்புக்கட்டியைக் காற்றடிக்கிறபோது இலவம்பஞ்சு எனக்கு என்ன புத்தி என்கிறதாம்.
column3: It is said that when the wind was driving along a piece of iron, the silk-cotton asked what was best for him to do.
When a piece of iron is flying away, where will silk-cotton find a resting place?

column2: இரும்பு அடிக்கிற இடத்தில் நாய்க்கு என்ன வேலை?
column3: What has a dog to do in a smith’s shop?

column2: இரும்பு பிடித்த கையும் சிரங்கு பிடித்த கையும் சும்மா இராது.
column3: The hand that has taken up an iron tool and the hand affected by the itch will not be still.

column2: இரும்பு அடிக்கிற இடத்தில் நாய்க்கு என்ன கிடைக்கும்?
column3: What will a dog get in a smith’s shop?
As if an ant had gnawed an iron pillar.

column2: இரும்பு துறட்டுக்கு அசையாத புளியங்காய் திருப்பாட்டுக்கு அசையுமா?
column3: Will the tamarind fruit that has resisted an iron hook shake at a sacred song?In illustration of this proverb the following may not be considered irrelevant. In the year 1826 when I went to my first Missionary station, Trincomalee on the Eastern coast of Ceylon, I called on the chief Mudaliar to pay my respects and to urge the claims of Christianity. The old gentleman who remembered the coercive system adopted by the Dutch for the propagation of Christianity, and well knowing too that Missionaries under the mild sway of Great Britain can use no weapon but the Gospel, uttered with great animation the above proverb. In view of the youthful character of his visitor he seemed to feel like Goliath in the presence of David.
Can an ant gnaw iron?
Can a rat eat iron?
The infamous liar says that the rat took the iron in its mouth.

column2: இரும்பைக் கறையான் அரித்தால் பிள்ளையைப் பருந்து கொண்டு போகாதா?
column3: If the white-ants can eat iron, why cannot a hawk carry off a child?
If the two agree what need of arbitrators?
Can an affair be effected by one that could not be accomplished by two?
The friendship of two depends on the forbearance of one.
Does the son of an Irulan, a wild tribe, suffer from a scarcity of young rats?
Can a savage put on the look of a king?

column2: இருளும் ஒரு காலம் நிலவும் ஒரு காலம்.
column3: A time of darkness, a time of moonshine.

column2: இரெட்டியாரே இரெட்டியாரே என்றால், கலப்பையைத் திட்டென்று போட்ட கதை.
column3: The story of one who dropped his plough when accosted O Reddy, O Reddy.

column2: இலக்கணம் கற்றவன் கலக்கம் அற மன்னர் சபை காண்பான்.
column3: The learned will appear undismayed in a royal assembly.
Beauty and ugliness are in the face.
Abstinence is the best medicine.
Are all that are born in Ceylon, Ràvanas?
The monkey that set fire to Lanka.

I am like the parrot that waited for the silk-cotton pod.Spoken of one who has been sadly disappointed in his expectations. The proverb is said to refer to a parrot that on seeing a green pod on a silk-cotton tree believed it would ripen into fruit, wheres it eventually burst and the cotton was scattered to the winds.
If the flower of ilupai (Bassia Longifolia) be turned, it will be found hollow on both sides.
He who feeds on leaves knows not the flavor of fruit.
Leaves green and sear are found every where.
The secular and the sacred are both necessary.
That which is not will not come, that which is, will not go.
Domestic life is better than that of the ascetic.
Domestic life is honourable, that of the ascetic is disgraceful.
He that is destitute is wicked.
That which is not, will not come out; that from which nothing is taken; will not lessen.
Uttering a falsehood and suffering the evil thereof.
What! is it to speak manfully after deserting thy wife?
He lacks nothing, nor does he possess any thing.
Is the wife of the destitute the friend or companion of all?
Even a lizard will not enter a house occupied by the niggardly.
It is natural for the destitute to beg.

column2: இவள் விலைமோரில் வெண்ணெய் எடுத்துத் தலைமகனுக்குக் கலியாணம் பண்ணுவான்.
column3: She will make butter out of the butter milk-given for sale, and make provision for the marriage of her first-born son.
There are seven points of agreement between this person and that.

column2: இவனுக்குச் சொல்லும் புத்தி கடலிற் பெருங்காயம் கரைத்தது போல் இருக்கிறது.
column3: The advice given to him is like assafœtida dissolved in the sea.
He is the child of the whole village.Said of a self-willed wandering youth.

column2: இவன் மகா பெரிய கள்ளன், காலாலே முடிந்ததைக் கையாலே அவிழ்க்கிறது அரிது.
column3: He is a clever rogue, what he ties with his feet it is difficult to untie with the hand.

column2: இவன் கல்லாது கற்றவன், உள்ளங்கையில் வைகுண்டம் காட்டுவான்.
column3: He has attained the unattainable; he can show Vaikundam Paradise in the palm of his hand.
His wit is as sharp as a wooden pestle.
Will she who attends a funeral remove her marriage symbol?
Harmony sets off a faulty stanza.
When the trail marks are before you, why look for footprints?
A scarlet cloth each thread of which is worth a thousand gold pieces.
Thoroughly pounded and properly boiled.

column2: இளகின இரும்பைக் கண்டாற் கொல்லன் குண்டியைத் தூக்கி அடிப்பான்.
column3: When the smith perceives that the iron is soft, he will raise himself to the stroke.The Indian smith squats at the forge, and rises to his feet only when he uses a two-handed hammer.
Does a young calf know fear?
Indolence, in youth will bring poverty in old age.
Industry in youth will support one in old age.
The habits of early life will never be forgotten.
Youth and senility do not properly belong to a man’s age.
Do you who cannot endure the early beams of the sun presume to walk over fire?
Although young he will play the man.
In the tresses of a poor woman are found nits and lice.
Associate with the friend of the poor.
O you weak fellow, sow the sessamum seed.
The younger sister feeds on leaves, the elder is accustomed to fruit.

column2: இளயாளே வாடி மலையாளம் போவோம், மூத்தாளே வாடி முட்டிக்கொண்டு சாவோம்.
column3: Come my younger sister, we will proceed to Malayalam; come my elder sister, we will strike our heads together and die.

column2: இறகு முற்றி்ப் பறைவை ஆனால், எல்லாம் தன் வயிற்றைத் தான் பார்க்கும்.
column3: When mature and on the wing, all birds will look after their own food.

column2: இறக்கும் காலம் வந்தால் பிறக்கும் ஈசலுக்குச் சிறகு.
column3: When death approaches, white-ants take wing.

column2: இறக்கும் காலம் வராமல் பிறக்குமா ஈசலுக்குச் சிறகு?
column3: Will white-ants take wing except on the approach of death?

column2: இறங்கும் துறையிலே நீத்தானால, இந்த ஆற்றை எப்படிக் கடக்கிறது?
column3: If on stepping into the river one is obliged to swim, how will he get across?
Take medicine at sun-set.
The dead is the pilot of the living.
The child of the dead is the ward of the living.
Sessamum and water offered to the dead.
A family out of debt is out of danger.

column2: இறுப்பானுக்குப் பணமும் கிடையாது. உழைப்பானுக்குப் பெண்ணும் கிடையாது.
column3: He who pays his debts cannot obtain money, nor can the labourer obtain a wife.
They who live on flesh are acquainted with the medicine for tooth-ache.
Is one to wear a neck-lace of bones because he eats flesh?This and the preceding proverb are somewhat singular but not unnatural among a vegetarian people.

column2: இறைத்த கிணறு ஊறும், இறையாத கிணறு நாறும்.
column3: The water of a well always drawn, is fresh, that of a well not drawn is fetid.
The water of a well always drawn springs up a-fresh.
Like a deer separated from its herd.
Hereafter is it to lift up the hands to a god?
Hereafter he may effect his purpose as far as the realms of Yama.
Joy and grief must be regulated by moderation.
Happiness is desired by all.
The body it is that is affected by pleasure and pain.
Those who are alive to-day may not be on the morrow.

column2: இன்றைக்கு இலை அறுத்தவன் நாளைக்குக் குலை அறுப்பான்.
column3: He who cut off the leaves to-day may possibly cut off the bunch to-morrow.
That which is practicable to-day may be so on the morrow.
If one die to-day, tomorrow will be the second day.

column2: இன்றை என்பதும் நாளை என்பதும் இல்லை என்பதற்கு அடையாளம்.
column3: To say to-day, to-morrow, indicates refusal.

column2: ஈ கலையாமல் தேன் எடுப்பார்கள், எடுக்காமற் பிடிப்பார்கள்.
column3: They will take the honey without dispersing the bees, they will take it without raising it up.
The liberal giver will be happy.
In a fly, the head is the seat of poison, in a scorpion, the tail.
All are against the winged white-ants.
The wing of a white-ant is the softest of all things.
The army perished like winged white-ants.
The frog leaps and swallows the swarming winged white-ants.
Nothing here is equal to God.
Date-palm toddy is the coolest of beverages.
The story of a buffalo that lived in a date-palm grove.
A strong man fears not hardship, nor does a bullock with a stunted tail fear gnats.
Do not oppose your inferior.
A wealthy family is not exposed to danger.
Learning is more substantial than accumulated riches.

column2: ஈட்டி எட்டியமட்டும் குத்தும், பணம் பதின்காதமும் குத்தும்.
column3: A spear wounds as far as it reaches, the effect of money reaches ten kathams (100 miles).

column2: ஈட்டுக்கு ஈடும் சோட்டுக்குச் சோடுமாய் இருந்தால் வாசி.
column3: Like for like, and equality in a pair are desirable.
The maiden who was given in marriage was equal to all the exigencies of life.
The smallest fraction will come out by division.
ஈப்பிசினி இரப்பதுகூடக் கக்கிசம்.A miser will find even begging difficult.
Lead may be melted; can iron?
He who has taken up lead will not listen, no matter what is said to him.
A niggardly savage is nowhere found.
The wicked obtain by rapacity what the miserly accumulate.
The wicked enjoy the property of the miser.
Flies and ants abound every-where.

column2: ஈயைப் பிடித்தால் கை வேறு, கால் வேறு.
column3: If you catch a fly, its limbs will be separated.
Clean as a fly and strong as an ant.
He will cut the throat after tying a wet cloth round it.
There is no bone in a slanderous tongue.

column2: ஈரம் அற்ற இடத்திலே ஈ மொய்க்குமா, ஈரம் உள்ள இடதித்லே ஈ மொய்க்குமா?
column3: Do insects swarm in a dry place or in a wet place?
In an onion there are twenty-eight coats.
He who thinks of a nit will not forget its name.
I magnified the nit into a louse, and the louse into Perumál.
Will lice be obtained when drawing nits between the finger and the thumb.
That which a bee ejects and that which a dog has stolen.
Will a fly that has fallen into the boiled rice go unless it be taken out?
To the giver a meal, to the beggar plenty.
The press is turned in Lanka, and the material under pressure is examined here.
It knows not how to bring forth, nor how to lick.

column2: ஈன்றவள் தாய் பாட்டி, இவள் தாயின் தாய் பீட்டி.
column3: The mother of one's mother is his grand-mother, her mother is his great grand mother.
Indulgence in the parent is not good for the child.

column2: உகமுடிய மழை பெய்தாலும் ஓட்டாங்கிளிஞ்சல் பயிர் ஆகுமா?
column3: Though it should rain to the end of the world, would broken oyster shells vegetate?
As a grind-stone fell on the whitlow.

column2: உங்கள் உறவிலே வேகிறதைப்பார்க்கிலும் ஒரு கட்டு விறகிலே வேகிறது நல்லது.
column3: It is better to be consumed by a faggot than by your friendship.

column2: உங்கள் பெண்டுகள் கொண்டல் அடித்தால் கண்கள் கொள்ளாது.
column3: The eyes will be over-powered by the dancing of your women.
I have washed my hands of you in the sea.

column2: உசு பிடி என்றால் நீ பிடி என்கிறதாம் நாய்.
column3: It is said that when he urged his dog to seize, it replied, you do so.
If slippered on the head, will the crown of the head become cool?

column2: உடம்பு எல்லாம் புழுத்தவன் அம்மன் கோவிலை கெடுத்தகதை.
column3: The story of a man who defiled Amman covil by his ulcerated body.
As the wearied ass went to the salt-pit.
All who have taken a body have taken an alms-dish.
Must one not invigorate the body and then proceed to meditation?

column2: உடம்பு எங்கும் சுடுகிற தழலை மடியிலே கட்டுகிறாய்.
column3: Thou huggest up in thy bosom the embers that warm every part of the body.
Of what consequence is cold to him whose whole body is drenched?

column2: உடலுக்கோ பால் வார்த்து உண்பது, ஊருக்கோ பால்வார்த்து உண்பது
column3: Do we feed on milk for the benefit of the body, or that our neighbours may know it?
Divided as regards body, one in soul.

column2: உடல் ஒருவனுக்குப் பிறந்து நா பலருக்குப் பிறந்தது.
column3: The body is born for one, the tongue for many.
Anxieties which the ocean cannot contain attend the body as long as it exists.
The body of one who has no kindred is exposed to desolation.
A garment not worn is a prey to moths.
The cloth one has put on bites as a snake.
The guana may go, it is enough if the hand be free.

column2: உடும்புக்கு இரண்டு நாக்கு மனிதனுக்கு ஒரு நாக்கு.
column3: The guana has two tongues, man only one.

column2: உடும்புக்கு இரண்டு நாக்கு, மனிதனுக்கு இரண்டு நாக்கு உண்டா?
column3: The guana has a double tongue, has man the same?
Can you kick against the thorns of the Acacia?
Learning is real wealth.
Wealth and poverty are not abiding.
What! give one’s property and sacrifice one’s reputation?
Will a crop not looked after by the owner flourish?
Pluck up the weeds about the mortar as your master bids you.
An embezzler is more obstinate than the owner.
Weed around the areca tree at thy master’s biding.

column2: உடையவன் இல்லாச் சேலை ஒரு முழம் கட்டை.
column3: The cloth whose owner is absent is a cubit too short.

column2: உடையார் வீட்டு மோருக்கு அகப்பைக் கணக்கு என்ன?
column3: What necessity is there for estimating the quantity of butter-milk by the ladle in the homestead of the chief?
You must sit before lying down, must you not?
Can he tie a man that is running who cannot tie one that is sitting!
What! beautify the outside of a wall while the inside is neglected?
Garnish the inside of the wall and then the outside.
A mirror reflects whatever is before it.
A mere instrument void of feeling and food without salt are alike.
What, is it to mix poison with the rice one is eating?

column2: உண்ட உடம்பிற்கு உறுதி, உழுத புலத்தில் நெல்லு.
column3: There is strength in a body well fed, and grain in the ploughed field.
A child that eats well thrives.
Is rice required for a full stomach, or oil for a baldhead?
That which has been eaten will be evidenced in the belching.
May hospitality be requited by treachery?

column2: உண்ட உடம்பு உருகும் தின்ற பாக்குச் சிவக்கும்.
column3: The body nourished by another’s bounty will melt, arecanut when chewed turns red.
Friendship cemented by food will compassionate.
The body shews who is well fed.
Hunger is appeased by eating, will it be so by looking at food?

column2: உண்டால் தின்றால் உறவு, கொண்டால் கொடுத்தால் உறவு.
column3: Friendship is promoted and strengthned by hospitality and also by intermarriage.
When you eat, do so to keep up your strength.
Poison destroys when taken.
Moderation in food becomes women.

column2: உண்டு தின்று உள்ளே இருவென்றால் உயர எழும்பி ஏன் குதிக்கிறாய்?
column3: When you are told to eat heartily and remain in-doors, why do you rise up and jump?

column2: உண்டு உறியில் இரு என்றால் உருண்டு தரையில் விழுகிறாய்.
column3: If I say eat and sit on the hanging-shelf, you fall to the ground.
If a crab gets fat, it will not stay in its hole.

column2: உண்டு தின்று உயரம் ஆனால் ஊரிலே காரியம் என்ன?
column3: What business has he in the village seeing that he is grown up?
His hand is weary with eating.
When I call you to eat, you come to strike me.
I will cut your uvula and smear it with chunam-lime.
Property not enjoyed comes to nought.

column2: உண்ணாமல் ஊர் எல்லாம் திரியலாம் உடுக்காமல் ஒரு வீட்டுக்கும் போகலாகாது.
column3: One may wander over the whole country without eating, but one may not go to a single house without dressing.

column2: உண்ணாமல் ஒன்பது வீடு போகலாம், உடுக்காமல் ஒரு வீடும் போகல் ஆகாது.
column3: One may go to nine houses without eating, but not to one without garments.
Mistleloe attached to edible greens.
Do not go to work without eating.

column2: உண்பான் தின்பான் சிவப்பிராமணன், குத்துக்கு நிற்பான் வீர முஷ்டி.
column3: The Saiva Brahman will freely eat, the ferocious mendicant will show fight.
Though you look at those who are eating, you may not look at those who are ploughing.

column2: உண்மை சொல்லிக் கெட்டாரும் பொய் சொல்லி வாழ்ந்தாரும் இல்லை.
column3: None ever perishes by speaking the truth, none flourishes by uttering falsehood.

column2: உதடு மன்றாடப் போய் உள்ளிருந்த பல்லும் போனாற்போல.
column3: As if the teeth also went out when the lips had gone to beg.

column2: உதட்டிலே உள்ள வாழைப்பழத்தை உள்ளே தள்ளுவார் உண்டோ?
column3: Are there any who push into the mouth the plantain that is already between the lips?

column2: உதட்டுக்கு மிஞ்சின பல்லும் திருட்டுக்கு மிஞ்சின கையும் ஆகாது.
column3: Protruding teeth and a hand given to thieving are bad.
Will the cow not yield its milk because it has sore lips?
A small particle adhering to the lip cannot be removed by blowing.
Divers disguises for the sake of the belly.
Thou art a useless vessel, a piece of brass without sound!
Like writing a chit to a thrifty merchant.
What, accident can harm those who help others?
A woman with a bustling gait came and destroyed what there was.
Even gold is a thing of nought to the generous.
Sympathy arises, does it not, from consanguinity?
Worms will breed in due course, will they not, in a foot accustomed to kick?
Before his kicking feet were eaten of worms his abdomen had ulcerated.
He that washes for him who kicks him is a first-rate washerman.
Contracts are needless as regards honest men and cheats. : The one needs no binding, and the other admits no obligations.
A faithful servant is more than a mother.
The good are exposed to no danger.
Exertion is a manly quality.
Facilities suited to one's office.
It is unfortunate when evil is returned for good.

column2: உபசரியாக வீட்டிலே உண்ணாதிருப்பதே ஒரு கோடி தனம்.
column3: It is a crore of wealth to abstain from entertainment in a house void of civility.
What! are the civil to be requited by incivility?

column2: உபசார வார்த்தை காசாகுமா, உண்டாலொழியப் பசி தீருமா?
column3: Will mere ceremony pass for money, will hunger be appeased otherwise than by eating?
Can that which may be realized by cunning be obtained by force?

column2: உப்பு இருந்த பாண்டமும் உபாயம் இருந்த நெஞ்சமும் தட்டி உடையாமல் தானே உடையும்.
column3: A salt-cup and a deceitful heart will of themselves come to destruction.

column2: உப்பு இருந்த பாண்டமும் உண்மை இல்லா நெஞ்சும் தட்டி உடையாமல் தாமே உடையும்.
column3: A vessel impregnated with salt, and a mind without truth need none to destroy them, they destroy themselves.
Unsalted food is fit only for the rubbish heap.
Remember through life those who have given you salt.
Every thing from salt to camphor is required.Said of things domestic and sacred.
He who has eaten salt will drink water.
Thou starest as the Brahmanee who had lost her salted mutton.In her bewilderment she had no remedy because she dared not make known the loss of a thing she ought not to have had in her possession.
Does the salt merchant know the price of camphor?
If there be a supply of salt the world will be sustained.
Are salt and camphor used together?

column2: உப்பு நீர் மேகம் உண்டால் உலகிற் பிரவாகம்.
column3: If the ocean were to become clouds, the world would be flooded.
Must the root of cusa grass be added to sea-water to make it fragrant?

column2: உப்பு மிஞ்சினால் தண்ணீர், தண்ணீர் மிஞ்சினால் உப்பு.
column3: Excess of salt calls for water, excess of water requires salt.
Did you spill the salt or the food?
Do not use salt both as food and condiment.
Using salt as a condiment you may swallow a mortar.
Nine things are required with salt, ten with pulse.

column2: உமக்கு என்ன, வயதுக்கு நரைத்ததோ மயிருக்கு நரைத்ததோ?
column3: Have you grown grey by age, or is your hair prematurely grey?
As the hand was wearied by beating husks in a mortar.
Though it may fly high, will a common bird become a hawk?
Like soul and body.
It is more natural to die than to continue in life.

column2: உயிரோடு திரும்பிப் பாராதவர் செத்தால் முத்தம் கொடுப்பாரா?
column3: Will he who would not turn to look at me when alive kiss me when dead?

column2: உயிரோடு ஒரு முத்தம் தராதவள் செத்தால் உடன்கட்டை ஏறுவாளா?
column3: Will she who would not kiss me when alive ascend my funeral pyre when I am dead?
If one is alive, he may subsist by bartering salt.
Will that which is in the mortar escape the pestle?

column2: உரலிலே தலையை மாட்டிக்கொண்டு உலக்கைக்குப் பயப்பட்டால் தீருமா ?
column3: Will your fear of the pestle avail aught after your head has become fast in the mortar?

column2: உரலுக்கு ஒரு பக்கம் இடி மத்தளத்துக்கு இரண்டு பக்கமும் இடி.
column3: The mortar is beaten at one end, the drum at both.
Does the rice mortar know what a famine is?

As the mortar went to the tomtom with its complaints.Used when one complains of his misfortunes to another who is in greater distress. The mortar is beaten at one end only, whereas the tomtom is beaten at both ends.
Supernatural power increases according to the intensity of the incantations.

column2: உருக்கம் உள்ள சிற்றாத்தை ஒதுக்கில் வாடி கட்டி அழ.
column3: O my loving aunt, come with me apart that we may embrace each other and weep.
The cat that has tasted nice things will leap on the swinging-tray.
When one deals fraudulently, even that which he has diminishes.
Frauds and tricks will destroy reputation.
Tricks and frauds and threats.
Like a beaded cat he appears as if he knew nothing.
As a beaded cat gave divine instruction.
As the figure is formed, beauty will become manifest.
Look to the temper, not beauty, to the connections not at money, when choosing a wife.

column2: உருவத்தினால் அல்ல, இன்பப் பேச்சினால் கிளி நன்குமதிக்கப்படும்.
column3: It is not for its form, but for its sweet prattling, that a parrot is esteemed.
A hero who does not sheath his sword.

column2: உலகத்துக்கு ஞானம் பேய், ஞானத்துக்கு உலகம் பேய்.
column3: To the world, wisdom is folly, to wisdom, the world is folly.
The world has many forms.
The whole country is at enmity with the thief who dwells there.
Will that which has resisted the strokes of the pestle shake at a sacred chant?
The pestle by wearing away has become the handle of a chisel.
The pestle grew and became a beam.
Nothing can be compared to the banquet of a miser.
You may cover the mouth of a rice-pot, but can you cover the mouth of the country?
The avaricious are subject to double expense.

column2: உல்லாசநடை மெலுக்குக்குக்கேடு மினுக்கு எண்ணெய் தலைக்குக் கேடு.
column3: An affected gait is injurious to graceful movement, and varnish is bad for the head.
If it be too cold to plough, why not put out the young plants?
A quarter of a measure to one’s friends, and sixteen measures to a religious mendicant.
Is the distinction of east and west observable in an ullak a small measure of capacity?
A dispute about an ullak.See the preceding.

column2: உழக்கு மிளகு கொடுப்பான் ஏன், ஒளித்திருந்து மிளகு நீர்குடிப்பான் ஏன்?
column3: Why give a measure of pepper, why drink the pepper water in a corner?
A plough share adapted to the tillage.
Nothing more advantageous than tillage.
Cattle unfit for the plough are kept outside.
If the ox be fit for the plough, will it not fetch a price in its own village?

column2: உழவுக்கு ஒரு சுற்றும் வராது, ஊணுக்கு முன்னே வரும்.
column3: At the plough it will not go a single round, at foddering time it comes first.

column2: உழவுக்குப் பிணைத்துவிடுகிற மாடும், கூட்டுக்குப் பிடித்துவிடுகிற ஆளும் உதவாது.
column3: The bullock yoked to the plough by force, and a man acting as a subsitute are alike useless.
Like one who, to avoid ploughing, feigned to be demonized.

column2: உழுகிற நாளையில் ஊருக்குப் போய், அறுக்கிற நாளையில் அரிவாள் கொண்டுவந்தாற்போல.
column3: As if one retired into the interior in the ploughing season, and returned with a sickle at the approach of harvest.

column2: உழுகிறமாடு பரதேசம் போனால், அங்கு ஒருவன் சுட்டி உழுவான் இங்கு ஒருவன் கட்டி உழுவான்.
column3: If a ploughing bullock go astray, it will be yoked wherever found.
Like beating the ploughing ox with the yoke.
As an ox licked a yoke of oxen when ploughing.
The ox treats the unskilful ploughman as his brother-in-law.
A ploughman is unable to keep village accounts.
When a ploughman balances his accounts, not even an ullak of grain remains.
Can a country be destroyed if there be no spy?
No spy, no theft.

column2: உளைவழியும், அடைமழையும், பொதி எருதும், ஒருவனுமாய் அலைகிறான்.
column3: He wanders alone after a pack-bull, through deep mud and incessant rain.
To be in debt to an inmate and to have the itch in the palm of the hand are intolerable evils.

column2: உள்ள பிள்ளை உரலை நக்கிக்கொண்டிருக்க, மற்றொரு பிள்ளைக்குத் திருப்பதிக்கு நடக்கிறாள்.
column3: While her child licks the mortar, she goes on a pilgrimage to Tirupati in expectation of another.

column2: உள்ளங்கைப் பாற்சோற்றை விட்டுப் புறங்கை நக்கியது போல.
column3: Like putting rice-milk in the palm and licking the back of the hand.
With what we have we may pretend to have that which we have not.
It is said that the whole body breaks out into ulcers when the truth is told.
Sell what you have and buy what is really good.
He is both an inmate and a thief.
I will tie nine knots of hair in the palm of the hand.
Like the Nelli berry (Phyllanthus simplex) in the palm of the hand.Said of something unmistakably evident.
If it be that hair has sprung up in the palm of the hand, a fool may yield to discipline.
When an inmate of a house joins the thief, stealing may be carried on till day-break.
If the truth be told, the blind woman will be greatly afflicted.
It is said that he took to wife a blind girl having given away all he had.

column2: உள்ளவன் பிள்ளை உப்போடு உண்ணும், இல்லாதவன் பிள்ளை சர்க்கரையோடு உண்ணும்.
column3: The child of the wealthy takes salt with his food, the child of the poor, sugar.
Our inmost thoughts are known to God.
When the truth is told, every one takes it ill.
The evil-eyed destroyed even what there was.
He who speaks the truth is regarded as a wicked person by the country.
Is association between an inmate and a thief to be tolerated?
Internal distress is known to the inmates.
A son-in-law of the same village and a ploughing buffalo are overwrought.

column2: உள்ளே கொட்டின தேனே ஒரு மந்திரம் சொல்லுகிறேன் கேளே.
column3: O thou scorpion thou hast thrust thy sting into me, listen, I will utter an incantation.
What! is it enmity at heart and friendship on the lips?
Bowels burning within, lips shedding ripe fruit.
What! is poison to be mixed with friendship?
What! to feign friendship and to sting as a wasp?
No doubt about friendship, but do not put your hand into my basket.
Wealth may aid both in friendship and enmity.

column2: உறவுக்கு ஒன்பது படி ஊருக்குப் பத்துப் படி.
column3: Nine measures for your friends and ten for the country.
The snapping of the cord of the swinging-tray is like a hunting excursion to the cropped dog.

column2: உறியிலே தயிர் இருக்க ஊர் எங்கும் போனாற்போல்.
column3: As if one sought curds throughout the village when a supply was on his own tray.
Why go about in quest of ghee when you have butter on your own tray?

column2: உறியிற் பணம் போய்த் தெருவிற் சண்டையை இழுக்கிறது.
column3: The money on the swinging-tray goes out and picks a quarrel in the public road.Money is not infrequently put on a swinging tray in a cup or small basket.
An affair firmly settled will never fail.
Is he a friend who helps not in adversity?
If encouraged, he will jump over the house top.
A true friend is the nectar éf life.
When the truth is told, a breach between friends may be healed.

column2: உற்றது சொல்ல ஊரும் அல்ல, நல்லது சொல்ல நாடும் அல்ல.
column3: This is not the village in which a man may speak the truth, nor the country in which, to give advice.

column2: உற்றார் தின்றால் புற்றாய் விளையும், ஊரார் தின்றால் பேராய் விளையும்.
column3: If friends consume one’s grain an ant hole, if strangers consume it, fame.

column2: உற்றாருக்கு ஒன்றும் கொடான், நமனாருக்கு நாலும் கொடுப்பான்.
column3: He will give nothing to friends, but to Yama the regent of the dead he will give even four i.e., all.
Will friends help or will strangers?

Pè, pè to you, and pè pè to your father also.The following story is told about this proverb:—a debtor had two creditors. One of them said if you will pay me I will suggest an expedient for getting rid of the other creditor. He then instructed him when again applied to for payment, to feign madness, and to say Pé, Pé a demon, a demon. The creditor not satisfied had him summoned before a Magistrate where he did as before. The Magistrate thinking the debtor mad, decided that he was not amenable to a court of justice.The other creditor then went to the debtor expecting to be paid according to promise. To his surprise the man said Pé, Pé, whereupon he said, do you deal with me also in this way, when, the debtor replied as the proverb stands.

column2: உனக்கு யான் அபயம் எனக்கு நீ உபயம்.
column3: I am under your protection, you are my patron.
Bullets will not fly by your strength.

column2: உன் சொல்லிலே உப்பும் இல்லை புளியும் இல்லை.
column3: In your words there is neither salt nor acid.

column2: உன் முற்றுக்குத் திருக்கு எல்லாம் என் உடுப்புக்குள்ளே.
column3: Your petulance and fraud are all in my dress.
Never trust one who has deceived you.

column2: உன்னைக் கொடுப்பேனோ சென்னைக் கூனி, நீ சுமை சுமந்தல்லோ கூனிப்போனாய்.
column3: O thou hunch-backed woman of Madras, how can I give thee up, hast thou not become crooked by bearing burdens?

column2: உகமுடிய மழை பெய்தாலும் ஓட்டாங்கிளிஞ்சல் பயிர் ஆகுமா?
column3: Though it should rain to the end of the world, would broken oyster shells vegetate?
As a grind-stone fell on the whitlow.

column2: உங்கள் உறவிலே வேகிறதைப்பார்க்கிலும் ஒரு கட்டு விறகிலே வேகிறது நல்லது.
column3: It is better to be consumed by a faggot than by your friendship.

column2: உங்கள் பெண்டுகள் கொண்டல் அடித்தால் கண்கள் கொள்ளாது.
column3: The eyes will be over-powered by the dancing of your women.
I have washed my hands of you in the sea.

column2: உசு பிடி என்றால் நீ பிடி என்கிறதாம் நாய்.
column3: It is said that when he urged his dog to seize, it replied, you do so.
If slippered on the head, will the crown of the head become cool?

column2: உடம்பு எல்லாம் புழுத்தவன் அம்மன் கோவிலை கெடுத்தகதை.
column3: The story of a man who defiled Amman covil by his ulcerated body.
As the wearied ass went to the salt-pit.
All who have taken a body have taken an alms-dish.
Must one not invigorate the body and then proceed to meditation?

column2: உடம்பு எங்கும் சுடுகிற தழலை மடியிலே கட்டுகிறாய்.
column3: Thou huggest up in thy bosom the embers that warm every part of the body.
Of what consequence is cold to him whose whole body is drenched?

column2: உடலுக்கோ பால் வார்த்து உண்பது, ஊருக்கோ பால்வார்த்து உண்பது
column3: Do we feed on milk for the benefit of the body, or that our neighbours may know it?
Divided as regards body, one in soul.

column2: உடல் ஒருவனுக்குப் பிறந்து நா பலருக்குப் பிறந்தது.
column3: The body is born for one, the tongue for many.
Anxieties which the ocean cannot contain attend the body as long as it exists.
The body of one who has no kindred is exposed to desolation.
A garment not worn is a prey to moths.
The cloth one has put on bites as a snake.
The guana may go, it is enough if the hand be free.

column2: உடும்புக்கு இரண்டு நாக்கு மனிதனுக்கு ஒரு நாக்கு.
column3: The guana has two tongues, man only one.

column2: உடும்புக்கு இரண்டு நாக்கு, மனிதனுக்கு இரண்டு நாக்கு உண்டா?
column3: The guana has a double tongue, has man the same?
Can you kick against the thorns of the Acacia?
Learning is real wealth.
Wealth and poverty are not abiding.
What! give one’s property and sacrifice one’s reputation?
Will a crop not looked after by the owner flourish?
Pluck up the weeds about the mortar as your master bids you.
An embezzler is more obstinate than the owner.
Weed around the areca tree at thy master’s biding.

column2: உடையவன் இல்லாச் சேலை ஒரு முழம் கட்டை.
column3: The cloth whose owner is absent is a cubit too short.

column2: உடையார் வீட்டு மோருக்கு அகப்பைக் கணக்கு என்ன?
column3: What necessity is there for estimating the quantity of butter-milk by the ladle in the homestead of the chief?
You must sit before lying down, must you not?
Can he tie a man that is running who cannot tie one that is sitting!
What! beautify the outside of a wall while the inside is neglected?
Garnish the inside of the wall and then the outside.
A mirror reflects whatever is before it.
A mere instrument void of feeling and food without salt are alike.
What, is it to mix poison with the rice one is eating?

column2: உண்ட உடம்பிற்கு உறுதி, உழுத புலத்தில் நெல்லு.
column3: There is strength in a body well fed, and grain in the ploughed field.
A child that eats well thrives.
Is rice required for a full stomach, or oil for a baldhead?
That which has been eaten will be evidenced in the belching.
May hospitality be requited by treachery?

column2: உண்ட உடம்பு உருகும் தின்ற பாக்குச் சிவக்கும்.
column3: The body nourished by another’s bounty will melt, arecanut when chewed turns red.
Friendship cemented by food will compassionate.
The body shews who is well fed.
Hunger is appeased by eating, will it be so by looking at food?

column2: உண்டால் தின்றால் உறவு, கொண்டால் கொடுத்தால் உறவு.
column3: Friendship is promoted and strengthned by hospitality and also by intermarriage.
When you eat, do so to keep up your strength.
Poison destroys when taken.
Moderation in food becomes women.

column2: உண்டு தின்று உள்ளே இருவென்றால் உயர எழும்பி ஏன் குதிக்கிறாய்?
column3: When you are told to eat heartily and remain in-doors, why do you rise up and jump?

column2: உண்டு உறியில் இரு என்றால் உருண்டு தரையில் விழுகிறாய்.
column3: If I say eat and sit on the hanging-shelf, you fall to the ground.
If a crab gets fat, it will not stay in its hole.

column2: உண்டு தின்று உயரம் ஆனால் ஊரிலே காரியம் என்ன?
column3: What business has he in the village seeing that he is grown up?
His hand is weary with eating.
When I call you to eat, you come to strike me.
I will cut your uvula and smear it with chunam-lime.
Property not enjoyed comes to nought.

column2: உண்ணாமல் ஊர் எல்லாம் திரியலாம் உடுக்காமல் ஒரு வீட்டுக்கும் போகலாகாது.
column3: One may wander over the whole country without eating, but one may not go to a single house without dressing.

column2: உண்ணாமல் ஒன்பது வீடு போகலாம், உடுக்காமல் ஒரு வீடும் போகல் ஆகாது.
column3: One may go to nine houses without eating, but not to one without garments.
Mistleloe attached to edible greens.
Do not go to work without eating.

column2: உண்பான் தின்பான் சிவப்பிராமணன், குத்துக்கு நிற்பான் வீர முஷ்டி.
column3: The Saiva Brahman will freely eat, the ferocious mendicant will show fight.
Though you look at those who are eating, you may not look at those who are ploughing.

column2: உண்மை சொல்லிக் கெட்டாரும் பொய் சொல்லி வாழ்ந்தாரும் இல்லை.
column3: None ever perishes by speaking the truth, none flourishes by uttering falsehood.

column2: உதடு மன்றாடப் போய் உள்ளிருந்த பல்லும் போனாற்போல.
column3: As if the teeth also went out when the lips had gone to beg.

column2: உதட்டிலே உள்ள வாழைப்பழத்தை உள்ளே தள்ளுவார் உண்டோ?
column3: Are there any who push into the mouth the plantain that is already between the lips?

column2: உதட்டுக்கு மிஞ்சின பல்லும் திருட்டுக்கு மிஞ்சின கையும் ஆகாது.
column3: Protruding teeth and a hand given to thieving are bad.
Will the cow not yield its milk because it has sore lips?
A small particle adhering to the lip cannot be removed by blowing.
Divers disguises for the sake of the belly.
Thou art a useless vessel, a piece of brass without sound!
Like writing a chit to a thrifty merchant.
What, accident can harm those who help others?
A woman with a bustling gait came and destroyed what there was.
Even gold is a thing of nought to the generous.
Sympathy arises, does it not, from consanguinity?
Worms will breed in due course, will they not, in a foot accustomed to kick?
Before his kicking feet were eaten of worms his abdomen had ulcerated.
He that washes for him who kicks him is a first-rate washerman.
Contracts are needless as regards honest men and cheats. : The one needs no binding, and the other admits no obligations.
A faithful servant is more than a mother.
The good are exposed to no danger.
Exertion is a manly quality.
Facilities suited to one's office.
It is unfortunate when evil is returned for good.

column2: உபசரியாக வீட்டிலே உண்ணாதிருப்பதே ஒரு கோடி தனம்.
column3: It is a crore of wealth to abstain from entertainment in a house void of civility.
What! are the civil to be requited by incivility?

column2: உபசார வார்த்தை காசாகுமா, உண்டாலொழியப் பசி தீருமா?
column3: Will mere ceremony pass for money, will hunger be appeased otherwise than by eating?
Can that which may be realized by cunning be obtained by force?

column2: உப்பு இருந்த பாண்டமும் உபாயம் இருந்த நெஞ்சமும் தட்டி உடையாமல் தானே உடையும்.
column3: A salt-cup and a deceitful heart will of themselves come to destruction.

column2: உப்பு இருந்த பாண்டமும் உண்மை இல்லா நெஞ்சும் தட்டி உடையாமல் தாமே உடையும்.
column3: A vessel impregnated with salt, and a mind without truth need none to destroy them, they destroy themselves.
Unsalted food is fit only for the rubbish heap.
Remember through life those who have given you salt.
Every thing from salt to camphor is required.Said of things domestic and sacred.
He who has eaten salt will drink water.
Thou starest as the Brahmanee who had lost her salted mutton.In her bewilderment she had no remedy because she dared not make known the loss of a thing she ought not to have had in her possession.
Does the salt merchant know the price of camphor?
If there be a supply of salt the world will be sustained.
Are salt and camphor used together?

column2: உப்பு நீர் மேகம் உண்டால் உலகிற் பிரவாகம்.
column3: If the ocean were to become clouds, the world would be flooded.
Must the root of cusa grass be added to sea-water to make it fragrant?

column2: உப்பு மிஞ்சினால் தண்ணீர், தண்ணீர் மிஞ்சினால் உப்பு.
column3: Excess of salt calls for water, excess of water requires salt.
Did you spill the salt or the food?
Do not use salt both as food and condiment.
Using salt as a condiment you may swallow a mortar.
Nine things are required with salt, ten with pulse.

column2: உமக்கு என்ன, வயதுக்கு நரைத்ததோ மயிருக்கு நரைத்ததோ?
column3: Have you grown grey by age, or is your hair prematurely grey?
As the hand was wearied by beating husks in a mortar.
Though it may fly high, will a common bird become a hawk?
Like soul and body.
It is more natural to die than to continue in life.

column2: உயிரோடு திரும்பிப் பாராதவர் செத்தால் முத்தம் கொடுப்பாரா?
column3: Will he who would not turn to look at me when alive kiss me when dead?

column2: உயிரோடு ஒரு முத்தம் தராதவள் செத்தால் உடன்கட்டை ஏறுவாளா?
column3: Will she who would not kiss me when alive ascend my funeral pyre when I am dead?
If one is alive, he may subsist by bartering salt.
Will that which is in the mortar escape the pestle?

column2: உரலிலே தலையை மாட்டிக்கொண்டு உலக்கைக்குப் பயப்பட்டால் தீருமா ?
column3: Will your fear of the pestle avail aught after your head has become fast in the mortar?

column2: உரலுக்கு ஒரு பக்கம் இடி மத்தளத்துக்கு இரண்டு பக்கமும் இடி.
column3: The mortar is beaten at one end, the drum at both.
Does the rice mortar know what a famine is?

As the mortar went to the tomtom with its complaints.Used when one complains of his misfortunes to another who is in greater distress. The mortar is beaten at one end only, whereas the tomtom is beaten at both ends.
Supernatural power increases according to the intensity of the incantations.

column2: உருக்கம் உள்ள சிற்றாத்தை ஒதுக்கில் வாடி கட்டி அழ.
column3: O my loving aunt, come with me apart that we may embrace each other and weep.
The cat that has tasted nice things will leap on the swinging-tray.
When one deals fraudulently, even that which he has diminishes.
Frauds and tricks will destroy reputation.
Tricks and frauds and threats.
Like a beaded cat he appears as if he knew nothing.
As a beaded cat gave divine instruction.
As the figure is formed, beauty will become manifest.
Look to the temper, not beauty, to the connections not at money, when choosing a wife.

column2: உருவத்தினால் அல்ல, இன்பப் பேச்சினால் கிளி நன்குமதிக்கப்படும்.
column3: It is not for its form, but for its sweet prattling, that a parrot is esteemed.
A hero who does not sheath his sword.

column2: உலகத்துக்கு ஞானம் பேய், ஞானத்துக்கு உலகம் பேய்.
column3: To the world, wisdom is folly, to wisdom, the world is folly.
The world has many forms.
The whole country is at enmity with the thief who dwells there.
Will that which has resisted the strokes of the pestle shake at a sacred chant?
The pestle by wearing away has become the handle of a chisel.
The pestle grew and became a beam.
Nothing can be compared to the banquet of a miser.
You may cover the mouth of a rice-pot, but can you cover the mouth of the country?
The avaricious are subject to double expense.

column2: உல்லாசநடை மெலுக்குக்குக்கேடு மினுக்கு எண்ணெய் தலைக்குக் கேடு.
column3: An affected gait is injurious to graceful movement, and varnish is bad for the head.
If it be too cold to plough, why not put out the young plants?
A quarter of a measure to one’s friends, and sixteen measures to a religious mendicant.
Is the distinction of east and west observable in an ullak a small measure of capacity?
A dispute about an ullak.See the preceding.

column2: உழக்கு மிளகு கொடுப்பான் ஏன், ஒளித்திருந்து மிளகு நீர்குடிப்பான் ஏன்?
column3: Why give a measure of pepper, why drink the pepper water in a corner?
A plough share adapted to the tillage.
Nothing more advantageous than tillage.
Cattle unfit for the plough are kept outside.
If the ox be fit for the plough, will it not fetch a price in its own village?

column2: உழவுக்கு ஒரு சுற்றும் வராது, ஊணுக்கு முன்னே வரும்.
column3: At the plough it will not go a single round, at foddering time it comes first.

column2: உழவுக்குப் பிணைத்துவிடுகிற மாடும், கூட்டுக்குப் பிடித்துவிடுகிற ஆளும் உதவாது.
column3: The bullock yoked to the plough by force, and a man acting as a subsitute are alike useless.
Like one who, to avoid ploughing, feigned to be demonized.

column2: உழுகிற நாளையில் ஊருக்குப் போய், அறுக்கிற நாளையில் அரிவாள் கொண்டுவந்தாற்போல.
column3: As if one retired into the interior in the ploughing season, and returned with a sickle at the approach of harvest.

column2: உழுகிறமாடு பரதேசம் போனால், அங்கு ஒருவன் சுட்டி உழுவான் இங்கு ஒருவன் கட்டி உழுவான்.
column3: If a ploughing bullock go astray, it will be yoked wherever found.
Like beating the ploughing ox with the yoke.
As an ox licked a yoke of oxen when ploughing.
The ox treats the unskilful ploughman as his brother-in-law.
A ploughman is unable to keep village accounts.
When a ploughman balances his accounts, not even an ullak of grain remains.
Can a country be destroyed if there be no spy?
No spy, no theft.

column2: உளைவழியும், அடைமழையும், பொதி எருதும், ஒருவனுமாய் அலைகிறான்.
column3: He wanders alone after a pack-bull, through deep mud and incessant rain.
To be in debt to an inmate and to have the itch in the palm of the hand are intolerable evils.

column2: உள்ள பிள்ளை உரலை நக்கிக்கொண்டிருக்க, மற்றொரு பிள்ளைக்குத் திருப்பதிக்கு நடக்கிறாள்.
column3: While her child licks the mortar, she goes on a pilgrimage to Tirupati in expectation of another.

column2: உள்ளங்கைப் பாற்சோற்றை விட்டுப் புறங்கை நக்கியது போல.
column3: Like putting rice-milk in the palm and licking the back of the hand.
With what we have we may pretend to have that which we have not.
It is said that the whole body breaks out into ulcers when the truth is told.
Sell what you have and buy what is really good.
He is both an inmate and a thief.
I will tie nine knots of hair in the palm of the hand.
Like the Nelli berry (Phyllanthus simplex) in the palm of the hand.Said of something unmistakably evident.
If it be that hair has sprung up in the palm of the hand, a fool may yield to discipline.
When an inmate of a house joins the thief, stealing may be carried on till day-break.
If the truth be told, the blind woman will be greatly afflicted.
It is said that he took to wife a blind girl having given away all he had.

column2: உள்ளவன் பிள்ளை உப்போடு உண்ணும், இல்லாதவன் பிள்ளை சர்க்கரையோடு உண்ணும்.
column3: The child of the wealthy takes salt with his food, the child of the poor, sugar.
Our inmost thoughts are known to God.
When the truth is told, every one takes it ill.
The evil-eyed destroyed even what there was.
He who speaks the truth is regarded as a wicked person by the country.
Is association between an inmate and a thief to be tolerated?
Internal distress is known to the inmates.
A son-in-law of the same village and a ploughing buffalo are overwrought.

column2: உள்ளே கொட்டின தேனே ஒரு மந்திரம் சொல்லுகிறேன் கேளே.
column3: O thou scorpion thou hast thrust thy sting into me, listen, I will utter an incantation.
What! is it enmity at heart and friendship on the lips?
Bowels burning within, lips shedding ripe fruit.
What! is poison to be mixed with friendship?
What! to feign friendship and to sting as a wasp?
No doubt about friendship, but do not put your hand into my basket.
Wealth may aid both in friendship and enmity.

column2: உறவுக்கு ஒன்பது படி ஊருக்குப் பத்துப் படி.
column3: Nine measures for your friends and ten for the country.
The snapping of the cord of the swinging-tray is like a hunting excursion to the cropped dog.

column2: உறியிலே தயிர் இருக்க ஊர் எங்கும் போனாற்போல்.
column3: As if one sought curds throughout the village when a supply was on his own tray.
Why go about in quest of ghee when you have butter on your own tray?

column2: உறியிற் பணம் போய்த் தெருவிற் சண்டையை இழுக்கிறது.
column3: The money on the swinging-tray goes out and picks a quarrel in the public road.Money is not infrequently put on a swinging tray in a cup or small basket.
An affair firmly settled will never fail.
Is he a friend who helps not in adversity?
If encouraged, he will jump over the house top.
A true friend is the nectar éf life.
When the truth is told, a breach between friends may be healed.

column2: உற்றது சொல்ல ஊரும் அல்ல, நல்லது சொல்ல நாடும் அல்ல.
column3: This is not the village in which a man may speak the truth, nor the country in which, to give advice.

column2: உற்றார் தின்றால் புற்றாய் விளையும், ஊரார் தின்றால் பேராய் விளையும்.
column3: If friends consume one’s grain an ant hole, if strangers consume it, fame.

column2: உற்றாருக்கு ஒன்றும் கொடான், நமனாருக்கு நாலும் கொடுப்பான்.
column3: He will give nothing to friends, but to Yama the regent of the dead he will give even four i.e., all.
Will friends help or will strangers?

Pè, pè to you, and pè pè to your father also.The following story is told about this proverb:—a debtor had two creditors. One of them said if you will pay me I will suggest an expedient for getting rid of the other creditor. He then instructed him when again applied to for payment, to feign madness, and to say Pé, Pé a demon, a demon. The creditor not satisfied had him summoned before a Magistrate where he did as before. The Magistrate thinking the debtor mad, decided that he was not amenable to a court of justice.The other creditor then went to the debtor expecting to be paid according to promise. To his surprise the man said Pé, Pé, whereupon he said, do you deal with me also in this way, when, the debtor replied as the proverb stands.

column2: உனக்கு யான் அபயம் எனக்கு நீ உபயம்.
column3: I am under your protection, you are my patron.
Bullets will not fly by your strength.

column2: உன் சொல்லிலே உப்பும் இல்லை புளியும் இல்லை.
column3: In your words there is neither salt nor acid.

column2: உன் முற்றுக்குத் திருக்கு எல்லாம் என் உடுப்புக்குள்ளே.
column3: Your petulance and fraud are all in my dress.
Never trust one who has deceived you.

column2: உன்னைக் கொடுப்பேனோ சென்னைக் கூனி, நீ சுமை சுமந்தல்லோ கூனிப்போனாய்.
column3: O thou hunch-backed woman of Madras, how can I give thee up, hast thou not become crooked by bearing burdens?

column2: எங்களால் ஒன்றும் இல்லை எல்லாம் உங்கள் கர்மம்.
column3: Nothing lies in our might, all must be done by you.

column2: எங்கும் மடமாய் இருக்கிறது இருக்கத்தான் இடம் இல்லை.
column3: Choultries everywhere, but no place of accommodation.
No matter where hit, a dog when struck, lifts up his leg.

column2: எங்கே புகை உண்டோ அங்கே நெருப்பு உண்டு.
column3: Wherever there is smoke there is fire.
Like belching after eating leavings.
Can hunger be appeased by eating leavings?

column2: எச்சிற்கல்லைக்கு மண்ணாங்கட்டி ஆதரவு மண்ணாங்கட்டிக்கு எச்சிற்கல்லை ஆதரவு.
column3: A clod is the support of the leaf-plate, and the leaf-plate is the support of the clod.

column3: What if the fruit of the etti tree (strychnos nux vomica) ripens; of what use is the prosperity of the niggardly?

column3: A buffalo eight years old is at a loss to find its way to the tank.

column3: No matter how skilled a woman may be in numbers and letters, her judgment will be second rate.Many modern Hindus of the day (1873) though themselves earnest about University honors, evince but very little interest regarding the intellectual and moral culture of their daughters. column2: எலியைக் கண்டு பூனை ஏங்கி ஏங்கிக் கிடக்குமோ?

A separate hole is to be preferred though it be a rat-hole.One’s own cot is desirable however humble.

Like infusing acidity into a lemon.
Like manuring a lemon tree.

column3: On all heads there are eight letters, on mine there are ten.

column2: எல்லாரும் தடுக்கின்கீழ் நுழைந்தால் அவன் கோலத்தின்கீழ் நுழைவான்.
column3: If all can hide themselves under a small mat, he can conceal himself under the ornamental drawing on the floor.The கோலம் here mentioned is a floor ornament very commonly formed at the entrance of a native house: the form is circular, These ornaments are renewed every morning. They contain various devices. In some a temple car as delineated, in others trees, birds, flowers. They are generally striking because the combinations indicate considerable skill. It may be that they are connected with the prevalent ideas regarding the evil eye.

All the skill in steering centres in the out-rigger.Out-rigger boats or canoes are common on the south western coast of Ceylon. They are very swift and most safe. In 1848 when at Colombo duty called me daily to a large ship at the time out in the offing anchored in seven fathoms of water. On the occasion under notice I went on board in an ordinary Cingalese out-rigger canoe. In the evening after having completed the berths, for my two daughters who were going home for education, I betook myself to the boat, and soon after we got clear of the ship the boat was struck in the stern, filled, and went down. When I emerged from the waves I found myself near one of the two brackets of the out-rigger. The canoe was submerged but could not sink lower than the ends of the brackets tied to her side. The out-rigger at the other end of the brackets was floating on the surface of the sea, the canoe being suspended by it like a pair of scales from a beam. Aided by one of those brackets, which I pressed to my bosom, and a Chubb's Palanquin box, I gained the rocks soon after sunset and escaped to land.
All our progress depends on the outrigger.
Are all men good men, are all stones rubies?

Though a broken one, a walking stick is necessary.
Though I cling to him, he repels me.

column2: ஒட்டினால் தொட்டிலிலும் கொள்ளும் ஒட்டாவிட்டால் கட்டிலிலும் கொள்ளாது.
column3: If compressed the crib will hold it, but if not even a bedstead will not contain it.
Is it to say draw one ulak of milk though the animal is lean?
As a camel is loaded.
Though the odina tree grow large, will it do for a pillar?
Though the odina tree grow large, will it serve for a mortar?

column2: ஒதி பெருத்து என்ன, உபகாரம் இல்லாதவன் வாழ்ந்து என்ன?
column3: What avails the growth of an odina tree? Of what use is the prosperity of the ungenerous?
Will an odina tree do for a pillar? will a broken oyster-shell pass as a coin?
Even an odina tree may prove useful on an emergency.

column2: ஒய்யாரக்கொண்டையாம் தாழம்பூவாம் உள்ளே மேயுமாம் ஈரும் பேனும்.
column3: Her tresses are graceful and ornamented with the flowers of the screw pine, but nits and lice breed therein.
Are two swords contained in one sheath?
Is there but one way to a village?

column2: ஒரு கட்டு வைக்கோலைத் தண்ணீரிற் போட்டு எட்டு ஆள் கூடி இழுத்தாற் போல.
column3: As a bundle of straw that had been dipped in water was dragged along by eight persons.
What, to tie two elephants to one pole?
To earn one cash is as difficult as to find a horse with horns.

column2: ஒரு காசு பேணின் இரு காசு தேறும்.
column3: One cash saved is two cash got.
He who has done a thing once, may do it again.

column2: ஒரு காசு கொடாதவன் ஒரு வராகன் கொடுப்பானா?
column3: Will he who refuses to give a cash, give a pagoda?A Pagoda is a gold coin worth about seven shillings.
He weeps when the word money is uttered.
One drop of butter-milk to a pot of milk.
Can a fort be taken with one ball?
