column2: ஒரு குலத்திற் பிறந்த தாமரையும் அல்லியும் ஒரே தன்மையுடையதல்ல அதுபோல ஒரு தாயின் வயிற்றில் பிறந்த சகோதரர் இருப்பார்கள்.
column3: The lotus flowers of a tank are not all of the same species, so the children of the same mother are not all alike.

column2: ஒரு கூடு முடைந்தவன் ஒன்பது கூடு முடைவான்.
column3: He who has platted one basket may plat nine.

column2: ஒரு கூடை கல்லும் தெய்வமானால், கும்பிடுகிறது எந்தக்கல்லை?
column3: If all the stones in a basket be gods, which shall I worship?
Can clapping be effected by one hand?

column2: ஒரு கை தட்டினால் ஓசை எழும்புமா, இருகை தட்டினால் ஓசை எழும்புமா?
column3: Can clapping be effected with one, or with both hands?
Can cubits be measured with only one arm?

column2: ஒரு கோபம் வந்து கிணற்றில் விழுந்தால் ஆயிரம் சந்தோஷம் வந்தாலும் எழும்பலாமா?
column3: When one has fallen into a well in a fit of anger, will a thousand joyous considerations help him out?
Two kinds of oil in one chatty.
A dog does not know the vessels used on fast-days.Separate vessels are kept for special occasions, and also for different purposes on ordinary days, as for boiling milk &c.

column2: ஒரு சாண் காட்டிலே ஒரு முழத்தடி வெட்டலாமா?
column3: Can a stick a cubit long be cut in a grove a span high?
Having fallen once are you not wiser?
An ex parte statement is straighter than a line.
Are there two punishments for one head?

column2: ஒரு தாய் அற்ற பிள்ளைக்கு ஊரெல்லாம் தாய்.
column3: The whole village is mother to the motherless.

column2: ஒரு நன்றி செய்தவரை உள்ள அளவும் நினை.
column3: Think of those who have done you even one favour as long as you live.
Like shaving the head for a single day’s dance.

column2: ஒரு நாளைக்கு இகழ்ச்சி ஒரு நாளைக்குப் புகழ்ச்சி.
column3: One day blame, another day praise.

column2: ஒரு நாளைக்கு இறக்கிறது கோடி பிறக்கிறது கோடி.
column3: Ten millions are born, and ten millions die, daily.

column2: ஒரு நாளும் சிரிக்காதவன் திருநாளிலே சிரித்தான், திருநாளும் வெறுநாளாயிற்று.
column3: A man who had never laughed before, laughed on a festival day, consequently it became a common day.
Although only one day, it is a festival day.

column2: ஒரு பனை இரண்டு பாளை ஒன்று நுங்கு ஒன்று கள்ளு.
column3: One palmyra has two spathes, one yields fruit, the other toddy.
One grain suffices to test a whole pot of boiled rice.

column2: ஒரு பிள்ளை பெற்றவளுக்கு உறியிலே சோறு நாலு பிள்ளை பெற்றவளுக்கு நடுத்தெருவிலே சோறு.
column3: The rice for a woman who has borne one child, is on the swinging tray, that of the woman who has borne four, is in the middle of the street.

column2: ஒருபிள்ளையென்று ஊட்டி வளர்த்தாளாம், அது செரியாக் குணம் பிடித்துச் செத்ததாம்.
column3: It is said that having only one child she fed it well, but that it died of indigestion.

column2: ஒரு புத்திரன் ஆனாலும் குரு புத்திரன் ஆவானா?
column3: Although he is an only son, will he become an obedient disciple?
Though only one item, note it.
What! did one tree yield all this fruit?

column2: ஒரு மரத்துப் பட்டை ஒரு மரத்திலே ஒட்டுமா?
column3: Will the bark of one tree stick to another?

column2: ஒரு மரத்துக் கொம்பு ஒரு மரத்தில் ஒட்டாது.
column3: The branch of one tree will not stick to another.
Be single-minded, assist those that teach the Vedas.

column2: ஒரு மிளகுக்கு ஆற்றைக் கட்டி இறைத்த செட்டி.
column3: A merchant who dammed up and drained a river to recover a grain of pepper.
One grain of pepper and four grains of salt will suffice.
What! is it to take up the soil by diving once?
A family divided against itself will perish together.
If known to one, the world may know it.
A secret known to one may spread through the world.

column2: ஒருவனுக்கு இருவர் துணை ஒருவனை அறிய இருவர் வேண்டும்.
column3: Two men may help one, two are necessary that one may be known.
One of illustrious name unknown to any.

column2: ஒருவனைக் கொன்றவன் உடனே சாவான் பல பேரைக் கொன்றவன் பட்டம் ஆளுவான்.
column3: He who kills one person immediately suffers death, he who kills many is rewarded with a crown.

column2: ஒருவனாய்ப் பிறப்பது ஒரு பிறப்பாமா ஒன்றி மரம் தோப்பாமா?
column3: Is it worth being to be an only offspring? Is a single tree a tope a grove?
May you cut off a man’s head because there is a ruby in it?

column2: ஒருவனாய்ப் பிறந்தால் தனிமை இருவராய்ப் பிறந்தாற் பகைமை.
column3: When only one is born there is loneliness, when two enmity.

column2: ஒருவர் அறிந்தால் இரகசியம் இருவர் அறிந்தால் பராசியம்.
column3: If known to one only it is a secret, if to two it is public.
Attach yourself to one person, and dwell in one house.
A single finger cannot snap.

column2: ஒவ்வாக் கூட்டிலும் தனிமை அழகு, ஒவ்வாப் பேச்சு வசையோடு ஒக்கும்.
column3: Solitude is preferable to disagreeable society, an unpleasant expression may be felt as a reproach.
If only as large as the palm of the hand a house that does not leak will suffice.
Virtue is superior to rank.
As a flood came into a leaky house.
As a chatty placed under a leak.

column2: ஒழுங்கு ஒரு பணம் சளுக்கு முக்காற் பணம்.
column3: Propriety is worth a fanam, pride, three fourths of a fanam.
Of what use is a mustache to a sneaking soldier?
When trying to conceal one’s self, is it fit to do so in the house of the chief?
Is the place too narrow for one who has gone to hide himself?

column2: ஒள்ளியர் தெள்ளியர் ஆயினும் ஊழ்வினை பைய நுழைந்து விடும்.
column3: However enlightened and clear-headed one may be, his destiny will quietly creep in.
Property received under mortgage, or as a dowry, must be taken into immediate possession.
As the demon that came for shelter destroyed the village.
As if a crow that came for shelter was strengthened in his position.

column2: ஒன்ற வந்த பிடாரி ஊர்ப் பிடாரியை ஒட்டினதுபோல்.
column3: As a demoness that came for shelter drove away the demoness of the village.

column2: ஒன்ற வந்த பிடாரி ஊர்ப் பிடாரி ஆனதுபோல.
column3: As a demoness that came for shelter became the demoness of the village.

column2: ஒன்றான பிரபு உறங்கிக் கிடைக்கையில் பிச்சைக்கு வந்தவன் தத்தியோனத்திற்கு அழுகிறானாம்.
column3: It is said that whilst a peerless nobleman was lying hungry and exhausted, a beggar cries for rice and curds.

column2: ஒன்றால் ஒன்று குறை இல்லை, முன்னாலே கட்டத் துணி இல்லை.
column3: Not a single want, yet no cloth to put on.
One thing meditated, another effected.

column2: ஒன்றும் அறியாளாம் கன்னி அவளைப் பிடத்ததாம் சன்னி.
column3: It is said that the virgin is innocent, and is seized with lock-jaw.
He who knows one thing does not know all things.

column2: ஒன்றும் இல்லையென்று ஊதினான் அதுதானும் இல்லையென்று கொட்டினான்.
column3: He blew a conch to report that there was nothing, and beat a drum to intimate that there was not even that.
Nine wives to him who has nothing.

column2: ஒன்றும் அற்ற தங்காளுக்கு ஒன்பது நாள் சடங்கா?
column3: Is a ceremony of nine days to be performed for a destitute woman?
One horse, one horseman.
One king, one horse.
If one thing only be obtained, it ought to be good.
The thing asserted must be maintained.
Though you get only one thing, get it quickly.
If you get only one as your hire, take a calf.
Though you touch but one thing, touch what is good.Most likely referring to the choice of a wife.

column2: ஓ கொடுப்பானுக்கு வாழ்க்கைப் பட்டு ஓட்டம் ஒழிய, நடை இல்லை.
column3: Having become the wife of the wretch, I am compelled to run instead of walking.

column2: ஓங்கில் அறியும் உயர் கடலின் ஆழம், பாங்கி அறிவாள் தன் பத்தாவின் வலிமை.
column3: An onkil fish knows the depth of the ocean, a wife knows her husband’s strength.
Ring the bell, and perform pujá.
Sounding brass, a soundless earthen vessel.

column2: ஓடம் விட்டு இறங்கினால் ஓடக்காரனுக்கு ஒரு சொட்டு.
column3: On landing, a cuff for the boatman.
Even a navigable river may burn the feet when dry.
A block of wood to which a boat is moored.
After crossing, a cuff for the boatman.
A boat may be sesen in a cart, and a cart in a boat.

column2: ஓடம் விட்ட இடம் அடி சுடும் அடி சுட்ட இடம் ஓடம் விடப்படும்.
column3: The foot may be burnt in a navigable river, and a boat may float where the foot was burnt.

column2: ஓடவும் மாட்டான் பிடிக்கவும் மாட்டான், ஓயாமல் பேசுவான்.
column3: He can neither run nor overtake, but he can talk continually.
I can neither run nor catch.

column2: ஓடி ஒரு கோடி தேடுவதிலும் இருந்து ஒரு காசு தேடுவது நலம்.
column3: It is better to acquire a cash in quiet than a lak by running about.

column2: ஓடி ஒன்பது பணம் சம்பாதிப்பதிலும் உட்காந்திருந்து ஒரு பணம் சம்பாதிப்பது நன்று.
column3: Better earn one fanam where you are, than nine fanams by running hither and thither.
The sole of the foot has become white by constant running.

column2: ஓடிப்போன புருஷன் வந்து கூடிக்கொண்டான், உடைமைமேல் உடைமை போட்டு மினுக்கிக்கொண்டாள்.
column3: The husband that ran away has returned and is reconciled, therefore she has adorned herself with jewels to excess.
He who entertained the man that fled ftom his own village was a Koundan-a man of that tribe.
He who is running away, does so singing.
The hare that ran away was a large one, was it not?
Though it is obscene, hear it out.
Having set out to run will it do for him to be behind an old woman?
Old enough to seize a running snake.
Seeing him who retreats, makes the efforts of his pursuer easy.
Will a rolling flood stay at the anicut?

column2: ஓட்டைச் சட்டி ஆனாலும் கொழுக்கட்டை வெந்தாற் சரி.
column3: The chatty may be cracked, what matters that if it bakes the cakes.
Like adorning a cracked measure with a metal rim.
A cracked pot will hold sugar.
Will sugar, because put in a cracked pot, taste bitter?
Though the bell may be cracked will it be void of sound?
Like the story relating to the swallowing of a bloodsucker.
The unlettered have neither good sense nor virtue.
All poets may be found at the entrance of the ploughman’s house.
Has the poet no country, has the ploughman no land?
Assist instructors-the brahmans.

column2: ஓந்தி வேலிக்கு இழுக்கின்றது, தவளை தண்ணீருக்கு இழுக்கின்றது.
column3: The blood-sucker draws its prey to a hedge, the frog to water.
As if a dog longed for consecrated food.Spoken by Síta to Ràvanà.
Continual rain, and ceaseless wind.
Thou, the object my unceasing love, take me under thy protection.
Will any employ a person who is given to one-sided statements?
Like the family of him who makes partial statements.

column2: ஓர் ஆண்டி பசித்திருக்க உலகம் எல்லாம் கிறுகிறென்று சுற்றுகிறதா?
column3: Is the whole world so giddy through famine as to allow a religious mendicant to suffer from hunger?

column2: ஓர் ஆறு தாண்ட மாட்டாதவன் ஒன்பது ஆறு தாண்டுவானா?
column3: Will he who cannot cross one river, cross nine?

column2: ஓர் ஊருக்குப் பேச்சு மற்றோர் ஊருக்கு ஏச்சு.
column3: That which is polite in one country may be abusive in another.
May one assume a different name in every village?
No fear about Avvai's sayings.

column2: ஔவையார்மேலும் குற்றம் உண்டு அண்ணாவியார்மேலும் பழுது உண்டு.
column3: Even Avvai may be charged with blame, a religious teacher also may have his defects.
Will a wild gourd become a good gourd by growing on the Ganges?
Will a crow become a swan by bathing in the Ganges?

column2: கங்கையில் ஆடினாலும், கணமும் விடாமற் செய்த பாவம் தீராது.
column3: Though you wash in the Ganges, sin repeated again and again will not be expiated.
Will sin be expiated by bathing in the Ganges?

column2: கங்கையிலே படிந்தாலும் பேய்ச்சுரைக்காய் நல்ல சுரைக்காய் ஆகாது.
column3: Though a wild gourd be dipped in the Ganges, its inferiority will remain.
A snail of the Ganges is not a Shalgram.The Shalgram is a flinty stone, containing the impression of one or more ammonitæ, supposed by the Hindus to represent Vishnu.
Can the fragrance of a flower be appreciated after bruising it?
The base do not enjoy the friendship of the learned.
Persons of imperfect learning have no reputation.
Ignorance is better than imperfect knowledge.
Wipe off the tears of him who comes weeping.

column2: கச்சற் கருவாடு மோட்சத்திற்குப் போனாலும், பிச்சைக்காரர் மோட்சத்திற்குப் போகமாட்டார்கள்.
column3: Though dried fish may attain final emancipation-heaven-beggars cannot.

column2: கஞ்சி வரதப்பா என்றால், எங்கே வரதப்பா என்கிறான்.
column3: If I say, O Varathappa give me some kanji, he replies, O Varathappa where.
The story relating to a picotta on the sea-side.
Like assafœtida cast into the ocean.

column2: கடலிலே துரும்பு கிடந்தாலும் மனதிலே ஒரு சொல் கிடவாது.
column3: A rush may remain in the sea, but a secret will not remain in the mind.
Like tamarind acid dissolved in the sea.
You must accomplish your undertaking, though you may have to fill up the ocean.
Though the sea be filled up, the thing cannot be effected.
Should the sea boil, whence water to cool it?

column2: கடல்நீர் நிறைந்து ஆவது என்ன, காஞ்சிரை பழுத்து ஆவது என்ன?
column3: What benefit arises from the water of the ocean, what good comes from the ripening of the kànjirai, strychnos nux vomica, fruit?

column2: கடல் தாண்ட ஆசை உண்டு, கால்வாய் தாண்டக் கால் இல்லை.
column3: He wishes to cross the ocean, but has no feet to cross a small drain.
As regards the fish of the sea, whatever name the fisherman gives is final.

column2: கடல் வற்றிக் கருவாடு தின்னலாமென்று உடல் வற்றிச் செத்ததாம் கொக்கு.
column3: It is said that the stork died while waiting for the ocean to dry, in the hope of getting a supply of dried fish.
Will the shore extend when the sea flows?
What avails the shore when the sea flows?

column2: கடற் கரை தாழங்காய் கீழ் தொங்கி என்ன மேல் தொங்கி என்ன?
column3: What matters it whether the wild pine fruit on the sea-shore hangs high or low?
Though my debt be increased, let me have a quarter of a fanam worth more of fragrant powder.
One fourth supply of kanji for the stomach is better than debt.
One must pay one’s creditor and give to a relation the portion due to him.

column2: கடன் வாங்கிக் கடன் கொடுத்தவனும்கெட்டான்; மரம் ஏறிக் கை விட்டவனும் கெட்டான்.
column3: He who borrowed to lend was ruined; and he who let go his hold of the tree he had climbed also perished.

column2: கடன் வாங்கியும் பட்டினி கல்யாணம் பண்ணியும் சந்நியாசி.
column3: Famished though he has borrowed, an ascetic though he is wedded.
What to famish after having borrowed money!

column2: கடன்காரனுக்குக் கடனும் பழிகாரனுக்குப் பழியும் கொடுக்க வேண்டும்.
column3: Debt to the creditor and retaliation to the wrong-doer are due.
Is there a stake for impaling debtors?
He was as disquieted as the mind of a debtor.

column2: கடா கடா என்றாலும் மருந்துக்கு ஒரு பீர் என்கிறான்.
column3: Though informed that it is a he-goat, he persists in asking a drop of milk for a medicinal purpose.
The drawing back of the he-goat shows that he is about to butt.
Does the cowherd know where the ploughshare has passed?
Like eating grass screened behind 2 bull.

column2: கடாவும் கடாவும் சண்டை போடுகிறபோது உண்ணி நசுங்கினாற்போல.
column3: As the tick was crushed to death when the goats fought.
A dog tied to a stick.

column2: கடிக்க ஒரு எலும்பும் இல்லை, காதில் மினுக்க ஒரு ஓலையும் இல்லை.
column3: She has not even a single bone to pick, nor has she an ear-ornament to polish.
Will the cobra be affected by friendly intercourse?
A short rope round the neck of a biting dog.
Will an obdurate child prove helpful to its parents?
A harsh word is more painful than a blow.

column2: கடித்த பாக்கும் கொடாத சிற்றப்பன், கடைத்தெரு வரையில் வழி விட்டானாம்.
column3: It is said that his uncle who would not give even a bite of an areca-nut, bore him company to the bazaar.
Like wiping the mouth after biting.

column2: கடித்த பாம்புக்கு பால்வார்த்தால் விஷத்தைத் தரும் அதுபோல பொல்லாதவர்க்குச் செய்யும் உபகாரம் இருக்கும்.
column3: When milk is placed before a biting snake, in return it gives poison, and such is the return for the favours done to the wicked.

column2: கடித்த மூட்டையும் சரி, கடியாத மூட்டையும் சரி.
column3: Bugs are all the same whether they bite or not.
Give vinegar to the dog that bit you.
No matter if it bite you, do not kill it.

column2: கடித்த நாயைப் பயித்தியம் கொண்டது என்று சொல்வார்கள்.
column3: They will call that a mad dog which has bitten some one.
A kind word is better than a harsh one.
Will they let a bug escape because it did not bite?
Both the bite and the stroke were quickly effected.
The Vaduga woman's wedding is come nigh.

column2: கடுகு போகிற இடத்தில் தடி எடுத்துக்கொண்டு திரிவான், பூசனிக்காய் போகிற இடம் தெரியாது.
column3: He walks about with a staff in a place that will admit a mustard seed, and yet he is ignorant of the place where a pumpkin might easily pass.
Though small, the mustard seed is not without pungency.

column2: கடுகு போன இடம் ஆராய்வார் பூசனிக்காய் போன இடம் தெரியாது.
column3: They search for a grain of mustard seed, but not for a missing pumpkin.

column2: கடுகு போன இடம் ஆராய்வார் மிளகு போன இடம் ஆராயார்.
column3: They search for a grain of mustard seed but not for a grain of pepper.

column2: கடுகுக் களவும் களவுதான் கர்ப்பூரக் களவும் களவுதான்.
column3: Theft is theft whether as regards a mustard seed or camphor.

column2: கடுக்காய்க்கு அகணி நஞ்சு, சுக்கிற்குப் புறணி நஞ்சு.
column3: The kernel of the gall-nut and the skin of ginger are poisonous.

column2: கடுங்காற்று மழை காட்டும், கடுஞ்சினேகம் பகை காட்டும்.
column3: Strong wind foretells rain, excessive friendship foreshows hatred.
Excessive intimacy will prove unfriendly to one’s eyes.
It is said that hard dealing fails in its object.
Unfair dealing destroys the eyes.
It is shocking to the ears to hear harsh words.
Peon, my fellow, you are too strict, you have kamboo rice only as wages.

column2: கடும்பசி கல் மதில் உடைத்தும் களவுசெய்யச் சொல்லும்.
column3: Extreme hunger will induce a man to break through a stone wall and steal.

column2: கடை கெட்ட வாழ்வு தலை கட்ட நேரம் இல்லை.
column3: Labouring in menial offices she has not leisure to put up her tresses.
Each bazaar will have its attendant.

column2: கடைசிச் சோற்றுக்கு மோரும், கால் மாட்டிற்குப் பாயும் வேண்டும்.
column3: Buttermilk for the last distribution of rice, and a mat for the foot of the bed are indispensable.
Like taking up a cocoanut in the bazaar and breaking it to Ganésa.
He buys in the bazaar, and bestows at home.

column2: கடையில் அரிசி கஞ்சிக்கு உதவுமா, அபிசாரி புருஷன் ஆபத்துக்கு உதவுவானா?
column3: Will the rice in the bazaar serve for kanji, will the paramour of an adulteress be of service to her in distress?

column2: கடையில் வந்ததும் அரிசியோ நடையில் வெந்ததும் சாதமோ?
column3: Is that which comes to the bazaar rice? Is boiled rice cooked by the way desirable?

column2: கடையிலே கட்டித் தூக்கினாலும் அழுகற் பூசணிக்காய் அழுகலே.
column3: Although rotten pumpkins are tied up in the bazaar, they are still rotten.
Like drinking a decoction of dry ginger after swallowing a crowbar.
Like a wood-louse on dry ground.
When told to tie and bring, he cuts and brings.
When she embraces and weeps, her hand is groping to steal.

column2: கட்டில் உள்ள இடத்தில் பிள்ளை பெற்றுச் சுக்குக் கண்ட இடத்தில் காயம் தின்பாள்.
column3: She brings forth her child where she sees a bedstead, and takes the prescribed tonic where she sees dry ginger.

column2: கட்டி வழி விட்டால் வெட்டி அரசு ஆளலாம்.
column3: If he organize an army and lead it, he may cut down the enemy and reign as a king.

column2: கட்டி அழுகையிலே என் மகளே உனக்குப் பெட்டியிலே கை என்ன?
column3: My daughter, why is your hand in the basket while you embrace and weep?

column2: கட்டி அடித்தால் என்ன விட்டு அடித்தால் என்ன?
column3: It makes no difference whether you flog one bound or unbound.
Many will find fault with a house newly built.
Many will express an opinion respecting a house just built.

column2: கட்டினவனுக்கு ஒரு வீடானால், கட்டாதவனுக்குப் பல வீடு.
column3: He who has built a house has only one, whereas he who has not built makes use of many.

column2: கட்டிவைத்த பூனையை அவிழ்த்து விட்டு வாபூஸ் வாபூஸ் என்றால் வருமா?
column3: On untying a cat and calling puss, puss, will it come?
Will head-ache go by turning the bedstead?
As if one displaced and carried away money carefully tied up.
Provide seed-corn, and sow having tilled the ground.

column2: கட்டு அறிந்த நாயும் அல்ல, கனம் அறிந்த கப்பரையும் அல்ல.
column3: He is not a dog accustomed to restraint, nor a mendicant’s dish that knows what self-respect is.
Every time you have to tie, you must tie it in a different way.

column2: கட்டுப்பட்டாலும் கவரிமான் மயிரால் கட்டுப் படவேண்டும், குட்டுப்பட்டாலும் மோதிரக் கையால் குட்டுப்பட வேண்டும்.
column3: When bound, it should be by the hair of the roe, and if cuffed, it should be by a hand ornamented with jewels.

column2: கட்டுச்சோற்று மூட்டையும் இளம் பிள்ளையும் எடுப்பது வருத்தம்.
column3: It is difficult to carry, besides a child, boiled rice tied up for a journey.
Are toe-rings necessary when a woman goes out to g the firewood?
If one sets out auspiciously, he may return with honour.
He is an axe splitting the tree of arithmetic.

column2: கணக்கன் கணக்கு அறிவான், தன் கணக்கைத் தான் அறியான்.
column3: The accountant is clever at numbers, but he is ignorant of his own accounts.

column2: கணக்குக் குஞ்சையும் காக்கைக் குஞ்சையும் கண்ட இடத்திலே கண்ணைக் குத்து.
column3: Bore through the eyes of a young kurnum and a young crow wherever you find them.
When accounts are examined, difficulties arise.
To suffer hunger is natural to an accountant.

column2: கணக்கு அறிந்த பிள்ளை வீட்டில் இருந்தால் வழக்கு அறாது.
column3: There will be constant disputes in a house should one of its inmates be a skilful accountant.
A marriage ceremony in the house of an accountant is a waste of oil.
All accountants are not learned.
The pooja of Ganèsa has immediate effect.
What! in a neighbour's house, separated from your husband?
If her ankle grow big, she will be deprived of her husband.
What the eye has seen the hand may do.
I was waiting like an eye-snake.
Is blindness curable by an external application?
Though blind, does he sleep the less?

column2: கண் கெட்ட பின்பு சூரிய நமஸ்காரம் செய்து என்ன பலன்?
column3: What benefit can you expect from the worship of the sun if you have lost your eyesight?
Tying one’s lock of hair in imitation of others’.

column2: கண்ட கண்ட கோயில் எல்லாம் கை எடுத்துக் கும்பிட்டேன்.
column3: I worshipped with raised hands in every temple I visited.
That which was seen was a snake, that which bit was the stone of a mango fruit.
Whatever is seen is a sight, whatever is received is a gift.
He will neither eat nor sleep without asking whatever he sees.
If she does not attend to the affairs of her family, her husband will beat her.

column2: கண்டறியாதவன் பெண்டு படைத்தால் காடு மேடு எல்லாம் இழுத்துத் திரிவானாம்.
column3: It is said that if an inexperienced man marries, he will wander about dragging his wife, through jungles and over hills.
By learning what he sees, a person becomes a pundit.
Never utter what you have seen and heard, nor stand under a wild tree.
That which he saw was a snake, but he was bit by the rough points of the palmyra stem.
Acquire what you can, and go ashore.
Will he who finds, not take up?
May not sheep-hair be known as soon as seen?

column2: கண்டால் முறை சொல்லுகிறது காணாவிட்டால் பெயர் சொல்லுகிறதா?
column3: If present, do you call mentioning the relationship of the party, and when absent, his name only?
If seen, duty, if not seen, fraud.

column2: கண்டு செத்த பிணம் ஆனால் சுடு காட்டிற்கு வழி தெரியும்.
column3: If one die under human observation, his corpse will find its way to the place of cremation.

column2: கண்டு பேசக் காரியம் இருக்கிறது முகத்தில் விழக்க வெட்கமாய் இருக்கிறது.
column3: I have something to speak of in person, but I am ashamed to look at his face.

column2: கண்டும் காணவில்லை, கேட்டும் கேட்கவில்லையென்று இருக்க வேண்டும்.
column3: Behave as though seeing, you see not, and hearing, you hear not.
Will he who finds and takes a thing give it up?
Is a witness needed to prove that which one has seen with his own eye?
That which one has seen is false, the sign of agappai is true.
Why the sign of an agappai in proof of that which one has seen?
That one has not seen with his own eye amounts to one third.

column2: கண்ணாலே சீவன் கடகட என்று போனாலும் வண்ணான் கழுதை சுமந்தே தீரவேண்டும்.
column3: The ass of a washerman must carry the pack through, though its life may depart with a rattling noise through the eyes.
He treats as common earth those who are precious as the eyes.
He wounds the heart which is as tender as the eye.

column2: கண்ணாலே சீவன் கடகடவென்று போனாலும் வண்ணானுக்கு மழை நஞ்சு.
column3: Though the life of the washerman is forced through his eyes by reason of drought, rain to him would be poison.

column2: கண்ணாடி நிழலிற் கண்ட பணம் கடனுக்கு உதவுமா?
column3: Will the money reflected in a mirror avail for the payment of debt?
You have wounded your dearest friends.
The twinkling of the eye, or the snapping of the fingers, is the measure of a moment of time.
He is dismayed like a deer caught in a snare.
He is agitated as an ensnared blackbird.

column2: கண்ணிற் பட்டாற் கரிக்குமா, புருவத்திற் பட்டாற் கரிக்குமா ?
column3: When will it give pain, when it hits the eye or the eye-brow?
He can count with his eyes—at sight.

column2: கண்ணிற் புண் வந்தால் கண்ணாடி பார்க்கல் ஆகாது.
column3: When the eyes become sore, one cannot see them reflected in a mirror.

column2: கண்ணின்முன்னே ஒன்று சொல்லிக் காணாத இடத்திலே இரண்டு சொல்லுகிறது.
column3: To assert a thing when in sight and to contradict it when out of sight.

column2: கண்ணிலே எண்ணெய் பட்டாற் கரிக்குமோ பிடரியில் பட்டால் கரிக்குமோ ?
column3: Will oil give pain when it falls into the eye, or on the back of the neck?
No one cuts off the hand because it has struck the eye.
Should it strike the eye is the finger to be cut off.
The eye brow has received that which threatened the eye.

column2: கண்ணில் கண்டது கோடி காணாதது அனந்தம் கோடி.
column3: That which was seen was a crore, and that not seen many crores.
One has the pleasure of seeing as long as the eyes are unimpaired.
The blind will lose his wealth.
Is the brow ten miles in advance of the eye?

column2: கண்ணுக்குப் புண்ணும் அல்ல, காண்பார்க்கு நோயும் அல்ல.
column3: It is neither an eye-sore, nor painful to beholders.

column2: கண்ணும் கருத்தும் உள்ளபோதே காணோம் அதன் பின்பு என்ன கிடைக்கும் ?
column3: We do not realize it when the eye and mind are unimpaired; what may we expect to gain afterwards?
Is the eye-lid far apart from the eye?

column2: கண்ணுக்குட் சம்மணம் கொட்டுவாள் கம்பத்தில் ஐந்தானை கட்டுவாள்.
column3: She will sit in one's eye cross-legged, and tether five elephants to the pole of a dancer.
As the eye-lash preserved the eye.
The deity that deprived him of sight gave him superior mental endowments.

column2: கண்ணைக் கொண்டு நடந்ததுபோல உன்னைக் கொண்டு நடந்தேன்.
column3: I guarded you as I did my own eyes.
As one was left in the jungle blindfolded.

column2: கண்ணைக் காட்டி அழைத்தால் வராதவள் கையைப் பிடித்து இழுத்தால் வருவாளா ?
column3: Will she who would not come when called by the expression of your eyes, obey when taken by the hand?
The deity that destroyed the eyes gave a supporting staff.
Is it to strike the head after covering the eyes?
Is it an eye, or a sore?
They who walk with their eyes open will not fall into the pit.
Take care that you walk with your eyes open.
If the eye have seen it, the hand may perform it.
When the door is shut, the door-frame is outside.
Will the sun say, I will not burn certain persons?
Weed the corn even at the risk of destroying the ears.

column2: கதிர் நூல் குறைந்தாலும் கள்ளச்சி கழுத்து நூல் குறையாது.
column3: Though the thread on the spindle diminishes, that on the neck of a kalla woman does not.
Mere rumours have neither head nor foot.

column2: கதைக்குக் கால் இல்லை பேய்க்குப் பாதம் இல்லை.
column3: Rumours have no legs, nor have demons feet.

column2: கதையோ பிராமணா கந்தையோ பொத்துகிறாய், அல்லடீ பேய் முண்டாய் சீலைப்பேன் குத்துகிறேன்.
column3: O brahman, do you repeat a story or mend old clothes? No, you foolish widow, I crush the lice in my clothes.

column2: கத்தாிக்காய் சொத்தை என்றால் அாிவாள் மணைக் குற்றம் என்கிறாள்.
column3: When it is said that the brinjal is worm eaten, she imputes it to a defect in the knife.
As if feet and hands shot forth in the brinjal.
The seed of a brinjal will not produce gourds.
When you buy a brinjal, will the bazaar people give you a pumpkin in at the bargain?
Like a knife and a he-goat.

column2: கத்தியைப் பார்க்கிலும் கன கோபம் கொலை செய்யும்.
column3: Great anger is more destructive than the sword.
The wife of a swordsman is at any moment liable to becomes widow.
Is the elk to be carried to the knife?
Bawl out as long as you like, and shut the door when you go.

column2: கத்து கத்து என்றால் கழுதையும் கத்தாது சொல்லு சொல்லு என்றால் புலவனும் சொல்லான்
column3: When repeatedly urged to bray, even an ass will not do so, when asked to sing, even a poet will refuse.
Does the perfumer appreciate the distinction of scents?
The hole is in proportion to the rag.
The more it is stitched and the stronger does the rag become.

column2: கந்தை ஆனாலும் கசக்கி உடு, கூழ் ஆனாலும் குளித்துக் குடி.
column3: Though only a rag; wash it and put it on, and if but kanji, drink it after ablution.

column2: கபட இன் சொல்லினும் கடிய சொல்லே நலம்.
column3: Better is a harsh word than one smooth and feigned.
Severe head-ache caused the eyes to sink.
Like dropping a bit of areca-nut in a ship.

column2: கப்பல் ஏறிப் பட்ட கடன் கொட்டை நூற்றுக் தீர்க்கவேண்டும்.
column3: Debt incurred by the sea-faring merchant must be discharged by spinning cotton.

column2: கப்பல் ஓடிப் பட்ட கடன் கொட்டை நூற்றுத் தீருமோ?
column3: Can a debt incurred on account of a sea-faring life be discharged by spinning?

column2: கப்பற்காரன் பெண்டாட்டி தொப்பைக்காரி, கப்பல் உடைந்தால் பிச்சைக்காரி.
column3: The ship owner's wife is in good condition as long as the ship is safe, but if that be lost she is a beggar.
The prosperity of the ship-owner is at the mercy of the wind.

column2: கமரிற் கவிழ்ந்த பால் அமரர்க்கு இட்ட அன்னம்.
column3: Milk poured into a crevice, rice offered to enemies.
Are not alms obtained by pole-dancing the lowest species of alms?
Is it to say that the boiled rice received in a cumbly is full of hair?

column2: கம்பளி விற்ற பணத்துக்கு மயிர் முளைத்து இருக்கிறதா?
column3: Does hair grow in the money for which cumblies have been sold?
Like food sticking to a cumbly.
Gum on a cumbly.

column2: கம்பன் வீட்டுக் கட்டுத் தறியும் கவி பாடும்.
column3: Even the peg to which a cow is tied at Kamban’s house will sing.

column2: கம்புக்குக் களை வெட்டினாற்போலும் இருக்கவேண்டும், தம்பிக்குப் பெண்கொண்டாற்போலும் இருக்கவேண்டும்.
column3: Arrange it so that when he goes to weed the rye, he may also engage a wife for his younger brother.
One must buy a smith’s cow after cropping her ears.

column2: கம்மாளன் வீட்டில் பிள்ளை பிறந்தால் தேவடியாள் தெருவில் சர்க்கரை வழங்க வேண்டும்.
column3: When a child is born in a smith’s family, sugar must be dealt out in the street of the dancing girls.
Will the smith’s dog be alarmed at the sound of a hammer?
Like a top without a string.
Trembling at a bit of rope thinking it to be a snake.
Water in a hand-pot will avail for a katham.
As a bear saw the new moon.

column2: கரடி துரத்தினாலும் கைக்கோளத் தெருவில் போக வழி இராது.
column3: Though one be pursued by a bear, he may not escape through weavers’ street.
He who has been kicked by a bear, fears the sight of one who sells cumblies.

column2: கரடி புற்றிலே வாய் வைத்தாற் போல், கருடன் காலிற் சதங்கை கட்டியதுபோல்.
column3: As the bear puts its nose into the anthill, as small bells were tied to the legs of a kite.
To fail in a somersault may be fatal.
Excessive cording will not diminish the weight of a package.

column2: கரிப்புக்கு இருந்து பிழை, கலகத்துக்கு ஓடிப் பிழை.
column3: In famine, save your life where you are, in war, by flight.
Like selling a child in famine.

column2: கரு இல்லாத வித்தையும் குரு இல்லா சீஷனும்போல்.
column3: Like an art without ingenuity to comprehend it, and a disciple without a teacher.

column2: கருக்கி உருக்கி நெய்வார்த்தாலும் கண்ட நியாயந்தான் சொல்லுவான்.
column3: Though you give him fried meat and pour melted ghee on his rice, he will say nothing but what he saw.

column2: கருங்கண்ணி பட்டால் கரையான் கண்ணாலும் திரும்பிப் பாரான்.
column3: When the karunkanni fish is caught, the fisherman does not even turn to look at it.
He will strip fibre from a piece of granite.
As a silver ferrule fastened to an ebony pestle.

column2: கருங்காலிக் கட்டைக்கு வாய் நாணாக் கோடாலி இன வாழைத் தண்டுக்கு வாய் நாணுமா?
column3: Will an axe whose edge is not blunted by cutting a piece of ebony, be made dull by cutting a stem of a plantian tree?
As a string of small bells was tied to the leg of a kite.
Like a snake in sight of a kite.
Stones may be found even in sugar candy.
Consistency of action is the measure of greatness.
A resolute man cares nothing about difficulties.

column2: கருமான் இருந்த இடமும் கழுதை புரண்ட களமும் சரி.
column3: A blacksmith’s shop, and the place in which asses roll themselves are alike.

column2: கரும்பிலும் தேன் இருக்கும் கள்ளியிலும் பால் இருக்கும்.
column3: There is honey in sugarcane, there is sap in milk-hedge.
Ants will of course be attracted by a lump of sugar-candy.
The story of one striking an ass with a sugar-cane.

column2: கரும்பைக் கழுதைக்குமுன் போட்டால் கழுதைக்கு தெரியுமோ கரும்பு ருசி.
column3: Does the ass enjoy the flavor of the sugar-cane that is placed before it?
What, a reward for eating sugar-cane?
Even sugar cane has become a margosa tree.

column2: கரும்புக்கு உழுத புழுதி காய்ச்சிய பாலுக்குச் சருக்கரை ஆமா?
column3: Will earth pulverised by the plough for sugar-cane, answer for sugar to be mixed with boiled milk?
If sugar-cane prove bitter to the palate, it is the fault of the mouth.
May sugar-cane be plucked up by the root because it is of fine flavor?
If taken to excess sugar-cane becomes bitter as margosa.

column2: கரும்பு கட்டோடே இருக்க எறும்பு தானே வரும்.
column3: Where bundles of sugar-cane are, there ants will come of themselves.

column2: கருவு இல்லாத முட்டையும் குரு இல்லாத வித்தையும்.
column3: An egg without yolk, an art without a teacher.

column2: கருவேலமரத்திற்கு நிழல் இல்லை, கன்னானுக்கு முறை இல்லை.
column3: The karuvél Acasia casts no shade, smiths observe no relationship in marrying.
As a doney out of sight of land.The common name of a country vessel, some of which are of considerable size. They have no deck and are thatched with palm leaves.
Like a stranded vessel.

column2: கரைப்பக்கம் பாதை இருக்கக் கப்பல் ஏறினவனும், சொல்லாததை மனையாளுக்குச் சொன்னவனும் பட்ட பாடுபோல.
column3: Like the misery endured by him who embarked when there was a way by land, and by the husband who revealed to his wife that which he ought not.
Under the management of the skilful, even a stone may be dissolved.
It is said that he who does not exaggerate when a tumult occurs will go to hell.
In regard to a bed is the distinction of head and foot observed in a battle-field?
In a general insurrection universal consternation prevails.
Popular agitation leads to justice.
If an insurrection exists, it is wrong not to make it known.

column2: கலக்கந்தைக் கட்டிக் காணப் போனால் இருக்கலக் கந்தை கட்டி எதிரே வந்தாள்.
column3: When I went in rags to see her, she appeared enveloped in double the quantity.
Though it may consume a kalam of gtain, the quail lives in the jungle.
Though never so troubled, the sea does not become muddy.
Like holding the hand after putting rice on the plate.
Will a kalam of paddy yield a kalam of husks?

column2: கலப் பணத்தைப் பார்க்கிலும் ஒரு கிழப் பிணம் நல்லது.
column3: The corpse of an old person is better than immense wealth.

column2: கலப் பால் கறக்கலாம் துளிப் பால் முலைக்கு ஏற்றலாமா?
column3: A kalam of milk may be drawn; can a drop be infused into the teat?
A drop of butter-milk or curds to a kalam of milk.
Though not pure, repeated gilding will give it the colour of gold.

column2: கலப் பாலில் ஒரு துளி விஷம் கலந்தாற்போலே
column3: Like putting a drop of poison in a kalam of milk.

column2: கலப் பால் குடித்த பூனை ஆழாக்குப் பால் குடியாதா?
column3: Will not a cat that has drunk a kalam of milk drink an álak?

column2: கல மா இடித்தவள் பாவியும், கப்பி இடித்தவள் புண்ணியவதியுமா?
column3: Is she who pounded a kalam of flour vicious, and she who pounded rolong virtuous?
When plates are interchanged, tribal marriages interchange.
Though a kalam of chaff be pounded, it will not become rice.

column2: கல உமி தின்றால் ஒரு அரிசி தட்டாதா?
column3: If a kalam of chaff be eaten may hot a grain of rice turn up?
It is said that he forgot to tie the táli owing to the bustle at the wedding.

column2: கலியாணத்திலும் பஞ்சம் இல்லை, களத்திலும் பஞ்சம் இல்லை.
column3: No scarcity at a wedding, nor in the threshing floor.

column2: கலியாண வீட்டில் பந்தற்காலைக் கட்டி அழுகிறவள் செத்த வீட்டில் சும்மா இருப்பாளா?
column3: Will the woman who wept as she clung to the post of a marriage pandal at be silent when she attends a funeral?

column2: கலியாண வீட்டிற்குப் போய் அறியான், மேளச் சத்தமும் கேட்டும் அறியான்.
column3: He has never been at a wedding, nor has he heard the sound of a tom-tom.
What, though married yet a Sanyási-ascetic?

column2: கல் ஆகிலும் கணவன், புல் ஆகிலும் புருஷன்.
column3: Though in heart hard as a stone, and worthless as a blade of grass, he is your husband.

column2: கல் எறிக்குத் தப்பினாலும் கண் எறிக்குத் தப்பக்கூடாது.
column3: Though one may escape the cast of a stone, he cannot escape the glance of an evil eye.The evil eye கண்ணூறு and the evil tongue நாவூறு are feared very generally. To avert their influence as regards fruits &c, in a garden, it is common to put up some object of attraction as a water-pot whitewashed, inverted on a stump, and dotted with black spots &c. In some enclosures grotesque images are seen.

column2: கல்லடிச்சித்தன் போகிற வழி, காடு மேடு எல்லாம் தவிடு பொடி.
column3: All the rough and rugged ground before the stone-cutter will be reduced to powder.
Do not contend with him who has studied kalladam.Kalladam a work on the third part of grammar is said to be extremely dry.
The poverty of the learned is better than the wealth of the unlearned.

column2: கல்லாத ஒருவன் குலநலம் பேசுதல் நெல்லுடன் பிறந்த பதராகும்மே.
column3: The illiterate who boast of high rank may be compared to the dust mixed with paddy-unhusked rice.
The enmity of the learned is better than the friendship of the illiterate.
Keep at a distance from the unlettered; cross the sea of passion.
The heart of a great fool is harder than a stone.
Like stripping fibre from a stone.
He speaks as effectively as the hitting of a stone on a cocoanut.

May you live as long as the rocks and the Cauveri exist.The river Cauveri rises in the western ghauts and flows into the bay of Bengal. It is the source of the great fertility of Tanjore and the adjacent districts. According to the legend it originated in the following manner. In ancient times when the people of southern India suffered from drought, Ganésa became incarnate in the form of a crow. As such he visited the abode of an ascetic on one of the western hills. He there alighted on the waterpot of a hermit and upset it. The water thus spilt, by reason of the hermit’s merit, became a river: the current spelling would make the name the lake of the crow: the legend requires the word to be spelt காவேரி crow mounting, whereas காவேரி lake of the crow is common.

column2: கல்லு வருகிற விசையைக் கண்டால் பல்லைச் சிக்கென மூடவேண்டும்.
column3: On seeing the rapid approach of a stone press your teeth and shut your mouth.

column2: கல்லை ஆகிலும் கரைக்கலாம், மூர்க்கன் மனதைக் கரைக்கல் ஆகாது.
column3: Even stones may be dissolved, but the mind of a fool cannot.

column2: கல்லைக் கண்டால் நாயைக் காணோம் நாயைக் கண்டால் கல்லைக் காணோம் .
column3: On finding a stone we see no dog, on seeing a dog we find no stone.

column2: கல்லைக் குத்துவானேன் கை நோகிறது என்று அழுவானேன்?
column3: Why beat a stone and then weep, exclaiming it has pained my hand?
Why weep for kanji while you have a husband as hard as a stone?
Like losing the hand by pinching a stone.
Wealth without learning is like beauty without chastity.

column2: கல்வி என்ற பயிருக்குக் கண்ணீர் என்ற மழைவேண்டும்.
column3: Plants of learning flourish in the rain of tears.
When inquiring what will prevail, one’s learning and rank naturally occur.
A well instructed youth is an old man.
Learning cannot be allotted in portions-to persons:
Do not stand in the way of a stone from a sling, it may break the head of the beholder.

column2: கவி கொண்டார்க்குக் கீர்த்தி, அதைச் செவிகொள்ளார்க்கு அவகீர்த்தி.
column3: Praise to those who approve of a poem, and blame to those who will not listen to it.
Are those who accept a poem and those who reject it alike praiseworthy?
O king, manage a poet laureate and a superior elephant without fear.
To effect your object, if necessary, cling to the legs of an ass.
If an ass be wanted, it may be found feeding near a ruinous wall.
If an ass be out of condition, it will be as useless as a ruinous wall.
Why a bridle for an ass?
Dust is medicine for the sores of an ass.
Are an ass and a horse equal?
Like yoking an ass and a horse together.
Can an ass appreciate fragrant powder?
Does an ass become a horse by being saddled?
The ass by wearing away has become an ant.
Why a bit or a bridle for an ass?

Like tying an ass and feeding a sacrificial fire.This proverb relates to the following story. A certain Maharàja had a barber who had evinced great skill in shaving hie royal master when he was asleep. To reward him the King resolved to elevate him in the social scale. The brahmans were called and ordered to make a brahman of the barber. They set to work, kindled a sacred fire and the barber was led round it, whilst the priests uttered the required incantations for accomplishing their purpose.The prime minister being of a somewhat facetious temperament determined to metamorphose an ass. As the proverb intimates he kindled a fire and led the animal round it. When the King saw him thus engaged he asked him what he was doing. The prime minister replied by stating that if the brahmans could make a brahman of a barber he thought an ass might be made into a horse, and he was therefore engaged in the ceremony.

column2: கழுதைக்கு உபதேசம் காதிலே சொன்னாலும் அபயக் குரல் அன்றி அங்கு ஒன்றும் இல்லை .
column3: Though religious instruction be whispered in the ears of an ass nothing will come of it but the accustomed braying.
Having married an ass, do you fear his kicking?

column2: கழுதை மேல் ஏறி என்ன, இறங்கி என்ன?
column3: What matters it whether you mount an ass, or alight from one?}கழுதைமேல் ஏறியும் பெருமை இல்லை, இறங்கியும் சிறுமை இல்லை.Though you may ride on an ass, you gain no honour, nor are you disgraced by dismounting.
Did the washerman become a farmer by ploughing with an ass?
Has the forester become a farmer by ploughing with an ass?

column2: கழுதை மயிர் பிடுங்கினால் இலாபம் என்ன நஷ்டம் என்ன?
column3: What profit or loss will arise from plucking off the hair of an ass?

column2: கழுதை மயிர் பிடுங்கித் தேசம் கட்டி ஆள்வானா?
column3: Can one reign as a king by selling ass-hair?
Does an ass appreciate the odour of musk?
The foreign country of an ass is a ruinous wall.
If an ass goes astray, it may be found near a ruinous wall.
Are there five kalams of fraud in the pack on an ass?

column2: கழுதை விட்டை ஆனாலும் கை நிரம்ப வேண்டும் என்கிறாய்.
column3: You say get a handful, although it be but ass dung.
Her name is pearl necklace, yet on her neck she has not a black bead.
What! is it to tread in the mud every time you wash your feet?
A kómutti exactly fitted to the stake.
It is said that he who steals grain from the stack, will frighten away the women who may sweep the threshing floor.

column2: களையக் கூடாததைக் கண்டால் அடி பெயர்ந்து அப்புறம் போ.
column3: If you cannot pluck up, pass by on the other side.
Do not fret yourself about that which cannot be avoided.

column2: கல் விற்றுக் கலப்பணம் சம்பாதிப்பதை விட கற்பூரம் விற்றுக் காற்பணம் சம்பாதிப்பது மேல்.
column3: It is better to gain a quarter of a fanam by selling camphor than a kalam by selling toddy.
He plays the thief and also holds the lamp.
The neck of the hypocrite is covered with rosaries.
Secure a thief by tying, and a flood by damming it up.
Are there any indulged ones among the children of kallars?
No one will ever approve of a thief.
The whole country owes implacable hatred to a thief.
A vicious bullock will not be brought to market.
A rogue may be trusted, but not a dwarf.
One may follow a rogue without risk, but not a dwarf.
The jungle is not large enough to contain the fear of rubbers.
An unwilling bullock is restless.
A guilty mind is agitated.

column2: கள்ளன் உறவு உறவும் அல்ல, காசா விறகு விறகும் அல்ல.
column3: The friendship of a rogue is not friendship, nor is kásáwood fuel.
The mind of a rogue is set on thieving.
Even the child of a thief is characterized by thievish propensites.
The wife of a thief is at any moment liable to become a widow.

column2: கள்ளன் மனையாளைக் களவுப் பொருளைக் காணும் குறி கேட்கலாமா?
column3: Can the wife of a thief be consulted as an augur for the recovery of stolen property?
A thief and a flood are alike.
One half to the rogue, and one half to the honest.
A gardener is another term for a thief.

column2: கள்ளனும் தோட்டக்காரனும் ஒன்று கூடினால் விடியுமட்டும் திருடலாம்.
column3: If the thief and a gardener are united, one may steal till day-break.
What matters it to what ferry the bull may go that has been bought by a thief?
He who points out a thief is hated by him.

column2: கள்ளி பெருத்து என்ன? காய் உண்டா நிழல் உண்டா?
column3: What if the kalli grow large? Has it any fruit or any shade?
The whole country is a wild fit for the growth of the kalli.
Like attaching a silver ferrule to a kalli stick.

column2: கள்ளி என்னடி கல் இழைப்பது? காதில் இருப்பது பித்தளை.
column3: Why you hypocrite, inlay with gems? that which is in the ear is made of brass.
After cutting the kalli to cut the kattalai.

column2: கள்ளிக்குத் தண்ணீர் கண்ணிலே, நீலிக்குத் தண்ணீர் நிமையிலே.
column3: The tears of a thievish woman are in her eyes, those of an abandoned woman are in her eyebrows.
A thievish woman has a kalam of tears in her eyes.

column2: கள்ளிக்கு வேலி ஏன், சுள்ளிக்கு கோடாலி ஏன்?
column3: What avails a fence against a thievish woman, or an axe for cutting twigs?

column2: கள்ளும் சூதும் இருக்கிற இடத்தில் கூத்திக் கள்ளனும் இருப்பான்.
column3: Where there is theft and gambling, there will also be a whore-monger.
Should he drink toddy, he will tell the truth.

column2: கறக்கிறது உழக்குப் பால், உதைக்கிறது பல்லுப் பறக்க.
column3: Yielding an ulak of milk, and kicking out the teeth.

column2: கறந்த பாலும் எச்சில் பிறந்த பிள்ளையும் எச்சில்.
column3: First drawn milk is unclean, and so is a first-born child.
Will the milk that has been drawn, again enter the udder?

column2: கறந்த பாலைக் காய்ச்சாமற் குடித்தால் காச வியாதி தானே எடுபடும்.
column3: If fresh drawn milk unboiled be drunk, consumption may be cured.

column2: கறுத்தது எல்லாம் தண்ணீர் வெளுத்தது எல்லாம் பால் என்கிறான்.
column3: Whatever is black he calls water, and whatever is white he calls milk.

column2: கறுப்பு வெளுப்பு ஆகாது, கசப்பு இனிப்பு ஆகாது.
column3: Black will not become white, nor bitter sweet.
Will a black dog become white?
The poet is reading the section on grumbling.
Like a snake that occupied an ant-hill.
An ant-hill is useful to a snake.
Will etti fruit made of sugar-candy taste bitter?

column2: கற்கையில் கல்வி கசப்பு, கற்றபின்பு அதுவே இனிப்பு.
column3: Learning when being acquired is bitter, but when possessed it is sweet.
Even a crow that lives in the kalpaka tree feeds on ambrosia.
He who gave the eye is he who taught.
He who preordained will protect or guide.
Beauty without chastity, a flower without fragrance.
To practise a trick on him who taught it?
The anger of the learned passes away as water recovers its surface after being divided.
What he has learnt is a handful, and what he has still to acquire is wide as the world.
What other service will he do for you who would not tell you what he has learnt?
To the artist his art is but a quarter of a náli.
Like one who boils and drinks his learning.
Be not a learned fool.
A learned man and a fruitful woman will not suffer from want of food.
The ill odour of the karráli and the bad smell emitted from brass will not leave them.
Though the karràlai be small, the elephant will not tread upon it.

column2: கற்றுக் கொடுத்த பேச்சும் கட்டிக் கொடுத்த சோறும் எத்தனை நாள் நிற்கும்.
column3: How long will words put into one’s mouth, and rice prepared for a journey, last?
Do not speak in a studied way.
Though he has learnt, he continues a learned fool.
What, to be a learned fool?
The learned know the worth of the learned.
Excessive desire entails great loss.
When rats are numerous, they do not burrow.
As it rained gold.
Dignity gains approval, meanness entails loss.
Solid jewels are invaluable.
Dignity or worth increases relatives, wealth secures society.
Virtue is the support of dignity.

column2: கனத்தைக் கனம் அறியும் கருவாட்டுப் பானையை நாய் அறியும்.
column3: Dignity is appreciated by dignity, the pot of dried fish is known to the dog.
A great fool loses his substance.
Even in its dreams the crow’s thoughts turn on eating filth.
Will the money seen in a dream meet one’s expenses?

column2: கனவில் கண்ட பொருள் கானலில் கண்ட புனல்.
column3: The wealth seen in a dream, the water observed in a mirage.
Will the rice consumed in a dream appease hunger?
The story of a woman given in marriage to a man seen in a dream.
Ripe fruit falls of itself.

column2: கனிந்த பழம் நீர் தின்றீர் காயை உலுக்கிவிட்டீர்.
column3: You have eaten the ripe fruit, and you have shaken off the unripe.
What, to leave ripe fruit and to eat unripe?
Caste arises from action, it is not from birth.
Will granite be dissolved by any amount of hail?
That which has come of action-sin-must go by alms giving religious merit.

column2: கன்று இருக்கக் காசத்தனை பால் கறவாப் பசு, கன்று செத்த பிறகு கலப்பால் கறக்குமா?
column3: Will a cow that gave milk worth a small coin while its calf lived, give a kalam after her calf is dead?

column2: கன்றுக்குட்டியை அவிழ்க்கச் சொன்னார்களா கட்டுத்தறியைப் பிடுங்கச் சொன்னார்களா?
column3: Did they tell you to loose the calf or to pull up the peg?

column2: கன்றும் பசுவும் காடேறி மேய்ந்தால் கன்று கன்று கன்றுவழியே பசு பசு வழியே
column3: When the calf and cow go out to graze, each takes its own way.

column2: கன்றுகளாய்க் கூடிக் களை பறிக்கப் போனால் வைக்கோல் ஆகுமா செத்தை ஆகுமா?
column3: If a herd of calves weed the corn, will there be any straw, or even dry stubble?
Like a cow that comes running on seeing her calf.
When the brazier begins to move, the potter will abandon his dwelling-place.
As an ass went on a pilgrimage before a copper-smith.
When a virgin is yet unmarried, may the youth-her brother-marry?
Are there any white crows?
To the eye of a crow the flower of the gourd is tinged with gold.

column2: காகம் கர் என்றால் கணவனை அப்பா என்று கட்டிக்கொள்ளுவாளாம்.
column3: It is said that when a crow caws she will exclaim, alas, and cling to her husband.
Will palmyra fruit fall because a crow alights on the tree?

column2: காகம் இல்லாத ஊர் பாவி இல்லாத ஊர்.
column3: Vicious persons are not found in a village which has no crows.
Like the dropping of the fruit when a crow alighted on the tree.

column2: காகம் இல்லாத ஊர் சோனகன் இல்லாத ஊர்.
column3: The country in which crows do not exist is without mussalmans.

column2: காகம் காலமே கா கா என்று கத்தும்.
column3: Crows will caw at day-break.

column2: காக்கனுக்கும் பூக்கனுக்கும் பூத்தாயோ புன்னை கண்ணாளர் வருந்தனையும் பொறுக்கலையோ புன்னை?
column3: O punnai tree, Alexandrian laurel, hast thou blossomed for Kakan and Pookan, couldst not thou have waited till the arrival of my husband?

column2: காக்காய்க் குஞ்சு ஆனாலும் தன் குஞ்சு பொன் குஞ்சு.
column3: Though but a young crow, it is a golden one to its mother.
Kalà fruit, carissa, is blacker than a crow.

column2: காக்கையும் குயிற் குஞ்சைத் தன் குஞ்சுபோல் வளர்க்கும்.
column3: Even a crow will bring up a young kuyil-Indian cuckoo-as her own.
The crow has five characteristics.
The alighting of the crow, and the falling of the palmyra fruit.

column2: காக்கையும் கத்திக் கத்திப் போகிறது கருவாடும் உலர்ந்து போகிறது.
column3: The crow continues to caw whilst the karuvadu, (salted fish) dries.
Crows and winds go but to return.

column2: காக்கையைக் கண்டு அஞ்சுவாள் கரடியைப் பிடித்துக் கட்டுவாள்.
column3: She is afraid on seeing a crow, and yet she will capture a bear.
Will not the branch shake on which a crow alights?

column2: காசி இரண்டு எழுத்துத்தான் காண எத்தனை நாட்செல்லும்?
column3: Kàsi is formed of two letters only, how many days will it take to reach it?
Though one has gone to Benares his sins are not removed.
Though taken to Benares, a wooden ladle will fetch but half a cash.

column2: காசிக்குப் போனாலும் கதி பெற வழி இல்லை.
column3: Although he may go to Kàsi, he will not find the way to heaven.
What! having gone to Kàsi is it to fall at the feet of a lame ascetic?

column2: காசியில் இருக்கிறவன் கண்ணைக் குத்தக் காஞ்சிபுரத்திலிருந்து கையை நீட்டிக் கொண்டு போகிறதா?
column3: If you wish to strike the eyes of one at Kási, do you go, for that purpose, with outstretched hand from Conjevaram?
Why should a penniless man talk about pagodas?

column2: காசு கொடுத்தால் வேசி வருவாள், கல நெல்லைக் கொடுத்தால் அவள் அக்காளும் ஆத்தாளும் கூட வருவார்கள்.
column3: If you are free with your money a harlot will come, if you give also a kalam of rice, her sister and her mother will come.

column2: காசுக்கு ஒரு குஞ்சு விற்றாலும் கணக்கக் குஞ்சு ஆகாது.
column3: Though young birds are sold a cash each, an accountant's young bird is not good.

column2: காசுக்கு ஒரு குஞ்சு விற்றாலும் கம்மாளக் குஞ்சு ஆகாது.
column3: Though sold for a single cash, never buy a smith's bird.

column2: காசுக்கு ஒரு புடைவை விற்றாலும் நாயின் சூத்து அம்மணம்.
column3: Though cloth may be cheap, dogs go naked.
The money basket has become a charcoal basket.

column2: காசுக்குப் போன மானம் கோடி கொடுத்தாலும் வராது.
column3: Chastity lost for a cash will not be recovered by a crore.
Will you buy a vicious cow at a high price?
If you go to Conjeveram, you may eat without labour.
Like going for vinegar and bringing curds.

column2: காடு காத்தவனும் கற்சேரி காத்தவனும் பலன் அடைவான்.
column3: Both will be benefited, he who watches the jungle and he who waits at the cutchery.

column2: காடு வா என்கிறது வீடு போ என்கிறது.
column3: Home bids me go, and the place of incremation says come.

column2: காடு விளைந்தாலும் ஒரு மேடு விளைந்தாலும் கடன் கழிந்து போம்.
column3: The debt will be discharged in either case, whether the jungle or highland yield a good crop.
Though the waste land has yielded nothing, will the tax be remitted?
Should the jungle be consumed, would the sandal wood tree escape?
If a quail crosses one's path to the left, he will govern a province.
Is it proper to betray one, and then stand aloof?

column2: காட்டில் ஆனையைக் காட்டி வீட்டில் பெண்ணைக் கொடுக்கிறதா?
column3: Is it to give a girl in marriage when an elephant in the jungle is shown as dower?
Though a man dies in the jungle, ceremonial uncleanness will attach to his house.
Does the wild cat observe the fast of Sivaratri?

column2: காட்டுக்கு எறித்த நிலாவும் கானலுக்குப் பெய்த மழையும்.
column3: Moonlight in the jungle and rain in the plains.

column2: காட்டு வாழை வந்தால் வீட்டு வாழ்வு போகும்.
column3: When wild plantains come, domestic prosperity will vanish.
Is a wild gourd fit for curry?
The hole in a mortar is paradise to a jungle fowl.
It has become the story of wild insects offering resistance.
That which was seen was like the new moon of November.
All that is seen is temporary.

column2: காணாமல் கண்டேனோ கம்பங்கதிரே சிந்தாமல் குத்தடி சில்லி மூக்கே?
column3: Have I seen kambu-millet for the first time, pound it without scattering thou nose bleeding woman?

column2: காணி ஆளன் வீடு வேகச்சே காலைப் பிடித்து இழுத்த கதை.
column3: The story of one pulling by the feet the landlord whose house was on fire.
While ⁠1/80

column2: ⁠ is added a crore is destroyed.
column3: காணி கவிழ்ந்து போகிறதா?Does land turn upside down?

column2: காணி காணியாய்ச் சம்பாதித்துக் கோடி கோடியாய்ச் செலவழிக்க வேண்டும்.
column3: We must acquire by eightieths and spend by crores.
The profit or loss on an eightieth part of a unit, will determine that on a crore.
A little indolence creates great trouble.
A little indolence will bring great sorrow.
Like ranging over the distance of two kathams instead of one.
Like giving a katham and getting two.

column2: காதவழி போய் அறியாதான் மாதம் எல்லாம் நடந்தானாம்.
column3: He who never walked a katham is said to have walked a whole month.
He who never walked a katham is an ass.
He whose reputation does not reach a kathan is like an ass.
Like an iron wire passed into the ear.
O Avanti, I have a boil in my ear, utter an incantation.
The hire for tearing the ear is readily paid.

column2: காது அறுத்தாலும் அறுக்கும் பேனைப் பார்த்தாலும் பார்க்கும் குரங்கு.
column3: A monkey may tear the ears or hunt lice if he choose.

column2: காது காது என்றால் நாதி நாதி என்கிறான்.
column3: If one says, my ear, my ear, he replies it is mine, it is mine.

column2: காது காது என்றால் செவிடு செவிடு என்கிறான்.
column3: If one cry an ear, an ear, he cries deafness deafness.
Can you not bear the boring of your ears?
If worn in the ears, they will add beauty to the face.
Having put on ear-rings he walks about shaking them.
It should be as if an ear, and an ear, were brought in contact.
Will he who cut off the ear strike the eye also?
A stranger took possession of the wife of him who was watching her.
Like a magnet and needle.
He who takes care of his property will not be robbed.
Like a wounded monkey.
Like a falling shadow on a sun-striken worm.

column2: காய்த்த மரத்தில் கல் எறிபடும் காயாத மரத்தில் கல் எறிபடுமா?
column3: Stones are thrown at a fruit bearing tree; are they thrown at that which does not bear?

column2: காய்த்த மரத்திலே கல் எறியும் சில் எறியும்.
column3: Stones and other missiles are thrown at a fruit-bearing tree.

column2: காய்த்த மரம் வளைந்து நிற்கும் நற்குணம் உடையவர் தணிந்து நிற்பார்.
column3: A fruit-bearing tree bends; the virtuous are lowly.
Will a dry tree bud?
Like a starving cow falling in the kambu-millet.
Like a hungry tiger falling on a flock of sheep.
A lean tiger falls on a cow.
Is water useless because it is boiled?
Rain after drought will not soon cease?
It was destroyed by the sun and by the rain.
Grass serves as food for kine, and as an ornament to a flower garden.

column2: காரிகை கற்றுக் கவி சொல்லுவதிற் பேரிகை கொட்டிப் பிழைப்பது நன்று.
column3: It is better to live by beating a drum than by composing verses according to the rules of prosody.
The ass grazes in the grounds of the industrious.
His eye is on the object, not on the attendant applause.
If necessary, secure your purpose by clinging to the feet of an ass.
Which is greater, success or boasting?
Wait till the kár paddy is reaped, and the brinjal blossoms.

column2: கார்த்திகைக்கு மேல் மழையும் இல்லை, கர்ணனுக்கு மேல் கொடையும் இல்லை.
column3: No rain like that of November, no liberality superior to that of Karnan.Karnan, one of the seven princes distinguished for great generosity.
I saw it like the third-day moon of November.
Time will effect that which mere parade cannot.
The world cannot accomplish that which time effects.
A benefit conferred opportunely.
Like seasonable rain.
Equipage suited to the occasion.

The sight of a crow on rising of a morning is ominous of evil.The crow being regarded as the vehicle of Saturn in the sight of a single crow is inauspicious; two are not so regarded. The cawing of a crow may excite apprehension. When this is the case the following formula may be used.Measure the length of your shadow by the foot and add 12 to the number of feet ascertained. Then divide by 7 and the remainder will determine the import of the bird's note (1) Good luck, (2) Gain, (3) Rain, (4) Conflict, (5) Feasting, (6) Victory. If on dividing by 7 there is no remainder the cawing of the crow is ominous of death.
Regulate your affairs with due reference to the times in which you live.
Like receiving alms when in distress.

column2: காலம் அறிந்து பிழையாதவன் வால் அறுந்த குரங்கு ஆவான்.
column3: He who does not live according to the times will become an ape.

column2: காலம் போம் வார்த்தை நிற்கும், கப்பல் போம் துறை நிற்கும்.
column3: Time goes, words remain, a ship goes, the shore remains.

column2: காலம் போன காலத்தில் மூலம் வந்து குறுக்கிட்டதுபோல.
column3: As one is subject to piles in his riper years.
A mariner in unfavourable weather.

column2: காலாலே நடந்தால் காதவழி, தலையாலே நடந்தால் எவ்வளவு தூரம்?
column3: If on foot it is a katham; how much more distant if one walk on the head?
What he tied with his feet others cannot untie with the hands.
After having hurt the foot is it to be attributed to planetary influence?

column2: காலில் விழுகிறது நல்லது மேலில் விழுகிறது கெட்டது.
column3: To fall at the feet is good, to fall on another is bad.
That which struck the feet was felt as if it had struck the eye.
If he puts ornaments on his legs, he will put ornaments on his head.

column2: காலுக்குக் கடுப்பே தவிர, கண்ட பலன் ஒன்றும் இல்லை.
column3: No benefit accrued but the trouble of walking.

column2: காலுக்குக் கை உதவி கைக்குக் கால் உதவி.
column3: The hand helps the legs and the legs help the hands.

column2: காலுக்குத் தக்க செருப்பும் கூலிக்குத் தக்க உழைப்பும்.
column3: A slipper suited to the foot, and labour suited to the hire.
Will a slipper that fits the foot do for the head?

column2: காலைச் சுற்றின பாம்பு கடித்தால் ஒழிய விடாது.
column3: The snake that has coiled round the leg will not leave without biting.

column2: காலைச் செல் பூத்தால் அடுத்த மழை அடங்கும்.
column3: Should winged white-ants come out in the morning the heavy rain will cease.
He who sleeps after day-break, and he who keeps awake in the fore part of the night are worthless.

column2: காலைப் பிடித்த சனியன் ஊரைச் சுற்றி அடிக்கும்.
column3: If Saturn seize the feet, he will drive one round the village.
Wealth is comparable to the morning dew, and the twinkling of an eye.
Dreams of bliss and premature wisdom are not lasting.

column2: கால் அளவே ஆகுமாம் கப்பலின் ஓட்டம் நூல் அளவே ஆகுமாம் நுண்சீலை.
column3: The speed of a vessel is proportioned to the force of the wind, the quality of cloth will be as the yarn used in its manufacture.

column2: கால் ஆடக் கோல் ஆடும் கோல் ஆடக் குரங்கு ஆடும்.
column3: The foot puts the stick in motion, as that moves, the monkey dances.

column2: கால் சிறிதாகில் கனல் ஊரும் கன்னியர் மேல் மால் சிறிதாகில் மனம் ஊரும்.
column3: Where there is a little wind fire will spread, where there is lust the affections will be fastened on women.

column2: கால் நடைக்கு இரண்டு காசு கை வீச்சுக்கு ஐந்து காசு.
column3: Two cash for walking on foot, five cash for the swing of the arms.
If one be tied cross-legged, will his weight diminish?
Can he who cannot leap over a channel leap over the sea?
The weight of a burden is felt by the bearer.
Chastity is a feminine grace.

column2: காவேரி ஆற்றை மறிப்பாய் கார்த்திகை மாதத்துக் கற்கடக சந்திரனையும் மறிப்பாயா?
column3: You may stay the Cauvery, but can you hinder the full moon of November if he be in Cancer?

column2: காவேரி கஞ்சியாய்ப் போனாலும் நாய் நக்கித்தான் குடிக்க வேண்டும்.
column3: Should the Cauvery become kanji, the dog would partake thereof by lapping.
The green leaves of the palm laughed because the dry ones fell off.
Even the katpaka tree in the garden of Durga is of no use to mankind.The katpaka tree is said to yield whatever a suppliant may require.
The name Kali may become obsolete, but not the (nicknane) Mooli, the earless.

column2: காற் காசுப் பூனை முக்காற் காசுத் தயிரைக் குடித்தது.
column3: A cat worth a quarter of a cash consumed curds worth three quarters of a cash.
The mind is agitated like a ship in a storm.
Is it as a security against the wind and rain that you are thus clad?
If one spit against the wind-the spittle-will strike his own face:
A dhony can not sail against the wind.
Will the wind blow so as to avoid certain persons?

column2: காற்று உள்ளபோது தூற்றிக்கொள் கரும்பு உள்ளபோது ஆட்டிக் கொள்.
column3: Winnow when the wind blows, work the sugar-mill when the cane is ripe.
Can one seize the wind and confine it in a small vessel?
Anchor a ship with reference to the wind.

column2: கானலை நீரென்று எண்ணி மான் ஓடி இளைத்ததுபோல.
column3: Like the deer that wearied itself in pursuit of a mirage imagining it to be water.

column2: கிடக்கிறது கிடக்கட்டும் கிழவனை எடுத்து மணையிலே வை.
column3: Leave the things as they are, and take the old man and put him on the stool to bathe.The first thing done before a corpse is removed from the homestead is to bathe it.
Dwelling in a ruinous hut and dreaming of a palace.

column2: கிட்ட வா நாயே என்றால் மூஞ்சியை நக்குகிறது.
column3: When called, the dog licks the face.
Forget at once what cannot be obtained.
It has happened as if a demon had sprung out of a well just dug.
What! is it to dig a well and supply it with frogs?
Whilst there is a well do not excavate a mountain-for water.
Must one dig a well to quench his thirst?
If there be water the undermost ring of the well will be wet.
The more a well is drawn, the better the spring.
Having escaped falling into the well, shall we fall into a tank?
If the well is to be filled up, the belly must be filled up.

column2: கிணற்றுத் தவளை தண்ணீர் குடித்ததைக் கண்டது ஆர், குடியாததைக் கண்டது ஆர்?
column3: Who knows whether the frog in the well did or did not drink water?
What has the frog in the well to do with the news of the country?
Having escaped falling into the well he jumped into the fire.
Will the sea shrink at the sight of a well?
Will the flood carry away the water of the well?
Water sprang up in the well.
The depth of the well and the length of the cord must be ascertained.
Will he who has fallen into a well once fall in again?
Like dropping a stone into a well.

column2: கிண்டக் கிண்ட அம்பட்டன் குப்பை மயிரே புறப்படும்.
column3: The more you dig in the refuse heap of the barber, the more will hair turn up.
The more you dig, the more will greens and hair appear.
Will shaving counteract the evil influence of a planet?
What! is it for an evil-doer to teach religious precepts?
The stream of grace is the source of all treasures.

column2: கிழக் குடலுக்குச் சோறும் இடிச் சுவருக்கு மண்ணும் இடு.
column3: Give rice to the aged and add mud to a ruinous wall.
Will the words of an old man go up to the assembly?

column2: கிழவன் ஆனாலும் கெட்டை ஆனாலும் கட்டிக் கொண்டவள் பிழைப்பாள்.
column3: She who marries will do well whether her husband be old or poor.
It is better to fall into a well than to marry an old man.
Will a musician listen to the speech of an old woman?
The old woman is a katham from the horse, and the horse a katham from the old woman.

column2: கிழவி இருந்த வீடும் கிளி இருந்த காடும் ஈடேற மாட்டாது.
column3: Neither the house of an old woman, nor the grove in which there are parrots, will be saved from ruin.
Two rags for a cash.
Two split tops for a cash.
Is it proper to train a parrot and give it into the paw of a cat?
Speech like that of a parrot, gait like that of a peacock.
It is said that it ripens by being pinched, and that parrots will nibble it leisurely.
One may endure those who rob, but not those who pilfer.
Like a snake bit by a mungoose.
Inveterate enmity exists between the mungoose and snakes.
Like a mungoose and a snake.
An opposition shop is good even among green grocers.
As if a parasite should spring from the lower part of a vegetable.

column2: கீரைக் கட்டை வெட்டச் சொன்னால் தோரணம் கட்டுகிறதா?
column3: When I order the greens to be pruned, is a garland put up?
Why sing eléla when plucking up greens?
Do not make two curries of a vegetable.
Will hunger be appeased by fame?
Whence sorrow to him who hag obtained praise?
If it is said that a snake is below, he looks up.
Though of low origin, the learned ranks with the highest class.
Her ears are rent at both ends; in an affray she is equal to Durga.
If lanced, the sore will heal.
கீTamil Proverbstranslated by Peter Percival
கூ

column2: குங்கிலியத் தூபம் காட்டிச் சன்னதம் குலைந்தால் கும்பிடு எங்கே?
column3: If indeed the incense has expelled the demon, where is the sign?-uplifted hands.
Can the ass which carried the kungumam-a fragrant resin-appreciate scents?

column2: குங்குமக் கோதைக்கு அஞ்சு பணம், குருட்டுக் கண்ணிக்கும் அஞ்சு பணமா?
column3: The donation to the perfumed dame being five fanams, is five fanams to be assigned to the blind woman also?

column2: குஞ்சிரிப்புக்கு மருந்து சாப்பிட உள்ள சிரிப்பும் போனாற்போல.
column3: As if one lost his natural smile by taking medicine to induee a simpering expression.
A lamp lit in an earthen pot.
Will gold in a pot dance?
Though the cow gives a large pot of milk, it is not equal to the horse in speed.

column2: குடப்பால் கறந்தாலும் கூரை பிடுங்குகிற மாடு ஆகாது.
column3: Though it may yield a large pot of milk, a cow that destroys the roof is not desirable.
As if a firebrand were put into a pot of water.
Habit is the rule of the stomach and the hair.

column2: குடல் கூழுக்கு அழுகிறதாம் கொண்டை பூவுக்கு அழுகிறதாம்.
column3: It is said that the stomach cries for gruel, and the tresses for flowers.
How far can a jackal run after its bowels are dislodged?
Whither will a fowl go after its entrails have been exposed?

column2: குடி இருந்து அறி வழி நடந்து அறி.
column3: Learn the character of a person by dwelling with him, and the condition of a road by walking in it.

column2: குடி இருந்து பார் கூட்டுப்பயிர் இட்டுப் பார்.
column3: Test his social qualities by living with him, and his honesty by joint tillage.

column2: குடி இருப்பது குச்சுவீடு, கனாக் கண்டது மச்சுமாளிகை.
column3: He who dwells in a humble cottage dreams of ceiled apartments.
Bandycootes will run about in an uninhabited house.

column2: குடி இல்லா ஊரிலே அடி இட ஆகாது.
column3: It is a risk to set a foot in an uninhabited village.
A jackal is king in a deserted village.
He is fit for kingship who is approved by the people.
He has gruel to drink, and washes his mouth with rose water.
What, is it to pour down the drinkable milk into a crevice?
What you drink is sour gruel, do you require a cup of fine gold for it?
Living on gruel and sitting on a throne.
Will the family of a drunkard prosper?
Whether is greater, the government of a family or of a state?

column2: குடித்தனம் மேலிட வேண்டி பிடாரியைப் பெண்டுவைத்துக் கொண்டான்.
column3: Wishing to elevate his family he married a Pidari-village goddess.

column2: குடித்தனமென்று பண்ணினால் நன்மையும் வரும் தீமையும் வரும்.
column3: If one becomes a householder, he must expect both good and evil.
As a lean dog watched a deserted house.
The madness of a drunkard may be cured by beating.
A drunkard and a mad man are alike.
The family of a drunkard is always benighted.

column2: குடியிற் பெண் வயிறு எரிந்தால் கொடியிற் சேலை நின்று எரியும்.
column3: If the bowels of the house-wife burn, her cloth hanging on the lino will burn also.
He is the only wealthy man in a deserted village.
Born in a good family, and playing the monkey.
Born in a good family and falling into a bush.

column2: குடி வைத்துக் கொண்டாயோ கொள்ளி வைத்துக் கொண்டாயோ?
column3: Have you taken a family to dwell with you? Or have you taken firebrands?
Is it proper to burn down a house after admitting a family?
As a wolf entered a fold of lambs.
He who breeds divisions in a family shall be cuffed to death.
The miser will give nothing though you tumble heels overhead.

column2: குட்டிச் சுவரிலே தேள் கொட்ட, கட்டுத் தறியிலே நெறி இடுமா?
column3: If a scorpion sting in a ruinous dwarf wall, will the glandular swelling appear in the stall?

column2: குட்டிச் சுவரிலே தேள் கொட்டத் தண்ணீர் மிடாவிலே நெறி கட்டுமா?
column3: Does the sting of a scorpion in a ruinous wall, produce a glandular swelling in a water-pot?
Is he so dim-sighted as to stumble against a ruinous wall?
He wanders about like a cat that has kittened.

column2: குட்டின குட்டும் குண்டிற் பாய்ந்த தண்ணீரும் வருமா?
column3: Can a cuff already inflicted and water that has run into a pit be recalled?
Though it become fat, the flesh of a kid is always clammy.
Like pups barking and creating a quarrel among dogs.
Like hunting with pups.

column2: குட்டு மானம் தப்பிக் குசவனோடே பேசினால், சட்டையும் பண்ணான், சட்டியும் கொடான்.
column3: If yon speak familiarly with a potter, he will not respect you, nor will he supply you with chatties.

No guroo-a religious teacher-can change a man's temperament.A Guru among the Hindus is regarded as a spiritual parent from whom the youth receives the initiatory mantra or prayer, and who conducts the ceremonies necessary at various seasons of infancy and youth, up to the period of investiture with the sacred thread or string: this person may be the natural parent or the religious preceptor. The word Guru is also used for a teacher or preceptor generally.
Is the weeping on account of the virtue of the deceased or does it relate to his corpse?

column2: குணம் உற்றவன் மணம் உற்றவன், குணம் அற்றவன் மணம் அற்றவன்.
column3: A good-natured man has an engaging exterior, an ill-natured man has a repulsive aspect.
His suffering is due partly to his faint-heartedness, and partly to a disease.

column2: குண்டாக் கரணம் போட்டாலும் பிண்டாச் சோற்றுக்கு வழி இல்லை.
column3: Though he makes somersaults to all he sees, he has no food to eat.
If starved even a horse will eat straw.

column2: குண்டி எத்தனை கோணற் கோணினாலும் சுமை வீட்டிற் போய்ச் சேர்ந்தாலே சரி.
column3: No matter how the body may be bent if the burden be carried home.
Like a kite with a broken tail.
He displays his horsemanship in an earthen pot.

column2: குண்டு பட்டுச் சாகாதவன் வண்டு கடித்துச் செத்தானாம்.
column3: It is said that he who survived a gunshot wound, died of the bite of an insect.
Can one turn a somersault in an earthen pot?
The bandycoote is also like a beetle.
A slanderer and a snake of deadly poison have each two tongues.
As kine increase, a household increases.

column2: குதி குதி என்பார்கள் எல்லாரும், கூடக் குதிப்பார் இல்லை.
column3: They will all say leap, leap into the pyre, but there is none willing to leap with me.

column2: குதித்துக் குதித்து மா இடித்தாலும் புழுக்கைக்கு ஒரு கொழுக்கட்டையே.
column3: Though she may leap joyously and pound the grain, the slave woman gets but one cake.

column2: குதிரை குருடானாலும் கொள்ளுத் தின்கிறதிற் குறைச்சல் இல்லை.
column3: Though blind, the horse does not eat the less grain.
When you have bought the horse, is there any occasion for disputing about its bit?

column2: குதிரையின் குணம் அறிந்தல்லவோ தம்பிரான் கொம்பு கொடுக்கவில்லை?
column3: Was it not because the Creator knew the nature of the horse that he did not provide him with horns?

column2: குதிரை செத்ததல்லாமற் சேணம் சுமக்க வேலை ஆயிற்று.
column3: Besides the death of the horse there is the saddle also to be carried.
The horse and the dog like a change of place.

column2: குதிரை ஏற அதிஷ்டம் இருந்தாற் குண்டியின் கீழ் வந்து நுழையாதா?
column3: If it be your good fortune to ride, will not your horse come and place itself under your seat?
It is certainly a good horse, but its circular marks are bad.The circlets of hair on a horse are generally observed by dealers and owners very carefully.

column2: குதிரை ஏறி என்ன, கோணக் கொம்பு ஊதி என்ன, வீணர்க்கும் கீர்த்திக்கும் வெகு தூரம்.
column3: What if you do ride a horse, and have a horn blown before you? There is a great distance between the worthless and reputation.

column2: குதிரை பாகனைத் தள்ளுவதும் அல்லாமல் குழியும் தோண்டுகிறதாம்.
column3: It is said that the horse has not only thrown its rider, but is digging his grave.

column2: குதிரை செத்தது அல்லாமல் குழி தோண்ட மூன்று பணமா?
column3: Besides losing the horse, have I to pay three fanams to bury him?
The horse is ten miles before you, and the old woman is ten miles behind you.

column2: குதிரை பிடிக்கச் சம்மட்டி அடிக்க கூப்பிட்டக் குரலுக்கு ஏனென்ன.
column3: You will attend to my horse, ply the sledge hammer and answer my calls.

column2: குதிரை இருப்பு அறியும் கொண்ட பெண்டாட்டி குணம் அறிவாள்.
column3: The horse knows his rider, and a wife her husband.

column2: குத்தி வடித்தாலும் சம்பா குப்பையிலே போட்டாலும் தங்கம்.
column3: Though pounded and boiled it is samba-superior rice-though cast on the rubbish heap it is gold.
A cuff may be borne, can hunger?
If you eat properly seated you may consume a heap of rice.
Even in the city of Kubèran there were firewood carriers.

column2: குபேரன் பட்டணம் கொள்ளைபோனாலும் அதிஷ்டவீனனுக்கு அகப்பைக் காம்பும் அகப்படுமா?
column3: Though the city of Kubèran be despoiled, will the unfortunate obtain even the handle of a wooden spoon?

column2: குப்புற விழுந்து தவம் செய்தாலும் குருக்களுக்கு மோட்சம் இல்லை.
column3: Though they may prostrate themselves and perform penance, religious teachers do not attain heaven.

column2: குப்புற விழுந்தும் மீசையிலே மண் படவில்லை என்கிறான்.
column3: Though he fell flat on his face, he says that his mustache did not touch the ground.
Will a plant grown on a dunghill answer for the mast of a ship?
As a creeper springing on a dunghill climbed on a roof.
Without manure the growing grain will not yield a good crop.
Though it lies on the dunghill the kunrimani-abrus precatorius-remains unchanged.
Though the kunrimani be buried in the dunghill its colour will not change.
The rubbish heap has risen, the tower has sunk.
How is it that the rubbish heap has risen, and the tower sunk?
The teacher and his disciple are like the dunghill and the fowl.

column2: குமரி ஒரு பிள்ளை கோடி ஒரு வெள்ளை.
column3: A woman is young till she bears a child, and cloth is new till it is washed.

column2: குமரி தனிவழியே போனாலும் கொட்டாவி தனிவழியே போகாது.
column3: Though a young woman may go on her way alone, yawning is never alone.

column2: குமரியாய் இருக்கையிற் கொண்டாட்டம் கிழவியாய் இருக்கிற போது திண்டாட்டம்.
column3: When young, a woman is full of hilarity, when old her portion is vexation.

column2: கும்பிட்டுக் கடன் கொடாதே கும்பிட்டுக் கடன் வாங்காதே.
column3: Neither lend nor borrow beseechingly.

column2: கும்பிடப் போன இடத்தில் கோயில் இடிந்து விழுந்தது.
column3: When I went to worship the temple fell.
As the deity one went to worship came to meet one cross wise.
The deity I worship is my hereditary deity.
Obsequious rogues, deceitful friends.

column2: குயவனுக்குப் பல நாளை வேலை, தடி அடிகாரனுக்கு ஒரு நிமிஷ வேலை.
column3: That which cost the potter many days labour, is but the labour of a moment to the breaker.

column2: குயவா கலசம் கொண்டுவா, இடையா பால் கொடு என்றாற் போல.
column3: Like saying to the potter bring a vessel, and to the shepherd fill it with milk.
Like the warbling of the Indian cuckoo.
Like the voice of the Indian cuckoo, and the beauty of the peacock.
A garland of flowers in the hand of a monkey.
The advice which the pendulous bird Loxia, gave to the monkey ended in the destruction of its own nest.
Even to the monkey its own young is precious.
A sore on a monkey never heals.
A sore of the monkey is enormously large.

column2: குரங்குப் பிணமும் குறப் பிணமும் கண்டவர் இல்லை.
column3: No one ever saw the dead body of a monkey, or the corpse of a kuravan-a mountaineer.

column2: குரங்கு தன் குட்டியின் கையைக்கொண்டு பதம் பார்க்கிறது போலப் பார்க்கிறான்.
column3: He tests it by another as a monkey tests an object by the hand of its young.
Lay hold of a thing as firmly as a monkey.

column2: குரங்கின் பீயை மருந்துக்குக் கேட்டால் கொப்பு கிளை எல்லாம் தத்திப் பாயும்.
column3: If a monkey be asked for his droppings for medicinal purposes, he will leap from branch to branch.
All monkeys have faces alike.
Though she be a mere monkey, one should take a wife in one's own tribe.
Is there a branch the monkey has not climbed?
All monkeys go about in troops.
The story of a firebrand in the hand of a monkey.
Is there a disciple without a Guru-teacher?

column2: குரு இல்லார்க்கு வித்தையும் இல்லை முதல் இல்லார்க்கு லாபமும் இல்லை.
column3: There is no art without a teacher, and no profit without capital.

column2: குரு என வந்தான் திரு உரை தந்தான்.
column3: He came as a Guru; he gave sacred instruction.
The escape of the religious teacher was as it were a second birth.
As an eel was caught in the hand of a blind man.
The need of a blind man is a staff.
What the blind want is eye-sight.
Can the blind lead the blind with a staff?
Is it a work for eight men to help a blind man to feed sheep?

column2: குருடன் கூத்துப் பார்க்கப் போனால் பயன் என்ன?
column3: What advantage will the blind man gain by attending a comedy?
To the blind milk was like a heron.
