The wife of a blind man became intimate with a hunch-backed man.
As if a blind man beat his wife.

column2: குருடனைப் பார்த்து இராச விழி விழிக்கச் சொன்னால் விழிப்பானா?
column3: If requested to assume the looks of a king, can a blind man do so?
As if a blind man was asked to assay metals.
Like snatching away the staff of the blind.

column2: குருடனுக்கு குருடன் வழி காட்டினால் இருவரும் குழியிலே விழுவார்.
column3: if the blind lead the blind, both will fall into the pit.
As the work of eight persons was stopped by reason of a blind woman going to fetch water.
As the blind and the deaf attended a comedy.
The excuse of a blind horse was his stumbling.
As a blind cat leaped on the cross beam.
The village tank is the witness of the blind crane.
As a blind fowl was over anxious to feed on bran.

column2: குருட்டுக் கண்ணுக்குக் குறுணி மை இட்டு ஆவது என்ன?
column3: Why adorn blind eyes with collyrium?

column2: குரு மொழி கேளாதவனும் தாய் வார்த்தைக்கு அடங்காதவனும் சண்டி.
column3: He who will not bear the words of this Guru-religious teacher-and he who disobeys his mother, are worthless.
He who forgets the words of his Guru will forfeit the divine favor and perish.
Can a palmyra fruit be suspended from the neck of a small bird?
Is not the weapon of Rama suited to the bird?
Raméswaram suited to the bird.This is I think a perversion of the foregoing proverb. An aged or feeble person may say, my Raméswaram, pilgrimage, efforts, must be moderate.
A burden suited to the bird.
Never disturb the nest of a bird with a stick.
Is there a disciple who is above his Guru?
He will deceive his own Guru.

column2: குருவுக்குத் துரோகம் செய்தாலும் குடலுக்குத் துரோகம் செய்யக்கூடாது.
column3: Though a Guru may be deceived, the stomach cannot.

column2: குரு வேஷம் கொண்டவன் எல்லாம் குரு ஆவானா?
column3: Are all religious teachers who have assumed the garb?
The handle of an axe that destroyed its own species.The tree from which its handle was taken, was felled by the axe.
It is said that a monkey will destroy its own species.
It becomes a lawful wife to honour her husband.

column2: குலமகன் குலத்துக்கு அழுவான், மூக்கறையன் மூக்குக்கு அழுவான்.
column3: The high-born mourns the loss of caste, and the man who has lost his nose, his nose.
Birth and external sign are at variance.
Though he has entered another tribe he is still in want.
Tribe goes with tribe, the flood with the river.
A tribe associates with its own tribe, and goats follow their own kind.
High birth lies on the dunghill, while wealth is at the festive board.
Arts appropriate to a family are partly intuitive and partly acquired.
A barking dog does not bite.
Like throwing a bone to a barking dog.
Will a barking dog avail in hunting?

column2: குலைக்கிற நாயின் வாயிலே கோலைக் கொடுத்தால் ஊர் எங்கும் கொண்டோடிக் குலைக்கும்.
column3: If a staff be put into the mouth of a noisy dog, it will carry it through the village barking.
A silent dog will bite the heels.
Infants and temple servants do not suffer from hunger.
Children and dogs are pleased with a change of place.
Children and gods best agree with their admirers.
The fever of children and the spite of a slanderer are bad.
The sleep of a child is advantageous to the mother.
Tender as the body of an infant.
My child, do not sink into the pit.
Has the stormy weather set the whole sea in motion?
O my youthful son, is your throat pained by drinking thick milk?
Is it right to train a parasite to the roof?
Like a jackal going round the grave of a child.
Is it proper to agitate the tank to supply prey for kites?
Are rain drops the weight of a mortar to a tank?
Having put it into the tank, do you seek it in the well?
Like the man who would not wash his feet in the tank because he was angry with it.

column2: குளப் படி நீர் இறைத்தாலும் கடற் பள்ளம் நிரம்புமா?
column3: Will the hollow of the sea be filled by draining a tank down to the lowest step?
When a tank bursts, do they dispute about turns?
Will not he who watches the tank drink?
Why dispute about your turn for drawing water seeing that the tank is dried up?
Before the tank is dug will the alligator go to dwell therein?
Like poking a wasp's nest with a stick.
What! throw a stone at a wasp's nest?
A green caterpillar is the offspring of a wasp.
Like a wasp changing worms to its own colour.
As one smeared himself with mud After bathing.
Pretending to be an extinguished firebrand, is it proper to destroy the house?

column2: குள்ளப் பார்ப்பான் பள்ளத்தில் விழுந்தான் தண்டு எடு தடி எடு.
column3: The dwarf Brahman has fallen into a pit, take a staff, take a stick.
He sounds the depth by means of a dwarf.

column2: குள்ளன் குடி கெடுப்பான் குள்ளன் பெண்சாதி ஊரைக் கெடுப்பாள்.
column3: A dwarf will destroy a family; his wife will destroy the whole village.

column2: குறத்தி பிள்ளை பெற்றால் குறவன் காயம் தின்பான்.
column3: If the wife of a mountaineer is brought to bed, her husband take the prescribed stimulant.

column2: குற வழக்கும் இடை வழக்கும் கொஞ்சத்தில் தீராது.
column3: Disputes among mountaineers and shepherds are not easily settled.
Disputes among chakliyars are more easily settled than those among mountaineers.

column2: குறுணிப் பால் கறந்தபோதிலும் கூரை பிடுங்கப் பார்த்திருக்கலாமா?
column3: May one suffer his cow to destroy his roof although she yields a kuruni of milk at a meal?
Though a kuruni of collyrium be applied to the eyes of the blind, the blindness remains.
A ceruminous ear will itch.
The supporter who puts an end to inhuman acts.
A thoroughly learned man is one of a lac.

column2: குறை குடம் தளம்பும் நிறை குடம் தளம்பாது.
column3: A water pot not full is agitated, a full pot is not.
Give more than you profess to sell.

column2: குறையுள்ளோர்க்கு உண்டு குறாவுதல் கறையுள்ளோர்க்கு உண்டு கரவு.
column3: The guilty are dejected, those that have a tarnished reputation elude observation.
An unfinished work ought not to be shown even to a Guru-master.
Is it proper to expose an unfinished work for sale or otherwise?

column2: குற்றமுள்ள நெஞ்சு குறுகுறு என்னும் குறும்பியுள்ள காது தினவு தின்னும்.
column3: A guilty conscience is agitated, a foul ear itches.
By concealing one fault another may arise.
A guilty conscience is an enemy that lives with its possessor.
Distinction attained by wrong makes reformation difficult.
Undeserved punishment is better than deserved punishment.
If every fault be noticed, all intercourse must cease.
Even a kunrimani seed-abrus precatorius-has a black spot on it.
An owl cannot see in the day-time.
He stares like an owl.
Whilst residing with one shall we thrust a firebrand into his house?
Although you may give an outer room, it is not proper to give an inner one.

column2: கூடம் ஒன்று போடுமுன்னே, சுத்தி இரண்டு போடும்.
column3: Before the big hammer strikes one, the little hammer strikes two.
If the party-wall be broken down, one room only will remain.

column2: கூடி இருந்து குலாவுவார் வீட்டில் ஓடி உண்ணும் கூழும் இனிதே.
column3: Gruel served in the house of a united family is enjoyable.
As food prepared for a journey was untied in a crowd.
Shivering and fever left with the body.

column2: கூண்டிலே குறுணி நெல் இருந்தால் மூலையிலே முக்குறுணித் தெய்வம் கூத்தாடும்.
column3: When there is a kuruni of paddy in the bin, three kurunies of gods will be dancing in a corner.

column2: கூத்தரிசி குத்துகிற வீட்டில் வாய்க்கு அரிசிக்கு வழி இராது.
column3: In the house where rice is pounded for sale, there is not enough to put into the mouth of a corpse.Before a corpse is removed the females of the family place a little raw rice near the mouth, the males do the same in the cemetry before the body is laid on the pyre.
Will the art of fencing avail in a battle-field?

column2: கூத்தாடிக்குக் கீழே கண் கூலிக்காரனுக்கு மேலே கண்.
column3: A rope-dancer directs his eye downwards, one who bears a burden upwards.

column2: கூத்திக்கு இட்டுக் குரங்கு ஆனான் வேசிக்கு இட்டு விறகு ஆனான்.
column3: By wasting his substance on concubines he has become spare as a monkey, and by giving to common women he has become as dry as a stick.
Who is the legal father of the child of a concubine?

column2: கூத்தியார் ஆத்தாள் செத்தால் கொட்டும் முழக்கும் கூத்தியார் செத்தால் ஒன்றும் இல்லை.
column3: When the mother of one's concubine dies, there will be beating of drums, but when the concubine dies, there will be no such display.

column2: கூத்தியார் செத்தால் பிணம், அவள் தாய் செத்தால் மணம்.
column3: When a concubine dies she is a mere corpse, whereas if her mother dies, her remains are honoured.By honouring the remains of her mother it is supposed that a man secures greater respect from his concubine.
He frequents his concubine's house as a dog wandering about.

column2: கூத்துப் பார்க்கப் போன இடத்தில் பேய் பிடித்ததுபோல.
column3: As one was seized by a demon when he went to see a comedy.
He whose words are keen, is of all the most powerful.
Kicking against thorns will cause pain.

column2: கூரை ஏறிக் கோழி பிடிக்கமாட்டாத குருக்கள் வானம் கீறி வைகுண்டம் காட்டுவாரா?
column3: If a guru is not able to go to the house-top to catch a fowl, how can he rend the heavens and show vikundam to his disciple?

column2: கூரைமேலே சோறு போட்டால் ஆயிரம் காகம் வரும்.
column3: If rice is thrown on the roof, a thousand crows will come.
The acute man is the upright man.
If the hire be diminished, the work will be spoiled.
Thou hast reduced my wages, and used false measures when paying me.

column2: கூலிக்காரன் பெண்டாட்டி பிள்ளை பெறப்போகிறாளாம் குப்பையிலே ஆமணக்கு முளைக்கப் போகிறதாம்.
column3: It is said that the wife of a labourer is about to be confined, and that a castor plant will spring upon the midden.

column2: கூலிக்கு நாற்று நட வந்தவனுக்கு எல்லைக்கு வழக்கோ?
column3: Does he who came on hire to transplant grain institute a boundary suit?
A vicious fellow prevented the payment of my wages.
Will they hire themselves to be impaled?
Will mere hirelings conquer?

column2: கூழ் ஆனாலும் குப்பை ஆனாலும் குடித்துக்கொண்டவன் பிழைப்பான்.
column3: Whether gruel or refuse, he who has drunk it will live.
Will an unripe mango fail before gruel?

column2: கூழுக்கு மாங்காய் கொண்டாட்டம் குரங்குக்குக் தேங்காய் கொண்டாட்டம்.
column3: An unripe mango improves the gruel, the kernel of a coconut is a feast to a monkey.
He wishes to drink the gruel, and he also wants a nice mustache.Spoken of things incompatible.
A chilly serves as a whip to gruel.It makes it pungent.

column2: கூழ் என்றாலும் வாழ்வுக்குத் தக்கதாக குடிக்கத்தான் வேண்டும்.
column3: Though only gruel, it must be drunk agreeably to one's circumstances.
If only gruel, he who drinks it will live.
Fellowship is undesirable even in drinking gruel.

column2: கூழ் புளித்ததென்றும் மாங்காய் புளித்ததென்றும் உணராமற் சொல்லலாமா?
column3: Is it proper to say thoughtlessly that the gruel was sour, and then again that it was the mango that was sour?

column2: கூனனைக் கொண்டு குழப்படி மாமி காணிக்குப் பிள்ளை பெற.
column3: O, mother-in-law, imcite a quarrel through the hunchbacked that an heir may be born.

column2: கூனன் குறை தீர்ந்தான் கொடியோன் வினை பெயர்ந்தான்.
column3: The hunchbacked is relieved, the cruel man has escaped.

column2: கூனி ஆனாலும் கூடை சுமந்துதான் கூலி பெற வேண்டும்.
column3: Although her back is crooked, she will have to carry the basket before she gets her hire.
As she (Kaikesi) was demoralized by listening to the words of Kuni.
They will listen to the advice of the wicked when their star is not favourable.
A decaying family will not listen to advic.e.
When you mediate another's ruin, your own will soon follow.
Learning can suffer no damage.
The deity will destroy those that injure others.
It is with the mouth we injure others; it is with the mouth we read.
One is blind to his own imprudence.
Make a pitfall for imprudence.
Thou wilt be ruined, think not of destroying another.
He who is on the way to ruin, will mediate another's ruin.

column2: கெட்ட காலத்துக்கு நாரை கெளிற்றை எடுத்து விழுங்கினது போல.
column3: As a crane in an evil time seized and swallowed a keliru fish.
The wretched dog feels satisfied with the beating he received.
A village doomed to ruin profits not by repeated precaution.
Is a premium of half a fanam demanded of one who is reduced in circumstances?
Will curdled milk become sweet again?
I have had enough of loss, sufferings and toil in watering the vegetable garden.
A vicious child in a poor family.
A child is born under the star kèttai in a poor family.
A vicious donkey has mischievous propensities.

column2: கெட்டவன் குடி கெட்டது வட்டி நஷ்டம் இல்லாமல் வாங்கவே வாங்கு.
column3: The family is ruined, do not fail to get the interest without reduction.

column2: கெட்டவன் குடி கெடட்டும் நீ குடி மிளகுசாற்றை.
column3: No matter whose family is ruined, you drink the pepper water.

column2: கெட்ட குடியே கெடும் பட்ட காலிலே படும்.
column3: The decaying family will be ruined. It will hit the leg which is already struck.

column2: கெட்ட மாடு தேடுமுன்னம் எட்டு மாடு தேடலாம்.
column3: You may procure eight cows before you can recover one that has strayed.

column2: கெட்ட மார்க்கத்திலே இருக்கிற ஒருவன் மற்றவர்களையும் அதில் இழுக்கப் பிரயாசப்படுவான்.
column3: He who pursues a vicious course will try to lead others into the same.
The impoverished have no friends even among their own kindred.

column2: கெட்டார் வாழ்ந்தால் கிளைகிளையாய்த் தளிர்ப்பார் வாழ்ந்தார் கெட்டால் வறையோட்டுக்கும் ஆகார்.
column3: When the ruined in circumstances flourish they cast out innumerable branches; when the prosperous are reduced to poverty they are not worth a potsherd.

column2: கெட்டான் பயல் பொட்டலிலே, விழுந்தான் பயல் சறுக்கலிலே.
column3: The boy suffered by walking in the arid tract, he fell in the slippery ground.

column2: கெட்டாலும் செட்டி செட்டியே கிழிந்தாலும் பட்டு பட்டே.
column3: Though reduced in circumstances a chetty is a chetty; silk is silk though never so torn.
The deception of the clever will be manifest in eight days.

column2: கெட்டிக்காரனுக்குப் பயம் இல்லை மட்டித்தனனுக்கு நயம் இல்லை.
column3: The clever fear nothing; the fool gains nothing.
The reputation of the adroit lasts only eight days.
Though you are ruined, cling to the city.

column2: கெட்டுப்போகிற காலம் வந்தால் துஷ்ட புத்தி தோன்றாதா?
column3: When the time of destruction comes, will not evil devices operate?

column2: கெட்டுப்போன பார்ப்பானுக்குச் செத்துபோகிற பசுவைத் தானம் பண்ணினதுபோல.
column3: Like giving a dying cow to a poor Brahman.
Can a family survive repeated misfortunes?
Baiting with a small fish and catching a large one.

column2: கெரடி கற்றவன் இடறி விழுந்தால் அதுவும் ஒரு வரிசை என்பான்.
column3: If the fencer fall, he will pretend he did so according to the rules of his art.
As the greedy saw milk and rice.
One free from loss, free from suffering.
A bad time is followed by an alms-dish.

column2: கேடு கெட்ட நாயே வீடு விட்டுப் போவேன்.
column3: You worthless dog quit my house.

column2: கேடு வரும் பின்னே மதி கெட்டு வரும் முன்னே, ஆனைவரும் பின்னே மணி ஓசை வரும் முன்னே.
column3: Indigence follows indiscretion, the elephent makes its appearance after the sound of its bells reaches the ear.

column2: கேட்டது எல்லாம் நம்பாதே நம்பினது தெல்லாம் சொல்லாதே.
column3: Believe not all you hear, tell not all you believe.
Fortitude under privations will tend to restore lost fortune.
Be not deceived by hearsay reports.
Thou the self-existent, thou art the powerful.
Friends and relations are denied to the ruined.
He who acted on hearsay destroyed the village.
Instruction is wealth, and learning is fame.
But half of what one hears is true.
Endeavour to obtain instruction.
The miser will not prosper.Literally will not “get ashore.” The marriage of a girl is phrased in the same way.

column2: கை இல்லாதவன் கரணம் போடலாமா, கால் இல்லாதவன் ஓடலாமா?
column3: Can one who has no hands turn a somersault, can one who has no feet run about?
It is the learned that have hands.
One has to fix his hands on the ground before he makes a somersault.
If the palm of the hand becomes callous by drawing water, the areca will bear.
That which reaches the hand does not always reach the mouth.
Like a sore on the leg of a weaver, and one on the head of a dog.

column2: கை தப்பிக் கண்ணிலே பட்டாற் கையைத் தறித்துப் போடுவார்களா?
column3: If the hand by accident strike the eye, will they cut it off?
If the hand missess its hold, a somersault.

column2: கை நிறைந்த பொன் இல்லாவிட்டாலும் கண் நிறைந்த கணவன் இருக்க வேண்டும்.
column3: Though not possessed of a handfull of gold, one should have a husband that fills the eye.
Handling makes a thing shine as glass.

column2: கைப்பழத்தைக் கொடுத்துத் துறட்டிப் பழத்துக்கு அண்ணாந்து நிற்பானேன்.
column3: Why give the fruit in your hand and stand gazing at that which can only be reached by a hook?
Learning is better than riches.

column2: கைப்பொருள் இல்லா வழிப்போக்கன் கள்வர் முன் படலாம்.
column3: The wayfaring man who has no money may encounter thieves with impunity.
If destitute of wealth, even one’s own married wife will not regard him.
The refuse that the widow has brought forth.
He came to the buffalo of the widow to learn to milk.

column2: கையால் கிள்ளி எறியும் வேலைக்குக் கனத்த கோடாலி வேண்டுமோ?
column3: Is a heavy axe required for a work that may be easily effected by the hand?

column2: கையால் கிழிக்கும் பனங்கிழங்கிற்கு ஆப்பும் வல்லீட்டுக்குற்றியும் ஏன்?
column3: Why a wedge and a mallet to split the edible root of the palmyra?

column2: கையிலும் இல்லை செட்டியார் பையிலும் இல்லை காசு.
column3: There is no money either in the hands or purse of the chetty.

column2: கையிலும் மடியிலும் இல்லாதவனைக் கள்வன் என்ன செய்யலாம்?
column3: What harm can a thief do to him who has nothing in hand or in his belt?
If the hands are dipped in turmeric water, the undertaking will prove successful.
When it (the stolen thing) is in the hand, why put the hand in (hot) ghee?
He who has nothing in hand is a thief.
Rice in the hand and water in the pot.
The fowl is not aware of its danger before it is caught.
Is a mirror required for a sore in the hand?

column2: கையிற் பறவையை விட்டுக் காட்டுப் பறவைக்குக் கண்ணி வைக்கலாமா?
column3: Why let a bird in the hand go and snare one in the jungle?
I have fallen into the sea with the child in my arms.
One must have an umbrella in the hand, and shoes on the feet.
May one pour into the cracked ground the nectar he has in his hand?
Will the money in the hand become a cake in the mouth?
The money in hand is just sufficient for current expenses.

column2: கையைப் பார் முகத்தைப் பார் என்று இருந்தாற் காரியம் ஆகுமா?
column3: If you say look at the hands and look at the face, will the work be accomplished?
Neglect not a work in which you are skilful.
Which attains swerga the mere boaster, or the self-immolated?

column2: கொக்கு இளங்குஞ்சும் கோணாத தெங்கும் கண்டது இல்லை.
column3: No one has seen a young crane, not a straight cocoanut tree.
Does the stork observe the rules of Linga-worship?The strict Saivite is a rigid vegetarian.
As if a falcon fell among herons.
As if a bandycoote rushed on to prey on a stork.
Does the crane know how to carry away a chicken?
O devotee, did you mistake me for a stork?
Why remain here when grain sells cheap in the Kongu country.
As a carbuncle appeared on the back of a mosquito.
Can you draw from a mosquito a kuruni of milk?

column2: கொசுகிலே பிளவை அதிலே நீரழிவு, அறுக்கிறது எங்கே அட்டை விடுகிறது எங்கே?
column3: A mosquito suffering from a carbuncle, has also diabetes: where shall we put in the lancet, and how apply leeches?

column2: கொசுகே கொசுகே தலை முழுகு நான் மாட்டேன் சனிக்கிழமை.
column3: O mosquito, mosquito, bathe your head; I will not, it is Saturday.
As if a hawk assailed a mosquito imagining it to be a rival.
What! do you strain out a gnat and swallow a camel?
Those who strain out gnats are naturally suspected.

column2: கொச்சிக்குப் போனவன் செய்தியைப் பார், தன் குருவை விற்றவன் செய்தியைப் பார்.
column3: See the result of his having gone to Cochin, and of one who betrayed his Guru.
What, is it a small thing for a monkey to drink pepper water?

column2: கொஞ்சத்தில் உண்மை இல்லாதவன் கோடியிலும் இருக்க மாட்டான்.
column3: Not one even of ten millions is unfaithful in a little thing.
Creepers spread over the trees that grow near them.
Is its fruit burdensome to the creeper?

column2: கொடி சுற்றிப் பெண் பிறந்தால் கோத்திரத்திற்கு ஆகாது.
column3: It is an evil omen to a tribe for a girl to be born with her navel-string round the body.
Palaniándi gives and Subbanándi eats.
He who was not disposed to contribute to the drama spoke disparagingly of it.
Whilst bestowing gifts why fall prostrate at the feet of a leper?
As a niggardly shepherd pointed to a sheep that was with young.

column2: கொடுங்கோல் மன்னன் ஆளும் நாட்டிற் கடும்புலி வாழும் காடு நன்று.
column3: A jungle inhabited by fierce tigers is better than a country ruled by a cruel tyrant.
It is not good to live under a tyrant king.
If that which has been given be demanded hatred ensues.
They praise the liberal, but reproach the niggard.
The liberal are fathers, niggards are useless.

The dwarf demon returns only that which had been given to it.Jugglery is generally attributed by the common people to the power of a dwarf demon which, retaining the materials entrusted to it, gives them one by one to the juggler as he may want them.

column2: கொடுத்தவருக்கு எல்லாம் உண்டு கொடாதவருக்கு ஒன்றும் இல்லை.
column3: The liberal have all things, the niggardly nothing.
Be liberal and friendly, avoid being called a talebearer.
Do not check the liberal.
The kind-hearted is yielding.

column2: கொடும் பாவி ஆனாலும் கொண்ட மாமியார் வேண்டும்.
column3: However cruel a mother-in-law may be, she is nevertheless desirable.
Can the woman who digs up roots dance before a temple idol?

column2: கொட்டிக் கொட்டி அளந்தாலும் குறுணி பதக்கு ஆகாது.
column3: Though measured again and again, a kuruni will not become a pathakku.
If it stings it is scorpion, if it does not sting is it only a gryllus?
If despised by her husband, all will slight her.

column2: கொண்டவனுடைய பலத்தைக் கண்டால் குப்பைமேடு ஏறிச்சண்டை செய்வாள்.
column3: If she finds out the strength of her husband, she will get on the rubbish heap and fight.
If husbands treat their wives angrily, others will do so too.
Do you speak lightly of a family into which you have chosen to marry?
To stand up to quarrel with a chaplet of flowers on the tresses.

column2: கொண்டைக்குப் பூ சூடுகிறதா தாடிக்குப் பூ சூடுகிறதா?
column3: Are flowers for the tresses, or for the beard?
All that are betrothed are not real wives.
Like keeping a firebrand in the border of her own cloth.
Can needles be sold in a street of smiths?

column2: கொல்லன் எளிமை கண்டு குரங்கு காலுக்குப் பூண் கட்டச் சொன்னதாம்.
column3: It is said that a monkey seeing the weakness of a blacksmith urged him to adorn his legs with rings.
As the smith's corpse became stiff.
Rice kills and it makes alive.
Like a wild jackal showing his teeth.
Like encouraging jackals in a field.
He is a palli plant,-Bachnera Asiatica-to a garden, and a Saguni to a family.Both are injurious.

column2: கொல்லையில் குற்றியை அடைந்த புல்லு உழவர் உழுபடைக்குக் கெடுமோ?
column3: Will the ploughshare destroy the grass at the foot of scarecrows in the field?
Are small birds famished because the fields lie waste?

column2: கொழுக்கட்டைக்குத் தலையும் இல்லை குடியனுக்கு முறையும் இல்லை.
column3: The steamed cake has no head, nor has a drunkard sense to regard the rules of relationship.

column2: கொழுக்கட்டைக்குத் தலையும் இல்லைக் காலும் இல்லை, குறவருக்கு நெறியும் இல்லை முறையும் இல்லை.
column3: The kolukkattai has neither head nor foot, foresters are neither virtuous nor mannerly.
He who is thin is a mere straw to him who is fat.

column2: கொள் என்றால் வாயைத் திறக்கிறது, கடிவாளம் என்றால் வாயை மூடுகிறதா?
column3: What! opening the mouth when one says gram, and covering it when one says bridle?
There are neither buyers nor sellers.
Do you scratch your head with a firebrand?

column2: கொள்ளிக்கு எதிர் போனாலும் வெள்ளிக்கு எதிர் போகல் ஆகாது.
column3: Though you may encounter a firebrand, you must not appear before the planet Venus.

column2: கொள்ளிக் கட்டையால் சுட்டால் கொப்பளிக்குமென்று வாழைப்பழம் கொண்டு வடுவடுவாய்ச் சுடுகிறாய்.
column3: Because burning with a firebrand will produce blisters, you are branding me with a plantain fruit,
Great pleasure till attained, great misery afterwards.
Even when going to plunder association is bad.
Escape from plunder and pestilence.
The learned are more than kings.
The sin arising from killing is expiated by eating the flesh so killed.
The handle of the axe is the enemy of its kind.
Are the wealthy and mendicants on a par?

column2: கோடி சீமான் துணியவேண்டும், அல்லது கோவண ஆண்டி துணியவேண்டும்.
column3: An attempt is to be made either by the wealthy, or by a beggar that has only a span-long cloth.

column2: கோடி தனம் இருந்தாலும் குணம் இல்லா மங்கையை மணம் முடிக்கல் ஆகாது.
column3: It is not good to marry a girl, no matter how great her wealth, if her natural temper is exceptionable.
Which is to be preferred as a possession, ten millions, or a hundred thousand?
As it thundered in summer.

column2: கோட் சொல்பவனைக் கொடும் தேள் என நினை.
column3: Treat him who carries tales, as a scorpion.
The mouth of the slanderer is as fire exposed to the wind.
Like concealing an ominous owl in the lap.

column2: கோட்டுச் சம்பா ஆக்கிவைத்தால் போட்டுச் சாப்பிட வருவார்கள்.
column3: If samba rice is boiled, they will come and eat.

column2: கோட்டையில் பெண் பிறந்தாலும் போட்ட எழுத்துப் போகுமா?
column3: Will destiny be averted because the female was born in a fort?
Can a great battle be fought in a fort?

column2: கோணக் கொம்பு ஏறி என்ன குதிரை ஏறி என்ன வீணர்க்கும் கீர்த்திக்கும் வெகு தூரம்.
column3: Whether they mount a palanquin or a horse, the distance between the vain and reputation is very great.
That which the sack contained the buffalo carried.

column2: கோணிக் கோணிக் கோடி கொடுப்பதிலும் கோணாமற் காணி கொடுப்பது நல்லது.
column3: It is better to bestow a small gift freely than lacs with a wry face.

column2: கோத்திரம் அறிந்து பெண் கொடு, பாத்திரம் அறிந்து பிச்சை இடு
column3: Having ascertained the character of the family give your daughter in marriage, and knowing the worthiness of the applicant give alms.
One of low birth consults a fortune-teller.

column2: கோபம் இல்லாத துரையும் சம்பளம் இல்லாத சேவகனும்.
column3: A master without anger, a servant without wages.
Anger is sin, sleep is an enemy.
Anger ends in cruelty.
When anger ceases, revenge ceases.
Where there is anger, there may be excellent qualities.
The irascible is like a man on horseback without a bridle.
He walks as if supporting a tower.
He carries like a goblin that bears a tower.
The foresight of a komutti-merchant-never fails.
One should reside near a temple and a tank.
May one dwell in a village in which there is no temple?
It is enough to be called the manager of the temple.
Will the temple cat reverence the deity?
Why should a temple cat fear?
Will the medical work by Korakkar assist one to find out the symptoms of disease?
Course grass is ruinous to the cultivator.
Even the root of the korai grass will be of use sometime or other.

column2: கோல் ஆடக் குரங்கு ஆடும், அதுபோல மனம் ஆடும்.
column3: As the staff moves the monkey moves, in like manner the mind moves.
Like a blind man who has lost his staff.
The child that handles a stick is in danger of becoming blind.
A sceptre of justice is the beauty of royalty.
Is a club needed to kill a fowl?

column2: கோழி களவுபோக ஆடு வெட்டிப் பொங்கல் இடுகிறதா?
column3: Do you attempt to recover a stolen fowl by sacrificial offerings?

column2: கோழி கூவி விடிகிறதா நாய் குலைத்து விடிகிறதா?
column3: Does the day dawn at the crowing of a cock, or at the barking of a dog?
As one fed a chicken with milk.
The fever of fowls and the jealousy of a spiteful woman have no remedy.

column2: கோழிக்கறி என்றதும் கொண்டாடிக் கொண்டதும் கீரைத்தண்டாணம் அடா சுப்பா கீரைத்தண்டாணம் அடா.
column3: All that ado about the fowl curry, Subba, ends in a mess of greens.
As a hen keeps her chickens under her wings.
Having herself stolen the fowl she weeps with the owner on account of its loss.

column2: கோழி தின்ற கள்ளனும் கூட நின்று உலாவுகிறான்.
column3: The thief who has stolen the fowl, walks about with the owner is search of it.
Not only is the fowl gone, but her voice also is gone by calling it.
Will a chicken become lame if the mother hen treads on it?

column2: கோழி முடத்துக்குக் கடா வெட்டிப் பலி இட்டதுபோல்.
column3: Like sacrificing a sheep for the recovery of a lame fowl.
Can hair be plucked off a hen’s egg?
Is the fowl to be consulted when it is to be prepared for the table?
Be not first at slander nor last at meals.
Why professions of respect regarding one about whom you are always telling tales?
Near the candle-stick it will be clear to all.
Touch the feet of those with whom you have aught to do.
Be mindful of your business.
Abusive words will create enmity.
Can anxiety add length to one’s body?
He who has no concern will not say any thing favourable.

column2: ஙப்பன் பிறந்தது வெள்ளிமலை, ஙாயி பிறந்தது பொன்மலை.
column3: My father was born in a silver mountain, and my mother was born in a golden mountain.
Bend yourself like the letter ங-be humble.

column2: சகதியிலே கல்லை விட்டு எறிந்தால் தன் துணி என்றும் அசலார் துணி என்றும் பாராது.
column3: If one throws stones into mud, his own cloth and those of others will be spattered.
Is there such a dearth of span-long-creepers that you are obliged to stay at your sister's husband’s house?
Associate with a person who is versed in every thing.

column2: சகல தேசத்து வார்த்தையும் கற்றுக்கொள்ளுகிறதே யாவருக்கும் நல்லது.
column3: To study the different dialects of each country is useful to all.
He who has a brother does not fear to fight.

column2: சக்குச் சக்கு என்று பாக்குத் தின்பான் சபைமெச்ச, வீட்டிலே வந்து கடைவாயை நக்குவான் பெண்டுகள் மெய்ச்ச.
column3: When out he chews betel to attract public notice, when he returns home he licks the corners of his mouth to secure the admiration of women.
Are fools and blockheads of service to the religious mendicant?

column2: சங்கிலே வார்த்தால் தீர்த்தம், சட்டியிலே வார்த்தால் தண்ணீர்.
column3: If poured into a conch, water becomes sacred, if into a chatty, it is what it is.

column2: சங்கு ஆயிரம் கொண்டு காசிக்கிப் போனாலும் தன் பாவம் தன்னோடே.
column3: Though one carries a thousand conchs to Benares, his sin sticks to him.
The mouth of the conch, the mouth of the religious mendicant.
When deprived of modesty, there will be no sense of honour.

column2: சடைத் தம்பிரான் சாற்றுக்கு அழுகிறானாம், லிங்கம் பஞ்ச அமிர்தத்திற்கு அழுகிறதாம்.
column3: It is said that the religious mendicant with matted hair cries for pepper water, and the linga he worships cries for the five delicacies, viz., milk, curds, ghee, sugar and honey.

column2: சடைத் தம்பிரான் சாதம் என்கிறபோது மொட்டைத் தம்பிரானுக்கு மோர் எங்கே கிடைக்கும்.
column3: When a religious mendicant with matted hair finds it difficult to obtain rice, where will the bald headed obtain butter milk.

column2: சடைத்தம்பிரான் தவிட்டுக்கு அழுகிறபோது லிங்கம் பரமானந்தத்துக்கு அழுகிறதா?
column3: When a religious mendicant is crying for bran, will the linga ask for delicacies?
If dragged by his matted hair, the religious mendicant may be brought near one.
The chatty burnt, the hand left it.
Can a ladle appreciate the flavour of curry?

column2: சணப்பன் வீட்டுக் கோழி, தானே விலங்கு பூட்டிக்கொண்டதுபோல.
column3: As a flax-dresser’s fowl fettered itself.
One moment he is bilious and the next rheumatic.
Like dry leaves before a strong wind.
A stubborn person well suited to the self-willed.
Is relationship recognised in a battle field?
She will call him Annamalai the gormandizer.

column2: சதகோடி சங்கத்திலே மொட்டைத் தாதனைக் கண்டாயா என்கிறதுபோல.
column3: Like asking one in a crowd, if the bald headed devotee has been seen.
The eaglewood-acquilla grows with the prickly pear.
The surgeon's knife must be judiciously applied.
Will the knife operate where there is no flesh?
The surgeon’s knife seeks the fleshy parts.

column2: சத்த மேகங்களும் கூடி நெருப்பு மழை பெய்தாற்போல.
column3: As if seven clouds simultaneously rained fire.
Is the order of the Naidu required in order to procure gruel at the choultry?
Is a divine hymn superior to that sung in a choultry?
Eating in a choultry and sleeping in a monastery.

column2: சத்தியத்திலே சாமி சாட்சி என்கிற சத்தியம் பெரிது.
column3: To say God is the witness of what one testifies, is the highest of all forms of oath.
Will the child that has no regard for truth go to weep with one when in sorrow?
Truthfulness is the best condition, daily think of death.
Truth prevails, falsehood kills.
Truth kills falsehood, truth prevails.
The truthful man has all other virtues.
To eat rice at the choultry, is the permission of the head brahman necessary?

column2: சந்துகள் ஓடு சந்துக்கள் சேர்வார், சந்தனத்தோடு கருப்பூரம் சேரும்.
column3: The powerful associate with the powerful, sandal paste mixes with camphor.
Love even your enemies heartily.
An enemy’s envy is his own punishment.
As a dog barked at the moon.
The moon shines even in the house of the outcast.
Who is the witness of him that has been flogged in the bazaar?
A crowd of people in a market-place is like a puppet-show.
Will those who frequent the same market aid as friends?
The pial-open verandah-is the home of the ascetic.
The pilgrimage of the mendicant is to jump off a pial.
The old temper of the ascetic will not leave him.
As an ascetic became a householder when he coveted a cloth.

column2: சந்நியாசியைக் கடித்த நாய்க்குப் பின்னாலே நரகமாம் சந்நியாசிக்கு முன்னாலே மரணம்.
column3: They say that the dog that bites an ascetic will go to hell eventually, the ascetic will die before.
It is said that hell awaits him who reproaches an ascetic.
Even an ascetic feels proud of his caste.
Timidity before an assembly is bad.
In the assembly of the learned he is a nakkíran-a sage-in a royal council a chèran-a king skilled in archery.
A lame man is a hero before a cripple.
What has a naked mendicant to do with a washerman?
When opportunity offers he may eat even Yama as a cake.
He will steal if opportunity offer.
Nothing is too great for a clever man.
Do cannon balls fly with intelligent force?

column2: சமர்த்தி என்ன பெற்றாள் சட்டிச்சோறு தின்னப் பெற்றாள்.
column3: What did the clever matron get, she got rice in chatty fulls.
Even a blade of grass is a weapon in the hands of a skilful warrior.

column2: சமர்த்துள்ள சேவகனுக்கு வாள் பழுதானாலும் கேடு இல்லை.
column3: Though his sword is injured, the skilful soldier suffers no loss.
In power, equal to Saturn.
He who cannot comprehend a sign is not clever.
He is inciting to fight by shewing signs.
It is a loss for family broils to get abroad.
Are the ocean and a ditch alike?
Like planting a picotta on the sea-side.
How vast the difference between the ocean and a span-wide ditch?
A deep treacherous fellow is a wretch.
As a dog crept into the cooking room.
As a hare of its own accord came into the cook room.
Abundance and straitness are closely allied.
Though one’s income is only half a fanam, an air of respectability must be preserved.
Though one’s hire is but half a fanam dependents must be protected.

column2: சம்பன்ன கிரகஸ்தன் வந்தான், செம்பு தவலையை உள்ளே வை.
column3: Our rich and honest neighbour is come, remove the copper vessels out of sight.
Like lice in a cloth that had been in the hands of a Jaina mendicant.
Bringing forth a child where the required conveniences are found.

column2: சரக்கு கண்ட இடத்திலே பிள்ளைக்கு அவிழ்தம் கொடுக்க நினைக்கிறது போல.
column3: Like intending to give medicine to her child where conveniences are found.

column2: சரக்கு அரிக்க நேரம் அன்றி குளிர் காய நேரம் இல்லை.
column3: He has only just tine to collect dry leaves; he has not time to warm himself.
Is the word sugar sweet?
A honey-fall in a sugar pandal-an open shed.
Are sugar and sand alike?
Are sugar and flour alike?

column2: சருக்கரை தின்று பித்தம் போனால், கைப்பு மருந்து ஏன் தின்னவேண்டும்.
column3: If bile can be removed by taking sugar, why take bitter medicine.

column2: சலம் நுழையாத இடத்தில் எண்ணெயும், எண்ணெய் நுழையாத இடத்தில் புகையும் நுழையும்.
column3: Oil gets in where water cannot, and smoke enters where oil cannot.
Drive not the coach by magic.
Like pulling out hair from civet.
Are you suffering from catarrh, or has Saturn seized you?
The fop is his own enemy, but a friend of the cloth merchant.
The friendship of the good is agreeable as molases of sugar.

column2: சனத்தோடு சனம் சேரும் சந்தனத்தோடு கர்ப்பூரம் சேரும்.
column3: Men associate with men, camphor and sandal paste blend together.
When a man has a strong party he will act with decision.

Bathe on Saturdays.This proverb is regarded by the people among whom I have lived as equivalent to a command, and is obeyed as such, by observing Saturday as a bathing day.Some interpreters put another construction upon it, making the word, usually translated Saturday, a verb, meaning to spring as water, and render it, bathe in springing, fresh, cool water. In some parts of the country, men bathe on Saturday, while women bathe on Friday or Monday. To bathe on Tuesday is regarded as fatal if repeated again and again.
Has Saturn seized, or one possessed by him, seized?

A Saturday corpse goes not alone.To avert evil as far as possible, it is not uncommon, with reference to the prevailing opinion expressed in this proverb, for Hindus to bury a fowl when a death happens on Saturday. A Native friend informed me that he saw a chicken put into the coffin of a deceased Native Christian. I have been informed that on some occasions the fowl is carried alive to the place of incremation or burial, the people scattering various kinds of grain on the way. In this case the fowl is killed near the corpse and its blood is sprinkled upon it.
Like procuring Saturn at a price.
A woman seized by Saturn will not find a rag even in the market.

column2: சனியன் பிடித்தவளுக்குச் சந்தையிற் போனாலும் புருஷன் அகப்படமாட்டான்.
column3: A woman seized by Saturn will not obtain a husband though she may go to the market.
If oracular power has failed, whence the ground of exultation?
The vicious wanders about famished; the householder is in the enjoyment of plenty.
The sea is but swimming depth to one that braves death.
There is time to die, but none to be consumed by fire.

column2: சாகிறவரையில் வைத்தியன் விடான், செத்தாலும் விடான் பஞ்சாங்கக்காரன்.
column3: A physician will not leave one till death, an astrologer will not leave even then.
Medicine must be given to the very last.
They who were apparently dying have recovered.
The ocean is but knee-deep to him who is dying.
Will one who is dying be afraid of Saturn?

column2: சாகிற வரைக்கும் சஞ்சலமானால் போகிறது எந்தக் காலமோ?
column3: If anxieties attend us till death, when will they be removed?
If we are to be troubled till death, when shall we enjoy prosperity?
As a dying dog showed courage.
As a dog about to die ascended the house top.For a dog to get on the roof of a house is regarded as an omen of some great calamity, and therefore the dog should be destroyed.

column2: சாக்கடைச் சேறு என்றாலும் சக்களத்தி என்றாலும் சரி.
column3: The mud of a ditch and a rival wife are alike.
Though despicable as a worm in a ditch, it is difficult to overcome a rival wife.
Where shall the worm that was born and bred in the ditch go?
Excuses are of no avail before God.
Is it the result of excuse, planetary influence or the matron’s word?
As a lid fitted to a jar.

column2: சாட்சிக்காரன் காலில் விழுவதினும் சண்டைக்காரன் காலில் விழலாம்.
column3: Better to fall at the feet of an opponent than at the feet of witnesses.
No death occurs without an ostensible cause.
He is able to spin a top without a string.
Will a top spin without a string?

column2: சாணான் எச்சில் கருப்புக்கட்டி, சர்க்கரை வெல்லம் உழவன் எச்சில்.
column3: Course sugar is defiled by a chanan’s-tree climber-saliva, and sugar by that of a ploughman.
Can you imagine the ocean and a dung-pit to be of equal magnitude?
Can you compare a cow-dung chatty with a brass pan?
Are dung and civet alike?
It differs at every span-length.
Is your stomach a span-long, or are you all stomach?
To advance a span, and slide back a cubit.
In every span of pavement I wash, there is a cubit of deep mud.
It is said that a span-long bird has a cubit-long tail.
What, a cubit of string for a span of matted hair?
Can a stick a cubit long be cut in a span-long copse?
A cubit stick for a span drum.
Although the snake may be only a span-long, a stick a cubit long is required to kill it.
Is it a span-long child or a bold man?

column2: சாதி அந்த புத்தி குலம் அந்த ஆசாரம்.
column3: Ideas suited to caste, manners suited to rank.
Caste and religious distinctions obtain even among religious mendicants.
When the meek are enraged, even a forest will not hold-their wrath.

column2: சாதுரியப் பூனை மீன் இருக்கப் புளியங்காயைக் கொண்டு போய்விட்டது.
column3: The cunning cat left the fish, and carried off the tamarind fruit.

column2: சாதுரியப் பூனை தயிர் இருக்கச் சட்டியை நக்கிற்று.
column3: The artful cat left the curds, and licked the chatty.

column2: சாத்தானி குடுமிக்கும் சந்நியாசி பூணூலுக்கும் முடி போடுகிறாய்.
column3: Thou art tying the knot of Sattàni’s hair to the sacred thread of the religious mendicant.
If the shasters are false, look at the eclipse.

column2: சாஸ்திரம் பார்த்துப் பெண்ணைக் கொள் கோத்திரம் பார்த்துப் பெண்ணைக் கொடு.
column3: Take a wife after consulting the shasters and give a daughter in marriage after ascertaining the character of the family into which she is going.

column2: சாஸ்திரம் பாராத வீடு சமுத்திரம், பார்த்த வீடு தரித்திரம்.
column3: The family that regards not the shasters has an ocean of wealth, whilst the one that regards them is in poverty.
He who is learned in the shasters is himself money.
The scent box has become a box of snakes.
Can you dandle a still-born child?
Better die than bear a still-born child.
Will she have joy who has borne a still-born child?
Though a still-born child is brought forth, there is no escape from the midwife’s fee.

column2: சாமி வரம் கொடுத்தாலும் பூசாரி இடம் கொடுக்கமாட்டான்.
column3: Though God may bestow the gift, the priest will not suffer you to enjoy it.
Sámi rice when boiled, and a girl of the shoe-maker class when matured, appear to advantage.

column2: சாம்பலைக் கிண்டி கோழி தானே விலங்கு இட்டுக்கொண்டதுபோல.
column3: As a hen fettered herself whilst scratching a rubbish heap.
Like rubbing the mouth with butter after having eaten ashes.
Pour in liquor and draw out the secret.

column2: சாலாய் வனைந்தால் என்ன சட்டியாய் வனைந்தால் என்ன?
column3: What matters it whether the potter makes a large or a small chatty?

column2: சாலோடே தண்ணீர் சாய்த்துக் குடித்தாலும் தாய் வார்க்கும் தண்ணீர் தாகம் தெளியும்.
column3: Though one may drink water out of a large pot, the water that one’s mother pours out allays one’s thirst.

column2: சாவாமற் கற்பதே கல்வி, பிறர் இடத்தில் ஏகாமல் உண்பதே ஊண்.
column3: That is learning which teaches us to escape death, and that is food which is obtained without dependence on others.
A community without learned men is a hamlet of mountaineers.
When hungry will a lion prey on frogs?

column2: சிங்கம் பசிக்கு ஆனையையே தேடிக் கொல்லும்; ,அதுபோல், பெரியோர் அக்கறைப்பட்டால் பெரிய காரியத்தையேச் செய்வார்கள்.
column3: When lions are hungry they go in search of elephants to prey on, in like manner, when the great are reduced to poverty they achieve great things-to relieve their wants.
A lion knows no danger.
As a palmyra fruit was placed on a small bird.
Do you discharge heavy arrows at small birds?
Hesitancy and delay lead to disagreeables.

column2: சிதம்பரத்திலே பிறந்த பிள்ளைக்குத் திருவெம்பாவை கற்றுக் கொடுக்கவேண்டுமா?
column3: Is it necessary to teach venba-holy vereses-to a child born and brought up at Chilambaram?
As a demon looks at the circle of Chilambaram.This proverb refers most likely to magical diagrams generally.

column2: சித்தன் போக்குச் சிவன் போக்கு ஆண்டி போக்கு அதே போக்கு.
column3: The manner of Chittan is like the manner of Siva, the manner of religious mendicant is like itself.

column2: சித்திரை மாதத்திற் செல்வன் பிறந்தால் சீரும் சிறப்பும் கெடும்.
column3: If a son is born in Chittirai-April-the state and reputation of the family will be ruined.

column2: சித்திரை மாதத்திற் பிறந்த சீர் கேடனும் இல்லை, ஐப்பசி மாதத்திற் பிறந்த அதிர்ஷ்டவானும் இல்லை.
column3: None born in Chittirai-April-is unfortunate, none born in Aipasi-October-is fortunate.

column2: சித்தி பெறாத மருந்தும் மருந்தோ பெற்றுப் படையாத பிள்ளையும் பிள்ளையோ?
column3: Is that medicine which does not take effect, is that which a woman has not brought forth and reared, her child?
Little Vengadamma is a hypocritical lady.
Form friendships after due deliberation, having done so do not give place to doubt.
On forming friendship try it, and on being convinced of its sincerity, rely on it.

column2: சிந்த அறுந்துபோகிற மூக்கு எந்த மட்டும் இருக்கும்?
column3: How long will the nose last that breaks off on blowing?

column2: சிப்பியிலே விழுந்த மழைத் துளி முத்தாகும்; அதுபோல, நல்லவர்க்குச் செய்த உதவி நிலைநிற்கும்.
column3: A rain drop that falls on an oyster-shell will become a pearl, so a benefit conferred on the virtuous will endure.
If when it is a twig it cannot be bent, will it bend when it has become a large tree?
If shaven-bald, if kept, kudumi.
If you shave, hire; if you serve, wages.
Is a woman who pilfers aware what expense means?
As a bear entered at the time of Siva puja.
Is a dancing bear produced at the time of Siva puja?
A rat on Siva linga.

column2: சிவியானுக்கு அடிமைப்பட்டால் காவவும் வேண்டும் சுமக்கவும் வேண்டும்.
column3: If subject to a palanquin bearer, one must bear both palanquins and burdens.
He will beat out gold even thinner than a feather.
Like a bird without wings.
Like a bird deprived of its wings.

column2: சிறியாரோடு இணங்காதே சேம்புக்குப் புளி விட்டு மசியாதே.
column3: Do not associate with the mean, do not macerate chambu greens with acid.

column2: சிறியார்க்கு இனியதைக் காட்டாதே சேம்புக்குப் புளி விட்டு ஆக்காதே.
column3: Do not offer sweets to children, nor mix acid with chambu greens.

column2: சிறியோர் செய்த சிறு பிழை எல்லாம் பெரியோர் ஆனவர் பொறுப்பது கடனே.
column3: It is the duty of the great to forgive the faults of inferiors.
All that are little are not inferiors.
If you eat little by little, you may consume a mountain.
Build a small house, and live thriftily.
He that sows little will reap little.
But little water is required to fill a small hole.

column2: சிறு குழந்தை இல்லாத வீடும் வீடு அல்ல, சீரகம் இட்டு ஆக்காத கறியும் கறி அல்ல.
column3: A house without an infant is not a house, nor is a curry without seerakam-cummin-a real curry.

column2: சிறுக்கி சின்னப் பணம், சிறுக்கி கொண்டை மூன்று பணம்.
column3: The young lady is worth a small fanam, and she requires three fanams to adorn her tresses.
Even a small fibre may serve as a tooth-pick.
Will that which did not bend when small, do so when it becomes large?

column2: சிறு பிள்ளை செய்த வேளாண்மை விளைந்தாலும் வீடு வந்து சேராது.
column3: The harvest of little children will never be housed.
Is it a small cloth worn by a little girl?

column2: சிறுபோது படியாத கல்வி அழுக்குச் சேலையில் சாயம் ஏற்றினதுபோல.
column3: Learning not acquired in early life is like a cloth dyed when dirty.

column2: சிறு மீன் எல்லாம் பெரு மீனுக்கு இரை.
column3: Little fish are the prey of great fish.
The lower and higher stations in society, are the result of each one’s exertions.

column2: சிறு ரூபத்தை உடைய பேரும் அரும்பொருளைச் செய்வார்; அதுபோல, சிறு விதை ஆகிய ஆலமரம் பெரு நிழலைக் கொடுக்கும்.
column3: Those who are of inferior stature may accomplish difficult things: the seed of the banyan is small, but the tree affords a large shade.
Learning acquired in youth, is an inscription on stone.

column2: சிறைச்சாலைக்கு அறை இல்லை, தேவடியாளுக்கு முறை இல்லை.
column3: A prison has no apartments, a temple girl observes no relationships.
A young hireling is equal to eight grown up servants.

column2: சிற்றாத்தை பிள்ளையும் பிள்ளையோ செத்தையிற் பல்லியும் பல்லியோ?
column3: Is the child of a maternal aunt a child? Is a lizard on the rubbish heap a lizard?Thia refers to a species whose chirp is not regarded in augury.
Those who have tasted real happiness, will not regard inferior pleasures.
Persons of little learning are always talkative.
Will an extended comedy be performed in a mere hamlet?
Moderate refection is sweet.
Little ants seek small ants; big ants seek great ones.
Will the nose cut off in anger, be restored by laughing?
Dot not speak reproachfully though provoked.

column2: சின்ன சின்னப் பேச்சுச் சிங்காரப்பேச்சு வன்னவன்னப் பேச்சு மெத்த வழக்கமான பேச்சு.
column3: Short expressions, beautiful expressions; florid expressions, colloqual expressions.
Even a demon will not enter a house that has pooh-poohed him.
Will a dog enter a jungle that is looked down upon?
A hasty mind is feeble.

column2: சீட்டாளுக்கு ஒரு முட்டாள் செருப்புத் தூக்கிக்கு ஒரு அடப்பக்காரனா?
column3: Does a letter-carrier, require a helper, or a shoeboy employ one to carry his betel pouch?
The birth of Sita was the ruin of Lanka.

column2: சீபுரத்துப் பள்ளி செத்தும் கெட்டான் இருந்தும் கெட்டான்.
column3: A Palli of Sípuram is ruined when dead, as well as when alive.
If the comb be concealed, will the marriage ceremony be delayed?
Though hatched at Shrirangam, will a crow sing the praises of Govinda?
As a man lost his wife on his way to Shrirangam.
As a pestle was given at Shrirangam.
As one going to the shrine of Shrirangam concealed an old jackal in his bosom.

column2: சீர் அற்றார் கையிற் செம்பொன் விலை பெறா.
column3: Fine gold in the hands of the unthrifty is of no value.
Though reduced to poverty, the virtuous are still virtuous.
Be kind to the virtuous.

column2: சீலைப்பாய் ஈழம் போய்ச் சீனி சருக்கரை கட்டுமா?
column3: Will a ragged cloth go to Ceylon to tie up sugar?
Do not make merchandise of your tongue for a livelihood.
Will fame go when life goes?
If you write the word sugar and lick it, will it taste sweet?
Grief and joy are a revolving wheel.

column2: சுகத்துக்குப் பின் துக்கம் துக்கத்துக்குப் பின் சுகம்.
column3: After joy grief, after grief joy.
We must either enjoy happiness or practise austerities.

column2: சுகத்தைப் பெற்றதும் அல்ல தவத்தைப் பெற்றதும் அல்ல.
column3: He has neither obtained happiness nor the fruit of austerities.

column2: சுகம் வந்தால் சந்தோஷப்பட்டுத் துன்பம் வந்தால் பின்வாங்குவானேன்?
column3: If when prosperous you rejoiced, why draw back when adversity supervenes?
A man of luxurious habits is an ass.

column2: சுக்கிர உதயத்தில் தாலி கட்டி, சூரிய உதயத்திற்குள் அறுத்தாள்.
column3: She who was married when Venus rose, was denuded of her marriage symbol at sunrise.
It is the infliction of Sugriva.Sugriva, a monkey, the ally of Ramachandra.
Is there any decoction without dried ginger?

column2: சுக்குத் தின்று முக்கிப் பெற்றால் தெரியும் பிள்ளை அருமை.
column3: The preciousness of children is known to her who has eaten dried ginger and borne one.It is common when a woman is confined, to give her a preparation of dried ginger and other spices.

column2: சுக்குக் கண்ட இடத்திலே முக்கிப் பிள்ளை பெருவாளா?
column3: Will she bring forth as soon as she sees the dried ginger?

column2: சுக்குக் கண்ட இடத்தில் பிள்ளை பெற்றுச் சூரிய நாராயணன் என்று பெயர் இடுவாள்.
column3: As soon as she sees the dried ginger, she will bring forth a child, and call it Suryanarayana.The name being the expression of her joy.

column2: சுக்கும் பாக்கும் வெட்டித் தாறேன் சுள்சுள் என்று வெயில் எறி.
column3: Shine out brilliantly, I will give you dried ginger and arica-nut.
If taxes are heavy, even chunambu cannot be had.
Taxes and gruel become heavier by being kept.

column2: சுடர் விளக்காயினும் தூண்டு கோல் ஒன்று வேணும்.
column3: Though it may be a bright burning lamp, a splinter is required for raising the wick.
The solemn thoughts occasioned by the funeral pyre, last till each one returns home.
The corpse that has gone to the place of incremation will not return.
The tree snake looks anxiously for the smoke of the funeral pyre.It is said that snakes enjoy the odour of a burning body.
May one burst the bund of a tank in order to get fried fish?
As a dog rolls burning charcoal.
Does the baking pan appreciate the flavour of a cake?

column2: சுட்ட சட்டியும் சட்டுவமும் கறிச் சுவை அறியுமா?
column3: Do the chatty and ladle know the flavour of curry?
Will burnt and moist earth unite?

column2: சுண்டைக்காய் காற் பணம் சுமைகூலி முக்காற் பணம்.
column3: The price of the chundakai is a quarter of a fanam, its carriage three quarters of a fanam.

column2: சுண்டைக்காய் அளவிலே, சாப்பிடுகிறது பாதியா, வைக்கிறது பாதியா?
column3: Of the food about the size of a chundaikkai, am I to eat half and leave half?
The speciality or charm of-arica-nut and betel-is in the chunambu.
A clean beast will join a clean beast.
To the real hero life is a mere straw.
No toll at a resting block.
Why waste your time in cutting the rotten kalákkai?

column2: சும்மா கிடக்கிற தாரையை ஊதிக் கெடுத்தான் அம்மான்.
column3: By blowing the unused trumpet my uncle spoiled it.

column2: சும்மா கலம் சுமக்க மாட்டான் நனைந்து முக்கலம் சுமப்பான்.
column3: In fair weather he will not carry one kalam of grain, in foul weather, when drenched with rain, he carries three.

column2: சும்மா போகிறவனைப் பிடிப்பானேன், இராத்திரி எல்லாம் கிடந்து பிதற்றுவான் ஏன்?
column3: Why seize one going along, and lie groaning all the night?

column2: சும்மா இருந்தால் சோறு ஆமா வாடா சித்தா கால் ஆட்ட.
column3: Will boiled rice come of itself? Come along Chitta, let us shake our legs.

column2: சும்மா இருந்த அம்மையாருக்கு அரைப் பணத்துத் தாலி போதாதா?
column3: Is not a marriage symbol worth half a fanam, a gain to the matron who is worth nothing?
Can one approach the feet of Chonachalan without effort?

column2: சுய காரிய துரந்தரன் சுவாமி காரியம் வழவழ.
column3: He who is intent about his own affairs will not mind the things of God.
Intent about his own affairs, he turns from others.
Who can act so as to please a woman?

column2: சுருட்டை சோறு இடும் பம்பை பால் வார்க்கும்.
column3: Curly hair will give rice, dishevelled hair will pour out milk.
When religious precepts and physical enjoyment are in harmony, happiness is the resultant.

column2: சுருதி குரு சுகானுபவம் மூன்றும் ஒத்தது மெய்ப்பொருளாம்.
column3: When the vedas, the priest and enjoyment meet together, there is real wealth.

column2: சுரைப் பூவுக்கும் பறைப் பாட்டுக்கும் மணம் இல்லை.
column3: The flower of a bottle-gourd is not fragrant, the song of a pariah is not chaste.
Even a wall may have ears.
You must first build the wall, must you not, and then adorn it with figures?
In the paradise of Indra a scavenger and a Vellala of the Tonda country are equal.
When on your way to heaven, do you carry a bundle under your arm?
When on your way to heaven, do you carry a spinning-wheel under your arm?
Is one expected to take his concubine along with him to the paradise of Indra?

column2: சுவாமி வரம் கொடுத்தாலும் முன்னடியான் வரம் கொடான்.
column3: Though God may grant a boon, the devotee in his presence will not.
Every bitter sneer leads to evil.
To grill dried fish and drink toddy.

column2: சுற்றத் துணியும் இல்லை நக்கத் தவிடும் இல்லை.
column3: No cloth to wear, no bran to lick.
It is the beauty of friendship to be surrounded-by friends.
Live close by your relations.
A burnt cat shuns the fire-place.
Is it proper to tie the mouth of the ox that treads out the corn?
Important ends are attained by comparatively insignificant means.
The great contriver who is inscrutable.
Know the efficacy of mercury by the glow of health on the cheeks.
The cheat is void of justice.
The ox will graze in the field of the intriguing.
Circumspection leads not to ruin.
It is easy to overcome an enemy by intrigue.
Gambling and boasting end in sorrow.
Desire not gambling.
A stringed brahman observes the shasters.
He dances like a puppet.
All under the sun is vanity.
Like hiding the sun with the hand.
Like darkness that has seen the sun.
It will vanish as the dew before the sun.
Like a fire-fly before the sun.
The earth is illumined by the sun.

column2: சூரியனைக் கிரகணம் பிடித்ததுபோல் உன்னைச் சனி பிடித்தது.
column3: Saturn has seized thee as the eclipse seizes the sun.
Form your plans before sunrise.
Fire-flies do not shine in the presence of the sun.
As the dog barked at the sun.
Like striking the sun with a stone.

column2: சூலாகு மீன்கள் ஒன்றை ஒன்று மோந்து கொள்ளும். .
column3: Like chula fish kissing each other.

column2: சூலி சூலி என்று சோற்றைத் தின்று மலடிவாயில் மண்ணைப் போடுகிறதா?
column3: Do you eat up the rice yourself on the plea of pregnancy, and put earth into the mouth of the barren?
Though you run round, you will have to enter by the gate.

column2: செக்கு உலக்கையை விழுங்கினவனுக்கு சுக்குக் கஷாயம் மருந்தாமா?
column3: Is a decoction of dried ginger a specific for him who has swallowed the pestle of an oil press?
Will conspiracy against a just administration avail?
He who holds the sceptre is the triune God.
If the sceptre turn aside, its effect will be felt every where.
If it would not bend as a sapling, will it bend as a tree?
The merchant and the farmer cherish innate hatred towards each other.
The merchant has a natural aversion to agriculture.

column2: செட்டி கூடிக் கெட்டான் சேணியன் பிரிந்து கெட்டான்.
column3: The Chetty was ruined by taking a partner, the weaver by separating from one.

column2: செட்டி போன இடம் எல்லாம் வட்டம் காற் பணம்.
column3: Wherever the merchant goes there is a discount of a quarter fanam.

column2: செட்டி பணத்தைக் குறைத்தான், சேணியன் காலைக் குறைத்தான்.
column3: The merchant reduced the money, the weaver lessened the width of the cloth.

column2: செட்டி படை வெல்லுமா, சேற்றுத் தவளை கடிக்குமா? .
column3: Can merchants disperse an army, can a toad inflict a wound?
While the Chetty is adorning himself, the city is besieged.

column2: செட்டியாரே செட்டியாரே என்றால் சீரகம் பணஎடை முக்காற் பணம் என்கிறார்.
column3: When I respectfully call him Chettiyar, he says that a fanam weight of cumin is sold for three fourths of a fanam.

column2: செட்டி வீட்டிற் பணம் இருக்கிறது, ஆலமரத்தில் பேய் இருக்கிறது.
column3: Wealth is in the houses of merchants, demons are in banyan trees.
Will the oxen of Chetties ascend and graze on the mountains?
Chettiyar, come and take charge of the bazaar.
As Níli-a cruel woman-followed the Chetty.
I paid the Chetty and also his bag.

column2: செட்டுக்கு ஒரு தட்டு, சேவகனுக்கு ஒரு வெட்டு.
column3: A slap for the merchant, a sword cut for the warrior.
The dead dog will bite no more.
Who was ever ruined in a house whose owner was dead?

column2: செத்தவன் வாயிலே மண், இருந்தவன் வாயிலே சோறு.
column3: Earth in the mouth of the dead, and rice in the mouth of the living.
Can a dead cow eat grass?

column2: செத்தவன் கண் செந்தாமரை கண், இருந்தவன் கண் நொள்ளைக் கண்.
column3: The eye of the dead is a red lotus, that of the living a sightless orb.
No matter what becomes of one after his death?

column2: செத்தவன் தலை கிழக்கே இருந்தால் என்ன மேற்கே இருந்தால் என்ன?
column3: What matters it whether the head of a corpse be towards the east, or west?Most Hindus, and some Native Christians even, are very particular about the position in which they lie down to sleep. The head should be towards the south. The north is the region of Yama. The west is avoided because the person so lying down may not rise again.
He that does not help his friends or relations is worse than a corpse.

column2: செத்த ஆடு காற்பணம் சுமைகூலி முக்காற் பணம்.
column3: The dead sheep is worth a quarter of a fanam, and three fourths of a fanam are required to remove it.
As they walk round a corpse.This may refer to the custom of a relative going round a corpse three times at the place of incremation.
As the survivor took the wife of the deceased.
He is charming a dead snake.

column2: செத்தவன் கையிலே வெற்றிலை பாக்குக் கொடுத்த சம்பந்தம்.
column3: Like putting betel and arica-nut into the hand of the dead.The giving of betel and nut is one of the marriage ceremonies.

column2: செத்த மாட்டை அறுக்காத கத்தி சொத்தைக் கத்திரிக்காயை அறுக்கும்.
column3: A knife that will not cut a dead cow, will cut a blighted brinjal.

column2: செத்த பாம்பு சுற்ற வருகிறதே அத்தை நான் மாட்டேன் என்றாற்போல.
column3: As if one should say, aunt, I will not, the dead snake comes to coil round my leg.

column2: செத்த பிணத்துக்கு அருகே இனிச் சாகும் பிணம் அழுகிறது.
column3: Those who will hereafter die weep by the body of the deceased.

column2: செத்த பிணத்துக்கு கண் ஏன், சிவசிவ ஆண்டிக்குப் பெண் ஏன்?
column3: Why an eye to the corpse, why a wife to the Saiva mendicant?
The wealth of the Chetty will be known after death.
How dexterously he juggles?
Is the title of Appa suited to a family that possesses no brass utensils?
Like a box and a ball.

column2: செம்பரம் பாக்கத்தான் பெயர் பெற்றான் மாங்காட்டான் நீர் பெற்றான்.
column3: Men at Champerempákum get a name, those at Mangadoo get water.
He will not give even a copper coin.
Black wood is stronger than red.
Copper utensils being introduced, the potter removes.

column2: செம்மறி ஆடு வெளியே ஓடத் திருட்டு ஓனாய் உள்ளே.
column3: The sheep is without, the thievish wolf is within.
While successful he knows no fear.
Every man’s actions will cleave to him.
Is it after death that benefits are to be requited?

column2: செய்யும் தொழில் எல்லாம் சீர்தூக்கிப் பார்க்கில் நெய்யும் தொழிலுக்கு நிகர் இல்லை.
column3: When all occupations are duly weighed, that of weaving will appear unequalled.
Do well, what you have to do.
Like giving a horse in compensation, to one who has been slippered.
The earnest paid for shoes is a straw.

column2: செருப்பின் அருமை வெயிலில் தெரியும் நெருப்பின் அருமை குளிரில் தெரியும்.
column3: The value of shoes is appreciated when the sun is hot, the value of fire is known when the weather is cold.
Is the foot to be cut off to try on the shoe?
Is it for the feet or head, that one buys shoes?
If expenses exceed income how can one thrive?
Expenses exceed the income.
On my saying do it he replies give me two.
Like ornamentation that costs nothing.

column2: செலவோடு செலவு கந்தப் பொடிக்குக் காற் பணம்.
column3: A quarter fanam for sweet scented powder, over and above the expenses already incurred.
Will a spoiled child fear rebuke?
Does indulgence cause you to slip, or are the door-steps slippery?

column2: செல்லப் பிள்ளை சீலை உடாதாம் பிள்ளை பெறுமட்டும்.
column3: A spoiled child will not put on clothes till it becomes a mother.
A coin not current is always so.
What discount for current cash?
There is a Chetty who can say whether the coin is current or not.

column2: செல்வம் பரமண்டலம் செல்லாது, எல்லா மண்டலத்தும் செல்லும்.
column3: Wealth goes anywhither but to heaven.

column2: செல்வம் உண்டாகும் காலம், செய்கை உண்டு, வல்லமை உண்டு.
column3: In time of prosperity there is exertion and power.
He is proud of his wealth though he has not means to obtain a cash.

column2: செல்வப்பொருள் கொடுத்தால் குறையும், கல்விப் பொருள் குறையுமோ?
column3: On giving, wealth diminishes, will learning also so diminish?
Wealth makes one proud, the door-steps are slippery.
It is an honor to the wealthy to protect their relatives.

column2: செல்வன் சொல்லுக்கு அஞ்சான், வீரன் போருக்கு அஞ்சான்.
column3: The wealthy fear no orders, the hero is not afraid of war.
Wealth is impermanent, live in subjection to a superior.
Like blowing a conch in the ear of the deaf.
As the deaf and the blind attended a comedy.
As the deaf listened to a song.
If one is deaf is he not dumb also?
As one born blind obtained his sight.

column2: சென்ற காரியத்தைப் பார்த்து வரும் காரியத்தை அறி.
column3: Learn the future by looking at things past.
Learning distinguishes one whithersoever he goes.
A demon laughs at malevolent thoughts.
Why a small sickle to gather chémbu greens?

column2: சேர இருந்தால் செடியும் பகை, தூர இருந்தால் தோட்டியும் உறவு.
column3: If too near even a shrub-a worthless fellow-is an enemy, if distant even a scavenger is a friend.
Associate with the agreeable.
A hamlet, a country, wealth and learning.
Is the habit natural or acquired?
Regard those as friends whose sincerity has been carefully tested.

column2: சேலைமேற் சேலை கட்டித் தேவரம்பை ஆடினாலும், ஓலைமேல் எழுத்தாணி ஊன்றும் பெண் ஆகாது.
column3: Though one wear cloth upon cloth, and is able to dance like a celestial, she is not to be desired if she can press a style on a palm leaf, i. e., if she can write.A strong dissuasive against female education. The proverb is nevertheless in harmony with the sentiments of the majority of Hindus who have received high education in English.
Even a crow will peck an elephant when it is in the mud.
As the water-lily blossoms in mud.
Like a pole set up in mud.
Why lift up the buffalo that wallows in the mire?
A wall of mud.
Are honorary distinctions bestowed on an army, or on its leader?
Those who cannot take a hint know nothing.
The cook of the vegetarian Muttaiya of the Modelly caste, is a pariah mendicant.
The size of the chatty is enough for the Chetty’s attendant.
The game of draughts is ruinous.
Like the young of a chottai válai fish.

column2: சொருகிக் கிடந்த அகப்பையும் சோறு அள்ளப் பறப்பட்டது.
column3: Even the ladle that had been cast aside as useless, is again serving out rice.

column2: சொல்லச் சொல்லப் பட்டி பெண்ணைப் பெண்ணைப் பெற்றாள்.
column3: Though again and again forbidden, Patti-a strumpet-brought forth only daughters.
Though frequently forbidden, Matti-a fool-eats earth.
He who is of few words is a pundit.

column2: சொல்லிச் செய்வார் சிறியோர்; சொல்லாமற் செய்வார் பெரியோர், சொல்லியும் செய்யார் கயவர்.
column3: Inferiors may keep their word, the great do a thing without promising, but the wicked act not even after making a promise.

column2: சொல்லிக் கொடுத்த சொல்லும் கட்டிக் கொடுத்த சோறும் எதுவரையில்?
column3: How long will the words put into one’s mouth, and the rice tied up for a journey avail us?
Saying one thing, and doing another.

column2: சொல்லுக்கும் பொருளுக்கும் எட்டாதான் சோதிக்கும் சாதிக்கும் நடுவானான்.
column3: He whom neither language nor wealth can influence, has come between God and man.
A word uttered may bring wealth as well as ruin.
Learning is conversant with words and things.
It is difficult to overcome the eloquent.

column2: சொல்வளம் இல்லா நற்கதை சொல்லில் அதுவே துர்க்கதை.
column3: A good story badly told, soon loses its effect.

column2: சொல்வது யார்க்கும் எளிது, அரியவாம் சொல்லிய வண்ணம் செயல்.
column3: Saying is easy, but doing is difficult.
It is easy to profess, but difficult to perform, is it not?
No matter what others say, have not those who hear, sense to judge for themselves?

column2: சொறிந்து தேயாத எண்ணையும் பரிந்து இடாத சோறும் பாழ்.
column3: Oil applied without rubbing the head, and boiled rice given with ill-will, are useless.
It is said that even toads go a hunting.
He has no sense, he will not listen to advice.
It becomes the learned to explain the force of words.
The parrot will utter what it is taught,
Say what you are told, you senseless, deformed wretch.
Neglecting what you were told to do, you pull up the beans.

column2: சொன்னபடி கேட்காவிட்டால் மண்ணை வெட்டி மாப் படைப்பேன்.
column3: If you do not do as I say, I will make an offering of earth.Addressed to a demon when driving him out.
It is shameful to tell it, and painful to weep over it.
As regards rice, he is famishing, but it would be a fault to say so.

column2: சோம்பல் இல்லாத் தொழில் சோதனை இல்லாத் துணை.
column3: Untiring service is reliable help.

column2: சோம்பலுக்குத் தொடர்ச்சி வறுமை; சும்மா இருத்தலுக்குத் தொடர்ச்சி மூடம்.
column3: Indolence leads to poverty, inaction to ignorance.
Indolence is the parent of want.
The sluggard eats his plantain, skin and all.
Is it the realm of Chola, or the realm of deceit?
Is it after snatching away his bag one gives alms to a beggar?
What can rice effect? whatever you like.

column2: சோறு சிந்தினால் பொறுக்கலாம்; சுணை சிந்தினால் பொறுக்கலாமா?
column3: If rice be spilt it may be picked up, but if one loses his sense of honour can he recover that?

column2: சோறு சிந்தினால் பொறுக்கலாம் நீர் சிந்தினால் பொறுக்கலாமா?
column3: If rice be spilt it may be picked up, can water?

column2: சோற்றில் இருக்கிற கல் எடுக்கமாட்டாதவன் முகனைக்கல் எடுப்பானா?
column3: Can he who would not pick a stone out of the rice, lift up the stone lintel of a temple gateway?

column2: சோற்றில் இருந்த கல் எடாதவன் சேற்றில் கிடக்கிற எருமையைத் தூக்குவானா?
column3: Can he who will not pick a stone out of his rice, lift a buffalo out of the mud?

column2: சோற்றுக்கு வீங்கின நாயே, மாட்டுப் பொங்கலுக்கு வாயே.
column3: Thou dog, greedy of boiled rice, come to the January ox-festival.

column2: சோற்றுக்கு இல்லாத பூசணிக்காய் பந்தலிலே கட்டி ஆட்டவோ?
column3: Of what use is a pumpkin which will not be available for food, is it to be suspended to a pandal swing?
The blind that came drawn by the smell of rice.
He is a waste of rice, he is a burden to the earth.
What, to cut one’s throat after giving rice?
Boast not of your strength.

column2: ஞாபகம் இல்லை என்று எவனும் சொல்வான், ஞானம் இல்லை என்று எவனும் சொல்லான்.
column3: Any may say I have forgotten, none says I have no sense.
Teachers without moral rules are vain.
Wisdom and learning are contained in a measure of rice. -

column2: ஞானம் எல்லாம் ஒரு மூட்டை; உலகம் எல்லாம் ஒரு கோட்டை.
column3: Collective wisdom is a bundle, and the whole world a fort.

column2: ஞானம் இல்லாச் சேயர்கள் ஆவின் கன்றிலும் அதிகமல்ல.
column3: Ignorant children, are not better than calves.
Wisdom gives wealth and honour.
The wise are not affected by pleasure and pain.
A wise man and a fool do not associate.
Will they fall headlong into the well because their father dug it?
What was given to the father, will be entailed on his first-born.

column2: தகப்பனுக்குக் கட்டக் கோவணத்துக்கு வழி இல்லை, மகன் தஞ்சாவூர் மட்டும் நடைப்பாவாடை போடச் சொன்னானாம்.
column3: Whilst the father is without waist-cloth, his son, it is said, asked him to spread cloth on the ground to walk on as far as Tanjore.

column2: தகப்பனைக் கொன்ற பாவம் மாமியார் வீட்டில் ஆறு மாதம் இருந்தால் போகும்.
column3: The murder of a father may be expiated by residing for six months in the house of one’s mother-in-law.The Hindus account it disgraceful in a son-in-law to live in the house of a mother-in-law at her expense. Bhárata when enumerating the various sins he would be guilty of, if he coveted the throne of his elder brother Ráma, mentions this as one.
The title of the father descends to the son, does it not?

column2: தகப்பன் தேடக் கர்த்தன், பிள்ளை அழிக்கக் கர்த்தன்.
column3: The father acquires wealth, the son destroys it.

column2: தகப்பன் பட்டத்தைப் பிள்ளைக்குக் கட்டினால் தகப்பன் சாஷ்டாங்கதண்டம் செய்யவேண்டும் அல்லவோ?
column3: If the title of the father descend to the son, the father must prostrate himself before him, must he not?
So live as to be reputed a deserving man.

column2: தங்கமுடி சூட்டினாலும் தங்கள் குணம் விடார் கசடர்.
column3: Though crowned with gold, the base will not abandon their natural dispositions.
May one cut himself with a knife because it is made of gold?

column2: தங்கத்தைக் குவிக்கிறேன் என்றாலும், தன் புத்தி விடுகிறது இல்லை.
column3: Though I promised to give a heap of gold, he persisted in his own ideas.
Though gold-dust is procurable, brick-dust is not.
As fine gold was melted and poured out.
Gold scattered on the ground, bran in an earthen vessel.

column2: தங்கம் புடத்தில் வைக்கப்பட்டாலும் தன் நிறம் போகாது.
column3: Though gold is put into the fire to be refined, its hue is not lost.

column2: தங்கம் தரையிலே, ஒரு காசு நாரத்தங்காய் உறியிலே.
column3: Gold scattered on the ground, and a cash-worth of lemons placed on the swinging tray.
To exchange all his gold for bran.

column2: தங்கைச்சி பிள்ளை தன் பிள்ளை ஆனால் தவத்துக்குப் போவான் ஏன்?
column3: If the child of his sister be his, why go to perform penance?
The knife should be applied where there is flesh.

column2: தச்சன் பெண்சாதி தரையிலே, கொல்லன் பெண்சாதி கொம்பிலே.
column3: The wife of the carpenter is on the floor, and the wife of the smith is on the branch of a tree.

column2: தச்சன் பெண்சாதி அறுத்தால் என்ன, கொல்லன் பெண்சாதி கூலிக்கு அறுத்தால் என்ன?
column3: What if the wife of the carpenter is bereaved of her husband? what if the wife of the smith is deprived of her hire?

column2: தச்சன் வீட்டிலே தயிரும் எச்சன் வீட்டிலே சோறும் எப்படிச்சேரும்?
column3: Will curds be found in the house of a carpenter, or boiled rice in that of a niggard?
O! Vellala do not long for the rice and milk in a carpenter’s house.

column2: தச்சன் அடித்த தலைவாசல் எல்லாம் உச்சியில் இடிக்க உலாவித் திரிந்தேன்.
column3: I walked about in all the entrances where the carpenter he had put up lintels, knocking my head against them.
To cheat one that has come for protection is bad.

column2: தடவிப் பிடிக்க மயிர் இல்லை அவள் பெயர் கூந்தல் அழகி.
column3: Not a hair to be felt; yet her name is the tressed beauty.

column2: தடவிப் பிடிக்க கை இல்லை அவன் பெயர் சௌரியப் பெருமாள்.
column3: He has no hand to feel and seize any thing, his name is the notoriously strong.
All are huntsmen who take up sticks.
A water pot too strong to be broken by a stick.
Like a monkey dancing for fear of the stick.
If the pot is too strong for the stick, what can be done?
A goldsmith will pilfer the gold-dust even of his mother.

column2: தட்டானும் செட்டியும் கண் சட்டியும் பானையும் மண்.
column3: The goldsmith and the Chetty have each two eyes, the chatty and the pot are of earth.
Like the goldsmith and the merchant.
The fool-hardy who associated with the goldsmith.

column2: தட்டான் தாழப் பறந்தால் தப்பாமல் மழை வரும்.
column3: If a butterfly flies low, it is sure to rain.

column2: தட்டாரச் சித்து தறிச்சித்து வண்ணாரச் சித்துக்கு வராது.
column3: The tricks of a goldsmith and of a weaver, are not equal to those of a washerman.
It is either in the possession of the goldsmith, or in his vessel.

column2: தட்டிப் பேசுவார் இல்லாவிட்டால் தம்பி சண்டப் பிரசண்டன்.
column3: When there is none to contradict tambi is very fierce.Tambi, a younger brother, is often used in a friendly way, when addressing a junior.
It is the bent bow that will shoot.
When you go into the army, one of two-life or death.
Double remuneration to those who go into the army.

column2: தண்டு ஒன்று இலை மூன்று ஆரை அடா மந்திரி.
column3: The arai plant, O minister, has one stalk and three leaves.

column2: தண்ணீரில் இருக்கிற தவளை குடித்ததைக் கண்டதார் குடியாததைக் கண்டதார்?
column3: Who knows whether the frog in the water drinks, or that it does not drink?
Although cold water be heated, it will quench fire.
Salt produced from water, must be dissolved in water.

column2: தண்ணீரிலே அமுங்கின முட்டை உப்புப் போடக் கிளம்பும்.
column3: An egg submerged in water will float, if salt be put upon it.
Even water will forgive a fault three times.Referring to a person who rises to the surface three times after having fallen into deep water.

column2: தண்ணீர் குடித்த வயிறும் தென்னோலை இட்ட காதும் சரி.
column3: The belly that has taken in water, and the aperture in the ear that has received a circlet of ola, are alike-each enlarges as pressed.
May one abuse water, or one’s mother?
He says that the frog will bite him, if he descends into the water.

column2: தண்ணீரில் இருக்கிற தவளையைத் தரையில் எடுத்து விட்டதுபோல.
column3: Like turning out, on dry land, a frog that lives in the water.
The number drowned in alcohol, is in excess of those drowned in water.
Water and anger seek low-persons or places.
He is an ascetic who understands the philosophy of nature.
There is no mantra superior to one’s father’s advice.
Put the illegitimate in salt.

column2: தப்பை அடித்தவன் தாதன், சங்கு ஊதினவன் ஆண்டி.
column3: A Vaishnava mendicant beats a drum, and a Saiva mendicant blows a conch.
In learning two, in austerities one.

column2: தம் இனம் தம்மைக் காக்கும் வேலி பயிரைக் காக்கும்.
column3: One’s own kin will preserve one, a hedge will guard a crop.
My younger brother is on foot, and his talk is in a palanquin.See proverb 3559.
The sayings of the young man must be written on water.
The hare my younger brother caught, has only three legs.
The water my younger brother drew, is like a sea.
My younger brother can plough, but he cannot reach the handle of the plough.
As soon as my younger brother was born, the house was levelled to the earth.

column2: தம்பி சமர்த்தன் உப்பில்லாக் கஞ்சி கலம் குடிப்பான்.
column3: My younger brother is very clever, he can drink a kalam of kanji without salt.
My brother is like a whirlwind as a rice consumer, but very tardy at work.

column2: தயிருக்குச் சட்டி ஆதாரம் சட்டிக்குத் தயிர் ஆதாரம்.
column3: The chatty supports the curds, and the curds the chatty.

column2: தயிர்ப் பானையை உடைத்துக் காகத்துக்கு அமுது இட்டாற்போல.
column3: Like breaking the vessel that contained the curds, and feeding the crows.

column2: தரத்தர வாங்கிக் கொள்ளுகிறாயா, தலையை முழுகிப்போட்டு போகட்டுமா?
column3: Will you receive by little and little what I owe, or shall I bathe my head and begone?
Vice will not conquer virtue.
Charity preserves the head, and the head the hair.
Charity guards the head.

column2: தலை அளவும் வேண்டாம் அடி அளவும் வேண்டாம் குறுக்கே அள அடா படியை.
column3: The measure of the head or foot is not wanted, you fellow, measure the cloth across.

column2: தலை இருக்கிற இடத்தில் கழுத்து வரட்டும் பார்த்து கொள்வோம்.
column3: We will attend to the matter when the neck takes the place of the head.
Will the tail wag as long as the head exists?

column2: தலை இடியும் காய்ச்சலும் தனக்கு வந்தால் தெரியும்.
column3: When overcome by them, one learns what head-ache and fever are.

column2: தலை இடியும் சங்கடமும் தனக்கு வந்தபின் தெரியும்.
column3: Head-ache and trouble are understood when actually experienced.
Can you efface the letters of destiny, written on the skull, by shaving the head?
They call the writing on the head, Ari-Vishnu-it is not that.

column2: தலை கீழாய் இருந்து தபசு செய்தாலும் கூடுகிற காலந்தான் வந்து கூட வேண்டும்.
column3: Though one perform severe austerities, standing on the head, a thing cannot be attained before the destined time.

column2: தலைக்கு மேற்பட்ட தண்ணீர் சாண் ஓடி என்ன முழம் ஓடி என்ன?
column3: When the water flows over your head, what matters it whether it is a span or a cubit?In either case death is inevitable without help.

column2: தலைக்குத் தலை பெரியதனம் உலைக்கு அரிசி இல்லை.
column3: One is greater than the other, there is no rice to cook.
Each aims to be the head.
Is it the head or the foot that is crowned?
If it ascend to the head, he will feel it.
You yourself have procured a firebrand to scratch your head.
The mother of a second child is midwife to the mother of a first child.

column2: தலைச்சன் பிள்ளைக்காரி இடைச்சன் பிள்ளைக்காரிக்குப் புத்தி சொன்னாளாம்.
column3: It is said that the mother of a first child, gave advice to the mother of a second child.
You yourself have procured a firebrand for the head of your bed.

column2: தலைமுறை தலைமுறையாய் மொட்டை, அவள் பெயர் கூந்தலழகி.
column3: Bald from generation to generation, and yet called the matron of beautiful tresses.

column2: தலை மேலே அம்பு பறந்தாலும் நிலையிற் பிரியல் ஆகாது.
column3: It is not good to move when arrows are flying, though overhead.
Does a head exist above the head?
Though his head strikes the lintel, he will not stoop.
He can split a mountain with a blow of his head.

When destiny is written on the skull, can you avert it by artifice?Writing on the head is another form of expression for destiny or fate, which is supposed to be written on the skull of every individual born into the world. Hence the Sanscrit proverb, Kapála, Kapála, Kapála múla. "The skull, the skull, the skull is the root of all." Kapála in Sanscrit means skull. The mundane history of all is determined by the actions of a former state of existence. This is one of the most prevalent doctrines of the Hindus. A man eats the fruit of his doings, not in our sense as the same individuated being, but as the same responsible agent who has previously been on the stage.

column2: “Actions done, whether good or bad, must of necessity be eaten; if not, they will not wear away in millions of ages.” Smriti.
column3: The preordinations of Brahma, as supposed to be written on the skull, are regulated by the actions of former births. An elaborate shaster on this subject was published in Calcutta upwards of forty years ago by a Native gentleman, a Rajah of great learning.
When the head is crazed, all is wrong.
He will learn to stoop when the lintel strikes his head.
