column2: தலையும் தலையும் பொருதினால் மலையும் வந்து பொறுக்கும்.
column3: When one head fights with another, the mountains will come to bear them up.

column2: தலையை நனைத்து ஆச்சுது, கத்தியும் வைத்து ஆச்சுது.
column3: The head has been already wet, the razor has been already applied.
That which would have gone with the head has gone with the turban.

column2: தலைவன் நிற்கத் தண்டு நிற்கும், தலைவன் மயங்கச் சர்வமும் மயங்கும்.
column3: While the general is firm his army is so, but if the chief is perplexed all are so.

column2: தலை வலிக்குத் தலை அணையைத் தானே மாற்றிப் போட்டாற்போல.
column3: As if one changed his pillow to cure head-ache.
Obey your superior, deviate not from the path of rectitude.
May the head be severed and put on the sea?
Austerity is an ornament, humility is honourable.

column2: தவளை தாமரைக்குச் சமீபமாக இருந்தும் அதின் தேனை உண்ணாது. அதுபோல, அறிவீனர் கிட்ட இருந்தாலும் கற்றுக் கொள்ளார்கள்.
column3: The toad living near the lotus drinks not its honey, the illiterate though they live near the learned remain ignorant.
The frog perishes by its own mouth.
Like requesting one who eats bran to feed on ambrosia.
Like telling one who is eating bran to blow a trumpet.

column2: தவிட்டை நம்பிப் போகச் சம்பா அரிசியை நாய் கொண்டு போயிற்று.
column3: When she went out in expectation of fetching bran, her samba rice was carried off by a dog.
The hand that is ready to steal bran will be ready to steal money also.

column2: தழைத்த மரத்துக்கு நிழல் உண்டு, பிள்ளை பெற்றவளுக்குப் பால் உண்டு.
column3: A flourishing tree has shade, a woman who has recently borne a child, has milk.

column2: தள்ளத் தள்ளத் தாழ்ப்பாளை, மெள்ள மெள்ள திறப்பான் ஏன்?
column3: Why open it gently again and again, when I am trying to bolt the door?

column2: தனக்குப் பிறந்தபிள்ளை தவிட்டுக்கு அழுகிறதாம் ஊரார் பிள்ளைக்குக் கூட்டுக் கலியாணம் செய்கிறானாம்.
column3: While his own child cries for bran, he is conducting the nuptials of his neighbour’s child.
When uncertain regarding his means, he promises a double allowance to his betel-pouch bearer.

column2: தனக்கு அழகு மொட்டை பிறர்க்கு அழகு கொண்டை.
column3: Touching one’s own comfort a bald head, in respect to others luxuriant hair, is preferable.
That which is left belongs to charity.

column2: தனக்கு என்னவென்று இருக்கல் ஆகாது நாய்க்குச் சோறு இல்லையாயின்.
column3: One must not be unconcerned, when there is no rice for his dog.
If one speaks to a dog, it will jump expecting to get something.

column2: தனக்கு மூக்குப் போனாலும் எதிரிக்குச் சகுனப்பிழை வேண்டும்.
column3: He is so intent on producing an ominous sign against his enemy that he is ready to give up his own nose.He is willing to cut his own nose to spite an enemy: to meet one without a nose is a bad omen.
If his own crop, even a child may weed it.

column2: தனக்குத் தகாத காரியத்திற் பிரவேசிக்கிறவன் குரங்கு பட்டபாடு படுவான்.
column3: He who undertakes a matter not suited to him, will suffer as did the monkey.

column2: தனக்கு என்றால் புழுக்கை கலம் கழுவி உண்ணாது.
column3: If for his own use a slave will not wash his plate before eating.

column2: தனக்குத் தகாத காரியம் செய்தால் ஆருக்கும் பிராண சேதத்துக்கு வரும்.
column3: Whoever undertakes an affair unsuited to him will endanger his life.

column2: தனக்கு ஆகாத பானை உடைந்தால் என்ன இருந்தால் என்ன?
column3: What matters it whether an earthen vessel not required is broken or not?

column2: தனக்குப் பின்னால் அகம் இருந்து என்ன கவிழ்ந்து என்ன?
column3: What matters it whether one’s house stands or falls after his death?

column2: தனக்கு என்று ஒருத்தி இருந்தால் தலைமாட்டில் இருந்து அழுவாள்.
column3: If he has a wife she will weep at the bed’s head-at his funeral.

column2: தனக்குத் தவிடு இடிக்கத் தள்ளாது ஊருக்கு இரும்பு அடிக்கத் தள்ளும்.
column3: He is unable to pound rice for himself, but he can beat iron for his neighbours.

column2: தனக்கு என்று அடுப்பு மூட்டித் தான் வாழும் காலத்தில் வயிறும் சிறுக்கும் மதியும் பெருகும்.
column3: When the time comes for one to make his own fire and prepare his meals, then his stomach will become small and the judgment developed.
Do not regard her as your wife who goes out unattended.

column2: தன் இச்சையை அடக்காவிட்டால் அது தன்னையே வருத்தும்.
column3: He who does not control his desires will thereby come to grief.
Feel for others as you feel for yourself.
He himself consumes his own life.

column2: தன் ஊருக்கு அன்னம் பிற ஊருக்குக் காகம்.
column3: A swan in his own village, a crow in the next.

column2: தன் ஊர்ச் சுடுகாட்டுக்கும் அயல் ஊர் ஆற்றுக்கும் அஞ்ச வேண்டும்.
column3: In his own village one should fear the place of incremation, in an adjacent country, a river.

column2: தன் ஊருக்கு ஆனை அயல் ஊருக்குப் பூனை.
column3: At home an elephant, abroad a cat.

column2: தன் ஊருக்குக் காளை அயல் ஊருக்குப் பூனை.
column3: A bullock at home, a cat abroad.

column2: தன் காரியதுரந்தான் பிறர் காரியம் வழவழவென்று விடுகிறவன்.
column3: He who attends to his own affairs is indifferent about the things of others.

column2: தன் காரியம் என்றால் தன் சிலையும் பதைக்கும்.
column3: If it is his own concern, even the folds of his garment will flutter.

column2: தன் காரியம் பாராதவன் சதைக்கு ஒரு புழு புழுப்பான்.
column3: He who does not attend to his own affairs, will be tormented by worms at every pore.
In his own affairs resolute as a tiger.
Abandon her who is careless of her own reputation.
No one on earth sees his own faults.

column2: தன் குற்றம் இருக்கப் பிறர் குற்றம் பார்க்கிறதா?
column3: Do you condemn others when guilty yourself?

column2: தன் குஞ்சென்று வளர்க்குமாம் குயிற் குஞ்சைக் காகம்.
column3: It is said that the crow nourishes the young of a cuckoo, under the impression that it is her own.

column2: தன் சோறு தின்று தன் புடைவை கட்டி வீண் சொல் கேட்க விதியா?
column3: Am I destined to be unlawfully abused while eating my own rice and wearing my own cloth?

column2: தன் நிலத்தில் இருந்தால் முயல் தந்தியிலும் வலிது.
column3: When in his own place a hare is stronger than a tusker-an elephant.
The remedy of his disease is with himself.
Having ascertained your own ability, display it in the assembly.

column2: தன் பணம் செல்லா விட்டால் தாதனைக் கட்டி அடிக்கிறது.
column3: When his own coin will not pass, he ties up and beats the Vaishnava mendicant.

column2: தன் பல்லைப் பிடுங்கிப் பிறர் வாயில் வைக்கலாமா?
column3: Can one take out his own teeth, and put them in another's mouth?

column2: தன் பல்லைக் குத்திப் பிறர் மூக்கில் வாசம் காட்டுகிறதுபோல.
column3: Like picking the teeth and holding the tooth-pick to the nose of another.

column2: தன் பிள்ளைக்குப் பதைக்காதவன் சக்களத்தி பிள்ளைக்குப் பதைப்பாளா?
column3: Can she who does not feel for her own child, feel for the child of her rival?

column2: தன் பிள்ளையைத் தான் அடிக்கத் தலையாரியைச் சீட்டுக் கேட்கிறதுபோல.
column3: Like applying to the village officer for permission to punish his own child.
His own mouth ruined him.
It is said that the tortoise perished by its own mouth.
The presence of the goddess of prosperity, will be evinced in the speech.

column2: தன் வினை தன்னைச் சுடும் ஓட்டப்பம் வீட்டைச் சுடும்.
column3: His own actions will burn him, a false cake will burn the house.

column2: தன் வீட்டுக் கதவைப் பிடுங்கி அயல் வீட்டுக்கு வைத்தாற்போல.
column3: As if one took down his door, and placed it in the house of his neighbour.

column2: தன் வீட்டு விளக்கென்று முத்தம் இட்டாற் சுடாதா?
column3: If one kiss his lamp because it belongs to his own house, will it therefore not burn him?

column2: தன் வீடு தவிர அயல் வீட்டுக்கு மேட்டுவரி என்கிறான்.
column3: He asserts that all houses are taxed but his own.

column2: தன் வீட்டுக் கதவைப் பிடுங்கி அயல் வீட்டுக்கு வைத்து விட்டு விடிகிற மட்டும் நாய் ஓட்டினதுபோல.
column3: Like driving away dogs till break of day, because he had lent his door to a neighbour.

column2: தன்னாலே தான் கெட்டால் அண்ணாவியார் என்ன செய்வார்?
column3: If a disciple is intent on his own ruin, what can his master do?
There is no artificer who does not praise himself.
He who has studied himself is his own master.
No excitement will make one forget himself.
He who knows himself may know his maker.
He who would keep himself must restrain anger.
You may kill even a cow that aims to kill you.
He knows not who laugh at him.

column2: தன்னைப் பெற்றவள் கொடும் பாவி, பெண்ணைப் பெற்றவள் மகராசி.
column3: Despising his own mother as worthless, whilst holding his mother-in-law in high estimation.
Know yourself before you begin to speak of others.
It is said that the monkey of Palaveram does not know that he is laughed at.

column2: தன்னைத் தானே தான் பழிக்குமாம் தென்னமரத்திலே குரங்கு இருந்து.
column3: It is said that the monkey that mocks at the top of the cocoanut tree mocks itself.
He mounts on the head by reason of continued indulgence.
The tabret of the Vaishnava mendicant is like cast-iron.
Can you wear a garland round your beard?

column2: தாடி பற்றி எரியும்போது சுருட்டுப் பற்றவைக்க நெருப்புக் கேட்டதுபோல.
column3: As one asked for fire to light his cigar when his beard was on fire.
Kindness leads to loss of wealth.

column2: தாட்டோட்டக்காரனுக்குத் தயிரும் சோறும், விசுவாசக்காரனுக்கு வெந்நீரும் பருக்கையும்.
column3: The deceitful feasts on rice and curds, while the faithful feeds on warm rice and water.
It is better to be alone than to associate with the fraudulent.

column2: தாட்டோட்டக்காரனுக்குத் தயிரும் சோறும் கூட்டாட்டக்காரனுக்குக் கூழும் தண்ணீரும்.
column3: The fraudulent enjoys curds and rice, while the honest man gets only gruel and water.
Like a Vaishnava mendicant and a pariah.
He moves about like a monkey in the hands of a juggler.
He who does not know how to feel one’s pulse is an empiric.
No cause, no complaint.
As a tender creeper without a prop.
Delay will lead to ruin.
He trembles like a drop of water on a lotus leaf.
The cord is rotten and the water pot is fractured.

column2: தாயும் தகப்பனும் தள்ளிவிட்ட காலத்தில் வா என்று அழைத்த வங்காரவாசி.
column3: A herb that welcomed a child cast off by its parents.

column2: தாயும் பிள்ளையும் ஆனாலும் வாயும் வயிறும் வேறே.
column3: Though they are mother and child their mouths and bellies are diverse.
The prosecutor of a matricide is in his own village.

column2: தாயைச் சேர்ந்த உறவு ஆனாலும் அறுத்துத்தான் உறவு ஆட வேண்டும்.
column3: Though related on the side of one’s mother he must be treated as a relation after thoroughly ascertaining his connections.

column2: தாயைப்பார்த்துப் பெண்ணைக் கொள்ளு பாலைப் பார்த்து பசுவைக் கொள்ளு.
column3: Before taking a woman in marriage ascertain the character of her mother, before buying a cow ascertain the quantity of its milk.
As is the mother such is the child, as is the yarn such is the cloth.
The daughter reproached her mother and went astray.
Look at the mother before you take her daughter in marriage.
Is there any conception unknown to the mother?
Is it right to strike a motherless child on the head?
A mother’s attachment, a dog’s attachment.

column2: தாய் ஏழடி பாய்ந்தால் மகள் எட்டு அடி பாய்வாள்.
column3: If the mother leaps seven feet, the daughter leaps eight.

column2: தாய் ஒரு பாக்கு, தான் கமுகந்தோப்பு என்கிறாள்.
column3: She says that her mother is only a nut, while she herself is a grove of arica-nut trees.

column2: தாய் கேட்டுப்பட்டி தகப்பன் காவடிப்பட்டி தங்கை மோருப்பட்டி தமக்கை சாதப்பட்டி.
column3: The mother is wicked, the father vain, the younger sister a consumer of butter milk, and the elder sister a consumer of rice.

column2: தாய் கையில் இருக்கிற தனத்தைப் பார்க்கிலும் தன் கைத் தவிடே மேல்.
column3: The bran in one’s own hand is preferable to the wealth in the hand of his mother.
That which is the mother’s, is the daughter's.This may refer to prosperity, temper, &c.
Next to one’s mother, is his wife.
Can the conception of an unmarried daughter be concealed from her mother?
It is not enough for one’s mother’s field to bear a crop, one’s own field must also be fruitful.
Does one acquire fame in a country because he feeds his mother?
He carries a torch at his father’s second marriage where the bride is younger than himself.

column2: தாய் செத்தால் மணம் மகள் செத்தால் பிணம்.
column3: If the mother die, a marriage, if the daughter die, a corpse.The wife is here called mother with reference to her children. In the event of her death the husband may marry again.
The mother is dead, the daughter is destitute.
When one rejects the advice of his mother, no precept can reform him.

column2: தாய் தவிட்டுக்கு அழுகையில் பிள்ளை இஞ்சிப் பச்சடி கேட்கின்றது.
column3: While the mother is crying for bran, the child is crying for ginger chutny.

column2: தாய்தனக்கு ஆகாத மகள் ஆர்தனக்கு நல்லவள் ஆவாள்?
column3: Who will approve of a daughter that is undutiful to her own mother?

column2: தாய் தூற்றினால் ஊர் தூற்றும், கொண்டவன் தூற்றினால் கண்டவன் தூற்றுவான்.
column3: If a mother should spread evil reports, the village also will do so; if a husband should defame his wife, a stranger will.

column2: தாய் முகம் காணாத பிள்ளையும் மழை முகம் காணாத பயிரும் செவ்வைப்படமாட்டாது.
column3: The child that has not seen the face of its mother, and the growing crop that has not seen rain, will not do well.
As one complained that his mother’s milk was insipid.

column2: தாய் வார்த்தை கேளாத பிள்ளை நாய் வாயிற் சீலை.
column3: A child disobedient to her mother, is like a cloth in the mouth of a dog.
A wife and a Guru are preordained.
Like a duck.

column2: தாராளந்தான் நெய், தண்ணீர் பந்தலில், நீர்ச்சோற்றுத் தண்ணீர் நெய்பட்ட பாடு.
column3: Ghee is plentiful in his feasts, while cold rice water in the charity pandal is as scarce as ghee.
Of what use is the favour of a midwife to a widow?

column2: தாலி அறுத்தவள் ஏன் இருக்கிறாள்? தாரம் தப்பினவனுக்குப் பொங்கியிட.
column3: Why does the widow survive her husband? that she may cook rice for a widower.
He languishes like a tender creeper without a prop.
Pot-bellied and needle-throated.

column2: தாழ் குலத்திற் பிறந்தாலும் புத்தியினால் அலரிப்பூவைப்போல் பிரயோசனப்படுவர்.
column3: Though low-born, they may by their good sense prove useful as an oleander flower.
If humble thou shalt prosper.
Gold has become low in estimation, and brass high.
The humble and obedient shall rise to eminence.
An unsubmissive youth is useless.
In adversity, manly bearing; in prosperity, humility.
He is the wealthiest in the place, and possesses all in abundance.
Desire to be charitable.
When the goddess of prosperity unsought visits you, is it right to kick her out?
He will neither eat himself, nor give to others.

column2: தானே கனியாத பழத்தைத் தடிகொண்டு அடித்தால் கனியுமோ?
column3: Can you mature a fruit by beating it with a stick when it does not ripen of itself?

column2: தானே தான் குருக்கள் என்பார் தனங்கள் வாங்கச் சதாசிவன் பேர் பூசை செய்வார்.
column3: To get money they call themselves gurus, and perform pujas in honour of Sadasiva.
He has grown great and distinguished by his penance.
She herself desires to prosper, and wishes that her first-born daughter may become a widow.
If he himself be under restraint, his race will be so.

column2: தான் அறியாச் சிங்களம் தன் பிடரிக்குச் சேதம்.
column3: An attempt to speak a language not understood may lead to one’s own hurt.
While one lives, the world subserves him.

column2: தான் ஒன்று நினைத்தால் தெய்வம் ஒன்று நினைக்கும்.
column3: If he thinks one thing, Deity thinks another.
Himself a thief, he trusts not others.
What you have learnt, teach to others impressively.

column2: தான் கெடுத்தது பாதி, தம்பிரான் கெடுத்தது பாதி.
column3: Half was spoiled by himself, and half by his superior.

column2: தான் சம்பாதித்தால் தனக்கு உதவும், ஊர் சம்பாதித்தால் உதவமாட்டாது.
column3: That which one acquires will be available, that which the country acquires will not be so.
No one takes medicine with a view to death.

column2: நான் செத்தபின் உலகம் கவிழ்ந்து என்ன நிமிர்ந்து என்ன?
column3: When one is dead, what matters it whether the world be overturned or not?
His tripping is as a staff to others.
Being herself a thief, she trusts not her neighbours.
He who takes poison will destroy himself.

column2: தான் தின்னத் தவிடு இல்லை வாரத்துக்குப் பன்றிக் குட்டியா?
column3: He has no bran to eat, why seek a young pig to rear for hire?
In eating he has a monopoly.

column2: தான் தின்னி பிள்ளை வளர்க்காள், தவிடு தின்னி கோழி வளர்க்காள்.
column3: A gluttonous mother will not feed her child, nor will one who feeds on bran keep fowls.

column2: தான் திருட்டுக் கொடுத்ததும் அல்லாமல் பயித்தியக்காரப் பட்டமும் கேட்டான்.
column3: Besides suffering the loss of the property stolen, he acquires the title of a fool.

column2: தான் தேடாப் பொன்னுக்கு மாற்றும் இல்லை உரையும் இல்லை.
column3: Gold not acquired by one's own exertions, has neither standard nor touch.

column2: தான் பத்தினி ஆனால் தேவடியாள் தெருவிலும் குடி இருக்கலாம்.
column3: If a woman be chaste she may live in the street of the harlots.

column2: தான் பிடித்த முசலுக்கு மூன்றே கால் என்கிறான்.
column3: He says that the hare he caught had three legs.

column2: தான் போனால் தாகத்துக்குக் கிடையாது, எழுதடா ஓலை நூறு குடம் தயிருக்கு என்கிறான்.
column3: He says that if he go himself he cannot get water to quench his thirst, but if he sends written olas he can obtain a hundred pots of curds.

column2: தான் போகிற காரியத்துக்கு ஆட்போனால் ஒரு சொட்டு.
column3: Faults will happen if another be deputed to do one’s business.

column2: தான் போய் மோர் இல்லாமல் வந்தவன் தயிருக்குச் சீட்டு எழுதினானாம்.
column3: It is said that he who went in person and could not obtain even butter-milk, sent a written order for curds.

column2: தான் போக வழியைக் காணாத மூஞ்சூறு விளக்கு மாற்றையும் கல்விக்கொண்டு போனதாம்.
column3: It is said that a musk-rat that was not able to find its way out, carried away the broom.
As if Saturn entered into the tánri fruit-Terminalia Bellerica,
What has a naked mendicant to do with the friendship of a washerman?
He wanders about like one who has trodden on the tigai plant.A plant whose touch is supposed to create aberration of mind and numbness.
God is the help of the destitute.

column2: திக்கு விஜயம் கொள்பவனுக்கு ஜெயம் அபஜெயகாலம் தெரியாது.
column3: A successful conqueror does not regard times whether relating to victory or defeat.

Look south on Monday and Saturday if leaving home.The direction given in this proverb is intended to convey a caution to those setting out on a journey on Monday or Saturday. It is based on the belief contained in a stanza of which the following is a translation.Sangara, Siva, as the author of good, plants his trident on the earth in certain directions on particular days and at stated hours.On Monday and Saturday in the east for eight náligais.[1]On Thursday, in the south for twenty náligais.On Friday and Sunday in the west, twelve náligais.On Tuesday and Wednesday in the north, twelve náligais.
Preserve your self-possession in the presence of enemies.

column2: திண்ணைக்குத் தேள் கொட்டிற்று தண்ணீர் மிடாவுக்கு நெறிகட்டிற்று என்றாற்போல.
column3: As if one should say that the pial was stung by a scorpion, and the water pot standing on it became inflamed.
If the sun rise on the pial, it will rise on the house also.
The miserly will not give though threatened with immediate death.
Rats in large numbers do not burrow.
As far as twisted it is a rope, that not twisted is mere fibre.
Neither the triad nor the thirty-three crores of supernals, are comparable to him.
When there is wealth, there is power.
A screw is strong, old cow-dung breeds worms.
Even those who have seen the holy one are not exempt from evil.
Those that have seen the holy one experience no evil.
Does a marriage pandal require shading?
As a scorpion stung the thief.
The wife of the thief is always a widow.
The Deity is witness against the thief.

column2: திருட்டுப் பயலுக்குத் திரட்டுப்பாலும் சோறும் விசுவாசக்காரனுக்கு வெந்நீரும் பருக்கையும்.
column3: A rogue is fed with thick milk and rice, while the honest get nothing but warm water and rice.
Like tying a string of bells round the neck of a thievish dog.

column2: திருட்டுப் பயல் கலியாணத்தில் முடிச்ச அவிழ்க்கிற பயல் பெரியதனம்.
column3: At the marriage of a thief, the pickpocket is the chief guest.Literally, the thievish fellow who unties a knot. Money or other valuables being often carried in the corner of the cloth worn as a dress, or in the corner of a handkerchief.

column2: திருட்டுப் பூனைக்குப் போடு திரட்டுப் பாலும் சோறும்.
column3: Put thick milk and rice before a thievish cat.
A master whom it is hard to please is useless.
The festival is over, the bustle has ceased.
Do you go to celebrate the holy day, or for the sake of food?
Holy ashes will not adhere to a killachi-kái.
The correct utterance of mantras secures the divine favour.
Though taken to Tripati, a broom will fetch only a cash.

column2: திருப்பதியில் மொட்டை அடித்ததும் பற்றதா, ஶ்ரீரங்கத்தில் சிரிப்பாய்ச் சிரித்ததும் பற்றாதா ?
column3: Is it not enough to have been shaven bald at Tirupati and disgraced at Shrírangam?
Did you see the baldheaded Vaishnava mendicant at Tripati?
It has become an emerald discovered by the king’s jester.
Even a religious mendicant at the temple gate will be of some use at times.
Those who are unaffected by Tiruvasakam will not be moved by any other composition.
Do the words of the great admit of contradiction?

Demons dwell in the house that is not illumined by a sacred lamp.To smear a room with cow-dung, especially on Friday, and to keep a lamp burning through the night, are observances pleasing to the goddess of prosperity.

column2: திருவிளக்கு இட்டாரைத் தெய்வம் அறியும்,நெய்வார்த்து உண்டாரை நெஞ்சு அறியும்.
column3: The Deity knows those who place sacred lamps, and the mind knows who eats ghee and rice.
Acquire wealth though compelled to cross the stormy ocean.
Lies and tricks.
Why seek the key of an open door?
As if a dog entered an open house.
Daily fix your mind on divine things.
Scratching is agreeable where itching exists.
He whose skin itches will scratch.

column2: தினைப்பயறும் பாலும் தின்னாதிருந்தும், வினைப்பயனை வெல்லுவது அரிது.
column3: Though one may abstain from eating millet-pulse and milk, he cannot escape from the effect of his evil actions.

column2: தினை விதைத்தவன் தினை அறுப்பான் வினை விதைத்தவன் வினை அறுப்பான்.
column3: He who sows millet, reaps millet, he who sows evil deeds, must reap the same.
Though well fed he is as lean as a sloth.
What one eats is little, human life is uncertain.
Chogai jaundice proportioned to the earth eaten.It is the opinion of some that jaundice is occasioned by eating sand or earth, a thing not uncommon.

column2: தின்ற நஞ்சு கொல்லும் தின்னாத நஞ்சு கொல்லுமா?
column3: Poison taken kills; will the poison not taken, kill?
He who has grown fat will be inclined to mischief.
Does any one desire to chew his betel over again?
A mother who is nursing a baby has a good appetite.
Why eat, seeing that you know not how to eat with moderation
One who takes food in a house in which he ought not.
Act as one who warms himself-do not burn yourself.
As the wind assists fire.
The notes of the kuyil bird to the sugar-cane-bowed Cupid.
Is the sharpness of the sword to be tried on the whetting board?
He who abstains from evil deeds is God.
A torch lit at a lamp.
Is there a lack of charred wood in a house on fire?
A burn is curable, but a wound occasioned by slander is not.

column2: தீ மிஞ்ச வைத்தாலும் பகை மிஞ்ச வைக்கலாகாது.
column3: Though fire may be in excess, hatred may not.
He who approves evil is guilty of it.

column2: தீயாரைச் சேர்ந்து ஒழுகல் தீது, தீயார் பண் செய்யனவும் தீது.
column3: To associate with the wicked is bad, to serve the wicked is also bad.

column2: தீயோரை விடுதலை ஆக்குகிறவன் நல்லோருக்கு நஷ்டம் செய்வான்.
column3: He who liberates the wicked injures the innocent.
The thoroughly learned may become a religious guide.
Unrestrained anger will end in mischief.
Unrestrained anger ends in strife.
Continued uncertainty leads to war.
An unsettled affair is bad.
God himself is the witness of the unquiet mind.
God is the witness in an undecided cause.
An intricate case will never end satisfactorily.
A demon does evil to him who does evil to others.

column2: துக்கம் உள்ள மனதிற்குத் துன்பம் ஏன் வேறு?
column3: Why additional pain to a mind already in grief?
He that is addicted to sleep loses his appetite.
Unsympathizing wives are like fire in the bosom.
Why should the hand be burnt when there is a ladle?
Like tying a silk tassel to a broomstick.
To the wicked, punishment, to the good, protection.
The people of the village will reprove a wicked child.

column2: துட்டுக்கு ஒரு குட்டி விற்றாலும் துலுக்கக்குட்டி மாத்திரம் ஆகாது.
column3: Though girls may be had at a pie a head, a Muhammadan girl is undesirable.

column2: துணிகிறவளுக்கு வெட்கம் இல்லை, அழுகிறவளுக்குத் துக்கம் இல்லை.
column3: An impudent woman has no shame, a weeping woman has no sorrow.

column2: துணிந்தார்க்குத் துக்கம் உண்டா? பணிந்தார்க்குப் பாடு உண்டா?
column3: Do the adventurous experience grief, or the humble, distress?

column2: துணிந்தவனுக்குத் துக்கம் இல்லை, அழுதவனுக்கு அகங்காரம் இல்லை.
column3: The adventurous know not sorrow, the sorrowful know not anger.
Though you may bear one company, do not become his security.
Do not go on a journey without a companion.
As a cannon was born of a musket.
The wicked will be abandoned.

column2: துயரப்பட்டால் ஆறுதல் உண்டு, துன்பப்பட்டால் தேறுதல் உண்டு.
column3: Grief leads to comfort, suffering to patience.

Like Saguni to the family of Duryodhana.Saguni was the maternal uncle of Duriyodhana, and had, like the latter, ninety-nine brothers. He imprisoned Saguni and his ninety-nine brothers. to prevent their combination against him, feeding them on gruel which was doled out from day to day. They all died of inanition excepting Saguni, who managed to secure sufficient nourishment to preserve his life. He afterwards became the counsellor of Duriyodhana and from sinister motives gave such advice as led to the ruin of his royal master, who lost his kingdom and also his ninety-nine brothers.

As the hanging-nest bird gave advice to the monkey.The story referred to is graphically told in, I think, Panchatantra. It is as follows: On a cold rainy day a monkey chilled by the inclemency of the weather approached a tree on the branches of which were hanging numerous nests of this ingenious bird. One of the inmates addressed his simian denizen of the forest expressing surprise that a creature endowed with limbs so well fitted for the purpose did not fabricate a suitable place of shelter. The monkey felt the reflection implied in the admonition, and unable to restrain his indignation, jumped into the tree and destroyed the residence of his innocent friend.In my journeys on foot in varions parts of this country I have frequently seen the nests of the pendulous bird. On a tree not many miles from Trincomalie on the eastern coast of Ceylon I once saw perhaps thirty or forty nests hanging from one tree not twenty feet high: they resembled large pears.

The opinion expressed in the proverb is also contained in a stanza of Nitinerivillakkam a poem on moral subjects, justly admired by the learned, for the terseness and beauty of its composition, as well as for the general purity of its moral sentiments. The poet says:—

column2: “When the goddess of prosperity finds that her favours are not appreciated, she introduces her elder sister, the goddess of adversity, to the sluggard, and then takes her departure.”
column3: The goddess of fortune, whose presence is a guarantee for happiness and prosperity, is said to dwell in the face of a horse of superior caste,-with the wise and good,-in all fragrant flowers,-in the tree of paradise that bestows whatever it’s votaries may desire,-in the beauty of a good and obedient wife,-in the ocean,-in the portal of a house when a marriage is being celebrated,-in a well governed country,-in grain,-in brilliant lamps and flaming torches,-in the words of the great,-with truth-speaking men,-in the arrows of Cupid,-and in vessels of milk.The goddess of misfortune is said to have her habitation with the glutton,-the irascible,-with liars and other abandoned characters,-with those clothed in rags,—with vicious women,-in a flock of sheep, and in the face of the dead.

column3: What if the jackal becomes fat, or the gall-nut ripen?
Is the young jackal to be trained to howl?

column2: நரிக்குப் பெரியதனம் கொடுத்தால் கிடைக்கு இரண்டு ஆடு கேட்கும்.
column3: If the jackal gains the mastery, he will demand two sheep from every flock.

column2: நரி நாலு கால் திருடன் இடையன் இரண்டு கால் திருடன்.
column3: The jackal is a quadraped thief, the shepherd is a biped thief.
As the crab made somersaults before a jackal.
Are viscera to be given into the hands of a jackal to be washed?
Like measuring the depth of the ocean with the tail of a jackal.

column2: நரை திரை இல்லை, நமனும் அங்கு இல்லை.
column3: He is not grey nor wrinkled; Yama is not there.
Evil communications will bring distress.
Friendship is stronger than close relationship.
A good man’s life may continue forty days.

column2: நல்ல எழுத்து நடுவே இருக்கக் கோணல் எழுத்துக் குறுக்கே போட்டது என்ன?
column3: A good letter-destiny-being in the middle, how were the crooked letters put in?

column2: நல்ல அமைச்சு இல்லாத அரசு விழி இன்றி வழிச்சொல்வோன் போலாம்.
column3: A king without a good counsellor, is like a wayfaring man who is blind.
When the good becomes bad, even a dog will not use it.

column2: நல்லதுக்கு ஒரு பொல்லாதது, பொல்லாததுக்கு ஒரு நல்லது.
column3: A good husband may have a bad wife, and a bad husband may have a good wife.
One who holds a fourth share in a prosperous family.

column2: நல்லது செய்து நடுவழியே போனால் பொல்லாது போகிறவழியே போகிறது.
column3: If you do good and walk in the middle of the road, the evil will find its own way.
Of the good only four kalams, of the bad nine kalams.

column2: நல்ல நாளையில் நாழிப் பால் கறவாதது கன்று செத்துக் கலப்பால் கறக்குமோ?
column3: In its best days the cow gave scarcely a measure of milk, will it yield a kalam after its calf is dead?This proverb may refer to the fact that milch, cows in this part of India and in Ceylon refuse to give their milk in the absence of the calf. The cow when going to a neighbouring house to be milked, is accompanied by its calf which is often muzzled like a dog. When the milkman is about to draw the milk he allows the calf to suck for a few moments and then tying it to the fore leg of the mother, he draws the milk, while she stands quiet licking her offspring. Cows do sometimes allow their milk to be taken when the calf is absent but it not infrequently happens that the cow refuses to give her milk, and the calf is fetched from the homestead to encourage the motherIf a calf die, its skin may be dried and stuffed. The cow deceived by the device licks the effigy of her calf and yields her milk.

column2: நல்ல நினைவை அனுசரித்தலே கெட்ட நினைவை நீக்கல்.
column3: Entertaining good thoughts, is in fact leaving evil thoughts.
As the earth-worm imitated the graceful movements of the cobra.

column2: நல்ல மரம் நச்சுக் கனியைத் தராது, நச்சு மரத்திலே நல்ல கனியும் வராது.
column3: A good tree yields not poisonous fruit, nor a poisonous tree good fruit.
Like a mistletoe growing on a good tree.

column2: நல்ல மாடு ஆனால் உள்ளூரில் விலை ஆகாதா?
column3: If the cow be a good one, will it not find a purchaser in its own village?

column2: நல்ல மாட்டிற்கு ஒரு அடி, நல்ல பெண்ணுக்கு ஒரு சொல்.
column3: A good bullock requires but one blow, and a good woman only one word.
Though it is before them the virtuous will not kill even a cobra.

column2: நல்லவன் என்ற பெயர் எடுக்க நாட் செல்லும்.
column3: Time must elapse before one can get a good name.

column2: நல்லவர்கள் ஒரு நாள் செய்த உபகாரத்தை மறவார்; அதுபோல, பனை ஒரு நாள் விதைத்துத் தண்ணீர் விட்டவனுக்குப் பலன் கொடுக்கும்.
column3: The good never forget a benefit; in like manner a palmyrah tree yields its produce to him who planted and watered it.

column2: நல்லவன் ஒருவன் நடுவே நின்றால் அறாத வழக்கும் அறும்.
column3: If there be but one good man present in a suit, the greatest difficulties will be solved.
He spoke conciliatory words, and touched my chin cringingly.
My good young calf, dont be frisky.
Domestic virtue is excellent.
He is a good servant, but the river carries him away.

column2: நல்ல உடலுக்கு இளைப்பாற்றி கொடாவிட்டாலும் நாவிற்குக் கொடு.
column3: Though you may not give rest to your body, give rest to your tongue.

column2: நல்லவர்கள் சங்காத்தம் நல்ல மணலில் விழுந்த நீர்போல உதவும்.
column3: The friendship of the good will prove useful like water falling on good soil.

column2: நல்லாரைக் கண்டால் நாய்போல, பொல்லாரைக் கண்டால் பூனைபோல.
column3: If he sees the good, a dog, if he sees the wicked, a cat.

column2: நல்லாரை நாவு அழியப் பேசினால் பல்லாலே பதக்குப் புழுச் சொரியும்.
column3: If one abuses his tongue by slandering the virtuous, maggots will drop from his teeth.

column2: நல்லாரும் நல்ல பாம்பைப்போலத் தங்கள் வலிமையை அடக்கி மறைத்திருப்பார் சில வேளை.
column3: The good, like the cobra, sometimes restrain their power and conceal themselves.

column2: நல்லாரை நாவில் உரை, பொன்னைக் கல்லில் உரை.
column3: Test the good by the words of the tongue, and gold on a touchstone.
The good and the bad may be known by their conduct.

column2: நல்லோர் ஒருவருக்குப் பெய்யும் மழை எல்லாருக்கும் ஆம்.
column3: The rain that falls on account of one virtuous person, is beneficial to all.
The acts of the virtuous are a terror to the wicked.
Grow up virtuous, observe not days.
A good disposition is the best treasure.

column2: நற்பெண்டீர் நல்லதைக் கண்டால் நமது நாயகனுக்கு என்பார்.
column3: When a faithful wife finds a good thing, she will keep it for her husband.
Would you wet your burden!

column2: நனைந்த கிழவன் வந்தால் உலர்ந்த விறகிற்குச் சேதம்.
column3: If the old man arive wet, there will be a consumption of dry fire wood.

column2: நன்மை செய்தார் நன்மை பெறுவார், தீமை செய்தார் தீமை பெறுவார்.
column3: Those who do good, obtain good; and those who do evil receive evil.
Evil will not spring from well-doing.
It is the greatest loss to destroy that which is good.
Good and evil are apparent in the present state.
To promote good and to diminish evil is the right way.
Persevere in that which is good.
The cakes prepared by Nalli are said to be excellent.
Forget not a benefit.

Like the story of killing a mungoose that had done well.The tale is-a mungoose seeing a deadly snake approach a sleeping infant, killed it. When the mother returned from the well she saw blood on the animal, and imagining that it had bitten her child she killed it.
When the tongue moves, the whole country moves.The allusion is to a despotic ruler whose word is law.
The letter ந is the seat of God.

column2: நாக்குக்கு எலும்பு இல்லை எப்படிப் புரட்டினாலும் அப்படிப் புரளும்.
column3: The tongue having no bone will turn any way.
Promise-breakers make excuses.

column2: நாங்களும் கங்கணம் கட்டினது உண்டு கழுத்துக்கு கங்கணம் கட்டினது இல்லை.
column3: We too tied coloured cords about our arms, but not a halter on the neck.

column2: நாச்சியாரும் ஒன்றைப்பற்றி வார்க்கிறாள் நானும் ஒன்றைப்பற்றிக் குடிக்கிறேன்.
column3: The mistress pours it out with one design, and I drink if wiht another.
Like murmuring in the absence of the mistress.
A master who bestows all desired blessings.
Is a sacred thread necessary for a brahman, who is known throughout the country?

column2: நாடு எங்கும் வாழ்ந்தால் கேடு ஒன்றும் இல்லை.
column3: If the whole country prospers, no evil will happen.
Do what is agreeable to the community.
The country has become a jungle, and the jungle has becomes a fruitful field.
A delayed gift becomes the hire for walking to receive it.

column2: நாட்டாள் பெற்ற குட்டி நாகரீகம் பேச வல்ல குட்டி.
column3: The child of the peasant is able to speak elegantly.
Does a boor require a page?

column2: நாட்டுக்கு நல்ல துரை வந்தாலும் தோட்டிக்குப் புல் சுமை போகாது.
column3: Though the country has a good ruler, the scavenger is not relieved of his burden of grass.

column2: நாட்டுக்கு ஒரு மழை நமக்கு இரண்டு மழை.
column3: The country has received one downpour of rain, we have received two i., e., too much.
No shame, no sense of honour.

column2: நாணம் இல்லாத வாத்திக்கு நாலு திக்கும் கூத்தி.
column3: Concubines on all sides to a shameless teacher.

column2: நாணம் இல்லாத கூத்திக்கு நாலு திக்கும் வாசல்.
column3: A shameless harlot has entrances on four sides.

column2: நாணம் இல்லாத பெண் நகைப்புக்கு இடம் வைப்பாள்.
column3: An impudent woman gives occasion to be laughed at.
The foot of diffidence deviates, that of activity stumbles.

column2: நாதமும் கீதமும் ஒத்திருப்பது போல, வேதமும் போதமும் ஒத்திருக்க வேண்டும்.
column3: As a melody and a song are in harmony, so must instruction be in harmony with the Veda.

column2: நாம் ஒருவருக்குக் கொடுத்தால் நமக்கு ஒருவர் கொடுப்பார்.
column3: If we give to others, some one will give to us.

column2: நாம் ஒன்று நினைத்தால் தெய்வம் ஒன்று நினைக்கிறது.
column3: When we think of one thing, the deity designs another.
Does a dog know which are sacred vessels?

column2: நாயின் பீயை மிதிப்பான் ஏன், நல்ல தண்ணீர் வார்த்துக் கழுவுவான் ஏன்?
column3: Why tread on the dung of a dog, and waste good water to wash it off?
Like a dog with a chatty of thick gruel.
A dog and a civet cat are both alike to you.
Even a dog is king in his own place.
Like dogs and cats.
Why keep a dog and clean up its filth?
I am but a dog, will my word reach the assembly?

column2: நாயை அடிப்பான் ஏன் பல் இழிவு பார்ப்பான் ஏன்?
column3: Why beat a dogn, why make it grin?
Why beat a dog and carry away his filth?
When you command a dog, he commands his tail.

column2: நாயைக் கண்டால் கல்லைக் காணோம், கல்லைக் கண்டால் நாயைக் காணோம்.
column3: If we see a dog, there is no stone, and if we see a stone, there is no dog.

column2: நாயைக் குளிப்பாட்டி நடுவீட்டில் வைத்தால் வாலைக் குழைத்துக்கொண்டு பீ தின்னப் போகும்.
column3: If you wash a dog and place him in the middle of the house, he will wag his tail and go out to eat filth.
If you caress a dog, he will lick your mouth.
Like pelting a dog with cakes.
Where there are dogs, there is quarreling.

column2: நாய் ஒரு சிறு எலும்புக்குச் சந்தோஷிக்கும்; அதுபோல, சிறியோர் சொற்ப காரியத்தை முடித்தாலும் சந்தோஷம் அடைவார்.
column3: A dog is pleased with a bone, in like manner the low are pleased with their own little acts.
The biting of a dog and the slippering of the wound to effect a cure are alike, painful.
Will the village be ruined by the barking of a dog?

column2: நாய் குலைத்து விடியுமா, கோழி கூப்பிட்டு விடியுமா?
column3: Does the day dawn because the dog barks, or because the cock crows?

column2: நாய் கெட்டகேட்டுக்கு மாமரத்து நிழல், அது கெட்ட கேட்டுக்குப் புளியிட்ட கறி.
column3: Mean as the dog is, he has the shade of a mango tree,—his curry is flavoured with acid.
Is a miserable dog to be fed with cocoanut milk and rice?

column2: நாய் கொண்டுபோன பானையை ஆர் கொண்டுபோனால் என்ன?
column3: What matters it who takes away the vessel that the dog had carried off?

column2: நாய் கோவிலுக்குப் போவான் ஏன் கோயில் காத்தவன் தெண்டம் இறுப்பான் ஏன்?
column3: Why should a dog go to a temple, or why the keeper of a temple pay a fine?
Like putting a bridle in the mouth of a dog.
Does a dog know how to catch a crane?
Does a dog appreciate an unhusked cocoanut?
Good cow ghee is not agreeable to a dog’s stomach.
What business has a dog in an iron bazaar?
Will a whole cocoanut suit a dog?

column2: நாய்க்கு வேலையும் இல்லை நிற்க நேரமும் இல்லை.
column3: A dog has nothing to do, and no time to rest.
If she becomes older than a dog, she will not be kind even to her mother.

column2: நாய்க்கு ஏன் தேங்காய், நடுவீட்டுக்குள் போட்டு உருட்டவோ?
column3: Why give a whole cocoanut to a dog, that he may roll it about the house?

column2: நாய்க்குப் பெயர் முத்துமாலை, அதற்கு ஆக்கிப் படைக்கிறது வரகந்தவிடு.
column3: The name of the dog is garland, its food is bran.
As a dog went to the market.
Although a dog may go to the sea, the water must be lapped.
Can a dog invest a lion with a title?
Will the ocean be diminished by the lapping of a dog?
Are the sufferings of a dog known to the stick, with which he was beaten?
Is the honey, defiled by a dog, fit for use?

column2: நாய் வாலிலே தேன் வைத்தால் ஆருக்குக் கூடும்?
column3: Who will benefit by the honey placed on the tail of a dog?

column2: நாய் வாசலைக் காத்து என்ன, கை இல்லாதவன் பணக்காரனைக் காத்து என்ன?
column3: What avails the waiting of a dog at the door, or the expectations of one who, having no hands, waits on the rich?
Can you change the shape of a dog’s tail?
May you descend into a river holding on by a dog’s tail?

column2: நாய் வாழ்ந்து என்ன, பூனை தாலி அறுத்து என்ன?
column3: What though a dog prosper, or a cat be bereft of her táli?
If you assume the guise of a dog, you must bark.

column2: நார் அற்றாற் கூடும் நரம்பு அற்றாற் கூடுமா?
column3: If a fibre snap it may be united, if a tendon break can it be united?
If relationship be traced to the fourth generation, even a barber may become an uncle.

column2: நாலாவது பெண் நாதாங்கி முளைக்கும் திக்கு இல்லை.
column3: A fourth born girl will not afford means even to procure a staple for a bolt.

column2: நாலு ஆறு கூடி ஒரு பாலாறு ஆயிற்று.
column3: If four rivers unite, the stream will be equal to the Pálár.

column2: நாலு பிள்ளை பெற்றவளுக்கு நடுத் தெருவிலே சோறு.
column3: A woman who has borne four children, eats her rice in the middle of the street.
The meeting of four persons is an assembly.

column2: நாலும் இரண்டும் சொல்லுக்கு உறுதி, ஆலும் வேலும் பல்லுக்கு உறுதி
column3: Náladiyár and the distichs of Valluvar are terse in construction, the twigs of the banian tree and the Acacia are good for the teeth.The tender fibre of the banian and Acacia are said to cure a gumboil, and therefore they are used for cleaning the teeth.
Are they the four, or are they celestials?
The testimony of four persons has the authority of a divine oracle.
The tongue produces good and evil.
If agreeable to the tongue, it will be metrical.

column2: நாழி அரிசி சோறு உண்டவன் நமனுக்கு உயிர் கொடான்.
column3: He that can eat a measure of rice may defy even the regent of the dead.

column2: நாழி நெல்லுக்கு ஒரு புடவை விற்றாலும் நாயின் அரை நிருவாணம்.
column3: Though a cloth is sold for a measure of rice, the dog goes naked.

column2: நாழி பணம் கொடுத்தாலும் மூளிப் பட்டம் போகாது.
column3: Though one give a measure of fanams, his ill fame will not be removed.
One measure cannot conta.in four measures.
Time effects that which the virtuous cannot achieve.

column2: நாளைக்குத் தின்கிற பலாப்பழத்திலும் இன்றைக்குத் தின்கிற களாப்பழம் நல்லது.
column3: Better is the kala berry eaten to day, than the jack fruit in prospect for to-morrow.
The jackal is pleased with drizzling rain.
As the cat looks at the fish which has become putrid.

column2: நாறல் சாணியை மிதிப்பான் ஏன் நல்ல தண்ணீர் வார்த்துக் கழுவுவான் ஏன்?
column3: Why tread on filth and waste good water in washing it off?
Why inquire if the rice is properly boiled, seeing that it is unfit for food?
A fetid body, a useless earthen vessel.
I have the command of four kalams of gruel.
Like a dog, if over forty.
Learning is a gem when compared to the four kinds of soil.They are the hilly,-forest,-agricultural and maritime tracts of a country.

column2: நானும் நரைத்து நரைமண்டை ஆனேன் காடு நடக்கக் கண்டது புதினம்.
column3: I have lived long and become grey-headed, but I have not seen a moving jungle.
I indeed do not know; nor will she tell a lie.

column2: நான் இட்ட மருந்தும் போக ஒட்டாது நன்னாரி வேரும் சாக ஒட்டாது.
column3: The medicine I gave, as a charm, will not allow him to go; the sarasparilia, an ingredient, will not suffer him to die.The proverb relates to charms, in which, as in magical arts generally, the Hindus in every part of India and Ceylon, have great faith. Servants have recourse to this device, and lovers also, for the purpose of influencing the affections and will of those whose favour or compliance they desiderate.The usual method, as indicated in the proverb, is to administer in the food of the party the ingredients that are fitted for the purpose contemplated; of course the preparation is surreptitiously added to the food of which it is known the individual is about to partake.A gentleman of my acquaintance recently dismissed all his servants and took into his service a person in whom he appeared to place the greatest confidence. The neighbours and the discarded servants believe that the gentleman acted under a charm.Some of the things used for certain purposes as charms, may not be mentioned here. The brain of a male child &c., are considered very potent.Professional magicians are employed when stolen property or buried treasure is sought. The magician uses a black preparation, a small quantity of which is placed on a betel leaf and put into the hands of an attendent boy, who is directed to look steadily at it, aided by the light of a lamp, while the magician invokes the presence and aid of certain deities. When the boy announces a phenomenon, say a tree, a monkey or a dog or a goblin, he is told to do obeisance to it to encourage further discoveries. The earth may cleave asunder and reveal the thing wanted, or a scene, including a house and certain persons going and coming, water &c., may appear, when the boy proceeds to describe the objects before him, as an earthen pot, or bangle &c., &c.,.as the case may be.To discover a thief among suspected persons sometimes dry grain as rice may be given to be eaten. The person whose mouth secretes no moisture for mastication is supposed to be the thief.

column2: நான் ஒன்றை எண்ண விதி ஒன்றை எண்ணிற்று.
column3: While I expected one thing, destiny ordained another.
I planted; god caused it grow.
The hare that I caught had three legs.
One thing ascertained will remove many evils.

column2: நிசாம் அல்லி தண்டில் நிசார்க்காரனைக் கண்டது உண்டா?
column3: Was any trousered sepoy seen in the army of Nizam Aly?

column2: நிஷ்டூரன் கண்ணைத் தெய்வம் கெடுக்கும், நீதிமான் கண்ணைப் பரிதானம் கெடுக்கும்.
column3: God will destroy the eyes of an oppressor, and a bribe will destroy the eye of an upright man.
Do any weep when deaths are of daily occurrence?
If you continually go thither, even the court-yard will be weary of you.
Constant trouble, long life.

May new moon fare be expected daily?The proverb refers to the ceremonies which are performed, at the period of the new moon, in honour of deceased ancestors. On these occasions the household eat only once during the day, but the food is of a superior kind and prepared with great care in cooking utensils that are kept for sacred purposes. If a brahman be present he offers oblations of sesamum grain and water to the manes of the dead, naming each in order as fur back as the third generation.In some parts of India ceremonies supposed to affect the state of the dead are performed no less than ninety-six times every year. The ceremonies of the Ancient Romans of like kind are hinted at by Virgil and Horace as is known to the classical student.
Sleep is an enemy.
Sleep is unconscious of enjoyment.
Speak no reproachful words, do justice.
Momentary pleasure is inferior pleasure.
A pleasure that lasts but a moment.

column2: நிமித்தம் பார்க்கிறவன் இரண்டகக்காரி மகன், பொருத்தம் பார்க்கிறவன் பொல்லாங்கன் புத்திரன்.
column3: He who consults omens is the son of a deceitful woman, he who enquires into the fitness of persons for marriage is the son of a vicious man.

column2: நிமிர்ந்து போட்டது என்ன குனிந்து எடுத்தது என்ன?
column3: What is lost by an erect posture, and what gained by stooping!
The vile body will not endure even for the twinkling of an eye.
It were an act of folly to wear clothes in a country where all go naked.
Plants decay on the ground from which they sprung.

column2: நிலத்திற்குத் தகுந்த கனியும் குலத்திற்கு தகுந்த குணமும்.
column3: Fruit appropriate to the soil, and quality agreeable, to one’s rank.
Can you clear the earth at a leap?
Like going to another country to escape from moonlight.
The words of the unstable are letters on water.
Swerve not from rectitude.
When out of your depth, swimming.
Ceaseless walking accomplishes great distances.
Learning alone is enduring, all else is evanescent.
The body may be compared to a shadow, and to theft,
The salubrity of shade is realized in sunshine.
The shade is good, the ants are bad.
Like dissolving assafœtida in a flooded river.
As an ass put its mouth into a full sack.

column2: நிறையக் குறுணி வேண்டாம் தலை தடவிக் குறுணி கொடு.
column3: I do not want a kuruni heaped up, give me a kuruni of grain level with the brim.
He will come before you think of him, he will give what you intend applying for.
Will heavy rain fall as we may wish?

column2: நினைத்தது இருக்க நினையாதது எய்தும், நினைத்தது வந்தாலும் வந்து நேரும்.
column3: When one thing is expected another may come, and that which is thought of may possibly come.

column2: நின்ற மரத்தில் நெடு மரம் போனால் நிற்கும் மரமே நெடுமரம்.
column3: When the lofty trees are felled, the remaining trees look tall.

column2: நின்ற வெள்ளத்தையும் வந்த வெள்ளம் கொண்டு போயிற்று.
column3: The flood that was, has been swept away by the flood that followed.
When standing, a tall tree, when fallen, a palmyrah tree.
If you fall as you stand, your head will be broken.
The base do not persevere in study.
Boasted learning will not avail.
The stretched hand will lade out fire.
Length to the carpenter, shortness to the smith.The former can easily shorten wood by cutting, the smith can lengthen iron by heating.
Will long grass afford a shade?A ready affirmation would answer this in central Africa.
Will sufficient rain fall in a city where justice cannot be obtained?
Will they cut off the head without judicial proceedings?

column2: நீதிமான் தீவினை செய்யிற் பிழைப்பானா, நீதி இல்லாதவன் நீதி புரிந்தால் மரிப்பானா?
column3: Will a virtuous man escape death if he do evil, will the unjust die if he do justice?

column2: நீ நீராலே விலகினாய் நான் நெருப்பாலே விலகினேன்.
column3: You have escaped an accident by water, I have escaped one by fire.
The river carries away him who cannot swim.

column2: நீ பிறர்க்கு உதவி செய்தால் தெய்வம் உனக்கு உதவி செய்யும்.
column3: If you help others, God will help you.
Live in a village where there is a good supply of water.
The body is an inscription on water.
The body is a bubble on water.
Water kills, and fire also kills.
Can water be divided by a stroke?
Did you spill water, or did you spill your character?

column2: நீர் ஆழம் காணலாம் நெஞ்சு ஆழம் காணப்படாது.
column3: The depth of water may be ascertained, but not the depth of the heart.
As the water rises, the rice plant rises.
The young fish will sport as long as the water lasts.
Can fire be quenched by pronouncing the word water?
When it was said to be water, it turned out to be fire.
As moss in water, it will not take root.
When the water goes, will the fish leap about?
The body is unstable as a bubble on water.
Do not play in water.
Is black dyed in blue?
To speak so as to confine the blue dye to the skirts of the cloth.
A termagant has her tears in the eyelids.
The shrewed are successful.

column2: நுண்ணிய ஞானம் உரைப்பார்கள் சொன்னதில் ஒன்றும் நடவார்கள்.
column3: They give wise counsels, but they themselves walk not according to any one of them.
Although a small thing, do it after due thought.
They become possessed of discrimination by imparting instruction on abstruse subjects.
Be not particular in food.
The affluence of this world is as froth.
Hunger cannot be satisfied by eating froth.

column2: நுழை விட்டு செய் நூல் கற்று அடங்கு.
column3: Build a house with an entrance, acquire learning and be humble.
Does a fisherman understand the value of gems?
The words of a fisherman will not reach the assembly.
Having ascended to the extremity of the bough, will they sever it from the tree?
Like making a garland of flowers without a string.
The learned only will gain eminence.
Though a hundred years old, diligently acquire knowledge.

column2: நூறு குற்றம் ஆறு பிழை கொண்டு பொறுக்கவேண்டும்.
column3: A hundred offences must be forgiven as if only six faults.
The learning acquired in a hundred days, will be lost by six days neglect.

column2: நூறோடு நூறு ஆகிறது நெய்யிலே சுடடி பணிகாரம்.
column3: Though the debt may increase to another hundred, bake the cakes in ghee.
Though a hundred years old, he is only a young plant to the regent of the dead.
Those who will live to be a hundred, will not die at fifty.
After a hundred a spring, after a thousand a flooded river.

column2: நூற்றுக்கு ஒரு பேச்சு ஆயிரத்துக்கு ஒரு தலை அசைப்பு.
column3: When he is worth a hundred, a word, when a thousand, a mere nod.
One measure of chaff spoiled a hundred of grain,
Ascertaining the rules of the Veda, live virtuously.
May one tell a lie knowingly?
There is no deceit which can be concealed from the mind.
It will be known in the river, who is tall and who is short.
Though you may cross the broad sea your learning will remain with you.
Even a long day has a sunset.
What has a weaver to do with a young monkey?
As the ants swarm on a ghee pot.
If the ghee pot is broken, the dog has a feast.
The breaking of the ghee pot, is a hunting excursion to the dog.

column2: நெய்யை உருக்கித் தயிரைப் பெருக்கிச் சாப்பிட வேண்டும்.
column3: Use ghee after melting, and curds diluted.

column2: நெருஞ்சி முள் தைத்தாலும் குனிந்தல்லவா பிடுங்க வேண்டும்?
column3: Though it be but a thistle that has entered your foot, you must sit down, must you not, to pull it out?
Will flies swarm in fire?
The distress of famine is worse than that of fire.
Shoes worse than fire.
Will worms breed in fire?
A river of fire, and a bridge of hair.
Will there be smoke where there is no fire?

column2: நெருப்பு நின்ற காட்டிலே ஏதாவது நின்றாலும் நிற்கும்; நீர் நின்ற காட்டில் ஒன்றும் நிற்காது.
column3: Something may possibly remain in a forest after a fire, but nothing remains after a flood.
By pronouncing the word fire, will the mouth be burnt?

column2: நெருப்பைக் கண்டு மிதித்தாலும் சுடும், காணாமல் மிதித்தாலும் சுடும்.
column3: Whether you tread on fire wittingly or unawares, it will burn you.

column2: நெருப்பைச் சேர்ந்த யாவும் அதன் நிறமாகும்; அதுபோல, பெரியோரைச் சேர்ந்தவரும் ஆவார்.
column3: Things put into fire partake of its colour, in like manner will it happen to those who join the great.
May you tie fire in the skirt of your cloth because a mere spark?

column2: நெருப்பைத் தலை கீழாய்ப் பிடித்தாலும் அதின் சுவாலை கீழாகுமா?
column3: Although you carry fire head downwards, will the flame burn in that direction?
Am I destined to carry both paddy and grass?
Will a mixture of paddy and salt be agreeable to the palate?

column2: நெல்லு வகை எண்ணினாலும் பள்ள வகை எண்ணக் கூடாது.
column3: Though one may enumerate the various kinds of rice, he cannot enumerate the varieties of the palla caste.

column2: நெல் விளைந்த பூமியும் அறியாய், நிலா எறிந்த இடமும் அறியாய்.
column3: You do not know a rice crop, nor a moonlight space.
It is useless to teach those who diviate from rectitude.
Cultivate rice.
The apple of the eye, is an ingenious piece of mechanism.
Love alone will abide.
Fasting without a vow is not good.

column2: நேர்ந்து நேர்ந்து சொன்னாலும் நீசக் கசடர் வசம் ஆகார்.
column3: Though repeatedly told, the base take no heed.
Behave evenly or agreeably.

column2: நேற்று வந்த மொட்டைச்சி நெய் வார்த்து உண்ணச் சிணுங்குகிறாள்.
column3: The bald headed woman who came but yesterday, is whining for ghee to eat with her rice.

column2: நேற்று வந்தானாம் குடி; அவன் தலையில் விழுந்ததாம் இடி.
column3: It is said that he took up his abode only yesterday, and he has been struck by lightning.
Naishadha is a cordial to poets.
Speak not roughly even to a beggar.
Though mollified by learning, speak effectively.
Go not near that which is pernicious.
Lose not even a moment of time.

column2: நொண்டி நொண்டி நடப்பானேன் கண்டதற்கு எல்லாம் படைப்பானேன்?
column3: Why walk limping, and why offer oblations, to every god you see?
To slip is the excuse of the lame horse.

column2: நொந்த கண் இருக்க நோகாக் கண்ணுக்கு மருந்து.
column3: Applying medicine to the sound eye, instead of to the one diseased.
Those who are well versed in classical Tamil know not want.
Will bruised rice bear boiling?
Those who masticate their food, live a hundred years.
Those who study unmindful of the pains attending it are devoted to learning.
I will beat you without giving pain, set up an unceasing cry.
Be careful to secure your object.
He whom we seek to see, has already seen us.

column2: நோயற்ற வாழ்வே வாழ்வு குறைவற்ற செல்வமே செல்வம்.
column3: Freedom from sickness is true happiness, and competence is true riches.
Like uttering soothing words to a sick person.

column2: நோயாளி விதியாளி ஆனால் பரிகாரி பேராளி ஆவான்.
column3: If destiny favours the patient, his doctor will obtain fame.
The sick person knows the intensity of his suffering.
The sick are like those possessed of demons.
As a lean cat licks the churnstaff.
Though the chetah is sick, it is stronger than on ox.
By neglecting religious austerities supreme good will be lost.

column2: நோவு ஒரு பக்கம் இருக்கச் சூடு ஒரு பக்கம் போட்டாற்போல.
column3: Like branding the side that is not affected by disease.
Not to take life for the purpose of eating, is to fast.
Youthful education will prove beneficial.
Know God when you are young.
He who is without youth, old age, or manhood.

column2: நௌவியும் வாழ்க்கையும் அழகல்ல, நற்குணம் ஒன்றே அழகு.
column3: Youth and affluence do not form one’s beauty, goodness alone does.

column2: பகலிற் பக்கம் பார்த்துப் பேசு, அர்த்தராத்திரியில் அதுதானும் பேசாதே.
column3: By day, look round and speak, at midnight do not utter a word.

column2: பகலிற் பசு தெரியவில்லை இரவில் எருமை தெரியுமா?
column3: Not being able to distinguish a cow by day, how can he distinguish a buffalo by night?

column2: பகிடிக்குப் பத்துக் காசு திருப்பாட்டுக்கு ஒரு காசு.
column3: Ten cash for a comedy, one cash for a sacred song.
As the snake bit the jester.

column2: பகுத்தல் இல்லாத துணிவு பாரம் இல்லாத கப்பல்.
column3: Daring without prudence, is as a ship without ballast.
Attempt nothing without consideration, and do nothing hastily.
Do not create enemies, act discreetly.
You must ruin the family of your enemy by feigned friendship.
The friendship of foes is like fire concealed in smoke.

column2: பங்காளி குடி கெடுக்க வெங்காயம் குளிபோடச் சொன்னது போல.
column3: Like advising his partner to plant onions in order to effect his ruin.

column2: பங்காளி வீடு வேகிறது சுங்கான்கொண்டு தண்ணீர் விடு.
column3: The house of my partner is on fire, pour water with a pipe.
Observe the proper time for cutting a partner and palmyrah fruit.

column2: பங்குனி என்று பருப்பதும் இல்லை, சித்திரை என்று சிறுப்பதும் இல்லை.
column3: It wont grow larger because it is Panguni,-March-nor smaller because it is Chittarai-April.
He that goes abroad in the day time in March, is a great sinner.
He who is not hungry cares not a hair about famine.

column2: பசி ஏப்பக்காரனும் புளி ஏப்பக்காரனும் கூட்டுப்பயிர் இட்டதுபோல.
column3: As two men, one belching through hunger, and the other by indigestion, were associated in joint tillage.
A cow with a long tail, an ox with a short one.

column2: பசிக்குக் கறி வேண்டாம், தூக்கத்துக்குப் பாய் வேண்டாம்.
column3: When hungry curry is not needed, nor a mat when sleepy.

column2: பசிக்குப் பனம் பழம் தின்றால் பித்தம் பட்ட பாடு படட்டும்.
column3: Eat palmyrah fruit to satisfy hunger, no matter about the bile.Palmyrah fruit is believed to be very bilious.
Songs are pleasant after refection.

column2: பசித்த பறையனும் குளித்த சைவனும் சாப்பிடாது இரார்.
column3: A hungry pariah and a saiva that has bathed, must have eaten first.

column2: பசித்தவன் தின்னாததும் இல்லை. பகைத்தவன் சொல்லாததும் இல்லை.
column3: There is nothing edible which the hungry will not eat, there is no reproach which the malevolent has not uttered.
Can confidence be placed in a hungry person?
It is said that a hungry merchant chewed betel and arica-nut.
Let the hungry sow peas.
As a hungry man looks at his old accounts.

column2: பசித்தார் பொழுதும் போம் பாலுடன் அன்னம் புசித்தார் பொழுதும் போம்.
column3: The sun goes down on the hungry, and also on those who eat their rice and milk.

column2: பசியாமல் மருந்து கொடுக்கிறேன் பழையது இருந்தால் போடு என்பதுபோல.
column3: Like saying, put before me some old rice if there be any, and I will give you medicine that will prevent your hungering again.

column2: பசி ருசி அறியுமா நித்திரை சுகம் அறியுமா?
column3: Does hunger appreciate flavours, or sleep enjoyment?

column2: பசி ருசி அறியாது, நித்திரை சுகம் அறியாது.
column3: Hunger is ignorant of flavours, sleep is ignorant of enjoyment.
Hunger dissipates the ten.The ten are the five senses and their organs.
Because the cow is black, is her milk also black?
Are there any gentle cows, or poor brahmans?
