My father not only neglected to put me to school, but left me to bear the expenses of the fire brand.Under this proverb a general note on funeral rites may not be out of place. The particulars given relate to Hindu families generally, not to brahmans.When a person dies, the grief of the females in the family bursts out into loud lamentations. On its being made known by a conch-blower that a death has occurred, the neighbouring females go to the house, add the expression of their sympathy, and all unite in one general wail. Frequently one of the company, the mother, or it may be a professional person, breaks out into an ascription of praise regarding the deceased uttering a sort of elegy in measured cadence. In this case when a climax is reached, all join in a chorus of grief, and some may smite their breasts and tear their hair. In such elegies, which are often of a touching kind, the personal beauty, the talents, the learning and the prowess of the deceased are dwelt on.The body being taken out is bathed and dressed as in life. In the case of females the gold and pearls and precious stones, if she possessed them, will be put on. The sectarial marks will be added. The chárpoy on which the corpse has been laid out is then carried into the house. Again the females seat themselves around it and pour forth a torrent of grief, or at any rate make a loud noise. A kinsman whose business it is to attend to the customary observances spreads a cloth on the chárpoy to hold rice, called,—rice for the mouth. The females of the family take a little of the grain and put it on the corpse near the mouth. The same ceremony is performed by the male members of the family at the place of cremation. The family barber appropriates the remaining rice. At the burning ground the kinsman who conducts the ceremonies carries a pot of water round the pyre and the conch-blower follows him and makes an incision in the pot with his conch, when the barber puts four bits of sandal or other wood into the hands of the kinsman and points out the places in the pyre where they are to be inserted. The leaking pot is then taken by the barber to be broken near the head of the corpse. The kinsman hastens away that he may not hear the noise of the pot when broken, nor see the burning pyre. Then the barber receives permission to break the pot and to ignite the pyre.On the next or on an early day the male friends of the deceased assemble at the place of cremation. The skull, the breast-bone and the bones of the hands having been abstracted from the ashes and deposited in a convenient place covered with a plantain leaf, are by the nearest of kin smeared with oil, ghee and honey and covered with flowers, whilst a priest recites appropriate incantations in the presence of the relatives. A pot of milk having been provided for this ceremony, the relatives take a sprig of coronilla grandiflora and having dipped it in the milk, sprinkle the relics, which are then put into an unburnt earthen vessel, covered with cloth to be cast into a sacred stream or bestowed on the ocean wave. Of course these elaborate rites are dispensed with by the poor.I have seen Hindus, not by any means in abject circumstances, convey a corpse to the seaside, where a grave had been hastily dug, take off the cloth and ornaments, wrap the body in a common mat, and bury it with the burial of a dog.In the year 1830 when travelling in Bengal I saw, on the bank of the Damooda, a sorrowing brahmanical father with two or three attendants dispose of a corpse with but little ceremony. The father himself who was much affected ignited the pyre.
A palli does not like to be secluded, nor does a brahman fast after bathing.
What is distance to a bird on the wing?
As a crow on the wing knows not on what branch it will alight.

column2: பறக்கும் குருவிக்கும் இருக்கும் கொம்பு தெரியாது, பரதேசிக்குத் தங்கும் இடம் தெரியாது.
column3: A bird on the wing knows not the branch on which it may alight, a stranger knows not his halting place.

column2: பறந்துபோகிற எச்சிற் கல்லைமேல் கல்லைத் தூக்கி வைத்தாற்போல.
column3: Like one placing a stone on a flying leaf that has been used for a rice-plate.

column2: பறப்பான் பயிர் இழந்தான் அறக்காஞ்சி பெண்டு இழந்தான்.
column3: A hasty man loses the produce of his field, and the jealous man his wife.
No further fear in a jungle, where one has been already robbed.

column2: பறைச்சி பிள்ளையைப் பள்ளிக்கு வைத்தலும் பேச்சிலே அய்யே என்னுமாம்.
column3: Though a pariah child be sent to school, he will still call his father ayyè.
Like striking grain to the level of the measure.
As a vilva tree sprang up in a pariah street.
A pariah’s song, his dialect, and a surai flower, have no fragrance in them.
Like a pariah and a brahman.
If a pariah boil rice as an offering to God, will it not be accepted?
Though a pariah is schooled, will his vulgar brogue be altered?
The work of a pariah is only half done.
Will the jackal of the palmyrah grove tremble at the rustling leaves?
If there be dew, there will be no rain.
Will the sea be filled by the falling dew?
Will the falling dew fill the tank?

column2: பனி பெய்தால் மழை இல்லை, பழம் இருந்தால் பூ இல்லை.
column3: When there is dew there is no rain, when fruit is ripe there is no blossom.
Can you sail a ship in heavy dew?
Like beginning to plough depending on the dew.
Like descending from a palmyrah tree without touching the flower-spathe.

column2: பனை ஏறி விழுந்தவனைக் கடா ஏறி மிதித்ததுபோல.
column3: Like a buffalo trampling on a person who has fallen from a palmyrah tree.

column2: பனை மரத்தின் கீழே பாலைக் குடித்தாலும் கள் என்று நினைப்பார்.
column3: Though what you drink in a palmyrah grove be milk, every one will believe it to be toddy.

column2: பனைமரத்திற்கு நிழல் இல்லை பறையனுக்கு முறை இல்லை
column3: A palmyrah tree casts no shade, a pariah has no rules.
How far can you support a man who is climbing a palmyrah tree?

column2: பனையின் நிழலும் நிழலோ பகைவர் உறவும் உறவோ?
column3: Is the shadow of a palmyrah tree a shade, is the acquaintance of an enemy friendship?
Like a snake biting one who has fallen from a palmyrah tree.

column2: பனை வெட்டின இடத்திலே கழுதை வட்டம் போட்டதுபோல.
column3: Like an ass going round a place where a palmyrah tree had been felled.
Does a young pig observe fasts?
Will the young pig become an elephant?
If a hog be shot he takes the whole, if an elephant be shot I shall have a share.

column2: பன்றி பல குட்டி, சிங்கம் ஒரு குட்டி.
column3: The sow has many young ones at a time, the lioness only one.
What, if a sow has a numerous litter?
A calf that goes with a pig will eat excrement.
It is said that the wife of a mat-maker died on the bare ground.
Do not tell old stories with affectation.
Will a bottle gourd spring from a págal seed?
He who has no ties is like a foreigner.

column2: பாடகக்காலி வாழ்ந்தால் பத்து எட்டுச் சனம் பிழைக்கும்.
column3: If the foot-ringed lady prosper, eight or ten people may be supported thereby.
Though you have learnt your lesson, do not throw away your book.

column2: பாடிப் பாடிக் குத்தினாலும் பதரில் அரிசி இல்லை.
column3: Though pounded with a song, chaff will not yield rice.
When pressed to sing, even the professional singer refuses.

column2: பாடு படாமற் போனால் பலன் இல்லாமற் போகும்.
column3: Where there is no labour, there is no profit.
I am become an ola in the band of a poet.
Though you may ascend the bier, forsake not your alms-dish.
He speaks as if arrows were darting forth.
Friendship with the wicked, is like playing with snakes.

column2: பாதிச் சுரைக்காய் கறிக்கும் பாதிச் சுரைக்காய் விதைக்குமா?
column3: Can you take half a churaikáy for curry, and use the other half as seed?

column2: பாத்திரம் அறிந்து பிச்சை இடு, கோத்திரம் அறிந்து பெண் கொடு.
column3: Give alms to the worthy, and your daughter to one of a good family.
Brahmans have no chiefs.

column2: பாமணி ஆற்றிலே பல்லை விளக்கு, முல்லை ஆற்றிலே முகம் கழுவு.
column3: Begin to clean your teeth at Pámani river, wash your face in Mullai river.
A snake charmer understands snakes, a thief understands thieving.

column2: பாம்பிலும் பாம்புக்குட்டி விஷம் அதிகம் வீரியமும் அதிகம்.
column3: A young snake is more poisonous and vigorous than an old one.

column2: பாம்பின் குட்டி பாம்பு அதன் குட்டி நட்டுவாக்காலி.
column3: The young one of a snake is a snake, and its young one is a scorpion.
Like a toad in the jaws of a snake.
Snakes have no chiefs.
When bitten by a snake, will the incantations suited to scorpion bites avail aught?
A bambu stick is king to a snake.
Are the enemies of snakes few?

column2: பாம்புக்குத் தலையைக் காட்டி மீனுக்கு வாலைக் காட்டி.
column3: Showing the head to snakes, and the tail to fish.Spoken of a two-faced person, in allusion to an eel that shows its serpent-like head to snakes, and its fish-like tail to fish.

column2: பாம்புக்குப் பால் வார்த்து வளர்த்தாலும் விஷத்தைக் கொடுக்கும்.
column3: Though you feed a snake with milk, it will yield poison.
Like pouring out milk to a snake.
Lik a snake and a mungoose.
I lived a long time with him or her as a snake with a mungoose.

column2: பாம்பு தன் பசியை நினைக்கும் தேரை தன் விதியை நினைக்கும்.
column3: The snake has regard to its hunger, the frog thinks of its fate.

column2: பாம்பு பசிக்கில் தேரையைப் பிடிக்கும். அதுபோல, சிறியோர்
column3: சிறிய காரியங்களையே செய்வார்.When a snake is hungry it will catch a frog, so the vile perform only mean acts.

column2: பாம்பு தின்கிற ஊரிலே போனால் நடு முறி நமக்கு என்று இருக்கவேண்டும்.
column3: If we go to a country of snake eaters, we must be prepared for the middle bit.
Is the snake hostile, and its skin friendly?
The hawk is inimical to the snake.

column2: பாம்பும் நோவாமற் பாம்பு அடித்த கோலும் நோவாமல் இருக்கவேண்டும்.
column3: So strike that neither the snake, nor the staff with which you strike, shall be hurt or injured.
Even an army will tremble at the word snake.
Have nothing to do with snakes.
Like reciting a portion of the vedas, to a cow about to gore you.
I have become like a crow on the top of a mast.
Like a vessel without a mast.
A wise bird has been caught with chaff.
Property not looked after perishes.

column2: பாரைக்கு ஊடாடாப் பாறை பசுமரத்தின் வேருக்கு ஊடாடிவிடும்.
column3: The rock that resists-a’ crowbar, gives way to the roots of a tender plant.
Thou drinkest milk, hoping to govern the world.
Is Friday a sufficient excuse for not returning the coin given you to look at?It is believed by many that, though it is fortunate to receive money on Friday, it is unfortunate to pay it.
A listening ear, is worse than a seeing eye.

column2: பார்த்த கண்ணும் பூத்துப் பகலும் இரவு ஆயிற்று.
column3: The eyes have failed by looking too long, and night has followed the day.
All the faces we see are diverse from one another.
In appearance like a cow, in action like a tiger.
Gazed at-by starving beggars-he eats and washes his hands.

column2: பார்த்திருந்தவள் பச்சை குத்தினாள், கேட்டிருந்தவள் வறுத்துக் குத்தினாள்.
column3: She who had seen the process pounded it undried, she who had heard of it pounded roasted.
It is impossible to comprehend the supreme Brahma.
Brahman matron, the cow has come.
As a brahman woman lost her salted mutton.

column2: பார்ப்பார் சேவகமும் வெள்ளைக் குதிரைச் சேவகமும் ஆகாது.
column3: The service of a brahman, and the care of a white horse are bad.
Do not waste your breath on a a brahman, nor converse with a mendicant.
A black brahman, and a fair pariah are not trustworthy.
Which is the more helpless, a brahman or a cow?

column2: பாலருக்குப் பலம் அழுகை, மட்சத்துக்குப் பலம் உதகம்.
column3: The strength of a child is crying, and that of fish is water.

column2: பாலர் மொழி கேளாதார் குழல் இனிது யாழ் இனிது என்பார்.
column3: Those who have not heard the lisping of their own children say, that the flute is sweet, the stringed instrument is sweet.
He has made enemies of all whose hearts are pure as milk.
He is the guard of the milk, and also the friend of the cat.
Will the cat that has come for new milk, drink buttermilk?

column2: பாலுக்குச் சீனி இல்லை என்றோர்க்கும் கூழுக்குக் கறி இல்லை என்றோர்க்கும் விசாரம் ஒன்றே.
column3: The care of those who want sugar with milk, and of those who want curry for their rice, are the same.
It is of use both as milk and medicine.
Is the rate of fresh milk that of buttermilk?
Milk is white and buttermilk is also white.
They may feed him with milk; can they feed him with good fortune?

column2: பாலைக்குடித்தவனுக்குப் பால் ஏப்பம் வரும், கள்ளைக் குடித்தவனுக்குக் கள் ஏப்பம் வரும்.
column3: He who has drank milk will belch milk, and he who has drank toddy will belch toddy.
Do you examine the milk or the vessel containing it?
Water mixed with milk looks like milk.
Though with milk, take your meals at the proper time.
Milk to an Aryan, and a cow for Rámanádaswámi.

column2: பால் இருக்கிறது பாக்கியம் இருக்கிறது, பாலிலே போட்டுக் குடிக்கப் பத்துப் பருக்கைக்கு வழி இல்லை.
column3: There is milk and money, but no means for procuring ten grains of rice.
One must draw milk after moistening the fingers with milk.

column2: பால் நக்காத பூனையும் பரிதானம் வாங்காத பிராமணனும் உண்டா ?
column3: Is there a cat that will not lap milk, or a brahman that will refuse a bribe?

column2: பால் பசுவைக் கன்றிலே தெரியும், பாக்கியவான் பிள்ளையை முகத்திலே தெரியும்.
column3: The milch cow is known by its calf, and the child of the wealthy by its face.
He will bathe after applying milk to the head.Intimating that a person inimical has been got rid of.
Though as grey as a pávattam flower, he has no sense.
He who does not give place to sin, will conquer it.
O my heart, do not sin!
The ability of a poet is best known to the learned.

column2: பாவியார் போன இடம் எல்லாம் பள்ளமும் திட்டியும்.
column3: Whithersoever the simple go, they meet with ups and downs.
Seizing a simpleton and making him dance like a snake.
That which is described in poetry is also seen in nature.
That which is spoiled is put in the mouth of a cow.
The jackal is king in a deserted village.
If in the pot, it will come into the spoon.

column2: பானையிலே பதக்கு நெல் இருந்தால் மூலையிலே முக்குறுணி தெய்வம் கூத்தாடும்.
column3: If there be a pathaku of rice in the pot, three kurunies of gods will dance in the corner.
It is useless to extol a miser.
As a madman entered the plantain grove.
Through fear of beggars do you refrain from lighting your fire?
It is said that he beat the mendicant and broke his alms-dish.
As Saturn entered into the rice of the mendicant.
What! scrupulous about leavings in rice got by begging?
Is an enchanted arrow discharged at a mendicant?
Do you propose giving your daughter in marriage to one who came to ask alms?
Merchandize is a little better than begging.
Is rice offered in charity refused because it is overboiled?
Is rice given in charity ever scarce?
The alms given are sufficient; tie up the dog.
Learning is good even when one is reduced to begging.
Has any one been ruined by giving alms?

column2: பிச்சை இட்டுக் கெட்டவனும் இல்லை, பிள்ளை பெற்றுக் கெட்டவனும் இல்லை.
column3: None is ruined by giving alms; none is ruined because he has a family.
Though you have to beg, destroy the family of your enemy.
Relying on the efficacy of the prescribed remedy, will you put your hand in a snake hole?

column2: பிடாரனுக்கு அஞ்சிப் பாம்பு எலிக்கு உறவு ஆச்சுதாம்.
column3: It is said that a snake afraid of the charmer, sought the friendship of a rat.
O, demon, the bull has come.

column2: பிடாரி வரம் கொடுத்தாலும் ஒச்சன் வரம் கொடுக்கிறது அரிது.
column3: Though a pidári may grant a favour, it will be difficult to obtain it through the priest.

column2: பிடி அழகி புகுந்தால் பெண் அழகி ஆவாள்
column3: If a woman ornamented with jewels enter, she will be regarded as a beautiful woman.
Are incantations to be used again and again.

column2: பிடித்த கொம்பும் விட்டேன் மிதித்த கொம்பும் விட்டேன்.
column3: I have left the branch I had seized, and also the one on which I was standing.

column2: பிடித்த கொம்பும் ஒடிந்தது மிதித்த கொம்பும் முறிந்தாற்போல் ஆனேன்.
column3: I have become as helpless as if the branch I seized, and the one I stood upon, both broke at the same time.
If tied, a bundle; if loose, bits of straw.
Is he the prince of beggars, or the first as possessing lacs?
Bury the corpse, and then celebrate the marriage.
Sorrow goes away to the place whither the corpse has gone.
Do not stand security, it will lead to endless evils.
That which cannot be obtained for sustenance, will be found to pay a fine.
Madness is of ten kinds i. e., many kinds.
Brass is innocent of its own odour.

column2: பித்தர்க்குச் சில புத்திகள் சொன்னால் பேச்சைக் கேட்பாரா?
column3: If advice be given to fools will they iisten to it?
The madman thinks his own character straighter than a line.
As the brahmachari upset the boat.
If Brahma wills it, is there any chance of your life being short?
As a grave occurrence befell one in the place to which he had gone for a livelihood.

column2: பிள்ளைக்காரன் பிள்ளைக்கு அழுகிறன் பணிசெய்வோன் காசுக்கு அழுகிறான்.
column3: The parent weeps on account of his child, the servant weeps for his hire.
That which is sport to the child, is death to the mouse.

column2: பிள்ளை பதினாறு பெறுவாளென்று எழுதி இருந்தாலும், புருஷசன் இல்லாமல் எப்படிப் பெறுவாள்?
column3: Though it were written in the horoscope that she would have sixteen children, how could that be without a husband?

column2: பிள்ளை பெற்றவனுக்கும் மாடு படைத்தவனுக்கும் வெட்கம் இல்லை.
column3: A parent and a cowherd know no shame.

column2: பிள்ளை பெற்றவளைப் பார்த்துப் பெருமூச்சு விட்டு ஆவது என்ன?
column3: What if you look at her who has borne a child, and sigh?
Does a barren woman understand the endearments of a child?
Like obtaining a wife for Ganésha.
As Saturn who had seized Ganésha seized also the tree-ficus Indica.

column2: பிள்ளையார் கோவிலில் திருடன் இருப்பான், சொன்னாலும் கோள்.
column3: The thief has taken refuge in the temple of Pillaiyár, it would however be a slander to mention it.
The attempt to form an image of Ganésha, ended in the formation of a monkey.

column2: பிள்ளை இல்லா வீட்டில் கிழவன் துள்ளி விளையாடுகிறானாம்.
column3: It is said that in the house in which there are no children, an old man plays like a child.

column2: பிள்ளையும் புழுக்கையும் சரி, பிள்ளைக்கும் புழுக்கைக்கும் இடங்கொடாதே.
column3: A child and a slave are alike, be not indulgent to a child or a slave.
Do you rock the cradle, while pinching the child?
Pretending to feed the child, the goblin swallows it.
Like losing her husband, when she went to ask the gift of offspring.
A mother knows the pain of travail, is it known to others?
If the family of the bridegroom consent, half the ceremony of marriage is over.

column2: பிறக்கிறபொழுதே முடம் ஆனால் பேய்க்கு இட்டுப் படைத்தால் தீருமா?
column3: If a person be a cripple from his birth, can he be cured by offerings made to demons?

column2: பிறந்த பிள்ளை பிடி சோற்றிற்கு அழுகிறது, பிறக்கப்போகிற பிள்ளைக்குத் தண்டை சதங்கை தேடுகிறார்களாம்.
column3: It is said that they are making silver bells for the child about to be born, while the child on the lap is crying for a handful of rice.
It is certain that he who is born will die.
Those who are born will die, what is visible will vanish.
The day of birth leads to death.

column2: பிறந்த ஊருக்குச் சேலை வேண்டாம், பெண்டு இருந்த ஊருக்குத் தாலிவேண்டாம்.
column3: A sumptuous cloth is not required in one’s native village, nor a tàli where one is known as a wife.

column2: பிறர் பொருளை இச்சிப்பான தன் பொருளை இழப்பான்.
column3: He who covets the property of others, will lose his own.
He who would not carry off a rush belonging to another's roof, robbed a brahman his master of his staff.
Are there any clever of speech who were born deaf?
As one born blind received sight.

column2: பிறன் மனை புகாமை அறம் எனத் தகும்.
column3: Not going to another man’s house, deserves to be called virtue.

column2: பின்னாலே வரும் பலாக்காயினும் முன்னாலே வரும் களாக்காய் நலம்.
column3: A kalàkkày is better in hand, than a jack fruit in prospect.

column2: பிள்ளை என்பதும் பேசாதிருப்பதும், இல்லை என்பதற்கு அடையாளம்.
column3: By and by, and silence, are tantamount to a refusal.
Preserve your strength and bear the burden.
A ragged cloth will not wear long.
A broken cord, and a ragged basket (well-bucket.)
That which was given to the praise-worthy, is a microscope.
Defend those who acknowledge your merit.
Though eaglewood produces smoke, it will do no harm.
The contempt of riches is the highest praise.
As a demon came out of a well that had been dug as an act of religious merit.

column2: புண்ணியத்துக்கு உழுத மாட்டைப் பல்லைப் பிடித்துப் பார்த்ததுபோல.
column3: Like looking at the teeth of a bullock that is ploughing for nothing.

column2: புண்ணியம் பார்க்கப் போய்ப் பாவம் பின்னே வந்ததாம்.
column3: It is said that evil followed the man who attempted a kind act.
A leader not virtuous is worthless.
Like thrusting a stick into a wound.
As if a sore had become acidified.
Whether calls for immediate action, the sore or its remedy?
New things require fresh consideration.
Employ a new washerman, but an old barber.
A new washerman will wash with great care.
Is it right to forsake old friends in reliance on new ones?
Water poured into a new pot.
Flies do not swarm on a new pot.

column2: புதுப் பெண்ணே புதுப் பெண்ணே நெருப்பு எடுத்துவா, உனக்குப் பின்னாலே இருக்கிறது செருப்பு அடி.
column3: O bride, O bride bring some fire, what awaits you is slippering.
Certainly it is a strange occurrence in kaliyuga.
The washerman inaugurated his washing by beat of tomtom.
Like one that has discovered buried treasure.

column2: புத்தி கெட்ட ராஜாவுக்கு மதி கெட்ட மந்திரி.
column3: A foolish king and an ignorant minister.

column2: புத்தி அற்றோரும் தூங்கினர், புத்தி உற்றோரும் தூங்கினர்.
column3: Both the foolish and the wise slept.
An intelligent man is strong.
There is nothing in which the mature in judgment fail.
As soundness of judgment increases, praise will follow.
Even that which fools perform earnestly is wrong.

column2: புத்தி ஈனர்கள் குளிக்கப் போய்ச் சேற்றைப் பூசிக் கொள்வார்கள்.
column3: When the foolish go to wash, they will daub themselves with mud.

column2: புரட்டிப் புரட்டிப் உதைக்கிறபோதும், மீசையில் மண் படவில்லை என்கிறான்.
column3: Even when kicked about and rolling in the dust, he affirms that his mustache did not touch the earth.
Like the younger brother of the husband, rebuking him for beating his wife.
The cloth covering the breast suited to the rank of her husband.

column2: புருவத்திற் பட்டால் கரிக்குமோ கண்ணிற் பட்டால் கரிக்குமோ?
column3: When does acid cause smarting, when it is applied to the eye-brow or the eyes?
Struck with fear before struck by the tiger.
Being born a tiger, will it be without claws?

column2: புலிக்குத் தன் காடு என்ன வேற்றுக்காடு என்ன?
column3: What matters it to the tiger whether he is in his native jungle or another?
Being born a tiger, will it become a cat?
As the deer was caught among tigers.
An elephant in rut whose ears were wrung off by a tiger.

column2: புலி நகம் படாவிட்டாலும் அதன் மீசை குத்தினாலும் விஷம்
column3: Though one may escape the claws of the tiger, even the pricking of its whiskers will prove malignant.
When the tiger is hungry, will he eat grass?
The crouching of the tiger is the prelude to a spring.
He is like one fed on tiger’s milk.

column2: புலியைப் பார்த்து நரி் சூடு இட்டுக் கொண்டதுபோல்.
column3: As the jackal branded itself in imitation of the stripes of a tiger.
The base do not talk decently, nor do they heed oaths.
Avoid lies, murder and theft.

column2: புல் உள்ள இடத்தில் மாட்டை ஆட்டை மேய ஒட்டாது.
column3: It will not allow either sheep or cows to graze where there is grass.
As long as vegetation and the earth exist.
As long as vegetation, earth, rocks and the Cauvery exist.
Selling flowers in a grass market.

column2: புல்லைத் தின்னும் மாடுபோலப் புலியைத் தின்னும் செந்நாய் உதவுமா?
column3: Will the red dog that eats tiger's flesh prove as useful as the cow that eats grass?
The low-born does not eat from a washed plate.

column2: புழுக்கை ஒழுக்கம் அறியுமா பிண்ணாக்கு சட்டி பதம் அறியுமா?
column3: Does the low-born appreciate good manners, does the oil cake pan know when the cake is baked?

column2: புழுக்கை ஒழுக்கம் அறியாது பித்தளை நாற்றம் அறியாது.
column3: Menials are ignorant of manners, brass is unconscious of its ill odour.
The sensibility of menials is in the neck.

column2: புழுங்கிப் புழுங்கிப் மா இடித்தாலும் புழுக்கைச்சிக்கு ஒரு கொழுக்கட்டை.
column3: Though the slave girl who pounds rice labours so as to perspire, her share is only a cake.
Fat money for rotten articles.

column2: புளிய மரத்தில் ஏறினவன் பல் கூசினால் இறங்குவான்.
column3: He who has climbed a tamarind tree will come down when his teeth are set on edge.
Do you come to infuse acidity into the tamarind fruit?
Even when boasting do it so as to secure belief.
Is a practical arithmetician fit to teach the theory of arithmetic?
The medicinal plant in the backyard will not avail in an emergency.
Like water on the outside of a pot.
Blinking like an owl on an ant-hill.
Coming forth like a swarm of winged white ants from an ant-hill.
Rushing as a beaver snake from its hole.

column2: புற்றிலே ஈசல் புறப்பட்டாலும், மண்ணிலே கறையான் கட்டினாலும் மழை வரவே வரும்.
column3: When winged white ants issue out of a hole, and white ants swarm, it will certainly rain.
Why weep for gold while you have the tulip tree?

column2: பூசணிக்காய் அத்தனை முத்துக் காதில் ஏற்றுகிறதா மூக்கிற் ஏற்றுகிறதா?
column3: If a pearl be as large as a pumpkin, where is it to be worn, in the ear or in the nose?
If one has taken a pumpkin, its mark may be seen on his shoulder.
Like a decayed pumpkin.
The more you gild it, the more like gold will it appear.

column2: பூசாரி பூ முடிக்கப் போனானாம், பூ ஆலங்காடு பலாக் காடாய்ப் போச்சுதாம்.
column3: The priest began to wear flowers, and the flower garden became a grove of jack trees.
Like driving a bear among those engaged in worship.
Insects do not perish, nor do worms die.

column2: பூட்டிப் புசிக்காமற் புதைப்பார் ஈயைப்போல் ஈட்டி இழப்பார்.
column3: Those who lock up their treasure and refuse to enjoy it will be deprived of it, as bees are deprived of their honey.
As a lock and its key.
The three lights which the whole world extol.
It is said that an indulged child tied a tali on his mother.

column2: பூத்துச் சொரியப் பொறுப்பார்கள் பூட்டிக் கட்டக் கலங்குவார்கள்.
column3: They can endure seeing their tree shedding its blossoms, but will be disquieted at seeing others string and wear them.
A virtuous person under whose footsteps the earth trembles not.
Till the soil, and enjoy its produce.
One cannot strive with one born under the star purádam.
The base are void of good manners.
His pedigree is weighty as mount Meru.
His title is lord of the world, but when examined he is found empty.
As grass is sold in a flower market.
May grass be sold in the flower market?

column2: பூ விற்ற காசு மணக்குமா புலால் விற்ற காசு நாறுமா?
column3: Is the money obtained from the sale of flowers fragrant, does that obtained from the sale of flesh stink?
I will enable him who sold flowers to sell gold.
As a fibre used for stringing flowers partook of their fragrance.

column2: பூவுள் மங்கையாம் பொற்கொடியாம் போன இடம் எல்லாம் செருப்படியாம்.
column3: It is said that she is Lackshmi residing in flowers, and she is a golden creeper, yet whithersoever she goes she is slippered.

column2: பூனை உள்ள இடத்திலே எலி பேரன் பேர்த்தி எடுக்கிறதாம்.
column3: The rat lives to see its grand-sons and grand-daughters in a place where there is a cat.
Can you tether an elephant in a place suited for tying up a cat?
Alms-giving and penance are not prribed to cats.
Amusement to the cat, and agony to the rat.
It is said that if a cat be merry, she will roll about on an old mat.
As a cat drinks milk.
He was quiet as a cat, and sprang like a tiger.
He cries as a parrot encountered by a cat.

column2: பூனையைத்தான் வீட்டுப் புலியென்றும், எலி அரசனென்றும் சொல்வார்கள்.
column3: A cat is called a domestic tiger, and the king of rats.
He was as quiet as a snake in a box.
The basket is torn, but the rim is strong.
Does the day break at the crowing of a hen?
Can a hen flap her wings and crow like a cock?
Even a demon will pity a woman.

column2: பெண்ணாசை ஒரு பக்கம் மண்ணாசை ஒரு பக்கம்.
column3: Love of women on the one hand, and love of property on the other.

column2: பெண்சாதி இல்லாதவன் பேயைக் கட்டித் தழுவினது போல.
column3: As the man who had no wife embraced a demoness.

column2: பெண் சாதியைத் தாய் வீட்டில் விட்டவனுக்கு ஒரு சொட்டு.
column3: A cuff for the man who left his wife at her mother’s.
Fettered with a wife, and muzzled with a child.
Enough of taking a wife and suffering the consequences.
Not to remonstrate with her husband is an ornament in a woman.
A straw twist for women to squat on.

column2: பெண்டுகளுக்கு பெற்றார் இடத்திலும் பிள்ளைகள் இடத்திலும் மூப்பு இல்லை.
column3: Women have no influence over parents and children.
Women are not in danger of forfeiting their rice.

column2: பெண்ணரம்பைக் கூத்துக்குப் போய் பேய்க் கூத்து ஆச்சுதே.
column3: The going to a dance of celestials ended as a dance of devils.

column2: பெண்ணின் குணம் அறிவேன் சம்பந்தி வாய் அறிவேன்.
column3: I know the character of the bride, and the boisterousness of her mother-in-law.

column2: பெண்ணுக்குப் பொன் இட்டுப்பார், சுவருக்கு மண் இட்டுப்பார்.
column3: Look at a woman after adorning her with jewels, and at a wall when you have plastered it with mud.
Is any one ever tired of women and wealth?
Having gone to take a wife, can you turn back because marriage is expensive?
For a wife, a mother-in-law, for a boy, a tutor.
Did she give one a wife, or did she give one her eye?
Are you content to lose your mother in order to pardon your wife?

column2: பெண்ணைக் கொண்டு பையன் பேயானான், பிள்ளைப் பெற்றுச் சிறுக்கி நாயானாள்.
column3: Having married a wife the boy has become a fool, having given birth to a child the damsel has become mean in appearance.

column2: பெண்ணை வேண்டும் என்றால் இளியற் கண்ணை நக்கு.
column3: If you want the woman, lick her bleared eyes.
He who listens to the advice of women is a fool.
The thoughts of women are after-thoughts.
The growing of women is that of a gourd creeper.
Secure the good will of the great.
All who are high in stature are not great.

column2: பெரியோர்முன் தாழ்ந்து பேசில், நாணலைப்போல் நிமிர்ந்து கொள்வார்கள்.
column3: When speaking submissively to the great, they preserve an erect posture like a reed.

column2: பெரியோர் முன் எதிர்த்துப்பேசில், வெள்ளத்திற்க்குமுன் மரங்களைப்போல் வீழ்வார்கள்,
column3: If they should contradict the great, they will fall like trees before a flood.
A tight knot cannot be formed in a thick rope.
An earthen pot in which assafœtida was kept.
Flying like cotton before a gale of wind.

column2: பெருங் குலத்தில் பிறந்தாலும் புத்தி அற்றவன் கரும்புப் பூப்போல.
column3: Though born in a high family, a fool is like a sugar-cane flower.
Beggars presume on large gifts.
Will moisture affect a great fire.
Like a creeping plant-Dioscorea sativa-round a large tree.
No feeling of cold in a heavy fall of rain.
As long as Perumal exists holy days will be observed.

column2: பெருமாள் என்கிற பெயரை மாற்றப் பெரிய பெருமாள் ஆச்சுது.
column3: The name Perumal being changed has become great Perumal.

column2: பெருமானைச் சேர்ந்தோர்க்கு பிறப்பு இல்லை, பிச்சைச் சோற்றிற்கு எச்சில் இல்லை.
column3: Those who have attained union with God are not subject to future births, rice given in alms is not refused because it is refuse.

column2: பெருமாள் நினைத்தால் வாழ்வு குறைவா, பிரமா நினைத்தால் ஆயுசு குறைவா?
column3: If God is pleased, will there be any lack of prosperity, if Brahma favour, will one’s life be short?
Pride will diminish one’s worth.
Pride in a poor man is vain.

column2: பெருமை ஒரு முறம் புடைத்து எடுத்தால் ஒன்றும் இல்லை.
column3: When a sieve, full of pride, is sifted, nothing remains.
The great and the little come from the mouth.

column2: பெருமைக்கு ஆட்டை அடித்துப் பிள்ளை கையில் காதைச் சுட்டுக் கொடுத்தான்.
column3: He killed a sheep to show his greatness, and gave the ear to his child after roasting it.

column2: பெரு ரூபத்தை உடையவரும் பிரயோசனமாய் இருக்கமாட்டார். அதுபோல, பனை விதை பெரிதாய் இருந்தும் நிழல் கொடுக்கமாட்டாது.
column3: The great are not always helpful, the lofty palmyrah casts no shade.

column2: பெரு வயிறு கொண்டது அறியாமல் சீமந்தத்திற்கு நாள் இட்டுக் கொண்டான்.
column3: Not knowing that his wife is affected with dropsy, he has fixed upon a day for the performance of her simantham ceremony.A ceremony relating to the first pregnancy, including bathing, the parting of the hair in the middle of the forehead, putting on jewels, &c., &c.

column2: பெற்ற தாய் இடத்திலோ கற்ற வித்தை காட்டுகிறாய்?
column3: Do you practise your arts on your mother?

column2: பெற்ற தாய் செத்தால் பெற்ற அப்பன் சிற்றப்பன்.
column3: If a mother dies, the father becomes uncle.

column2: பெற்ற தாய் பசித்திருக்கப் பிராமண போஜனம் செய்ததுபோல.
column3: Like feeding brahmans when one’s mother is starving.

column2: பெற்றது எல்லாம் பிள்ளையா இட்டது எல்லாம் பயிரா?
column3: Are all that are brought forth children, is all that is sown available for use?

column2: பெற்றது எல்லாம் பிள்ளையோ வளைந்தது எல்லாம் குசக்கலமோ?
column3: Are all that are brought forth children, is all earthen ware perfect that is fashioned by a potter?

column2: பெற்ற மனம் பித்து, பிள்ளை மனம் கல்லு.
column3: The mother’s heart is soft, that of her child is as a stone.
The wise are not moved by relationship or anger.
Any language may be learnt by continual speaking.
No fault arises if nothing is spoken.
When he speaks it is to no purpose, when he does not speak he is accounted dumb.
A noisy dog is not fit for hunting.
In speech he is Ravana, but he turns out to be Kumbhakarnaan.
Is contradiction becoming?
He sells his words, and cooks and drinks.
Like a weapon in the hands of a hermaphrodite.
Like a sword in the hands of a hermaphrodite.
He who is impartial is just.

column2: பேதைகள் வெள்ளத்திலே நின்றும் தாகத்திற்குத் தண்ணீருக்கு அலைவார்கள்.
column3: Fools in the midst of a flood will wander about for water to drink.
Simplicity is the ornament of a woman.
Though like a demon she is a mother, though mere slop, it is buttermilk.
Even a demon will have some reason to assign.
Like a pole on which a demon dances.
One may take a demon, but not take a wife.
Like pouring out toddy to a demon.
As margosa leaves before a demon.
Like setting a demon to work.
A devil dance is a garden of castor oil plants, if one gets in he is not seen again.
If a demon smiles it is bad, and if he weep that too is inauspicious.
Will a child struck by a demon survive?
As a demon ascended a tamarind tree.
The avaricious must be overcome by notorious lies.
Avarice ends in loss.

column2: பேர் இல்லாச் சன்னதி பாழ், பிள்ளை இல்லாச் செல்வம் பாழ்.
column3: The presence of one without reputation does no good, wealth without a child is useless.
Yield not to a demon.
He is worthy to be sung by poets.
If you walk gently, the earth will bear you.

column2: பைய மிதித்தது வேடன் அடி, பதறி மிதித்தது பன்றி அடி.
column3: The light footstep is that of the hunter, the firm footstep is that of the hog.
If masticated slowly even a palmyrah tree may be chewed.
The wealth tied up in a bag was lost.
It is said that a toothless dame appreciated the rice flour.

column2: பொங்கும் காலம் புளி பூக்கும், மங்கும் காலம் மா பூக்கும்.
column3: In times of plenty the tamarind tree blossoms, in times of scarcity the mango bears in abundance.
Am I to measure out the hire, before measuring out the load?

column2: பொதியை வைத்து விட்டுப் பிச்சைக்குப் போனான், அதையும் வைத்து விட்டுச் செத்துக் கிடந்தான்.
column3: Having loaded his bullock, he went abegging; the product he put on one side, and died.
Am I to measure out the hire before adjusting the load!
Like a fleshy gourd.

column2: பொய் இருந்து புலம்பும் மெய் இருந்து விழிக்கும்.
column3: Falsehood will never cease to weep, truth will ever be conspicuous.

column2: பொய் உடை ஒருவன் சொல் வன்மையினால் மெய்போலும்மே மெய்போலும்மே.
column3: The falsehood of a liar by reason of its force, may appear like truth, may appear like truth.
The mouth accustomed to lies will be deprived of food.
The mouth accustomed to lies will be deprived of even parched corn.

column2: பொய் சொல்லி வாழ்ந்தவனும் இல்லை, மெய் சொல்லிக் கெட்டவனும் இல்லை.
column3: No one ever prospered by telling lies, no one was ever reduced to poverty by speaking truth.
Will falsehood conquer truth?
The false love of money, will take away the real love of divine grace.

column2: பொய்யும் ஒரு பக்கம் பொறாமையும் ஒரு பக்கம்.
column3: Falsehood on one side, and envy on the other.
Though you tell lies, do so consistently.
Preserve your substance and live.
Will covetous desires and conscience agree?
Giving one’s wealth and incurring censure.
Wealth and pleasure will be separated from us, but virtue will abide.
Whithersoever wealth goes, sorrow follows in the same path.
The wicked heart resists reproof.
Avoid whatever is evil.
Inauspicious times come without giving notice.

column2: பொல்லாதவர்கள் சினப்பட்டால் கல்வின் பிளவு போல ராசியாகமாட்டார்கள்.
column3: The anger of the wicked is like a fracture in a stone, they are not easily reconciled.
Is there any medicine for a bad temper?

column2: பொல்லாதவர்கள் சங்காத்தம் உப்பு மணலில் வீழ்ந்த நீர் போல.
column3: The friendship of the wicked is as bitter as water in brackish soil.
A bad child is worse than none.

column2: பொழுது பட்ட இடம் விடுதி விட்ட இடம்.
column3: Halting where the sun sets.
The day has dawned, sin is ended.
He alone who conquers his senses is a teacher of wisdom.
Forbearance is greater than the ocean.

column2: பொறுத்தல் கசப்பாய் இருந்தாலும் பொறுக்கப் பொறுக்கத் தித்திப்பு.
column3: Although suffering may be bitter, continued endurance will make it sweet.
Those who put up with injuries may rule the earth.
They who endure will reign as kings, the irascible will wander through the jungles.

column2: பொறுமை புண்ணியத்திற்கு வேர் பொருளாசை பாவத்திற்கு வேர்.
column3: Patient endurance is the root of religious merit, avarice is the root of sin.

column2: பொற் கலம் ஒலிக்காது வெண் கலம் ஒலிக்கும்.
column3: A gold vessel does not sound, a brass one does.

column2: பொற் காப்புக்கு ஆசைப்பட்டுப் புலியின் கையில் அகப்பட்டது போல.
column3: Like one whose desire for a gold bracelet, hurried him into the claws of a tiger.

column2: பொற் பூவின் வாசனையும் முருக்கம் பூவின் வாசனையும் சரி.
column3: The smell of a flower of gold and that of the murukku flower are alike.
Does a golden flower diffuse fragrance?

column2: பொன் ஆபரணத்தைப் பாரக்கிலும் புகழ் ஆபரணமே பெரிது.
column3: The ornament of reputation is greater than ornaments of gold.

column2: பொன் இரவல் உண்டு, பூ இரவல் உண்டா?
column3: Gold may be lent, can flowers?
If the word gold be uttered, even a corpse will open its mouth.
May one cut his throat with a knife because it is made of gold?
Like the demon that guarded treasure.
Though golden slippers, they must be put on the feet.
Why should a woman who has no gold beads be called Ammani?
One should wear either a gold crown, or matted hair.
If one has a golden house, what house can he not get?

column2: பொன்னம்பலத்துக்கும் புவனகிரிப் பட்டணத்துக்கும் என்றைக்கும் உண்டான இழவு.
column3: The golden hall of-Chidambaram, and the town Puvanagiri, are always in trouble.

column2: பொன்னாங்காணிக்குப் புளி விட்டு ஆக்கினால் உண்ணாக்கு எல்லாம் தித்திக்கும்.
column3: If acid be mixed with ponnánkáni-Illecebrum sessile-its flavour will be agreeable to the whole palate.

column2: பொன்ணாங்காணிக்குப் புளி விட்டு ஆக்கினால் உண்ணாப் பெண்ணும் ஒரு உழக்கு உண்ணும்.
column3: The compound of ponnánkáni greens-illecebrum-and tamarind, will enable a girl who has lost her appetite to eat an ulak of rice.
He ulcerates the golden mind.

column2: பொன்னாலே கலம் உண்டானாலும் மண்ணாலே சுவர் வேண்டும்.
column3: Though one may possess vessels of gold, the wall of his house must be of mud.

column2: பொன்னாலே மருமகளானாலும் மண்ணாலே ஒரு மாமி வேண்டும்.
column3: Though the daughter-in-law is made of gold, she must have a mother-in-law made of earth.

column2: பொன்னின் கலப்பை வரகுக்கு உழப் போனதாம் வரகு சேருக்கு வரகு பட்டதாம்.
column3: Golden ploughs were used for the cultivation of the millet, and the crop was less than the seed-corn.

column2: பொன்னின் குடத்திற்குப் பொட்டு இட்டு பார்க்க வேண்டுமா?
column3: Must a royal mark be inscribed on a golden pot, that it may appear the more beautiful?

column2: பொன்னின் குடம் உடைந்தாற் பொன் ஆகும் என்ன ஆகும் மண்ணின் குடம் உடைந்தாக்கால்?
column3: Though broken to pieces a golden pot will still be gold, of what use is an earthen pot when broken?

column2: பொன்னை வைக்கிற கோயிலிலே பூவையாவது வைக்க வேண்டும்.
column3: Flowers, at least, must be offered in a temple in which gold is offered.
Although you may throw away gold, you may not throw away edible herbs!
Inquire the way to the village whither you are not going.

column2: போகாத இடத்திலே போனால் வராத சொட்டு வரும்.
column3: If you go where you ought not, you will receive a cuff that might have been avoided.
An impudent person is greater than a king.

column2: போக்கு அற்ற நாய்க்கு போனது எல்லாம் வழி.
column3: A hungry dog finds a way wherever be goes.
Though one’s food be slight, the dish must be large.

column2: போதகர் சொல்லைத் தட்டாதே, பாதகர் இல்லைக் கிட்டாதே.
column3: Obey your religious teacher, approach not the house of the wicked.
A religious teacher meets with many temptations.

column2: போதும் என்ற மனமே பொன் செய்யும் மருந்து.
column3: A contented mind is a specific for making gold.
Akin to seeking a needle in a heap of straw.
Having stacked your corn, why go abegging?

column2: போரோடு தின்கிற மாட்டுக்குப் பிடுங்கிப் போட்டுக் கட்டுமா?
column3: When an ox is accustomed to eat at a heap, will it be satisfied with a handful?
Do not practise the art of war.
It is said that those who steal from a corn-stack, will frighten those who glean stealthily.

column2: போலைக்கு ஒரு பொன்மணி கிடைத்ததாம், அதைத் தூக்கக் கண்ணில் தொட்டுத் தொட்டுப் பார்த்ததாம்.
column3: It is said that the hollow-headed woman obtained a gold bead, and that she examined it when she was sleepy.

column2: போலை பொறுக்கப் போச்சாம் பூனை குறுக்கே போச்சாம்.
column3: It is said that the destitute went out to gather orts, and a cat went across the path.
That which one eats as the fruit of his own labour, is properly called food.
Like inviting a fever that has subsided, by giving acids.
He lacks sense who broods over the past.

column2: போன மாட்டைத் தேடுவாரும் இல்லை, மேய்த்த கூலி கொடுப்பாரும் இல்லை.
column3: There is no one to seek the lost cow, none to pay the hire of the cowherd.
Be graceful as the moon.
The greatness of the ocean shows the work of God.

column2: பௌவம் உற்றது ஆக்கை, செவ்வை அற்றது வாழ்க்கை.
column3: The body is like a bubble, one’s present existence is not lasting.
When butted by a ram of Mahadévar, even the demon shakes its head.

column2: மகளுக்குப் புத்தி சொல்லித் தாய் அவசாரி போனாளாம்.
column3: The mother, having given advice to her daughter, played the harlot.
The daughter is dead, the mother is become destitute.
If the daughter die her remains are regarded as a pinam; if the son, his corpse is a savam.

column2: மகன் செத்தாலும் சாகட்டும் மருமகள் கொட்டம் அடங்கினாற் போதும்.
column3: No matter if my son should die, it will suffice if the arrogance of my daughter-in-law is checked.
Mahámaham festival is celebrated every twelfth year.
Even a crow if it arrive at Maháméru is said to assume a golden hue.

column2: மகா ராஜாவின் கலியாணத்தில் நீராகாரம் நெய் பட்ட பாடு.
column3: At the marriage feast of the Maha Rajah, even cold rice water is rare as ghee.
As Maha Lakshmi went on a pilgrimage.
Illustrious beauty, leave the door ajar.

column2: மகிமைக்கு அஞ்சிய மருமகனே எருமைக் கன்றைக் கொல்லாதே.
column3: Thou modest son-in-law, do not kill the young buffaloa.This was said to a man by his mother-in-law. Wishing to appear to her a small eater he stinted himself at his ordinary meals, and yet appeared to flourish. Casting about for a solution of the mystery her attention was called to the ill condition of a young buffaloa. Suspecting that her son-in-law was the cause of this, she resolved to watch his movements. The following night she saw him emerge from his room and proceed to the buffaloa, whose milk he exhausted, and returned to his own quarters. On the morrow when he took his food she addressed him in the language of the proverb.
Death to the distinguished, homage to the cowherd.

column2: மகிமையிலே ஒரு பெண் குவளையிலே வாழுகிறாள், அதில் ஒரு பெண் அறுத்துவிட்டு அழுகிறாள்.
column3: One matron flourishes in the water-lily, and one weeps bereft of her marriage symbol.
The dishonest tricks of children, and the husks of varagu are alike.
To be fed by children is a disgrace to parents.
Like going on a pilgrimage to Mecca, and catching a crane.
Montgomery came, and it rained fine gold.

column2: மங்கும் காலம் மாங்காய் பொங்கும் காலம் புளியங்காய்.
column3: In time of scarcity, mangoes, and in a season of plenty, tamarind fruit, are abundant.

column2: மங்கை தீட்டானால் கங்கையிலே முழுகுவாள் கங்கை தீட்டானால் எங்கே முழுகுவாள்?
column3: If the damsel is polluted she may be cleansed by the ganges, but if the ganges be polluted whither can she go?

column2: மச்சத்தின் குஞ்சுக்கு இப்படி என்றால், மாதாவுக்கு எப்படியோ?
column3: If such be the condition of the young fish, what will be that of the mother?

column2: மச்சானைப் பார்க்க உறவும் இல்லை, மயிரைப் பார்க்க கறுப்பும் இல்லை.
column3: No friendship superior to that of a cousin, nothing blacker than hair.The word மச்சான் is used for a brother-in-law, and also for a maternal uncle’s son.

column2: மச்சான் செத்தால் மயிறு போச்சு கம்பளி மெத்தை நமக்கு ஆச்சு.
column3: If my brother-in-law die I care not a hair, his cumbly mattress will be mine.
If the roof be destroyed, the house will not answer for a hut.

column2: மஞ்சனமும் மலரும் கொண்டு துதிக்காவிட்டாலும் நெஞ்சகத்தில் நினைப்பதே போதகம்.
column3: Though one may not worship god by bathing him, and scattering flowers on him, we are taught to keep him in mind.
A prudent youth is superior to a stupid old man.
A stupid question needs no answer.

column2: மடப் பெருமையே தவிர நீராகாரத்திற்கும் வழி இல்லை.
column3: Besides a choultry, there is nothing,—not even water.

column2: மடம் பிடுங்கிக் கொண்டு போகும்போது நந்தவனத்திக்கு வழி எங்கே என்கிறான்.
column3: After the choultry has been destroyed, he asks the way to the flower garden.
Are you about to behead one upon whom you have forced mango fruit?
If you have money in your waist-cloth, you may be afraid on the way.

column2: மடியைப் பிடித்து கள்ளை வார்த்து மயிரைப் பிடித்து காசு வேண்டுகிறதா?
column3: Having forced one to take toddy, do you seize him by the hair and demand payment?
Moderation in eating exhilerates the mind.

column2: மட்டி எருக்கலை மடல் மடலாய்ப் பூத்தாலும், மருக்கொழுந்து வாசனை வருமா?
column3: Though the erukkalai bears bunches of flowers, has it the fragrance of southern-wood-artemisia austriaca?

column2: மட்டு இல்லாமற் கொடுத்தாலும் திட்டுக் கேட்கல் ஆகாது.
column3: Though he gives liberally, it is not right to hear his abuse.
Palm-stem charcoal and Madappallis are not to be trusted.

column2: மணலின்மேல் விழுந்த மழைத்துளி உடனே மறையும்; அதுபோல, பொல்லாதவர்களுக்குச் செய்த உபகாரம் மறைந்துபோம்.
column3: Drops of rain falling on sand will instantly disappear, in like manner favours shewn to the wicked will be soon forgotten.
Like picking out stones when eating a dish of sand.
So to govern a kingdom as not to move even the tongue of a bell.
May one descend into a river relying on a mud horse?
The knot of hair must be proportioned to the size of the head.
Like writing on the skull and covering it with the hair.
As long as the head remains phlegm will abide.
They may have house and grounds who know how to take care of them.
Dried cow-dung is the proper form of wedding cake, when the bridegroom is made of sand.
Made of earth, thou art earthy.
Whilst on earth pervert not judgment, in the case of a woman.
Though what you may eat be sand, eat it in a secluded place.
It were better to be born of the earth, than to be your brother.
Do not live by extortion.
Though earthen, one’s own child is precious.

column2: மண் பூனை ஆனாலும் எலிப் பிடித்தால் சரி.
column3: Though the cat is made of mud, if it catch rats, it is enough.
Is a hoe sensible of cold?
Are you engaged in making earthen ware, or sores?
A child has neither the power of singing nor discretion.
He is comparable to Mathana mountain.
Love of father, caste, and country.
It is not well to tread even on the threshold of a house in which you are not respected.
Thy purpose and thy destiny.The settled judgment, and the decrees of God, are in harmony.
As the stars surround the moon.
He is like a cat on a wall.Spoken of one who makes the most of his position.
Like the uproar of a honey drop.
The way to Madura is in the mouth.
The incantation is one fourth, and common sense three fourths.
Will mangoes fall by a charm?
Religious ceremonies not regulated by a form, will continue till sun-set.
It is the attribute of a minister to foretell things likely to occur.

column2: மந்திரி இல்லா யோசனையும் ஆயுதம் இல்லாச் சேனையும் கெடும்.
column3: Decisions without councillors, and troops without arms, will perish.
Do you expect milk in the fold, and curds at home?
The damsel who has a fine head of hair, combs and dresses it.

column2: மயிர் ஊடாடாதார் நட்புச் சிறிதுபொருள் ஊடாடக் கெடும்.
column3: Friendship so close that a hair cannot be introdued between the parties, will be destroyed if money matters interpose.
Can charcoal be formed by burning hair?
Like seeking means to split a hair.

column2: மயிலாப்பூர் ஏரி உடைத்துப்போகிறது என்றால், வருகிற கமிட்டிக்கு ஆகட்டும் என்றாற்போல.
column3: Like saying it may be deferred to the next committee, on hearing that the Mylapore tank has given way.
If you exclaim O peacock, O peacock, will it give you its feathers?
The peacock-eyed bride is phrensied, her bridegroom is in anguish.
Be more afraid of a vicious course than of death.
There are endless ways that lead to death.
As an ox trampled under foot a man that had fallen from a tree.
The fruit of a tree will fall at its foot.

column2: மரத்தை இலை காக்கும் மானத்தைப் பணம் காக்கும்.
column3: Leaves cover a tree, money covers one’s nakedness.
Can you obtain musk from a polecat?
The tree affords shade to the man who is felling it.

column2: மரம் வெட்டுகிறவனுக்கு நிழலும், மண் தோண்டுகிறவனுக்கு இடமும் கொடுக்கும்.
column3: The tree affords shade to him who fells it, the earth supports him who digs it.

column2: மரம் ஏறிக் கை விட்டவனும், கடன் வாங்கிக் கடன் கொடுத்தவனும் கெட்டான்.
column3: He who let go his hold after climbing a tree, and he who borrowed money to lend, came to grief.
He who planted the tree will water it.
He who was not respectful lost his reputation.
Mary’s domestic life has come to an end.
To a gloomy eye all obscure things are demons.

column2: மருத்து மாவுக்குச் சயிக்கினை, மடங்காக் குதிரைக்குச் சவுக்கடி.
column3: A sign to a fleet horse, whipping to an obstinate one.
Medicine one fourth, common sense three fourths.
Three days for testing a medicine, and for a feast.
Though the quantity be little, like medicine, distribute before eating it.

column2: மருமகனுக்கென்று சமைத்ததை மகனுக்கு இட்டு வயிறு எரிந்தாளாம்.
column3: It is said that what she cooked for her son-in-law she gave to her son, and was distressed about it.
Like remembering, when hungry, the food of the marriage feast.
Does the barren woman understand the pains of parturition?
Does the barren woman know the endearments of children?
If the barren woman be asked to bring forth a child, will she do so?

column2: மலரில் மணமும், எள்ளில் எண்ணையும், உடலில் உயிரும், கலந்தது போல.
column3: All pervading, like fragrance in a flower, oil in sesamum seed, and life in the body.
When commodities are abundant, they come to market.

column2: மலை அத்தனை சுவாமிக்குத் தினை அத்தனை புஷ்பம்.
column3: A flower, as small as a millet-seed, is dedicated to an idol as large as a mountain.

column2: மலை அத்தனை சுவாமிக்குத் மலை அத்தனை புஷ்பம் போடுகிறார்களா?
column3: Do they dedicate flowers as large as mountains, to idols as large as monntains?
If a mountain be the target, even a blind man may shoot.
It is difficult to roll a stone to the top of a hill.
Though you ascend the mountains, do not leave behind your brother-in-law, or the son of your maternal uncle.
Will mountain honey come to the lame man?

column2: மலை நெல்லிக்காய்க்கும் கடல் உப்புக்கும் உறவு செய்தவர் ஆர்?
column3: Who created the affinity between the mountain nelli fruit, and sea-salt?
It is said that when a brahman who was equal to a mountain was dying, his wife was weeping for his tuft of hair.
All that has come upon thee like mountains, shall pass away as dew.
As a mountain amidst thunder brought forth a mouse.
Can the wild hog rush on these who are on the mountain top?
Although produced on the mountains, the rice must be prepared for use in a mortar.

column2: மலையே மண்ணாங்கட்டி ஆகிறபோது, மண்ணாங்கட்டி எப்படி ஆகும்?
column3: When a mountain becomes a sod, what will the sod be like?
Should a mountain fall, the head must bear it.
Like excavating a mountain and catching a rat.

column2: மலையைச் சுற்றி அடித்தவனைச் செடியைச் சுற்றி அடியேனா?
column3: After having chased and beaten him round a mountain, will it be difficult to do so round a bush?
Is not a small chisel sufficient to perforate a rock?
As if an adze and chisel came to perforate the rock.

column2: மலையைப் பார்த்து நாய் குலைத்தால் மலைக்குக் கேடோ நாய்க்குக் கேடோ?
column3: If a dog bark at a mountain, will the mountain be injured or the dog?
A rock supports a rock, can a sod do so?
Will a door be a difficulty to my aunt who has swallowed a mountain?
Is a sod used as a chutney, to swallow a mountain?
When one spits lying on his back, the spittle will fall on his breast.
Silence may defy a mountain.
ம being elided, ப has appeared.

column2: மழைக்கால் இருட்டானாலும் மந்தி கொம்பு இழந்து பாயுமா?
column3: Even in the darkness of the rainy season, will a monkey when leaping, miss the branch?
Who draws and pours water into the clouds?
Can you put up a hurdle to keep out the rain?
Is the hurdle to keep out rain, or the thunderbolt?

column2: மழை பெய்து நிறையாதது மொண்டு வார்த்தால் நிறையுமா?
column3: Can that be filled with a watering pot which cannot be filled by the rain?

column2: மழை முகம் காணாத பயிரும், தாய் முகம் காணாத பிள்ளையும்.
column3: Vegetation without rain, a child without a mother.
Even Mahadéva does not know when it will rain, nor when a child will be born.
Though the rain has ceased, the drizzling has not.
An article forgotten is not good even for one’s children.

column2: மறந்து செத்தேன் பிராணன் வா என்றால் வருமா?
column3: If one say, I died through forgetfulness, will life return?
Seek not empty pleasures to purify the heart.
Mental anxiety will diminish one’s strength.
If the conscience condemn, other evidence is unnecessary.
Enmity at heart, friendship on the lips.
A secret that should be concealed in the mind is uttered by a fool.

column2: மனதிற்கு மனதே சாட்சி, மற்றதற்குத் தெய்வமே சாட்சி.
column3: The heart is its own witness, God is the witness of the rest.
Can a mind be ignorant of its own falsehood?
He who utters prayers daily has no anxiety of mind.
That which is agreeable to the mind is a palace.
Be not confused.
Although willing he will lack means.

column2: மனிதர் காணும் பொழுது மவுனம், இராதபொழுதில் உருத்திராக்ஷப்பூனை.
column3: Silent in the presence of men, in their absence a beaded cat.
Man forgets, is reduced in circumstances, changes and vanishes.

column2: மனுஷன் தலையை மான் தலை ஆக்குகிறான் மான் தலையை மனுஷன் தலை ஆக்குகிறான்.
column3: He can transform a man’s head into the head of a deer, and he can make a man’s head out of a deer’s head.

column2: மனையாள் விடியுமுன் எழுந்து வீட்டுப் பண் செய்வாள்.
column3: A wife gets up before day-break and looks after her domestic affairs.

column2: மனையாளுக்கு உற்றது ஒன்றும் சொல்லவேண்டாம், மாற்றானை ஒரு நாளும் நம்பவேண்டாம்.
column3: Do not disclose your secrets to your wife, nor trust an enemy at any time.
Is there any remedy for mental sickness?

column2: மன்னவர்கள் செத்தார்கள், மந்திரிகள் செத்தார்கள் முன் இருந்தோர் எல்லாம் முடிந்தார்கள்.
column3: Kings have perished, their prime ministers have perished, and all who lived before, are dead.
The government of kings depends on the vigor of their councillors.
A sceptre of justice is the beauty of a king.

column2: மா இருக்கிற மணத்தைப் போல் அல்லவோ கூழ் இருக்கிற குணம்.
column3: As is the flour so is the gruel.
If there be flour, cakes may be baked.
The accretion of a particle to a mountain increases its size.
He who drove back the cows was Arjuna.
Is a beef-eater accustomed to decent words?
When cows return fatigued the household will suffer want.

column2: மாடு மேய்க்காமற் கெட்டது, பயிர் பார்க்காமற் கெட்டது.
column3: Without pasture cows die; without care vegetation perishes.
A bull's term of existence is in his sack.
A cow is otherwise called a large he-goat.

column2: மாட்டைப் புல் உள்ள தலத்திலும், மனுஷனைச் சோறு உள்ள தலத்திலும் இருக்க ஒட்டாது.
column3: It will not allow the cow to be content in its pasture, nor a man with his boiled rice.
It is said that he tended the cows and threw away the goad.
There is a spare leg, for the one made of a ruby.
He will leap like a deer, ten miles in a month.
The faults of a mother are visited on her children.

column2: மாதா மனம் எரிய வாழாள் ஒரு நாளும்.
column3: Whilst her mother’s heart is wounded, she will not prosper even for a day.
If one exceed his limit, his ancestry will be exposed.
The more the flour is leavened the better for the cakes.

column2: மாப் பொன் இருக்க மக்களைச் சாவக் கொடுப்பேனா?
column3: Whilst I possess a particle of gold, will I allow my children to die?

column2: மா மறந்த கூழுக்கு உப்பு ஒரு கேடா?
column3: Does the absence of salt spoil gruel made without flour?
Though a mother-in-law may be reconciled, the broken pieces of an earthen pot cannot be reunited.

column2: மாமியார் உடை குலைந்தால் வாயாலும் சொல்லக்கூடாது, கையாலும் காட்டக்கூடாது.
column3: If the dress of a mother-in-law be out of order, it must not be spoken of, or pointed at by the hand.

column2: மாமியார் உடைத்தால் மண்கலம், மருமகள் உடைத்தால் பொன்கலம்.
column3: If broken by the mother-in-law it is an earthen vessel, if by the daughter-in-law, it is a golden vessel.
As the son-in-law is embarrassed in the presence of his mother-in-law.

column2: மாமியார் செத்த ஆறாம் மாதம் மருமகள் கண்ணில் கண்ணீர் வந்ததாம்.
column3: It is said that six months after the death of the mother-in-law, a tear came into the eye of the daughter-in-law.
Like the wailing of a daughter-in-law, on account of the death of her mother-in-law.
Will my mother-in-law never die, will my sorrows never end?
The house of the mother-in-law is very comfortable.

column2: மாயக்காரர் எல்லாம் பாதகர், மாறுபாட்டுக்காரர் எல்லாம் சாதகர்.
column3: All impostors are perfidious villains, all double-dealers are practically so.
A hypocrite is worse than a demon.
Without rain nothing can be effected.

column2: மாரி காலத்தில் பதின் கல மோரும், கோடை காலத்தில் ஒரு படி நீரும் சரி.
column3: Ten kalams of buttermilk in the wet season, are worth one measure of water in the hot season.
The hire for beating their breasts is at once on the lap.
If the breasts be pendent, they must be supported by the stomach.
Touch your breast, and keep it in your mind.

column2: மாலை குளித்து மனையில் புகும், தன் மனையில் ஆசையும் சேர்க்காது.
column3: In the evening a crow bathes before it goes to its nest, and will not admit a stranger.Many Natives attribute to the crow five peculiarities of wich that indicated in this proverb is one. The peculiarities, or habits are as follows:—(a) Going forth very early of a morning. (b) Never being seen to pair. (c) Eating together. (d) Bathing before going to their nest. (e) Warning their companions of apparent danger.

column2: மாலை சுற்றிப் பெண் பிறந்தால் மாமனுக்கு ஆகாது.
column3: For a girl to be born with a garland round her neck is ominous to her maternal uncle.This மாலை may be either the navel-string or a thin membrane that covers the head, and which, sometimes decends like a ring, to the neck, it is then called மாலை, if otherwise முகமூடி a veil.

column2: மாலை சுற்றிப் பிறந்த பிள்ளை மாமனுக்கு ஆகாது.
column3: If a child be born with its navel string round its neck, it is ominous to its uncle.
A share in the dough, a share in the mangos.
Cakes proportioned to the flour.
If you eat the dough you will not get your ‘'share of'’ cake.
Will the tree be pliable because the sapling was so?
Yield not to a foe.
The eye is on the heavens, the mind is on the bridegroom.

column2: மானத்தை வில்லாய் வளைப்பான் மணலைக் கயிறாய்த் திரிப்பான்.
column3: He is able to bend the sky as a bow, and he can twist ropes of sand.
When honour is perishing, is life worth preservation?
Is honour great, or is life?
Does the spacious earth refuse to uphold any one?

column2: மானுக்கு ஒரு புள்ளி ஏறி என்ன, குறைந்து என்ன?
column3: What matters it whether the deer has more or fewer spots?
Among mankind one destitute of humanity is as chaff.
More beautiful than the eye of a deer, more rapid ‘'than its'’ speed.
As the tiger sprang on a herd of deer.
The greater the inordinate desire, the greater the loss.

column2: மிகுதி உள்ளவனுக்கும் வஞ்சனைக்காரனுக்கும் பகை, வேதக்காரனுக்கும் உலகத்திற்கும் பகை.
column3: Enmity exists between the wealthy and the hypocrite, and between a religious man and the world.
Both, excess and want, lead to disease.
Do not exaggerate.
It is not good for one to go westward carrying refuse.
A thing done through inadvertence may produce fear.
Be humble, and seek unfailing bliss.
The habiliments of an ascetic are to be preferred before poverty.
The haughty sells his goods sitting.
Are there any snakes that will not bite those who tread on them?
Among beasts an elephant is the biggest, and a lion the strongest.

column2: மினுக்கு உள்ள அம்பு துன்பம் செய்யும். அதுபோல, அந்தம் உள்ளவர்களும் துன்பத்தைத் தருவார்கள்.
column3: A shining arrow will occasion pain, in like manner, those who are handsome in person may produce pain.
It is said that a barber who had nothing to do, shaved a cat.
Does a fire fly dispel darkness?

column2: மின்னலைப்போல் பல்லை விளக்கானும், மினுக்கக் கொள்வானும் பதர்.
column3: He who will not clean his teeth so as to shine like lightning, and he who is fond of show, are chaff.
Does the thunder-bolt fall without previous lightning?
Does it rain without previous lightning?
As a thunder-bolt fell without lightning and thunder.
All that glitters is not gold.
Lightning is always followed by rain.
An unpiloted vessel will not sail.
Be not gluttonous.

column2: மீந்த சுண்ணாம்பையும் மெலிந்த ராஜாவையும் கைவிடல் ஆகாது.
column3: The remains of chunambu, and an enfeebled king, should not be renounced.
Like a fish swallowing a fish.
Are young fish taught to swim?
Fish curry or honey curry?
Will the distortion of the countenance be removed by looking into a mirror?
One face is a mirror to another face.

column2: முகத்துக்கு அஞ்சி மூத்தாரோடு போனால் குலத்துக்கு எல்லாம் ஈனமாம்.
column3: If a woman elope with her husband’s elder brother out of personal regard for him, it will be a disgrace to the whole family.
If the face be ugly, what can the mirror do?

column2: முகம் சந்திர பிம்பம்; அகம் பாம்பின் விஷம்.
column3: A face like the moon, a mind of deadly poison.
What! a dagger under a veil?
What, is it to make signs under a veil?
Though carried thirty miles, a hare is carried in the hand.

column2: முக்காலம் காகம் முழுகிக் குளித்தாலும் கொக்கு ஆகாது.
column3: Though a crew bathe three times a day, it will not thereby become a white crane.

column2: முக்காலம் காகம் முழுகிக் குளித்தாலும் கொக்கு ஆகுமா?
column3: Will a crow by bathing three times a day become a crane?
A triparty business is always involved.
Like a dog crouching after starting a hare.
Crooked grass prevents the flow of six kalams of water.
A lame man is very boisterous before a cripple.
As a lame man longed for the honey that hung from a branch.
Like a lame man going to market.
He who does not accomplish his object lacks training.

column2: முடிச்சுப் போனதும் அல்லாமல் இளித்தவாய்ப்பட்டமும் கூடக் கிடைத்தது.
column3: You have not only lost the bundle of money, but also incurred a reproachful name.

column2: முடிய முடிய நட்டால் கோட்டை கோட்டையாய் விளையுமா?
column3: If you plant bundles, will heaps be produced?
Never undertake a matter without due consideration as to how it is to be accomplished.

column2: முடி வைத்த தலைக்குச் சுழிசுத்தம் பார்க்கிறது, கொண்டு குலம் பேசுகிறதுபோல் இருக்கிறது.
column3: Examining the circlets of hair in a crowned head, is like discussing the tribe of a girl after marrying her.

column2: முட்ட நனைந்தவனுக்கு ஈரம் இல்லை, முழுதும் கெட்டவனுக்குத் துக்கம் இல்லை.
column3: When completely drenched one does not feel wet, when entirely ruined one feels no sorrow.

column2: முட்டரோடாடிய நட்புக் கருங்காலிக் கட்டை ஊடாடிய கால்.
column3: Friendship with the rude is like the foot of a traveller among stumps of ebony.
What double dower to a worthless woman?
Two persons for one fool.
The wrath of a fool is on his nose.

column2: முட்டிகைபோல முனியாது வைகலும், கொட்டி உண்டாகும் குறடுபோற் கைவிடுவார்.
column3: Even those who like a pair of pincers uphold their dependants and daily feed them, will leave them like a pair of tongs.
A calf that drains the udder is almost weaned.

column2: முட்டுக்கு முட்டல்ல மூடக் கதவும் அல்ல, சன்னிதிவாசலுக்குச் சார்த்தக் கதவும் அல்ல.
column3: It is neither an obstruction, nor a door to shut, nor even a screen to the temple gateway.

column2: முட்டுப்பட்டும் ஜெயம் வருமானால் குட்டுப்பட்டாற் குறை என்ன?
column3: If victory comes by being pressed with want, what matters a cuff on the head?
Do not spend on your capital.
Clever at the beginning, indolent at the end.

column2: முதலுக்கு மோசமாக இருக்கிறது இலாபத்துக்குச் சண்டை போடுகிறதா?
column3: When the principal is in danger, do you quarrel about the interest?
Already weak, and withal pregnant.
A crocodile cares not whether the water is deep or shallow.

column2: முதலை தன் இடத்து, மலை ஒத்த ஆனையையும் இழுத்துச் செல்லும்.
column3: In his own element, the alligator will carry off an elephant as big as a mountain.
Like merchants who do not increase the capital they invest.

column2: முதல் இல்லார்க்கு ஊதியம் இல்லை, மதலையாம் சார்ப்பில்லார்க்கு நிலை இல்லை.
column3: Those who have no capital have no gain, those who have no sons to lean on, have no support.
Those not possessed of capital, have no gains.
When the funeral procession was moving forward, the tomtom beater died.
What, dim-sighted at the beginning of the Alphabet?
If crooked at first, it will be so throughout.

column2: முதுகிலே புண் உண்டானால் செடியிலே நுழையப் பயம்.
column3: If one has a wound on his back, he will fear to pass under a bush.

column2: முத்தால் நத்தை பெருமைப்படும், மூடர் எத்தாலும் பெருமைப்படார்.
column3: A snail is precious by reason of its pearl, fools have naught wherewith to attain greatness.

column2: முத்திலும் சொத்தை உண்டு, பவழத்திலும் பழுது உண்டு.
column3: Flaws may be found in pearls and also in coral.

column2: முந்தி வந்த செவியைப் பிந்தி வந்த கொம்பு மறைத்ததாம்.
column3: It is said that the ears which came first, were covered by the horns which sprung up afterwards.

column2: முந்தினோர் பிந்தினோர் ஆவர் பிந்தினோர் முந்தினோர் ஆவர்.
column3: The first shall be last, and the last first.

column2: முந்தின சோத்தைத் தட்டினால் பிந்தின சோறு பீயும் சோறும்.
column3: If the first food be rejected, worse may be offered.

column2: முந்நாழி கறக்கிற பசுவானாலும் இறப்பைப் பிடுங்குகிற பசு ஆகாது.
column3: Though a cow yields three measures of milk, it is not desirable if it pulls down the roof.
Obstinacy at thirty, civility at forty.
Though one may give thirty fanams, the nickname, crop-eared, will not be removed.

column2: முப்பதிலே வாழ்ந்தவன் மூடன், முன்னும் பின்னும் தெரியாதவன் குருடன்.
column3: He is a fool who prospered at thirty, he is blind who does not see before and behind.

column2: முப்பணி இட்ட பெண்ணுக்குக் கொப்பு ஒன்றுதான் குறை.
column3: To the woman adorned with three jewels, the ear ornament is the only want.

column2: முப்பது வருஷம் வாழ்ந்தவனும் இல்லை, முப்பது வருஷம் தாழ்ந்தவனும் இல்லை.
column3: None ever continued to prosper or decay for thirty years.
He is the triad who is the first and the last of all.
The industrious will never be put to shame.
A stubborn wife, a mat rolled up.
What if the murukku tree grow large, will it do for a pillar?
Will the mention of the murungai fruit affect a prescribed diet?

column2: முலைகொடுத்து வளர்த்தவள் மூதேவி, முன்றானை போட்டவள் சீதேவி.
column3: The one that nursed and brought up the child is Múdévi, the wife is Shridévi.
Is the pain in the breast of the nurse, known to the suckling?
Like the pleasure experienced when the elbow is struck.

column2: முழுகி முப்பது நாளாச்சு, இறங்கி உப்பு அள்ளக்கூட இல்லை என்கிறான்.
column3: It is thirty days since he bathed, and he says that he is so clean that touching salt would defile him.

column2: முழுக் கட்டி பெயர்க்கிற பன்றிக்குக் கொழுக் கட்டி விட்டது போல.
column3: Like arming a hog in the snout with a ploughshare, that can tare up the ground without it.
A perfect sluggard is like a hedge of thorns.
He whose share is only one three hundred and twentieth part, is more persistent than he who has a whole one.
Like attempting to conceal a whole pumpkin in a plate of rice.
Does a gemmed ferrule require an ornamental rim?

column2: முளைத்த மயிர் மூன்று அதிலும் இரண்டு புழுவெட்டு.
column3: His beard consists of three hairs, of which two are rotten at the root.

column2: முளையில் கிள்ளாததை முற்றினால் கோடாரிகொண்டு வெட்ட வேண்டும்.
column3: That which was not nipped in the bud will have to be felled with an axe when matured.
Thorns are extracted by thorns.
By nature the thorn is sharp, and the tulasi fragrant.
Who sharpens the point of a thorn?

column2: முள்ளுமேல் சீலை போட்டால் மெள்ள மெள்ள வாங்கவேண்டும்.
column3: If a cloth be spread on a thorn bush, it must be taken off with great care.
Though one may endure being struck with a sieve, he cannot endure being brow-beaten.
What is done in the forenoon will result in good or evil in the afternoon.
Do those who are drenched complain of being wet?
Do not stand in the face of a battle.
The same measure that was used before, must be used afterwards.

column2: முன் ஒன்று ஓதிப் பின் ஒன்று ஆடேல்.
column3: Do not say one thing and do another.
If the fore-arm be stretched, the elbow will be so also.
Anger first, and pity afterwards.

column2: முன் விட்டுப் பின் நின்று கழுத்து அறுக்கலாமா?
column3: Having given one the lead, will you follow and cut his throat?

column2: முன்னவனே முன் நின்றால் முடியாத பொருள் உளதோ?
column3: If the eternal be with you, will anything be impossible?
Biting before, and kicking behind.

column2: முன்னே பார் பின்னே பார் உன்னைப் பார் என்னைப் பார்.
column3: Look before, look behind, look at yourself, look at me.

column2: முன்னே போனால் சிசுவத்தி பின்னே போனால் பிரமத்தி.
column3: When you go before you are guilty of infanticide, when you follow you are guilty of brahmanicide.
In the forenoon a ship owner, in the afternoon a beggar.

column2: முன்னே வந்த காதைப்பார்க்கிலும் பின்னே வந்த கொம்பு பலம்.
column3: The horn that came after, is stronger than the ear that came before.
Like showing a noseless man a mirror.

column2: மூக்கறையனுக்கு வாழ்க்கைப்பட்டால் முன்னும் போகவிடான் பின்னும் போகவிடான்.
column3: If a woman is married to one whose nose is rent, he will not allow her to go before or after him.
A crop-nosed ass does not fear driving rain.
A man with a sore nose ought to become a Vaishnava mendicant, ought he not?
The pain will be felt as keenly as when the hair in the nose is plucked out.

column2: மூக்கு மயிர் பிடுங்கினால் ஆட் பாரம் குறையுமா?
column3: Will a person’s weight be diminished by pulling the hair out of his nose?
If the nostrils be closed, life will depart.

column2: மூக்கைப் பிடித்தால் வாய் ஆ வென்னத் தெரியாது.
column3: He knows not how to open his mouth when one closes his nostrils?
A bamboo mat, and an obstinate wife.
Thou art a dew drop depending from the leaf of a bamboo.

column2: மூடரோடு ஆடிய நட்புக் கடுவழியிற் கட்டை ஊடாடிய கால்.
column3: The friendship of fools is as the feet that have travelled by a jungle path covered with stumps of trees.
Speak not harshly before fools.
The companionship of fools invariably leads to loss.
Danger attends the friendship of fools.

column2: மூடனாய் இருக்கிற பிள்ளையினாலே எப்போதும் நஷ்டமே வரும்.
column3: One always sustains loss if he has a stupid child.
Adversity and disgrace form the lot of fools.

column2: மூடன் சண்டை மூட்டுப் பிரிக்கும், மோர்க்கடன் வீட்டைத் தொடும்.
column3: The quarreling of fools will break friendship, debt on account of buttermilk will affect one’s house.

column2: மூடின முத்து மூன்று லோகம் பெறும், மூடாத முத்து முக்காற் காசும் பெறாது.
column3: A pearl concealed is worth the three worlds, one that is uncovered wont fetch three quarters of a cash.
A porter’s sense is in his knees.
The first-born is a hornless animal, the younger is a bull.
The utterances of elderly persons are ambrosia.
The charitably disposed exert themselves even in old age.

column2: மூப்பு ஏன் பிடிப்பது, மூதேவி வாசத்திற்கு அடையாளம்.
column3: Why does one grow old? it is a sign that he is under the influence of the goddess of misfortune.
The stubborn and crocodiles are alike.
The goddess of misfortune dwells in the face of the stubborn.

column2: மூர்க்கனைச் சேர்ந்தவன் வாழான், மூடனைச் சேர்ந்தவன் படியான்.
column3: He who associates with the angry will not prosper, and he who associates with fools will not learn.

column2: மூர்க்கம் உள்ள ராஜாவும் மூட மந்திரியும் அழிவார்கள்.
column3: A king that is easily provoked, and a prime minister wanting discretion, will come to ruin.
Associate not with the angry.
He who is acquainted with botany may govern the three worlds.
It will be clear if you loose the third knot.

column2: மூன்று முழமும் ஒரு சுற்று, முப்பது முழமும் ஒரு சுற்று.
column3: A cloth of thirty cubits is put on with as much ease as one of three cubits.

column2: மூன்று வீட்டுக்கு முக்காலி நான்கு வீட்டுக்கு நாற்காலி.
column3: A three legged seat to three houses, and four legged seat, to four houses.

column2: மூன்று பெயர் வழிக்குத் துணை, இரண்டு பெயர் பிணையல் மாடு, ஒருவன் போனால் பரதேசி.
column3: Three may help one another on the way, two are like a yoke of oxen, one is like a pilgrim.

column2: மூன்று பொருளையும் தேடு முதிர்வயதில் ஊன்றுகோல் ஆகும்.
column3: Secure the three things virtue, wealth and happiness, they will serve as a staff in old age.

column2: மூன்றே முக்கால் நாழிகைக்குள் முத்து மழை பெய்தது, வாரி எடுக்கும்முன்னே மண்மாரி பெய்தது.
column3: It rained pearls for three and three fourths of a náligai, but before they could be gathered it rained earth.
To the profoundly learned rice is sugar.
He who is very learned, is a fool.
Externally sympathising, internally envying.
A soft bed is favourable to sleep.
Shining with truth, burning with lies.

column2: மெய் சொல்லி வாழாதான், பொய் சொல்லி வாழ்வானா?
column3: Will he who cannot prosper by truth, prosper by falshood?
Honest occupation always secures substantial results.
Learning is real wealth.

column2: மெய் மூன்றாம் பிறை, பாெய் பூரண சந்திரன்
column3: Truth is the crescent of the third day, falsehood is the full moon.
When you speak truth, the world will honour you.
Truth is beneficial.
He is the truthful man who knows the vedas.
Truth in one who cannot speak easily, may appear like falsehood.

column2: மெலிந்தவளுக்கு மெத்தப் பெலன், மேனி மினுக்கு இட்டவளுக்கு மெத்தக் கசம்.
column3: A lean woman is strong, a gaudy woman is consumptive.

column2: மெல்லப் பாயும் தண்ணீர் கல்லையும் குழியப் பாயும்.
column3: Gently flowing water will hollow even a rock.
Live with your wife.
As a dog entered a house whose floor was smeared with cow-dung.

column2: மேட்டிமைக்காரருக்கு எதிர்த்து நிற்க வேண்டும், மெத்தனக்காரருக் கிருபை அளிக்கவேண்டும்.
column3: Submit not to the haughty, and to the humble shew pity.
It is said that he is afraid, of the hill, and alarmed at the high road.

column2: மேயப் போகிற மாடு கொம்பிலே புல்லைக் கட்டிக்கொண்டு போகிறதா?
column3: Do cattle going to graze, carry grass tied to their horns?
As the cow that grazes is interrupted by the one tha3t licks it.
It is said that the braying ass interrupted the ass that was grazing.
He who praises the cowherd.

column2: மேய்த்தால் மைத்துனியை மேய்ப்பேன் இல்லாவிட்டாற் பரதேசம் போவேன்.
column3: If I am to rule I must rule over my sister-in-law, otherwise I shall go on a pilgrimage.

column2: மேய்த்தாற் கழுதை மேய்ப்பேன் இல்லாவிட்டால் பரதேசம் போவேன்.
column3: I will tend the donkeys, or go on a pilgrimage.

column2: மேலாம் மினுக்கியைக் கொண்டவன் கெட்டான், மேட்டிலே பயிரிட்டவன் கெட்டான்.
column3: He who marries a woman of great beauty will be ruined, he who sows on hilly ground, will be impoverished.
I will marry some time hence; be still till then, my neck.
Those who plough late will cry for want of food.
The wealth of the plough is unfailing.
Sages are intent upon self-denial.
Listen to the words of the great.
The most excellent is the possessor of the highest state of bliss.
Rub your back without spoiling the paint.
Live far removed from prostitutes who paint their eye-lids.
Avoid the house of a prostitute.
Like joining a bald head and the knee, by tying a knot of hair.An impossibility.

column2: மொட்டைத் தலையில் பட்டம் கட்டி ஆள வந்தானோ?
column3: Has he come to reign with a crown on his bald head?
Will lice attach themselves to a bald head?
Will a demon come on a bald head?
Like a louse on a bald head.
A bald-headed man fears not to fight.

column2: மொட்டைத் தலைக்கு ஒரு கொட்டுக் கூடை, மோழைத் தலைக்கு ஒரு தாற்றுக் கூடை.
column3: To a bald head, a cup-shaped basket, to a hornless head, a basket of goads.
A bald-headed man is a perfect cheat.
A noseless man, fit for a bald woman.

column2: மொண்டு ஆளுகிற வீட்டில் கொண்டு ஆண்டால் நிறையுமா?
column3: Will an affluent household be content to live from hand to mouth?
He beats a tomtom to get a mouthful of rice.
A promise breaker is in the wrong way.
He who is true to his word, swerves not from rectitude.
If one break his promise, his undertaking will fail.
Speak decisively.
Renounce lust.

column2: மோகம் முப்பது நாள் ஆசை அறுபது நாள்.
column3: Lust continues thirty days, desire sixty days.

column2: மோகனக் கல் ஆனாலும் பளு ஏறினால் உடையாதா?
column3: Will not even a door-step break under excessive pressure?
Danger slumbers on a mat.
To bite when apparently kissing.
When going for buttermilk why carry the pot concealed behind you?
Why conceal the vessel when going for buttermilk?
As a linga was tied to the neck of a buttermilk seller.
Silence is the bulwark of wisdom.
Silence puts an end to quarrels.
A reserved woman will destroy her family.
Matured meditation ends in disease.

column2: யானை கறுத்தால் ஆயிரம் பொன் பெறும், பூனை கறுத்தால் என்ன பெறும்?
column3: A black elephant is worth a thousand gold peices, what will a black cat fetch?

column2: யானைக்கு அறுபது அடி, அருங் குள்ளனுக்கு எழுபது அடி.
column3: Sixty feet from an elephant, seventy from a dwarf.

column2: யானை தன் தலையிலே மண்ணை வாரிக் கொட்டிக்கொள்கிறது போல.
column3: As an elephant throws sand on its head.
Like a blighted wood-apple.
Will they survive who have been trampled on by an elephant?
As a hare strained itself before an elephant.

column2: யானைமுதலான பெரிய ஜெந்துக்கள் தாழ விழுந்தால் பிழைக்கா; அதுபோல, பெரியோர் கீழே விழுந்தால் தேறமாட்டார்.
column3: Large beasts such as elephants &c., when they fall down from a high place, live not, so are the great.

column2: யானையை ஆயிரம் பொன்னுக்கு வாங்கி இருப்பு அங்குசத்திற்கு ஏமாந்து நிற்பான் ஏன்?
column3: Having bought an elephant for a hundred pieces of gold, why hesitate to buy its iron goad?

column2: யானையைத் தேடக் குடத்துக்குட் கை வைத்தது போல.
column3: As one put his hand into a jar when he was seeking an elephant.
Suppose it rain to the end of the Yuga, will a potsherd be thereby dissolved?
It turned out to be a kid that had sucked two dams.
Abstinence is the best medicine.
Double expense to the niggard.
The lies of a paramour reach as far as the door.

column2: வங்கம் தின்றால் தங்கம், வங்கம் கெட்டால் பங்கம்.
column3: When lead is eaten it becomes gold; when it is spoiled it becomes useless.

column3: The high-born are afraid of reproach, a menial is afraid of kicks.
